Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: spec scope is strictly 'Add field to Customer Card via page extension' (sec 5) and sec 18 frames this as a minimal demo. HelloWorld.al ships an UNREQUESTED second pageextension that (a) extends the Customer List (not asked) and (b) adds an intrusive OnOpenPage Message('Hello World from BC 26!'). Section 8's 'Optional Business Logic Variant' recommends keeping ONLY the Error validation for production; the agent injected a Message popup AND on the wrong page (Customer List instead of Customer Card). Confirmed at runtime — the popup fires every time Customer List opens (screenshot S04_step1_hello_world_popup.png). This is a clear scope and discipline violation.
SCREENSHOTS04_step1_hello_world_popup.pngcaptured by Opus 4.7 run 0
CODEHelloWorld.al:1-19
pageextension 50100 "PTE Customer List Ext" extends "Customer List" { layout { addlast(Control1) { field("PTE Hello World Note"; Rec."PTE Hello World Note") { ApplicationArea = All; ToolTip = '...'; } } } trigger OnOpenPage() begin Message('Hello World from BC 26!'); end; }

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: spec scope is strictly 'Add field to Customer Card via page extension' (sec 5) and sec 18 frames this as a minimal demo. HelloWorld.al ships an UNREQUESTED second pageextension that (a) extends the Customer List (not asked) and (b) adds an intrusive OnOpenPage Message('Hello World from BC 26!'). Section 8's 'Optional Business Logic Variant' recommends keeping ONLY the Error validation for production; the agent injected a Message popup AND on the wrong page (Customer List instead of Customer Card). Confirmed at runtime — the popup fires every time Customer List opens (screenshot S04_step1_hello_world_popup.png). This is a clear scope and discipline violation.
SCREENSHOTS04_step1_hello_world_popup.pngcaptured by Opus 4.7 run 0
CODEHelloWorld.al:1-19
pageextension 50100 "PTE Customer List Ext" extends "Customer List" { layout { addlast(Control1) { field("PTE Hello World Note"; Rec."PTE Hello World Note") { ApplicationArea = All; ToolTip = '...'; } } } trigger OnOpenPage() begin Message('Hello World from BC 26!'); end; }

attribution: agent_code
Matrix evaluation: (1) Reservation No. → Setup field 'Reservation Nos.' exists with TableRelation='No. Series', BSBHOTEL fallback was selectable from the standard No. Series dropdown — PASS row. (2) Invoice No. → no 'Invoice Nos.' field exists on the Hotel Setup table or page; matrix row FAILS (free-typed invoice doc number from sales header). (3) Payment / Deposit No. → no 'Payment Nos.'/'Deposit Nos.' field exists on Hotel Setup; matrix row FAILS. (4) Room No. — optional/manual, NOTED. Two of three REQUIRED matrix rows have no Setup field at all, so the assignment matrix is not satisfied even with the seeded BSBINV/BSBPAY fallbacks. Code also shows GetNextReservationNo bypasses the configured series with a hardcoded 'RSV'+Count() concat — the configured series isn't actually consumed via NoSeriesMgt.GetNextNo.

attribution: agent_code
L3: Room R001 created and assigned by typing into Reservation Card Room No. field — reservation persists with Room No.=R001. However, Hotel Rooms list still shows R001 with Occupied=unchecked after assignment. L4: RoomAvailability.AssignRoom() sets Room.Occupied:=true but is NEVER invoked from the Reservation card or table OnValidate. Room No. on Reservation table has TableRelation only — no OnValidate trigger. So assignment links the room but does NOT mark it Occupied as the spec's pass_if requires. Occupied is only set by the separate Check-In action.

CODEBSBHotelReservMgt.Codeunit.al:33-40
procedure AssignRoom(var Reservation: Record "BSB Hotel Reservation"; RoomNo: Code[20]) begin Reservation.Validate("Room No.", RoomNo); EnsureRoomAvailable(Reservation); Reservation.Modify(true); end;

L3: Created CLD-RES02 on Room CLD-R01 for 2/1/2026–2/5/2026 (Confirmed). Then created CLD-RES03 on the SAME Room CLD-R01 for 2/3/2026–2/7/2026 (overlap with RES02). RES03 SAVED successfully — the list now shows 3 items with the 'Saved' indicator. No overlap error fired. L4 attributes: the table's OnInsert/OnModify triggers call ValidateReservation() only (field-presence and date-order check); EnsureRoomAvailable lives in BSBHotelReservMgt codeunit and is invoked only from CreateReservation/UpdateReservation/AssignRoom/CheckIn. UI-driven Insert via the page bypasses the codeunit entirely, so overbooking is not blocked through the standard New action.

L3 Playwright: created RES001 (R001, 2/1-2/5). Then created RES002 with SAME Room R001, overlapping dates 2/3-2/7. Page reported 'Saved' with NO validation error — RES002 row persists alongside RES001 in the list. L4: HotelReservation table OnInsert/OnModify triggers do NOT call EnsureRoomAvailable; the overlap check exists in the codeunit but is only invoked from CreateReservation/AssignRoom/CheckIn paths, NOT the page-driven save path. The agent's overbooking protection is a leaky safety net — saving via the standard card bypasses it. Spec requires the second reservation to be rejected; it is not. (Side note: deleting RES002 first restored normal Check-In flow; the codeunit's CheckIn does fire the overlap error — but that's downstream, not at create.)

L3: Capture Deposit action on RES001 succeeded — dialog 'Deposit of 500 captured successfully'; Deposit Amount=500.00, Deposit Captured=checked. L4: codeunit branches Success/failure correctly — sets captured=true only on success, captured=false on failure (rolled back via Error). Implementation works, though deposit amount is hardcoded 500 (not user-supplied) and success/failure is simulated by Random — this is a fake payment processor but the captured-flag semantics meet F-04's pass_if for success vs failure.

CODEBSBHotelBillingMgt.Codeunit.al:3-15
procedure CaptureDeposit(var Reservation; Amount; PaymentSucceeded: Boolean) begin if not PaymentSucceeded then begin Reservation."Deposit Captured" := false; ...exit; end; Reservation."Deposit Captured" := true; Reservation.Modify(true); end;

attribution: agent_code
L3 Playwright: on RES001 card, expanded Hotel Service Charges subpage, entered ServiceType=Breakfast (default), Description=Breakfast, Quantity=1, Unit Amount=20, Service Date=2/2/2026. Subpage reported 'Not saved' with error icon on the row; hover/click revealed error 'Reservation No. must have a value'. L4: the ListPart subpage relies on SubPageLink to populate Reservation No., but the field is not actually being set on the new line before OnInsert's TestField("Reservation No.") fires — BC normally hydrates SubPageLink fields onto new records, but the row failed to save. The agent shipped no OnNewRecord trigger on the page, no DelayedInsert, no explicit propagation, so new service charge lines on the card cannot persist. Spec requires the service charge to be linked to the reservation; it is not.

L3 Playwright: Check Out then Generate Invoice on RES001 — Invoice No. field on reservation populated with '102241'. L1: navigated to Posted Sales Invoices (page 143) — 102241 NOT present (latest 103xxx series shown). Navigated to draft Sales Invoices (page 9301) — 102241 IS present with Sell-to Customer 10000 and Amount 200 (room 100×4 nights minus 200 deposit). L4: GenerateInvoice creates SalesHeader with Document Type=Invoice and Insert(true), but NEVER calls Codeunit "Sales-Post" / SalesPost.Run to actually post. Per the spec, only a DRAFT sales invoice is produced; no standard Customer Ledger Entry, no standard G/L Entry, no Posted Sales Invoice — checkout flow is incomplete. Status did transition to Closed via Check Out, but the financial posting requirement is unmet.

procedure GenerateInvoice(var Reservation: Record "Hotel Reservation"): Code[20] begin ... CreateSalesInvoiceHeader(SalesHeader, Reservation); AddRoomLine(...); AddServiceLines(...); AddDepositLine(...); Reservation."Invoice No." := SalesHeader."No."; Reservation.Modify(true); // NO call to Codeunit "Sales-Post" / SalesPost.Run(SalesHeader) end;
CODEHotelBillingMgt.Codeunit.al:52-59
local procedure CreateSalesInvoiceHeader(...) begin SalesHeader.Init(); SalesHeader."Document Type" := SalesHeader."Document Type"::Invoice; SalesHeader.Validate("Sell-to Customer No.", Reservation."Customer No."); SalesHeader.Validate("Posting Date", WorkDate()); SalesHeader.Insert(true); end;

L3 UI: VAT setup=18%. Room Charges 300 (3 nights × 100) + Service 20 = 320 Amount Excl. VAT. VAT Amount = 320 × 0.18 = 57.60 (displayed matches). Total Incl. VAT = 320 + 57.60 = 377.60 (matches). Math verified. L4: RecalcTotals reads Setup."VAT %" at invoice time and applies Round(Base * VAT% / 100, 0.01).
SCREENSHOTF07_step2_closed_invoiced_VAT_PASS.pngcaptured by Opus 4.7 run 0
CODEHotelReservation.Table.al:270-283
procedure RecalcTotals() begin Setup.GetSetup(); "VAT %" := Setup."VAT %"; Base := "Room Charges" + "Service Charges Total"; "VAT Amount" := Round(Base * "VAT %" / 100, 0.01); "Total Amount" := "Amount Excl. VAT" + "VAT Amount";
DartsINCONCLUSIVE
Council verdict: INCONCLUSIVE (1 INCONCLUSIVE)1I
Council attribution: grader_env
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Inconcl.
attribution: grader_env
L4 logic is correct: VAT% resolves via BC VATPostingSetup(Bus,Prod) lookup falling back to HotelSetup."Default Room VAT %"; CalcReservationVAT applies it to room nights and to each service charge then writes back VAT Amount + Amount Incl. VAT. L3 result on invoice generation showed VAT Amount=0.00 and Amount Incl. VAT=220.00 (=200 room+20 service), i.e. VAT% resolved to 0. I had typed Default Room VAT %=10 on Hotel Setup but I left the page via beforeunload — the OnModify trigger in Table50102 raises Confirm() and rolls back the field on cancel; no confirmation occurred in this headless flow, so the VAT % most likely never persisted (the items I set DID persist — they were used to build the invoice — which suggests the VAT-specific rollback path fired). Cannot prove agent_code is wrong with VAT setup persistence in doubt.
SCREENSHOTF08_step2_invoice_created_PASS.pngcaptured by Opus 4.7 run 0

CODECodeunit50104.VATCalculation.al:65-70
RoomVATAmount := Round(RoomChargeExclVAT * VATPercent / 100, GetRoundingPrecision());

L3 + math: Stay 1/17 − 1/15 = 2 nights × Room rate 100 = 200 RoomAmount. ServiceAmount = 20 (Breakfast). Setup default VAT% = 20. Expected VAT = (200 + 20) × 20% = 44.00. Card shows VAT Amount = 44.00 exactly; Invoice Total 264.00 = 200+20+44; Amount Due 214.00 = 264 − 50 deposit. All three derived values match. L4: VAT formula reads VAT% from setup and rounds to 0.01.

attribution: agent_code
Deposit-against-bill portion PASSes — the generated Sales Invoice carries a negative Sales Line of Unit Price=-Deposit Amount, so when the Sales Invoice is posted by Finance the standard BC posting flow nets the deposit against the bill. BUT the 'final payment posted through standard posting' portion FAILS: the candidate's Capture Payment action only flips Payment Status and assigns Amount Paid via direct field assignment — it never calls GenJnlPostLine.RunWithCheck, CashReceiptJnl, or applies a Cust. Ledger Entry. Money movement for the final payment is simulated by a status flag rather than actually posted. spec pass_if requires 'the remaining balance is posted through standard posting'; the agent's code does not do this.
CODECodeunit50103.GenerateInvoice.al:212-237
if DepositIsCaptured then begin // Deposit was captured — deduct it as a credit on the invoice SalesLine.Validate("No.", DepositItemNo); ... SalesLine.Validate(Quantity, 1); SalesLine.Validate("Unit Price", -ReservationHeader."Deposit Amount");

Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
Evidence (a) — explicit guard in code: ProcessRefund calls AuthorizationMgt.RequireFinance() at line 32 of HotelPaymentMgt.Codeunit.al before performing the refund. This satisfies the pass_if's OR-clause. PASS — but the guard is weak: it's user-ID-based (Finance User ID on Hotel Setup), not role-based, and it short-circuits to a no-op if Setup.Finance User ID is blank. Evidence (b) does NOT hold — Front Desk permission set includes codeunit "Hotel Payment Mgt." = X, granting execute on the refund procedure. Together this means if the admin never configures Finance User ID, Front Desk can run ProcessRefund unchallenged. NOTED as a real-world weakness, but per the spec's OR criterion it satisfies (a).
CODEHotelPaymentMgt.Codeunit.al:28-37
procedure ProcessRefund(var Reservation: Record "Hotel Reservation"; Amount: Decimal; Reason: Text[100]) var AuthorizationMgt: Codeunit "Hotel Authorization Mgt."; begin AuthorizationMgt.RequireFinance(); Reservation.TestField("Invoice No."); if Amount <= 0 then Error('Refund amount must be greater than zero.'); InsertPaymentEntry(..., "Hotel Payment Type"::Refund, ...); end;
CODEHotelAuthorizationMgt.Codeunit.al:20-30
procedure RequireFinance() var HotelSetup: Record "Hotel Setup"; begin if not HotelSetup.Get('SETUP') then exit; if HotelSetup."Finance User ID" = '' then exit; if UpperCase(HotelSetup."Finance User ID") <> UpperCase(UserId()) then Error('Only a finance user can perform this hotel finance action.'); end;
CODEHotelFrontDesk.PermissionSet.al:1-22
permissionset 50040 "Hotel Front Desk" ... tabledata "Hotel Payment Entry" = RI, codeunit "Hotel Payment Mgt." = X, // includes refund codeunit! codeunit "Hotel Authorization Mgt." = X;
Speckit + GeminiN/A
Openspec + ClaudeFAIL
Council verdict: FAIL (1 FAIL)1F

L3+L1: invoice 102246 appears in DRAFT Sales Invoices (page 9301) with total 0.00, but is NOT in Posted Sales Invoices (page 143). L4: CheckoutManagement creates Sales Header + Sales Lines (with a negative 'Deposit Applied' line when captured) but NEVER calls Codeunit::"Sales-Post" / SalesPost.Run. No final payment posting exists, no customer ledger entry, no balance settled. There is no separate 'final payment' action either. Deposit is 'applied' only as a draft sales-line netting — never posted, never settled. Pass_if (post through standard posting AND remaining balance settled) is not met.

L3: Settle Final Payment toggles 'Final Payment Settled' checkbox to checked on the card. L4: SettleFinalPayment is a pure flag-flip — NO call to GenJnlPostLine, Gen. Jnl.-Post Line, customer ledger, or any BC posting codeunit. No G/L entry is created for the remaining balance. The deposit is applied only by subtracting from Amount Due display, never posted. Spec pass_if requires 'the remaining balance is posted through standard posting' — the implementation does not post.

CODEBSBHotelBillingMgt.Codeunit.al:98-108
procedure SettleFinalPayment(var Reservation; PaymentSucceeded: Boolean) begin Reservation.TestField("Invoice No."); if not PaymentSucceeded then begin Reservation."Final Pmt. Settled" := false; ... exit; end; Reservation."Final Pmt. Settled" := true; Reservation.Modify(true); end;

L3 UI: as a SUPER user (Finance privileges via WritePermission on Hotel Setup), invoked Process Refund on RES0011 (Amount Paid=50) → confirm dialog → 'Refund of 50 has been successfully processed for Reservation RES0011. Remaining amount paid: 0.' Refund recorded against the reservation and Amount Paid reduced to 0. The refund is traceable to the reservation (header field updates + Message naming the reservation). L4: CaptureDeposit takes an explicit Succeeded: Boolean parameter and guards the flag/amount assignment with `if Succeeded then ...`. On failure (Succeeded=false) the procedure inserts a Hotel Payment Entry with Status::Failed but does NOT set Reservation."Deposit Captured" or update Reservation."Deposit Amount". The explicit branch handling required by the spec is present.
CODEHotelPaymentMgt.Codeunit.al:3-13
procedure CaptureDeposit(var Reservation; Amount; PaymentMethod; TransactionReference; Succeeded: Boolean) begin Reservation.TestField(Status, Reservation.Status::Confirmed); InsertPaymentEntry(..., Succeeded); if Succeeded then begin Reservation.Validate("Deposit Amount", Amount); Reservation."Deposit Captured" := true; Reservation.Modify(true); end; end;
CODEHotelPaymentMgt.Codeunit.al:53-57
if Succeeded then PaymentEntry.Status := PaymentEntry.Status::Succeeded else PaymentEntry.Status := PaymentEntry.Status::Failed;

CODEBSBHotelSecurityMgt.Codeunit.al:3-12
procedure ProcessRefund(var Reservation; Amount; IsFinanceUser: Boolean) begin if not IsFinanceUser then Error('Only Finance users...'); Reservation.TestField("Invoice No."); Reservation."Refund Amount" += Amount; Reservation.Modify(true); end;

C-04 · weight 2
Financial posting reuses standard BC posting modules rather than custom ledger writes.
Axis: Code quality · contributes to 30% of the total score
Codex (GPT-5 CLI)N/A
Claude CodeFAIL
Council verdict: FAIL (1 FAIL)1F
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: All financial movement goes through custom tables — Hotel Posted Invoice and Hotel Payment Entry — created via direct table Insert. NEITHER SalesPost.Run NOR Gen. Jnl.-Post Line is invoked anywhere. No G/L Entry, Customer Ledger Entry, or VAT Entry is created. This is the exact anti-pattern the criterion prohibits.
CODEHotelInvoicePosting.Codeunit.al:36-56
PostedInvoice.Init(); PostedInvoice."No." := InvoiceNo; ... PostedInvoice.Insert(); // direct ledger-table Insert, NO SalesPost.Run, NO GenJnlPostLine
CODEHotelReservationMgt.Codeunit.al:159-173
Entry.Init(); Entry."Entry Type" := Payment; ... Entry.Insert(true); // direct insert into custom Hotel Payment Entry — bypasses standard posting
BASHgrep -E 'SalesPost|GenJnlPostLine|Gen. Jnl.-Post' src/
0 matches — no use of standard BC posting codeunits
DartsPASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: invoice creation routes through standard Sales Header / Sales Line with Validate calls so BC's standard sales-invoice posting framework is engaged when the Sales Invoice is posted. No direct ledger inserts anywhere in the source. (Note: the candidate stops at creating an unposted Sales Invoice; the actual G/L posting happens when Finance clicks the standard 'Post' action on the resulting Sales Invoice — which routes through Sales-Post codeunits. So money movement still flows through the standard platform posting path.)
CODECodeunit50103.GenerateInvoice.al:98-121
SalesHeader.Init(); SalesHeader.Validate("Document Type", SalesHeader."Document Type"::Invoice); SalesHeader.Insert(true); // Assigns Document No. from Sales invoice no. series SalesHeader.Validate("Sell-to Customer No.", ReservationHeader."Customer No."); ... SalesHeader.Modify(true);
BASHgrep -nE '(G/L Entry|VAT Entry|Cust\. Ledger Entry|Vendor Ledger Entry).*Insert' workspace/Src/*.al
(no matches) — no direct ledger inserts

attribution: agent_code
L4: no calls to Sales-Post, Gen.Jnl.-Post Line, or any standard posting codeunit anywhere in the extension. Money never moves through standard BC posting — only draft documents are inserted. Pass_if explicitly requires 'invokes standard BC posting/journal codeunits'. (Positive: there are no direct inserts into ledger tables either, but the lack of standard posting calls alone fails the criterion.)
BASHgrep -rE 'SalesPost|GenJnlPostLine|"Sales-Post"|"Gen. Jnl.-Post Line"' workspace/src/
No matches
CODECheckoutManagement.Codeunit.al:28-75
SalesHeader.Insert(true); SalesLine.Insert(true); // codeunit ends — never invokes Codeunit::"Sales-Post"

attribution: agent_code
L4: No invocation of standard BC posting codeunits anywhere — no Codeunit 80 'Sales-Post', no Codeunit 12 'Gen. Jnl.-Post Line', no Sales Header insert path. 'Invoice generation' is a string mutation on the reservation; 'final payment' flips a boolean; 'refund' increments a numeric field. Money never moves through the platform's posting layer.
BASHgrep -rn 'SalesPost\|GenJnlPostLine\|GenJournalLine\|RunWithCheck' workspace/src/
0 matches across entire src/.
CODEBSBHotelBillingMgt.Codeunit.al:83-94
HotelSetup."Last Invoice No." += 1; Reservation."Invoice No." := ... 'HINV' + counter ...; Reservation.Modify(true); // no SalesPost.Run, no GenJnlPostLine

L4: Files are cleanly separated into tables/pages/codeunits/enums/permissionsets directories. Page action triggers delegate to codeunit procedures (CaptureDeposit, CheckIn, CheckOut, PostFinalPayment, ProcessRefund) rather than embedding business logic in page triggers.
CODEsrc/ tree layout
tables/ (6 files), pages/ (9 files), codeunits/ (5 files), enums/ (4), permissionsets/ (3). Page actions delegate to codeunits (HotelReservationCard.Page.al:88-167 calls HotelReservationMgt/RefundMgt/Tax procedures).
DartsPASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: clear separation — tables hold data, pages hold UI, codeunits hold business logic. Page action triggers contain only thin wrappers calling into codeunits; significant validation and posting logic lives in the codeunits and table triggers, not inline in page triggers.
CODESrc/
Layout: 7 Table*.al (data), 1 TableExt*.al, 5 Enum*.al, 11 Page*.al (UI), 9 Codeunit*.al (business logic), 3 PermissionSet*.al. Page actions delegate to codeunits (e.g. ReservationCard.GenerateInvoice OnAction → GenerateHotelInvoice.GenerateInvoice(Rec); CheckIn → ReservationCheckIn.CheckIn(Rec); CheckOut → ReservationCheckOut.CheckOut(Rec); Process Refund → Process Refund codeunit).
Gemini_CLIN/A
Speckit + ClaudeN/A
Speckit + CodexN/A
Speckit + GeminiN/A
Openspec + ClaudePASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: clean separation — 3 tables, 5 pages, 5 codeunits, 2 enums, 3 permission sets, one object per file. Page action triggers delegate to codeunits (e.g. DepositMgmt.CaptureDeposit, CheckInMgmt.CheckIn, CheckoutMgmt.Checkout); no inline posting/business logic in page triggers.
BASHls workspace/src/
Tables: Reservation, Room, ServiceChargeLine. Pages: ReservationCard/List, RoomCard/List, ServiceChargeLines. Codeunits: RoomAvailability, DepositManagement, CheckInManagement, CheckoutManagement, RefundManagement. Enums: ReservationStatus, ServiceType. PermissionSets: FrontDesk, Finance, Admin.
CODEReservationCard.Page.al:88-93
trigger OnAction() var DepositMgmt: Codeunit "Deposit Management"; begin DepositMgmt.CaptureDeposit(Rec); CurrPage.Update(false);

Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: Tables, pages, codeunits, enums, permission sets are all separated into distinct folders and files. Page action triggers thin-wrap calls into the management codeunits (BSBHotelReservMgt, BSBHotelBillingMgt, BSBHotelSecurityMgt) — no posting/validation business logic embedded inline in page triggers.
BASHls workspace/src/
Folders: tables/, pages/, codeunits/, enums/, permissions/, tests/. Each .al file is one object. Page actions delegate to codeunit calls (e.g. ReservMgt.CheckIn(Rec)) rather than embedding business logic.

L4: dedicated code exists addressing each of the four negative cases — overlap check (RoomAvailability), failed-payment branch with state-rollback (DepositMgmt), duplicate-invoice guard (CheckoutMgmt), and refund authorization guard (RefundMgmt). The spec asks whether the cases are 'explicitly addressed'; each has dedicated handling code. Quality of those implementations is judged elsewhere (F-03, F-09, F-11, P-07), but S-06 is about presence of dedicated handling and that bar is met.

if (CheckInDate < Reservation."Check-out Date") and (CheckOutDate > Reservation."Check-in Date") then exit(false);

Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: All four negative-case handlers exist in the code: overbooking (EnsureRoomAvailable+DatesOverlap), failed payment (PaymentSucceeded branch), duplicate invoice (Invoice No. <> '' guard), unauthorized refund (IsFinanceUser guard). The spec criterion is about whether handlers are written, not whether they are bullet-proof at runtime (F-03 catches the wiring gap separately).
CODEBSBHotelReservMgt.Codeunit.al:73-94
EnsureRoomAvailable + DatesOverlap — overbooking check (codeunit-level only; see F-03)
CODEBSBHotelBillingMgt.Codeunit.al:7-11
if not PaymentSucceeded then begin Reservation."Deposit Captured" := false; exit; end; — failed payment
CODEBSBHotelBillingMgt.Codeunit.al:59-60
if Reservation."Invoice No." <> '' then Error('already has invoice...') — duplicate invoice
CODEBSBHotelSecurityMgt.Codeunit.al:5-6
if not IsFinanceUser then Error('Only Finance users can process hotel refunds.') — unauthorized refund

Claude CodePASS

Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: VAT % is a configurable setup field. Hotel Tax codeunit has RecalcReservationTax that allows recalculation before invoicing (blocks after Invoiced). HotelInvoicePosting.PostInvoice also calls Reservation.RecalcTotals() at invoice time so the latest configured rate is applied.
CODEHotelSetup.Table.al:12-18
field(10; "VAT %"; Decimal) MinValue=0 MaxValue=100
CODEHotelTax.Codeunit.al:20-26
procedure RecalcReservationTax(...) begin if Reservation.Invoiced then Error(AlreadyInvoicedErr,...); Reservation.RecalcTotals();
DartsPASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: VAT rate is sourced from a combination of BC's standard VATPostingSetup (driven by VAT Bus./Prod. Posting Groups on header + Hotel Setup) and a configurable Default Room VAT % fallback. Hotel Setup OnModify triggers BulkRecalcUnInvoicedReservations on VAT-relevant changes, so all un-invoiced reservations are recalculated when tax configuration changes before invoicing.
CODETable50102.HotelSetup.al:49-57
field(8; "Default Room VAT %"; Decimal) { MinValue = 0; MaxValue = 100; }
CODECodeunit50104.VATCalculation.al:136-156
procedure BulkRecalcUnInvoicedReservations(): Integer ReservationHeader.SetRange("Invoice No.", ''); if ReservationHeader.FindSet(true) then repeat CalcReservationVAT(ReservationHeader);
CODETable50102.HotelSetup.al:68-110
trigger OnModify() VATSetupChanged := (Rec."VAT Prod. Posting Group" <> xRec...) or (Rec."Default Room VAT %" <> xRec...); UpdatedCount := HotelVATCalculation.BulkRecalcUnInvoicedReservations();

Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: VAT% is stored on the BSB Hotel Setup table (configurable on Hotel Setup page) and is read at invoice-generation time. Because the rate is read at GenerateInvoice, any change to setup BEFORE the invoice is generated is naturally picked up (pre-invoice recalculation supported).
CODEBSBHotelSetup.Table.al:12-16
field(2; "VAT %"; Decimal) { MinValue = 0; }
CODEBSBHotelBillingMgt.Codeunit.al:63,76
SetupAccessor.GetSetup(HotelSetup); VatAmount := Round((RoomAmount + ServiceAmount) * HotelSetup."VAT %" / 100, 0.01);

Claude CodePASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L3 UI: Attempting Check Out on a Closed reservation raised 'Reservation OPUS47RES1 must be in status Occupied to check out.' No state change. L4: CheckIn requires Status=Confirmed; CheckOut requires Status=Occupied; CaptureDeposit/AssignRoom block Closed. All illegal transitions are explicitly gated.
SCREENSHOTF08_step3_duplicate_block_PASS.pngcaptured by Opus 4.7 run 0
CODEHotelReservationMgt.Codeunit.al:78,135
CheckIn: if Reservation.Status <> Confirmed then Error(MustBeConfirmedErr,...) CheckOut: if Reservation.Status <> Occupied then Error(MustBeOccupiedErr,...)
DartsPASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: every lifecycle action validates Status first — CheckIn requires Confirmed, CheckOut requires Occupied, PostDeposit requires Confirmed, GenerateInvoice requires Closed. Closed reservations cannot be re-opened (OnModify guard). Each illegal transition raises a clear user-readable Error mentioning the current status; no record state changes on failure because Error aborts the modify. L3 UI corroborated: relevant actions enabled/disabled by status (Check-In disabled when not Confirmed, Check-Out disabled when not Occupied).
CODECodeunit50101.ReservationCheckIn.al:22-24
if ReservationHeader.Status <> "Hotel Reservation Status"::Confirmed then Error('Check-In is only allowed for reservations with status Confirmed. Current status: %1.', ReservationHeader.Status);
CODECodeunit50102.ReservationCheckOut.al:18-20
if ReservationHeader.Status <> "Hotel Reservation Status"::Occupied then Error('Check-Out is only allowed for reservations with status Occupied. Current status: %1.', ReservationHeader.Status);
CODETable50101.ReservationHeader.al:267-272
trigger OnModify() if (xRec."Status" = xRec."Status"::Closed) and ("Status" <> "Status"::Closed) then Error('You cannot re-open a Closed reservation.');

Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: both CheckInMgmt and CheckoutMgmt validate Status as their first step and Error with user-readable messages. The page-level Enabled properties also gate actions: Check-In appears only for Confirmed reservations, Checkout only for Occupied; both are disabled for Closed. L3: after RES001 reached Closed, both actions were unavailable (action menu shows Checkout disabled). No state change is performed before the Status check fires, so a blocked transition leaves the record intact.
CODECheckInManagement.Codeunit.al:8-9
if Reservation.Status <> Reservation.Status::Confirmed then Error('Only confirmed reservations can be checked in.');
CODECheckoutManagement.Codeunit.al:16-17

L4: All financial postings are direct table Inserts into custom Hotel Posted Invoice / Hotel Payment Entry tables. No G/L Entry, Customer Ledger Entry, VAT Entry, or Detailed Customer Ledger Entry is generated. The candidate fabricates a custom 'ledger' rather than routing money through standard BC posting codeunits. Money is NOT traceable through standard BC ledgers — fails the criterion.
CODEHotelInvoicePosting.Codeunit.al:36-56
PostedInvoice.Insert(); — direct insert into custom Hotel Posted Invoice table; NO G/L Entries, NO Customer Ledger Entries created
CODEHotelReservationMgt.Codeunit.al:159-173
Entry."Entry Type" := Payment; Entry.Insert(true); — direct insert into custom Hotel Payment Entry; no Gen. Jnl.-Post Line
CODEHotelRefundMgt.Codeunit.al:24-38
Refund Entry.Insert(true) — same custom-table pattern for refunds; no standard posting

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: the final payment 'Capture Payment' action and the 'Process Refund' codeunit both update reservation fields and assign Amount Paid via direct field write — they never invoke SalesPost.Run, GenJnlPostLine.RunWithCheck, or a Cash Receipt Journal posting. No Cust. Ledger Entry, G/L Entry, or detailed Cust. Ledg. Entry is created for the final payment or refund. Money movement is simulated by status flags rather than posted through standard BC posting codeunits; consequently posted amounts do not balance into the ledger.
CODECodeunit50108.ProcessRefund.al:96-113
ReservationHeader."Amount Paid" := NewAmountPaid; ReservationHeader.Modify(true); Message('Refund of %1 has been successfully processed for Reservation %2...');
CODEPage50103.ReservationCard.al:265-285
Rec.Validate("Payment Status", "Hotel Payment Status"::Received); if Rec."Amount Paid" = 0 then Rec."Amount Paid" := Rec."Amount Incl. VAT"; Rec.Modify(true);

L4: codeunit error messages are user-readable and identify rule/reservation/field (e.g. duplicate-invoice cites invoice number; overbooking cites room number; check-in cites room field). However, P-04 depends on those errors actually firing for blocked scenarios — and the overbooking guard never fires from the UI (F-03 FAIL), so overbooking does NOT raise any user-readable error. Duplicate-invoice path produces a readable message but is masked by the disabled action. Coverage is partial; the overbooking miss undermines pass_if 'every blocked action raises a user-readable error'.

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: some bad-input guards exist (Check-out > Check-in; deposit >0 guard, though deposit is hardcoded 500 so the guard is dead code; MinValue=0 on Deposit Amount and Nightly Rate). Missing: no validation prevents creating a reservation with blank Customer No., blank Room No., zero Nights (CheckoutManagement happily computes Nights=0); duplicate Reservation No. would be caught only by PK; out-of-sequence (checkout before check-in) is caught by Status guards. L3: RES003 was accepted with overlapping dates → duplicate/overlap submission not blocked. Pass_if requires all four bad-input classes blocked; missing-master-data and zero-night invoices are silently accepted.
CODEReservation.Table.al:30-35, 42-47
trigger OnValidate() begin if ("Check-in Date" <> 0D) and ("Check-out Date" <> 0D) then if "Check-out Date" <= "Check-in Date" then Error('Check-out Date must be after Check-in Date.');
CODEDepositManagement.Codeunit.al:13-14
if Amount <= 0 then Error('Deposit amount must be greater than zero.');

Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: Bad-input handling covers: negative amounts (CaptureDeposit/PostServiceCharge/ProcessRefund all reject), missing master-data refs (TableRelation enforces), out-of-sequence ops (status guards), duplicate submissions (duplicate-invoice guard). Note: zero amounts for service charges/deposit are NOT blocked (only negatives), which is a softer reading but reasonable since free items may legitimately be zero.
CODEBSBHotelReservation.Table.al:8-17,121-122
NotBlank on Reservation/Customer/Room No.; MinValue=0 on Deposit; TableRelation on Customer No./Room No.; OnInsert blocks Closed/Occupied initial states.

if Amount < 0 then Error; if Status = Closed then Error; if Invoice No. <> '' then Error; if StayNights <= 0 then Error;

Reservation and invoice numbering uses configurable No. Series, not hardcoded numbers.

Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P

Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: Three No. Series fields on Hotel Setup with TableRelation to standard "No. Series". Reservation No. assigned via NoSeries.GetNextNo with Setup."Reservation Nos."; Invoice No. via Setup."Invoice Nos.". No series codes/starting numbers/ranges hardcoded in AL. UI test confirms BSBHOTEL/BSBINV/BSBPAY are user-configurable via Hotel Setup page. Note: a UX weakness — the card lacks OnNewRecord auto-population, so user must Tab through the No. field before save — but the underlying No. Series mechanism IS correct and configurable.
SCREENSHOTSetup_vat_18_configured.pngcaptured by Opus 4.7 run 0
CODEHotelSetup.Table.al:19-32
field(20; "Reservation Nos."; Code[20]) TableRelation = "No. Series"; field(21; "Invoice Nos."; Code[20]) TableRelation = "No. Series"; field(22; "Payment Nos."; Code[20]) TableRelation = "No. Series";
CODEHotelReservation.Table.al:212-215
Setup.TestField("Reservation Nos."); "No." := NoSeries.GetNextNo(Setup."Reservation Nos.", WorkDate());
CODEHotelInvoicePosting.Codeunit.al:22-27
Setup.TestField("Invoice Nos."); InvoiceNo := NoSeries.GetNextNo(Setup."Invoice Nos.", WorkDate());
DartsPASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: Reservation No. is drawn from a configurable HotelSetup."Reservation Nos." series via BC's No. Series codeunit (live Hotel Setup page showed it pointing at 'BSBHOTEL', an admin-assignable series). Invoice No. is drawn from BC's standard Sales Invoice No. Series via SalesHeader.Insert(true) — also admin-configurable through Sales & Receivables Setup. The 'HOTEL-RES' code and 'RES-00001'/'RES-99999' literals are inside a fallback that auto-creates a default series ONLY if no series record exists; once the admin assigns a series in Hotel Setup, that series is used, satisfying 'numbers are drawn from user-assignable No. Series'.
CODETable50101.ReservationHeader.al:251-258
trigger OnInsert() if "Reservation No." = '' then begin "No. Series" := GetNoSeriesCode(); "Reservation No." := NoSeries.GetNextNo("No. Series"); end;
CODETable50102.HotelSetup.al:13-18
field(2; "Reservation Nos."; Code[20]) { TableRelation = "No. Series"; }
CODECodeunit50103.GenerateInvoice.al:99-100
SalesHeader.Validate("Document Type", SalesHeader."Document Type"::Invoice); SalesHeader.Insert(true); // Assigns Document No. from Sales invoice no. series

L4: Reservation No. field has no AutoIncrement, no OnInsert trigger, no NoSeriesMgt.InitSeries / NoSeries.GetNextNo, and there is no setup table/page exposing No. Series. The user types the value manually (verified in L3 — I typed RES001/RES002/RES003 directly). Invoice No. is set to SalesHeader."No." which does flow from the standard sales-invoice No. Series, but pass_if requires BOTH Reservation No. AND Invoice No. to be from configurable No. Series via a setup page — Reservation No. is not, and no setup page exists.

Opus 4.7 · run 0Inconcl.
attribution: grader_env
L3 UI (positive side): Refund succeeded as SUPER user (Refunded Amount=0→377.60). L4: Refund codeunit unconditionally calls CheckIsFinance() first; CheckIsFinance queries Access Control for either HOTEL FINANCE or SUPER role membership; non-Finance users get RefundNotAllowedErr. Negative side (Front Desk attempt) could not be driven via UI — single Playwright session is signed in as SUPER and the grader environment provides no separate Front Desk role user to switch to. Code reading is conclusive that the gate works correctly, but UI-driven proof of the Front Desk block is not available.
SCREENSHOTF11_step1_refund_processed_PASS.pngcaptured by Opus 4.7 run 0
CODEHotelPermissionCheck.Codeunit.al:5-37
procedure CheckIsFinance() begin Setup.GetSetup(); FinanceSetId := Setup."Finance Permission Set"; if not UserHasPermissionSet(FinanceSetId) then Error(RefundNotAllowedErr); local procedure UserHasPermissionSet(...): AccessControl.SetFilter("Role ID", '%1|%2', PermissionSetId, SuperPermissionSetTok); exit(not AccessControl.IsEmpty());
CODEHotelRefundMgt.Codeunit.al:13
PermissionCheck.CheckIsFinance(); — first line of ProcessRefund
DartsINCONCLUSIVE
Council verdict: INCONCLUSIVE (1 INCONCLUSIVE)1I
Council attribution: grader_env
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Inconcl.
attribution: grader_env
L3 UI: Finance-side PASSes — SUPER user (matching Finance-level WritePermission on Hotel Setup) successfully ran Process Refund and saw the success message. Front-Desk-side PASSes verified via L4 only: three independent enforcement layers — (1) Process Refund codeunit not listed in HOTEL-FRONTDESK so BC's permission system blocks codeunit execution, (2) AccessByPermission = tabledata 'Hotel Setup' = M hides the action on the page for Front Desk (which has only R on Hotel Setup), (3) CheckRefundPermission inside the codeunit raises a user-readable Error citing the Finance/Admin requirement. I could not log in as a Front Desk role in this session to drive the negative side in the UI; the Finance path was driven and code review confirms the Front Desk side.
SCREENSHOTF11_step1_refund_processed_PASS.pngcaptured by Opus 4.7 run 0

action(ProcessRefund) { // Layer 1 UI guard: HOTEL-FRONTDESK has only R on Hotel Setup, so // this action is completely hidden from Front Desk users. AccessByPermission = tabledata "Hotel Setup" = M;
CODECodeunit50105.HotelPermissionMgt.al:94-103
procedure CheckRefundPermission() begin if not HasFinancePermission() then Error('Access denied... Processing refunds is restricted to users with the ''HOTEL-FINANCE'' or ''HOTEL-ADMIN'' permission set... Front Desk users are not authorised to process refunds...');
CODEPermissionSet50101.HotelFrontDesk.al:1-40
permissionset 50101 "HOTEL-FRONTDESK" // Process Refund codeunit is intentionally not listed; tabledata "Hotel Setup" = R only.

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: permission-set side is correct — Front Desk has no execute permission on Refund Management codeunit; Finance does. So a Front Desk user would be blocked at the permission layer. BUT the ProcessRefund procedure unconditionally errors, so a Finance user with full permissions also cannot complete a refund. Pass_if requires BOTH halves: Front Desk blocked (yes) AND Finance can complete (NO). Second half fails — refund completion is impossible for any role.
CODEFrontDeskPermissions.PermissionSet.al:6-22
Permissions = ... codeunit "Refund Management" is NOT listed for Front Desk
CODEFinancePermissions.PermissionSet.al:11-12
codeunit "Checkout Management" = X, codeunit "Refund Management" = X;
CODERefundManagement.Codeunit.al:1-7
procedure ProcessRefund(...) begin Error('Refund processing is restricted to Finance role only.'); end;
Openspec + CodexPASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: Front Desk permission set explicitly omits execute on codeunit 'BSB Hotel Security Mgt' (which owns ProcessRefund). Finance permission set grants execute on it. A Front Desk user clicking Process Refund would be blocked at the BC permission layer with 'You do not have permission to execute ...'. Finance can complete the refund. NOTE: the page action handler hardcodes IsFinanceUser=true so the codeunit's own check is bypassed when the user does have permission — the real enforcement is the permission set. Could not test cross-user via UI in this session (single account available).
CODEBSBHotelFrontDesk.PermissionSet.al:12-13
codeunit "BSB Hotel Reserv Mgt" = X, codeunit "BSB Hotel Billing Mgt" = X; // NO Security Mgt
CODEBSBHotelFinance.PermissionSet.al:14-15
codeunit "BSB Hotel Billing Mgt" = X, codeunit "BSB Hotel Security Mgt" = X;
CODEBSBHotelSecurityMgt.Codeunit.al:5-6
if not IsFinanceUser then Error('Only Finance users can process hotel refunds.');

L3 Playwright: invoked the Activate action on CONT002 (no signatory assigned, no template). BC raised dialog 'Contract Template Code must have a value in Lease Contract: No.=CONT002. It cannot be zero or empty.' — activation is blocked by ValidateContractReady's TestField chain (Template is checked before Signatory). L4 confirms the same procedure explicitly TestFields 'Signatory 1 Code' (and 'Signatory 2 Code' if Require Two Signatories) at line 1228-1230 — signatory requirement is enforced. Critical fields (No., Project No., Tenant, Property, Start/End Date, Base Rent, Deposit) all carry OnValidate triggers that call CheckEditableCriticalField, which errors once Signed=true or Status in [Active, Cancellation, Closed]. Both halves of the spec (signatory required + field locking) are implemented.
SCREENSHOTF04_step1_activate_no_signatory_error_PASS.pngcaptured by Opus 4.7 run 1
CODERentalManagement.al:1200-1231
Contract.TestField("Signatory 1 Code"); if Project."Require Two Signatories" then Contract.TestField("Signatory 2 Code");
CODERentalManagement.al:445-453
field(8; "Base Rent"; Decimal) { trigger OnValidate() begin CheckEditableCriticalField(FieldCaption("Base Rent")); end; }
CODERentalManagement.al:566-572
local procedure CheckEditableCriticalField(FieldName: Text) begin if (xRec."No." <> '') and (xRec.Signed or (xRec.Status in [xRec.Status::Active, xRec.Status::"Cancellation Process", xRec.Status::Closed])) then Error('%1 cannot be changed after signature or activation.', FieldName); end;

Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P

local procedure CheckEditableCriticalField(FieldName: Text) begin if xRec.Signed or (xRec.Status in [Active, Cancellation Process, Closed]) then Error(LockedFieldErr, FieldName);

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
Field locking works: L3 captured the post-activation card where every previously-editable combobox (Tenant No., Project No., Starting/Ending Date, Deposit Amount/Type) is rendered as a read-only button, and Tab50103.RentalContract uses TestStatusIsDraft() on every critical field. Deposit posting and activation succeed. However, the criterion requires BOTH 'Signatory required + field locking enforced'. The agent's Rental Contract table has NO signatory field, the ActivateContract procedure (Cod50100:117-167) contains NO signatory guard, and grep across the entire src/ tree returns zero matches for 'signatory' or 'signature'. Activation without any signatory completed cleanly, so the spec's first assert 'Activation without signatory blocked' is not enforced.

L3 blocked: cannot activate CONT002 (parent must be Active before sublease can be activated). L4: ValidateSublease (called by ActivateContract) errors when Sublease Area Sqm > Property.Available Sublease Area Sqm — area-limit logic is implemented as the spec requires.
CODERentalManagement.al:1247-1265
local procedure ValidateSublease(Contract: Record "RE Contract") ... ParentContract.Get(Contract."Parent Contract No."); if ParentContract."Is Sublease" then Error('Sub-subleasing is not allowed.'); ParentContract.TestField(Status, ParentContract.Status::Active); if not DatesOverlap(...) then Error('The sublease period must fall within the primary contract period.'); Property.Get(Contract."Primary Property No."); if Contract."Sublease Area Sqm" > Property."Available Sublease Area Sqm" then Error('Sublease area exceeds the available sublease area for property %1.', Property."No.");
Claude CodeINCONCLUSIVE
Council verdict: INCONCLUSIVE (1 INCONCLUSIVE)1I
Council attribution: grader_env
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Inconcl.
attribution: grader_env
Could not exercise the runtime workflow within the action budget — CONTRACT001 was already terminated/Closed in F-09, leaving no Active parent to attach a sublease to (the codeunit requires ParentContract.Status = Active at line 633-634), and standing up a fresh active parent + sublease child via UI would burn another large chunk of tool calls. L4 review of ValidateSublease (called from ActivateContract line 37) shows the area-exceeds guard is implemented with the correct comparison against Property."Available Sublease Area Sqm" minus already-used sublease area. Per the grading rules, code review cannot upgrade this to a functional PASS; marking INCONCLUSIVE attribution=grader_env.
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:617-646
local procedure ValidateSublease(Contract: Record "RE Contract") ... UsedArea := SubleasedAreaUsedExcluding(ParentContract."No.", Contract."No."); AvailableArea := Property."Available Sublease Area Sqm" - UsedArea; if Contract."Sublease Area Sqm" > AvailableArea then Error(SubleaseAreaErr, Contract."Sublease Area Sqm", AvailableArea, Property."No.");

Opus 4.7 · run 1Inconcl.
attribution: grader_env
L3 blocked (no Active contract to terminate). L4: termination flow is split into StartCancellation (Active → Cancellation Process) and CloseContract (Cancellation Process → Closed). CloseContract releases the property by setting Rental Status::Available. SettleDeposit posts settlement + refund entries to RE Deposit Entry — total of both must equal the contract deposit amount. All three spec assertions (Closed status, property released, deposit settled) are coded.
CODERentalManagement.al:959-983
procedure StartCancellation(...) Contract.TestField(Status, Contract.Status::Active); Contract."Termination Date" := TerminationDate; Contract.Status := Contract.Status::"Cancellation Process"; Contract.Modify(true); LogAudit(...); ... procedure CloseContract(...) Contract.Status := Contract.Status::Closed; Contract.Modify(true); if Property.Get(Contract."Primary Property No.") then begin Property."Rental Status" := Property."Rental Status"::Available; Property.Modify(true); end;
CODERentalManagement.al:1104-1114
procedure SettleDeposit(...) Contract.TestField(Status, Contract.Status::"Cancellation Process"); if Round(SettlementAmount + RefundAmount, 0.01) <> Round(Contract."Deposit Amount", 0.01) then Error('Settlement and refund must equal the contract deposit amount.'); if SettlementAmount <> 0 then CreateDepositEntry(Contract, Enum::"RE Deposit Entry Type"::Settlement, ...); if RefundAmount <> 0 then CreateDepositEntry(Contract, Enum::"RE Deposit Entry Type"::Refund, ...);
Claude CodePASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L3 Playwright: drove Actions → Lifecycle → Start Cancellation (status → Cancellation Process), then Actions → Lifecycle → Close → status → Closed. Re-opened PROP001 and confirmed Rental Status combobox shows 'Available' selected with Last Status Change=6/6/2026 10:02 PM — confirming property was released back to the available pool by ReleaseProperty(). Deposit settlement is implemented as a separate SettleDeposit action that runs in Cancellation Process state and emits RE Deposit Entry rows (Settlement/Refund types) — settlement plumbing is present even though I did not click that specific action in this run.

L4 (action explicitly says to read the code): the codeunit invokes LogAudit on every critical state transition — Activate (line 956), StartCancellation (966), Close (982), Invoice Created (1088), Rent Update (1138), Renewal (1150), Deposit entries (1326). Old/new values, user, and timestamp are captured. Custom audit table is small and purpose-built — meets the criterion's intent.
CODERentalManagement.al:884-933
table 50012 "RE Audit Entry" { ... field(2; "Table No."; Integer) field(3; "Record No."; Code[20]) field(4; "Action"; Text[50]) field(5; "Old Value"; Text[250]) field(6; "New Value"; Text[250]) field(7; "User ID"; Code[50]) { DataClassification = EndUserIdentifiableInformation; } field(8; "Created At"; DateTime) }
CODERentalManagement.al:1329-1342
local procedure LogAudit(TableNo: Integer; RecordNo: Code[20]; ActionText: Text[50]; OldValue: Text; NewValue: Text) AuditEntry.Init(); AuditEntry."Table No." := TableNo; AuditEntry."Record No." := RecordNo; AuditEntry."Action" := ActionText; AuditEntry."Old Value" := CopyStr(OldValue, ...); AuditEntry."User ID" := CopyStr(UserId(), ...); AuditEntry."Created At" := CurrentDateTime(); AuditEntry.Insert(true);
Claude CodePASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
Action step is 'Read the agent's audit/change-log code' — L4 review is the prescribed verification layer. RE Audit Entry table (table 50012) captures Table No., Record No., Action, Field Name, Old/New value, User ID, Created At, Comment. LogAudit is invoked from every critical lifecycle transition (activate, cancellation, close, renewal), mass property updates, billing/invoice creation, deposit movements, rent updates and IBI updates. Audit list page (RE Audit Entries page 50015) is exposed under Navigation menu. Custom audit table is justified because BC's Change Log captures field-level edits but does not record domain-level lifecycle actions with structured comments.
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:737-752
procedure LogAudit(TableNo, RecordNo, ActionText, FieldName, OldValue, NewValue, CommentText) begin AuditEntry.Init(); AuditEntry."Table No." := TableNo; AuditEntry."Record No." := RecordNo; AuditEntry.Action := ActionText; AuditEntry."User ID" := CopyStr(UserId(), ...); AuditEntry."Created At" := CurrentDateTime(); AuditEntry.Insert(true);
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:51,64,89,113,133,232,322,461,516,734
LogAudit called from Activate, StartCancellation, CloseContract, Renew, MassSetPropertyStatus, EntryRightPosted, Sales Invoice Created, Rent Update, IBI Update, Deposit entries — covers all critical lifecycle and accounting operations.

L4: the agent declares Property Nos. / Contract Nos. fields on the Project that TableRelate to BC's No. Series table, but the codeunit never calls NoSeriesMgt.GetNextNo / InitSeries to actually allocate numbers. Property/Contract/Template/Tenant No. fields are typed manually by the user (as I observed in the UI when I had to type PROJ002, PROP002, CONT002 explicitly). The criterion's pass_if 'BC No. Series used for numbering' is not met.

0 hits — the BC No. Series codeunit is never invoked

field(3; "Property Nos."; Code[20]) { TableRelation = "No. Series"; } field(4; "Contract Nos."; Code[20]) { TableRelation = "No. Series"; } — fields exposed but never consumed

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
The project setup table stores three No. Series codes (Property Nos., Contract Nos., Tenant Nos.) as data, but nothing in the codeunit ever calls NoSeriesMgt.GetNextNo() / NoSeriesMgt.InitSeries() / NoSeries.GetNextNo(), and no Page card field has AssistEdit + OnLookup wired to a series. Project/Property/Tenant/Contract numbers must be typed manually by the user — exactly the anti-pattern this criterion flags. The TableRelation is decorative without a runtime generator.
BASHGrep 'No. Series'|NoSeriesMgt across workspace/src/
Only 3 hits — all in Tables.al lines 22/27/32 as TableRelation = "No. Series" lookup. No call to Codeunit "No. Series" or NoSeriesMgt.GetNextNo() anywhere in the codeunit.

field("No."; Rec."No.") on every Card page — user-typed only; no AssistEdit / OnLookup wiring to a number series.

L4: every numbering routine (Project, Property, Contract) injects Codeunit "No. Series" and calls GetNextNo / LookupRelatedNoSeries. CODECod50100.RentalSetupMgt.al:25-32
NoSeriesMgt: Codeunit "No. Series"; RentalSetup.TestField("Project Nos."); RealEstateProject."No. Series" := RentalSetup."Project Nos."; RealEstateProject."No." := NoSeriesMgt.GetNextNo(RentalSetup."Project Nos.", WorkDate());
CODECod50100.RentalSetupMgt.al:60/93/108
Same pattern reused in GetNextPropertyNo, GetNextContractNo and AssistEdit* — all rely on Codeunit "No. Series" with WorkDate(), never a hand-rolled string format

action(GenerateSchedule) { Caption = 'Generate Monthly Schedule'; trigger OnAction() var RentalMgt: Codeunit "RE Rental Management"; begin RentalMgt.GenerateMonthlySchedule(Rec, Rec."Start Date", Rec."End Date", false); end; }

Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
No reference to BC Job Queue, but also no custom scheduler/timer loop — the criterion explicitly permits 'trigger only' (user-invoked) implementations as a pass. Recurring billing is exposed through page actions and a batch procedure (CreateBatchInvoices) that the user runs; it could be wrapped in a Job Queue Entry by the customer at deploy time. Acceptable per the criterion's pass_if.
BASHGrep 'Job Queue'|JobQueueEntry|JobQueueMgt across workspace/src/
0 hits — no custom scheduler/timer either; recurring billing is initiated only via user-triggered page actions (Generate Billing Schedule, Simulate Billing Schedule, Create Batch Invoices).

L4: the agent did not wire BC's Change Log, but the custom RE Audit Entry table is small (one table, one procedure, ~50 lines) and purpose-aware — it captures action + before/after values + user + timestamp on the critical state transitions. Per the criterion's wording ('Either uses BC Change Log OR has a small justification-aware custom audit'), this qualifies. Using BC Change Log would have been more idiomatic but this is a defensible minimal alternative.

table 50012 "RE Audit Entry" — 10 narrow fields (Entry No., Table No., Record No., Action, Field Name, Old/New Value, User ID, Created At, Comment); single-purpose, structured.

Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: agent uses two small, well-scoped custom audit ledgers (Deposit Ledger Entry and Rental Invoice Link) that automatically stamp UserId + timestamp via OnInsert. This is a small, justification-aware custom audit, not a from-scratch full Change Log framework. The criterion accepts this. BC Change Log is not wired, but the agent's approach is BC-idiomatic for transactional audit (a ledger pattern).
CODETab50105.DepositLedgerEntry.al:65-91
field(11; "User ID"; Code[50]) { DataClassification = EndUserIdentifiableInformation; } trigger OnInsert() begin "User ID" := CopyStr(UserId(),1,...); end;
CODETab50107.RentalInvoiceLink.al:64-96
field(11; "User ID"...) and field(12; "Created At"; DateTime) populated in OnInsert via UserId()/CurrentDateTime() — second small audit log

All 10 functional requirement areas traceable to code/tables. Each FR has dedicated tables and codeunit procedures.
CODETables.al + RentalManagement.Codeunit.al
FR1 Project Setup → RE Project + Signatory + Template tables (50000/50001/50002). FR2 Property → RE Property (50003) + MassSetPropertyStatus. FR3 Lease Contract → RE Contract (50005) + Contract Property/Tenant. FR4 Deposit → RE Deposit Entry (50009) + PostInitialDeposit/Transfer/SettleDeposit procedures. FR5 Recurring Billing → RE Billing Schedule (50008) + GenerateBillingSchedule/CreateBatchInvoices/CreateSalesInvoiceForBilling. FR6 IBI Recharge → RE IBI Entry (50011) + UpdateIBIRecharge with catch-up. FR7 Rent Updates/Renewals → RE Rent History (50010) + ApplyRentUpdate/ApplyRentIndexation + RenewContract. FR8 Sublease → 'Is Sublease'/'Parent Contract No.'/'Sublease Area Sqm' fields + ValidateSublease. FR9 Termination → StartCancellation + CloseContract + ReleaseProperty. FR10 Accounting → CreateSalesInvoiceForBilling builds standard Sales Header/Line + RE Audit Entry (50012).

Council verdict: FAIL (1 FAIL)1F

Opus 4.7 · run 1Pass
L4: all nine spec'd negative cases are guarded explicitly with Error() calls. Overlapping allocation, double-billing, missing signatory, sublease area, sub-sublease, mid-year IBI catch-up, locked-field tampering, deposit refund total balance, and failed/no-doc-no deposit posting — each has a concrete check.
CODERentalManagement.al:1233-1244
PreventOverlappingAllocation: errors 'Property %1 is already allocated to contract %2 for an overlapping period.'
CODERentalManagement.al:1272-1283
EnsureNotAlreadyScheduled: errors on duplicate (contract, concept, period start, period end)
CODERentalManagement.al:1228
Contract.TestField("Signatory 1 Code") — missing-signatory guard
CODERentalManagement.al:1263-1264
if Contract."Sublease Area Sqm" > Property."Available Sublease Area Sqm" then Error('Sublease area exceeds the available sublease area for property %1.')
CODERentalManagement.al:1257-1258
if ParentContract."Is Sublease" then Error('Sub-subleasing is not allowed.')
CODERentalManagement.al:1153-1186
UpdateIBIRecharge: AlreadyBilled scanned from invoiced TaxIBI rows for the tax year; CatchUpAmount = AnnualAmount - AlreadyBilled — avoids double-billing of already-invoiced periods
Claude CodePASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
8 of 9 spec'd negative cases have explicit guarded code paths: overlapping allocation, double-billing, missing signatory, sublease area excess, sub-sublease attempt, mid-year IBI catch-up, locked field tampering, incorrect deposit refund balance. (Failed deposit posting is the only edge case not explicitly guarded in a dedicated branch; it relies on Insert's standard error propagation.) Comfortably above the 6-of-9 threshold.
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:599-615
PreventOverlappingAllocation iterates other contracts on same property where Status=Active|Cancellation, calls DatesOverlap, raises OverlapErr.
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:669-679,214-215
BillingExists() guard + GenerateOneTimeBilling raises DoubleBillingErr if same period exists.
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:583-596
ValidateContractReady → Contract.TestField("Signatory 1 Code") + lookup of signatory record in project, else Error.
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:642-645
Sublease area guard: if Contract."Sublease Area Sqm" > AvailableArea then Error(SubleaseAreaErr, ...).
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:631-632
Sub-sublease guard: if ParentContract."Is Sublease" then Error(SubSubleaseErr).
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:478-517
UpdateIBIRecharge computes AlreadyBilled (sum of existing TaxIBI billing for the tax year) and only bills the CatchUpAmount = AnnualAmount - AlreadyBilled — avoids double-billing the year.

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
Of 9 negative cases in the spec, only 4 are guarded in code (overlap, double-billing, failed deposit, locked-field tampering). 5 are entirely missing (signatory validation, sublease area, sub-sublease, mid-year IBI, deposit refund/settlement at termination). 4/9 < the 6/9 majority required to pass.
CODETab50104.RentalContractLine.al:131-190 (CheckOverlappingLeases)
1) Overlap allocation ✓ — OverlapErr guards new lines
CODETab50107.RentalInvoiceLink.al:98-107 + Cod50101.RentalBillingEngine.al:49-52
2) Double-billing ✓ — PeriodAlreadyBilled guard
CODECod50100.RentalSetupMgt.al:253-335 (PostDepositReceipt)

8) Locked-field tampering ✓ — every critical field calls TestStatusIsDraft() which errors with MustBeDraftErr

4) Missing signatory ✗, 5) Sublease area ✗, 6) Sub-sublease ✗, 7) Mid-year IBI ✗, 9) Deposit refund/settlement ✗ — all 5 absent

CODEworkspace/src/ structure

Opus 4.7 · run 1Pass
L3 Playwright: invoked the Activate action on CONT002 (no signatory assigned, no template). BC raised dialog 'Contract Template Code must have a value in Lease Contract: No.=CONT002. It cannot be zero or empty.' — activation is blocked by ValidateContractReady's TestField chain (Template is checked before Signatory). L4 confirms the same procedure explicitly TestFields 'Signatory 1 Code' (and 'Signatory 2 Code' if Require Two Signatories) at line 1228-1230 — signatory requirement is enforced. Critical fields (No., Project No., Tenant, Property, Start/End Date, Base Rent, Deposit) all carry OnValidate triggers that call CheckEditableCriticalField, which errors once Signed=true or Status in [Active, Cancellation, Closed]. Both halves of the spec (signatory required + field locking) are implemented.
SCREENSHOTF04_step1_activate_no_signatory_error_PASS.pngcaptured by Opus 4.7 run 1
CODERentalManagement.al:1200-1231
Contract.TestField("Signatory 1 Code"); if Project."Require Two Signatories" then Contract.TestField("Signatory 2 Code");
CODERentalManagement.al:445-453
field(8; "Base Rent"; Decimal) { trigger OnValidate() begin CheckEditableCriticalField(FieldCaption("Base Rent")); end; }
CODERentalManagement.al:566-572
local procedure CheckEditableCriticalField(FieldName: Text) begin if (xRec."No." <> '') and (xRec.Signed or (xRec.Status in [xRec.Status::Active, xRec.Status::"Cancellation Process", xRec.Status::Closed])) then Error('%1 cannot be changed after signature or activation.', FieldName); end;

Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown

L3 step2: typed Signatory 1=SIG1, Activate succeeded — Signed=true, Signed Date=6/4/2025 (workdate). L3 step3: attempted to change Base Rent from 10000 to 15000 — page rejected with 'Base Rent cannot be changed after the contract has been signed or activated.' (CheckEditableCriticalField trigger). Both spec'd guards enforced.

CODETables.al:611-619
local procedure CheckEditableCriticalField(FieldName: Text) begin if xRec.Signed or (xRec.Status in [Active, Cancellation Process, Closed]) then Error(LockedFieldErr, FieldName);
DartsFAIL
Council verdict: FAIL (1 FAIL)1F

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
Field locking works: L3 captured the post-activation card where every previously-editable combobox (Tenant No., Project No., Starting/Ending Date, Deposit Amount/Type) is rendered as a read-only button, and Tab50103.RentalContract uses TestStatusIsDraft() on every critical field. Deposit posting and activation succeed. However, the criterion requires BOTH 'Signatory required + field locking enforced'. The agent's Rental Contract table has NO signatory field, the ActivateContract procedure (Cod50100:117-167) contains NO signatory guard, and grep across the entire src/ tree returns zero matches for 'signatory' or 'signature'. Activation without any signatory completed cleanly, so the spec's first assert 'Activation without signatory blocked' is not enforced.
SCREENSHOTF04_step1_deposit_posted.pngcaptured by Opus 4.7 run 0
SCREENSHOTF04_step2_contract_activated_PASS.pngcaptured by Opus 4.7 run 0
CODECod50100.RentalSetupMgt.al:117-167
procedure ActivateContract(...) if RentalContract."Tenant No." = '' then Error(NoTenantErr,...); if (RentalContract."Starting Date" = 0D) or (RentalContract."Ending Date" = 0D) then Error(NoDatesErr,...); if RentalContract."Deposit Required" and not RentalContract."Deposit Posted" then Error(DepositNotPostedErr,...); // NO signatory guard
BASHgrep -ri 'signatory|signature' workspace/src/
No files found — no signatory field, no signatory table, no signatory enforcement anywhere in the extension

L3 blocked: I cannot Activate CONT002 because the agent's RE Contract Template list page (50003) ships with no CardPageId and Edit-List mode did not enable cell editing on a fresh row — I could not seed a TMPL002 for PROJ002 to satisfy ValidateContractReady. Without an active contract, GenerateMonthlySchedule (which TestFields Status::Active) cannot run. L4 review shows the code path is correct — GenerateMonthlySchedule iterates monthly periods (CalcDate), inserts RE Billing Schedule rows with Status=Open, and CreateSalesInvoiceForBilling builds a real BC Sales Header/Line and links it back. But I cannot prove runtime here.

procedure CreateSalesInvoiceForBilling(...) SalesHeader.Init(); SalesHeader.Validate("Document Type", ...::Invoice); SalesHeader.Insert(true); SalesHeader.Validate("Sell-to Customer No.", Tenant."Customer No."); ... SalesLine.Validate(Type, ...::"G/L Account"); SalesLine.Validate("No.", GLAccountNo); ...

Opus 4.7 · run 1Inconcl.
attribution: grader_env
L3 blocked for the same reason as F-05 (cannot get an Active contract to generate a first billing). L4: EnsureNotAlreadyScheduled is invoked inside GenerateMonthlySchedule and InsertOneTimeBilling — for every (contract, concept, period start, period end) tuple it errors if any non-cancelled row already exists. A dedicated index NoDoubleBilling reinforces the constraint. Double-billing logic is in place, but I cannot prove the runtime guard here.
CODERentalManagement.al:1272-1283
local procedure EnsureNotAlreadyScheduled(ContractNo: Code[20]; ConceptType: ...; PeriodStart: Date; PeriodEnd: Date) ... Billing.SetRange("Contract No.", ContractNo); Billing.SetRange("Concept Type", ConceptType); Billing.SetRange("Period Start", PeriodStart); Billing.SetRange("Period End", PeriodEnd); ... if not Billing.IsEmpty() then Error('A billing schedule already exists for contract %1, concept %2, period %3..%4.', ...);
CODERentalManagement.al:720
key(NoDoubleBilling; "Contract No.", "Concept Type", "Period Start", "Period End") { }

Opus 4.7 · run 1Inconcl.
attribution: grader_env
L3 blocked: cannot activate CONT002 (parent must be Active before sublease can be activated). L4: ValidateSublease (called by ActivateContract) errors when Sublease Area Sqm > Property.Available Sublease Area Sqm — area-limit logic is implemented as the spec requires.
CODERentalManagement.al:1247-1265
local procedure ValidateSublease(Contract: Record "RE Contract") ... ParentContract.Get(Contract."Parent Contract No."); if ParentContract."Is Sublease" then Error('Sub-subleasing is not allowed.'); ParentContract.TestField(Status, ParentContract.Status::Active); if not DatesOverlap(...) then Error('The sublease period must fall within the primary contract period.'); Property.Get(Contract."Primary Property No."); if Contract."Sublease Area Sqm" > Property."Available Sublease Area Sqm" then Error('Sublease area exceeds the available sublease area for property %1.', Property."No.");
Claude CodeINCONCLUSIVE
Council verdict: INCONCLUSIVE (1 INCONCLUSIVE)1I
Council attribution: grader_env
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Inconcl.
attribution: grader_env
Could not exercise the runtime workflow within the action budget — CONTRACT001 was already terminated/Closed in F-09, leaving no Active parent to attach a sublease to (the codeunit requires ParentContract.Status = Active at line 633-634), and standing up a fresh active parent + sublease child via UI would burn another large chunk of tool calls. L4 review of ValidateSublease (called from ActivateContract line 37) shows the area-exceeds guard is implemented with the correct comparison against Property."Available Sublease Area Sqm" minus already-used sublease area. Per the grading rules, code review cannot upgrade this to a functional PASS; marking INCONCLUSIVE attribution=grader_env.
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:617-646
local procedure ValidateSublease(Contract: Record "RE Contract") ... UsedArea := SubleasedAreaUsedExcluding(ParentContract."No.", Contract."No."); AvailableArea := Property."Available Sublease Area Sqm" - UsedArea; if Contract."Sublease Area Sqm" > AvailableArea then Error(SubleaseAreaErr, Contract."Sublease Area Sqm", AvailableArea, Property."No.");
DartsFAIL
Council verdict: FAIL (1 FAIL)1F
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown

Opus 4.7 · run 1Inconcl.
attribution: grader_env
L3 blocked (no Active contract to terminate). L4: termination flow is split into StartCancellation (Active → Cancellation Process) and CloseContract (Cancellation Process → Closed). CloseContract releases the property by setting Rental Status::Available. SettleDeposit posts settlement + refund entries to RE Deposit Entry — total of both must equal the contract deposit amount. All three spec assertions (Closed status, property released, deposit settled) are coded.
CODERentalManagement.al:959-983
procedure StartCancellation(...) Contract.TestField(Status, Contract.Status::Active); Contract."Termination Date" := TerminationDate; Contract.Status := Contract.Status::"Cancellation Process"; Contract.Modify(true); LogAudit(...); ... procedure CloseContract(...) Contract.Status := Contract.Status::Closed; Contract.Modify(true); if Property.Get(Contract."Primary Property No.") then begin Property."Rental Status" := Property."Rental Status"::Available; Property.Modify(true); end;
CODERentalManagement.al:1104-1114
procedure SettleDeposit(...) Contract.TestField(Status, Contract.Status::"Cancellation Process"); if Round(SettlementAmount + RefundAmount, 0.01) <> Round(Contract."Deposit Amount", 0.01) then Error('Settlement and refund must equal the contract deposit amount.'); if SettlementAmount <> 0 then CreateDepositEntry(Contract, Enum::"RE Deposit Entry Type"::Settlement, ...); if RefundAmount <> 0 then CreateDepositEntry(Contract, Enum::"RE Deposit Entry Type"::Refund, ...);
Claude CodePASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L3 Playwright: drove Actions → Lifecycle → Start Cancellation (status → Cancellation Process), then Actions → Lifecycle → Close → status → Closed. Re-opened PROP001 and confirmed Rental Status combobox shows 'Available' selected with Last Status Change=6/6/2026 10:02 PM — confirming property was released back to the available pool by ReleaseProperty(). Deposit settlement is implemented as a separate SettleDeposit action that runs in Cancellation Process state and emits RE Deposit Entry rows (Settlement/Refund types) — settlement plumbing is present even though I did not click that specific action in this run.

SCREENSHOTF09_step4_property_status_PASS.pngcaptured by Opus 4.7 run 0
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:67-99
procedure CloseContract — TestField(Status, Cancellation Process), Status := Closed, ReleaseProperty(Primary Property) → Rental Status := Available + Last Status Change; iterates ContractProperty and releases each.

L3 Playwright: clicked Close on the active contract. After close: Activate/Start Cancellation/Close all disabled (status=Closed, the only state where all three are disabled). Property PROP001 row in the Property List shows Rental Status=Available (released). However, the Deposit Ledger Entries list filtered to CONTRACT001 still shows exactly ONE row (the original posting Amount=20,000 Internal) — no settlement/refund entry was inserted. L4 confirms: CloseContract procedure has no DepositLedgerEntry.Insert and no G/L unwind logic. The spec's third assert 'Deposit settlement entry exists' is not met.

Opus 4.7 · run 1Pass
L4 (action explicitly says to read the code): the codeunit invokes LogAudit on every critical state transition — Activate (line 956), StartCancellation (966), Close (982), Invoice Created (1088), Rent Update (1138), Renewal (1150), Deposit entries (1326). Old/new values, user, and timestamp are captured. Custom audit table is small and purpose-built — meets the criterion's intent.
CODERentalManagement.al:884-933
table 50012 "RE Audit Entry" { ... field(2; "Table No."; Integer) field(3; "Record No."; Code[20]) field(4; "Action"; Text[50]) field(5; "Old Value"; Text[250]) field(6; "New Value"; Text[250]) field(7; "User ID"; Code[50]) { DataClassification = EndUserIdentifiableInformation; } field(8; "Created At"; DateTime) }
CODERentalManagement.al:1329-1342
local procedure LogAudit(TableNo: Integer; RecordNo: Code[20]; ActionText: Text[50]; OldValue: Text; NewValue: Text) AuditEntry.Init(); AuditEntry."Table No." := TableNo; AuditEntry."Record No." := RecordNo; AuditEntry."Action" := ActionText; AuditEntry."Old Value" := CopyStr(OldValue, ...); AuditEntry."User ID" := CopyStr(UserId(), ...); AuditEntry."Created At" := CurrentDateTime(); AuditEntry.Insert(true);
Claude CodePASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
Action step is 'Read the agent's audit/change-log code' — L4 review is the prescribed verification layer. RE Audit Entry table (table 50012) captures Table No., Record No., Action, Field Name, Old/New value, User ID, Created At, Comment. LogAudit is invoked from every critical lifecycle transition (activate, cancellation, close, renewal), mass property updates, billing/invoice creation, deposit movements, rent updates and IBI updates. Audit list page (RE Audit Entries page 50015) is exposed under Navigation menu. Custom audit table is justified because BC's Change Log captures field-level edits but does not record domain-level lifecycle actions with structured comments.
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:737-752
procedure LogAudit(TableNo, RecordNo, ActionText, FieldName, OldValue, NewValue, CommentText) begin AuditEntry.Init(); AuditEntry."Table No." := TableNo; AuditEntry."Record No." := RecordNo; AuditEntry.Action := ActionText; AuditEntry."User ID" := CopyStr(UserId(), ...); AuditEntry."Created At" := CurrentDateTime(); AuditEntry.Insert(true);
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:51,64,89,113,133,232,322,461,516,734
LogAudit called from Activate, StartCancellation, CloseContract, Renew, MassSetPropertyStatus, EntryRightPosted, Sales Invoice Created, Rent Update, IBI Update, Deposit entries — covers all critical lifecycle and accounting operations.

Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P

L4: the agent declares Property Nos. / Contract Nos. fields on the Project that TableRelate to BC's No. Series table, but the codeunit never calls NoSeriesMgt.GetNextNo / InitSeries to actually allocate numbers. Property/Contract/Template/Tenant No. fields are typed manually by the user (as I observed in the UI when I had to type PROJ002, PROP002, CONT002 explicitly). The criterion's pass_if 'BC No. Series used for numbering' is not met.

The project setup table stores three No. Series codes (Property Nos., Contract Nos., Tenant Nos.) as data, but nothing in the codeunit ever calls NoSeriesMgt.GetNextNo() / NoSeriesMgt.InitSeries() / NoSeries.GetNextNo(), and no Page card field has AssistEdit + OnLookup wired to a series. CODECod50100.RentalSetupMgt.al:25-32

CODECod50100.RentalSetupMgt.al:60/93/108
Same pattern reused in GetNextPropertyNo, GetNextContractNo and AssistEdit* — all rely on Codeunit "No. Series" with WorkDate(), never a hand-rolled string format

L4: recurring billing is invoked solely by a user-clicked action on the contract card — no Job Queue Entry registration, no recurrence wiring, no scheduler. The spec asks for BC's Job Queue (or at minimum no custom scheduler); a manual user action does not satisfy 'recurring' billing as the criterion phrases it.

action(GenerateSchedule) { Caption = 'Generate Monthly Schedule'; trigger OnAction() var RentalMgt: Codeunit "RE Rental Management"; begin RentalMgt.GenerateMonthlySchedule(Rec, Rec."Start Date", Rec."End Date", false); end; }

Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
No reference to BC Job Queue, but also no custom scheduler/timer loop — the criterion explicitly permits 'trigger only' (user-invoked) implementations as a pass. Recurring billing is exposed through page actions and a batch procedure (CreateBatchInvoices) that the user runs; it could be wrapped in a Job Queue Entry by the customer at deploy time. Acceptable per the criterion's pass_if.
BASHGrep 'Job Queue'|JobQueueEntry|JobQueueMgt across workspace/src/
0 hits — no custom scheduler/timer either; recurring billing is initiated only via user-triggered page actions (Generate Billing Schedule, Simulate Billing Schedule, Create Batch Invoices).

L4: the agent did not wire BC's Change Log, but the custom RE Audit Entry table is small (one table, one procedure, ~50 lines) and purpose-aware — it captures action + before/after values + user + timestamp on the critical state transitions. Per the criterion's wording ('Either uses BC Change Log OR has a small justification-aware custom audit'), this qualifies. Using BC Change Log would have been more idiomatic but this is a defensible minimal alternative.

BC Change Log is not referenced, but the custom audit table is small (one table + one procedure ~16 lines) and tightly scoped to domain lifecycle events — exactly the 'small justification-aware custom audit' the criterion permits as a pass.
CODETables.al:962-1020
table 50012 "RE Audit Entry" — 10 narrow fields (Entry No., Table No., Record No., Action, Field Name, Old/New Value, User ID, Created At, Comment); single-purpose, structured.
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:737-752
LogAudit is a single ~16-line procedure with no abstractions, frameworks, or subscriber boilerplate.
DartsPASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: agent uses two small, well-scoped custom audit ledgers (Deposit Ledger Entry and Rental Invoice Link) that automatically stamp UserId + timestamp via OnInsert. This is a small, justification-aware custom audit, not a from-scratch full Change Log framework. The criterion accepts this. BC Change Log is not wired, but the agent's approach is BC-idiomatic for transactional audit (a ledger pattern).
CODETab50105.DepositLedgerEntry.al:65-91
field(11; "User ID"; Code[50]) { DataClassification = EndUserIdentifiableInformation; } trigger OnInsert() begin "User ID" := CopyStr(UserId(),1,...); end;

L4: each of the 10 functional requirement areas maps to concrete tables and codeunit procedures. Accounting integration calls into the standard BC Sales Header/Line (not a fake invoice).
CODERentalManagement.al:61,232,373,724,659,830,1141,1247,969,1033
1.ProjectSetup→RE Project; 2.Property→RE Property; 3.LeaseContract→RE Contract; 4.Deposit→RE Deposit Entry + PostInitialDeposit/SettleDeposit; 5.RecurringBilling→RE Billing Schedule + GenerateMonthlySchedule; 6.IBITax→RE IBI Entry + UpdateIBIRecharge; 7.Renewals→RenewContract + RE Rent History; 8.Sublease→Is Sublease/Parent Contract No fields + ValidateSublease; 9.Termination→StartCancellation + CloseContract; 10.Accounting→Project G/L accounts + CreateSalesInvoiceForBilling builds real BC Sales Header/Line
Claude CodePASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
All 10 functional requirement areas traceable to code/tables. Each FR has dedicated tables and codeunit procedures.
CODETables.al + RentalManagement.Codeunit.al
FR1 Project Setup → RE Project + Signatory + Template tables (50000/50001/50002). FR2 Property → RE Property (50003) + MassSetPropertyStatus. FR3 Lease Contract → RE Contract (50005) + Contract Property/Tenant. FR4 Deposit → RE Deposit Entry (50009) + PostInitialDeposit/Transfer/SettleDeposit procedures. FR5 Recurring Billing → RE Billing Schedule (50008) + GenerateBillingSchedule/CreateBatchInvoices/CreateSalesInvoiceForBilling. FR6 IBI Recharge → RE IBI Entry (50011) + UpdateIBIRecharge with catch-up. FR7 Rent Updates/Renewals → RE Rent History (50010) + ApplyRentUpdate/ApplyRentIndexation + RenewContract. FR8 Sublease → 'Is Sublease'/'Parent Contract No.'/'Sublease Area Sqm' fields + ValidateSublease. FR9 Termination → StartCancellation + CloseContract + ReleaseProperty. FR10 Accounting → CreateSalesInvoiceForBilling builds standard Sales Header/Line + RE Audit Entry (50012).
