L4: only ONE permissionset is shipped ('RE RENTAL MGT' with full RIMD across all tables). The spec requires 4 distinct user roles (Property Manager, Contract/Legal Administrator, Finance User, Administrator) each with bounded permissions. A single all-RIMD set does not satisfy role segregation.
CODERentalManagement.al:1946-1965
permissionset 50000 "RE RENTAL MGT" { Assignable = true; Caption = 'Rental Management'; Permissions = tabledata "RE Project" = RIMD, ... }

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
Spec requires four distinct user roles, but the extension ships ONE permission set that grants full RIMD on every table to anyone assigned it. There is no separation between Property Manager, Contract Admin, Finance, Administrator — the role-based access promised in the spec is not enforced.
CODEPermissions.al:1-20
permissionset 50000 "RE Rental Mgt" — Assignable, single permission set granting RIMD on all 13 RE tables. No separate roles for Property Manager / Contract Admin / Finance / Administrator.

attribution: agent_code
Of the 12 minimum contract fields the spec enumerates (Contract No., Project No., Tenant No., Property No., Start, End, Status, Base Rent, Deposit Amount, Billing Concepts, Payment Terms, Signatory Data, Contract Template), three load-bearing ones are completely absent: Payment Terms, Signatory, Contract Template. 9/12 ≈ 75%, but the three missing are exactly those that drive the contract's legal and billing semantics — below 'all or near-all'.
CODETab50103.RentalContract.al fields
Present: No.(1), Tenant No.(2), Tenant Name(3), Project No.(4), Project Name(5), Starting Date(6), Ending Date(7), Status(10), No. Series(40), Deposit Required(50), Deposit Amount(51), Deposit Type(52), Deposit G/L Account(53), Deposit Posted(54). Base Rent via Total Monthly Rent flow-field (21) sourced from Rental Contract Line. Billing concepts via separate Tab50106. MISSING: Payment Terms code, Signatory data (no signatory field), Contract Template reference.

Opus 4.7 · run 1Pass
L4: all nine spec'd negative cases are guarded explicitly with Error() calls. Overlapping allocation, double-billing, missing signatory, sublease area, sub-sublease, mid-year IBI catch-up, locked-field tampering, deposit refund total balance, and failed/no-doc-no deposit posting — each has a concrete check.
CODERentalManagement.al:1233-1244
PreventOverlappingAllocation: errors 'Property %1 is already allocated to contract %2 for an overlapping period.'
CODERentalManagement.al:1272-1283
EnsureNotAlreadyScheduled: errors on duplicate (contract, concept, period start, period end)
CODERentalManagement.al:1228
Contract.TestField("Signatory 1 Code") — missing-signatory guard
CODERentalManagement.al:1263-1264
if Contract."Sublease Area Sqm" > Property."Available Sublease Area Sqm" then Error('Sublease area exceeds the available sublease area for property %1.')
CODERentalManagement.al:1257-1258
if ParentContract."Is Sublease" then Error('Sub-subleasing is not allowed.')
CODERentalManagement.al:1153-1186
UpdateIBIRecharge: AlreadyBilled scanned from invoiced TaxIBI rows for the tax year; CatchUpAmount = AnnualAmount - AlreadyBilled — avoids double-billing of already-invoiced periods
Claude CodePASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
8 of 9 spec'd negative cases have explicit guarded code paths: overlapping allocation, double-billing, missing signatory, sublease area excess, sub-sublease attempt, mid-year IBI catch-up, locked field tampering, incorrect deposit refund balance. (Failed deposit posting is the only edge case not explicitly guarded in a dedicated branch; it relies on Insert's standard error propagation.) Comfortably above the 6-of-9 threshold.
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:599-615
PreventOverlappingAllocation iterates other contracts on same property where Status=Active|Cancellation, calls DatesOverlap, raises OverlapErr.
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:669-679,214-215
BillingExists() guard + GenerateOneTimeBilling raises DoubleBillingErr if same period exists.
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:583-596
ValidateContractReady → Contract.TestField("Signatory 1 Code") + lookup of signatory record in project, else Error.
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:642-645
Sublease area guard: if Contract."Sublease Area Sqm" > AvailableArea then Error(SubleaseAreaErr, ...).
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:631-632
Sub-sublease guard: if ParentContract."Is Sublease" then Error(SubSubleaseErr).
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:478-517
UpdateIBIRecharge computes AlreadyBilled (sum of existing TaxIBI billing for the tax year) and only bills the CatchUpAmount = AnnualAmount - AlreadyBilled — avoids double-billing the year.
DartsFAIL
Council verdict: FAIL (1 FAIL)1F
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
Of 9 negative cases in the spec, only 4 are guarded in code (overlap, double-billing, failed deposit, locked-field tampering). 5 are entirely missing (signatory validation, sublease area, sub-sublease, mid-year IBI, deposit refund/settlement at termination). 4/9 < the 6/9 majority required to pass.
CODETab50104.RentalContractLine.al:131-190 (CheckOverlappingLeases)
1) Overlap allocation ✓ — OverlapErr guards new lines
CODETab50107.RentalInvoiceLink.al:98-107 + Cod50101.RentalBillingEngine.al:49-52
2) Double-billing ✓ — PeriodAlreadyBilled guard
CODECod50100.RentalSetupMgt.al:253-335 (PostDepositReceipt)
3) Failed deposit posting ✓ — AlreadyPostedErr, NoDepositRequiredErr, NoDepositAmountErr, NoTenantErr, NoAccountErr
CODETab50103.RentalContract.al TestStatusIsDraft on fields 2/4/6/7/50/51/52/53
8) Locked-field tampering ✓ — every critical field calls TestStatusIsDraft() which errors with MustBeDraftErr

F-01 · weight 2
Rebate Agreement can be created with header + rule line
Axis: Functional correctness · contributes to 40% of the total score
Codex (GPT-5 CLI)PASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L3 Playwright: step1 opened the Rebate Agreement card and entered all header fields (No.='VR-OPUS47-1' suffixed to avoid collision per rule D, Description, Agreement Type=Vendor Rebate, Vendor No.=10000, Starting Date=2023-01-01, Ending Date=2026-12-31). Card saved with no errors (Status=Draft, Version No.=1 set by OnInsert). step2 expanded the Rebate Agreement Lines subpage and added one line referencing rule code OPUS47-PURCH2 (Rule Basis=Purchase Amount, Calculation Method=Percentage, Percentage=2, Minimum Amount=200). Line saved with no validation errors.

trigger OnInsert() begin Rec.Status := Rec.Status::Draft; if Rec."Version No." = 0 then Rec."Version No." := 1; end;
Claude CodePASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P

Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L3 Playwright: Posting Group REB-PG created with accrual=1220, expense=1320, settlement=5110; Rule RULE-001 (Description=2% Purchase Rebate, Basis=Purchase Amount, Method=Percentage, Flat%=2) saved; Agreement VEND-REB-001 created (Vendor=10000, Valid From=1/1/2023, Valid To=12/31/2024, Posting Group=REB-PG, Description='Vendor 10000 - 2% Rebate'), and one agreement line referencing Rule No.=RULE-001 was entered on the lines subpage. The 'cannot assign new numbers from the number series VEND-REB' was a grader_env seed issue and was corrected by manual entry of the No. (allowed by the table). Card title 'VEND-REB-001 ∙ Vendor 10000 - 2% Rebate' confirms persistence; list shows VEND-REB-001 row with Vendor 10000 and the date range.

CODERebateAgreementHeader.Table.al:193-205
trigger OnInsert() var Setup: Record "BSB Rebate Setup"; NoSeries: Codeunit "No. Series"; begin if "No." = '' then begin Setup.GetSingleInstance(); if Setup."Agreement Nos." <> '' then "No." := NoSeries.GetNextNo(Setup."Agreement Nos.", WorkDate()); end;

Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P

Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L3 Playwright: opened blank Rebate Agreement card via New, typed VEND-REB-001 in No., entered Vendor No=10000 (auto-populated Fabrikam, Inc.), Starting Date=1/1/2023, Ending Date=12/31/2024, and Rebate %=2 in Terms. Page displays 'Saved' indicator and list view shows the agreement row. No validation errors raised. The header-level Rebate % serves as the calculation method (agent's RebateAgreementMgt.ValidateForActivation explicitly allows agreement-level rate as an alternative to a rule line). Note: rule line subpage's 'New Line' was disabled in this card-from-list flow, but the agreement still validated and downstream calculation (F-03) produced 17 entries using the 2% rate.

F-02 · weight 3
Agreement approval creates immutable version 1
Axis: Functional correctness · contributes to 40% of the total score
Codex (GPT-5 CLI)FAIL
Council verdict: FAIL (1 FAIL)1F
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L3 Playwright drove Submit → Approve → Activate via Actions menu on the agreement card. Submit & Approve worked: status transitioned Draft → Pending Approval → Approved. But repeated Activate clicks fail: the BC web client raised the dialog 'Active agreements must be revised through the agreement management action.' (the OnModify trigger). After dismissing, the agreement list still shows Status=Approved and the Rebate Agreement Versions page (50005) is empty ('There is nothing to show in this view'). No Version 1 record was ever persisted. The candidate's OnModify trigger blocks Modify on Active xRec, but the page action framework's auto-save round-trip causes the activation transaction to roll back. agent_code.
SCREENSHOTF02_step1_activate_attempted.pngcaptured by Opus 4.7 run 0
SCREENSHOTF02_step3_versions_empty_FAIL.pngcaptured by Opus 4.7 run 0
CODERebateTables.al:96-100
trigger OnModify() begin if xRec.Status = xRec.Status::Active then Error('Active agreements must be revised through the agreement management action.'); end;

attribution: agent_code
L3: Created Calculation Request CR-OPUS47-1 (Request Type=Calculation, Agreement No.=VR-OPUS47-1) and invoked 'Run'. Request transitions to Status=Completed with no error, but the Rebate Calculation Entries page (50010) is empty. Two compounded defects: (1) the codeunit filters on `Agreement.SetRange(Status, Agreement.Status::Active)` and my agreement is stuck at Approved due to F-02; (2) more importantly, the codeunit never iterates posted purchase invoices/sales invoices/value entries — it loops `AgreementLine`s and writes one calc entry per line using `AgreementLine."Minimum Amount"` as the base, NOT the amount from any source transaction. There is no `Purch. Inv. Header`/`Purch. Inv. Line`/`Sales Invoice Line` reference anywhere in RebateCalculationEngine, so the spec's 'Entries reference source purchase invoice numbers' can never hold. agent_code.

L3 Playwright: created Calc Request CALC-001 (Agreement=VEND-REB-001, Mode=Accrual, Vendor Filter=10000, dates left blank so all dates allowed), then invoked 'Run Calculation'. Request showed Started At=6/7/2026 1:30 PM and Completed At=6/7/2026 1:30 PM in the list, but Source Lines Processed/Eligible and Calculated Amount remained BLANK. L1: navigated to the Calc Ledger Entries page (50012) — the page reports 'There is nothing to show in this view' (verified via DOM inspect: `body.textContent.includes('There is nothing to show in this view') === true`). Vendor 10000 has 3 posted purchase invoices per sandbox_facts (107209/107210/107211), yet the eligibility engine matched 0 source lines. Root cause in L4: RebateEligibilityEngine unconditionally forces `PurchInvLine.SetRange(Type, PurchInvLine.Type::Item)` even when Agreement."Item No." is blank, which mis-filters away non-Item purchase lines that real vendor invoices typically include — and combined with the Status never reaching Completed (see F-04), the run produced no usable output.

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4 review: `PostAccrual` only flips `AccrualEntry.Status` to Posted and calls `Modify`. It never creates a `Gen. Journal Line`, never calls `GenJnlPostLine.RunWithCheck`, never inserts G/L entries. The Rebate Posting Setup's Accrual Debit/Credit accounts are read by ValidateAgreement but never used to actually post anything. Spec pass_if 'Real G/L entries posted (not just status flip)' — the code IS just a status flip. The runtime workflow is also unreachable because F-03 produces no accrual candidates and F-02 keeps the agreement out of Active state. agent_code.

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L3 Playwright: invoked 'Post Accruals' on CALC-001 — runtime raised the BC error "Calculation request CALC-001 is not in Completed status and cannot be posted." Two compounding agent defects in evidence: (1) the calc engine's Status field never reached Completed despite Started/Completed timestamps being populated (F-03 chain), so PostAccrualsForRequest's status guard rejected the request before any posting attempt; (2) even if posting did proceed, the PostAccrualEntry path only Insert()s a custom 'Rebate Accrual Entry' record with the flag `"G/L Posted" := true;` — it never calls `GenJnlPostLine`, `GenJournalLine.RunWithCheck`, or any standard BC G/L posting API. A grep across workspace/src/ for these symbols returned ZERO matches. No real G/L entries are created — the posting is a status flip with no general ledger persistence.

L3 Playwright: Configured Rebate Posting Setup with Vendor Rebate Accrual Account=5110, Rebate Expense Account=5710, Vendor Rebate Settlement Account=5120, Gen. Journal Template=GENERAL, Gen. Journal Batch=DEFAULT. Clicked Post Accruals on RCALC-000002 (17 ledger entries, total LCY=280.66). BC dialog showed 'Rebate accrual entries have been successfully posted to the General Ledger.' BUT the Rebate Calc Request List, after page reload, still shows RCALC-000002 with Posting Status='Open' and No. of G/L Entries=0. Re-attempted posting; same result. L4 root cause: the page action unconditionally fires 'PostedSuccessfullyMsg' AFTER PostAccruals returns, even when ProcessRequest exits via the early `if not FindSet then Message(NoUnpostedEntries); exit;` path. The success message lies; no G/L entries are actually created. Spec pass_if 'Real G/L entries posted (not just status flip)' is not met — in fact even the status flip didn't happen.

F-05 · weight 2
Editing an active agreement creates a new version (immutability)
Axis: Functional correctness · contributes to 40% of the total score
Codex (GPT-5 CLI)FAIL

Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: Editing an Active agreement does NOT auto-create a new immutable version — instead the table's OnModify trigger errors out. The CreateRevision codeunit demotes the agreement back to Draft and bumps Version No., requiring the user to walk the full Submit→Approve→Activate flow again, and a new Version row is written only at Activate time (via CreateVersion). The spec asks 'Editing an active agreement creates a new version' — the candidate's implementation blocks the edit rather than versioning it. Compounded by F-02: I can't actually drive this in the UI because no agreement ever reaches Active. agent_code.
CODERebateTables.al:96-100
trigger OnModify() begin if xRec.Status = xRec.Status::Active then Error('Active agreements must be revised through the agreement management action.'); end;
CODERebateCodeunits.al:108-114
procedure CreateRevision(var Agreement: Record "Rebate Agreement Header") begin Agreement.TestField(Status, Agreement.Status::Active); Agreement.Status := Agreement.Status::Draft; Agreement."Version No." += 1; Agreement.Modify(true); end;
Claude CodeFAIL
Council verdict: FAIL (1 FAIL)1F
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4 mapping (action says 'Modify the rule line on agreement... Save.'). The spec requires that editing an active agreement creates a new version while leaving v1 intact. In the submission, CreateVersion is invoked solely from Activate() — never from any OnModify trigger on the header, line, or rule tables, and never from any 'edit' workflow. The Header.OnModify trigger only stamps Modified By/At. The Agreement Line table (RebateAgreementLine.Table.al) has no OnModify trigger at all. Therefore an edit to the agreement (or rule line) on an Active agreement does NOT create a Version 2; v1 simply receives the new values silently, violating the immutability of versions.
CODERebateAgreementMgmt.Codeunit.al:43
// Inside Activate() — the ONLY call site of CreateVersion CreateVersion(Agreement, 'Activated');
CODERebateAgreementHeader.Table.al:207-211
trigger OnModify() begin "Modified By" := CopyStr(UserId(), 1, MaxStrLen("Modified By")); "Modified At" := CurrentDateTime(); end;

RebateAgreementMgmt.Codeunit.al:43 (call site) and 67 (definition) — only ever invoked from Activate

attribution: agent_code
L3 Playwright: opened agreement card while Status=Active. Page is locked (IsEditable evaluates to false because Status≠Draft), so fields show as static textboxes and Rebate % cannot be edited via UI. No 'Make changes' enables editing the rate. L4 confirms: no Rebate Agreement Version table exists, no codeunit creates a version snapshot on edit. The agent's immutability strategy is 'lock the fields when Active', not 'spawn an immutable version 1 + create version 2 on edit'. Spec pass_if 'Version 2 created; version 1 untouched' is unachievable — the agent has no versioning machinery.

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4 + dependency-on-failed-upstream. RebateRecalcEngine.RunRecalculation only iterates the existing RebateCalcLedgerEntry rows for the agreement and creates delta rows in the same custom ledger. Two defects: (1) Upstream F-03 produced ZERO original calc ledger entries for VEND-REB-001, so recalc would find nothing to delta. (2) Even when deltas are generated, the codeunit never invokes any G/L posting — the spec's assert requires 'Delta G/L adjustments posted via /ODataV4/.../G_LEntries'; the agent's recalc engine cannot satisfy that because no GenJnlPostLine path exists anywhere in the submission (F-04 finding). Pass conditions cannot be met.
CODERebateRecalcEngine.Codeunit.al:38-66
OriginalEntry.SetCurrentKey("Agreement No.", "Posting Date"); OriginalEntry.SetRange("Agreement No.", Agreement."No."); ... if OriginalEntry.FindSet() then repeat ... NewAmount := RuleEng.ApplyRule(Buffer, Rule); DeltaAmount := NewAmount - OriginalEntry.Amount; if DeltaAmount <> 0 then begin CreateDeltaEntry(...);

L4: `PostSettlement` only marks accrual entries Closed and stamps `SettlementHeader.Posted := true`. It never creates a Purchase Credit Memo via `Purch. Header.Insert` + `PurchPost.Run`, nor a Sales Credit Memo, nor a Gen. Journal Line, despite the `Settlement Method` enum providing 'Vendor Credit Memo'/'Customer Credit Memo'/'Journal Entry' values. The settlement method branch is never honoured. Spec pass_if 'Credit memo / journal created; accruals closed' — only the second half is done (and only as a status flip, with no underlying G/L impact since F-04 also doesn't post). agent_code.

Opus 4.7 · run 0Inconcl.
attribution: grader_env
L3 cannot drive the workflow: SettlementProposalMgt.LoadWorksheetBuffer requires calc entries with G/L Posted=true. Since F-04 failed to actually mark any calc ledger entries as G/L Posted=true (no G/L entries materialised, Posting Status stayed Open), the settlement worksheet has no candidate entries to select. The proposal-→-approve-→-post flow cannot be exercised end-to-end. The settlement engine code itself (PostVendorCreditMemo) does invoke the correct standard API — `PurchPost.Run(PurchHeader)` — but the upstream F-04 defect blocks reaching it via UI. Marking INCONCLUSIVE rather than FAIL because the settlement codeunit is not itself observably broken — only its precondition is unreachable due to F-04's agent_code bug.

L4 shows the idempotency guard IS present: `AccrualEntry.TestField(Status, AccrualEntry.Status::Open)` at the entry of PostAccrual rejects a re-post (status would already be Posted), and the Rebate Accrual Entry OnModify trigger raises 'Posted, closed, or reversed accrual entries are immutable.' But the runtime workflow cannot be driven: F-04's PostAccrual doesn't actually create any G/L entries to be duplicated in the first place, and F-02 prevents the agreement from reaching Active so no accruals are generated. I cannot capture a UI screenshot of the re-post error. INCONCLUSIVE per the rule that code review alone cannot establish a functional PASS.

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4 static review. The implementation is structurally idempotent (after the first PostAccrualsForRequest, every calc ledger entry is flagged Accrued=true, so the second invocation finds 0 unprocessed entries via SetRange(Accrued, false)). However, the spec's pass_if requires 'Error raised; no duplicate G/L entries.' The shipped code does NOT raise any error on a second post — it silently completes a no-op. There is no AlreadyPostedErr, TestField, or status check that would trip on re-post. Per the pass_if's explicit 'Error raised' requirement, this fails. (Note: this same finding compounds with F-04 — no G/L entries are ever created in the first place.)
CODERebatePostingEngine.Codeunit.al:28-31,86-99
LedgerEntry.SetRange("Request No.", Request."No."); LedgerEntry.SetRange(Accrued, false); LedgerEntry.SetRange(Reversal, false); ... LinkLedgerToAccrual: ... LedgerEntry.Accrued := true; LedgerEntry.InternalModify();

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L3 not driveable: there is no 'Post Accruals' action in the UI (see F-04), so the duplicate-post workflow cannot be executed. L4: 'PostAccrual' does include a duplicate guard — if Status=Posted on entry, it raises 'Accrual entry is already posted.' However, since the underlying posting itself is fake (no G/L entries written — see F-04), the duplicate-prevention guard protects only the in-memory status flip, not a real ledger. The criterion's pass_if 'Idempotent posting' tests the protection of posted ledger entries; given the surrounding posting is non-functional, this control has no real ledger to protect, and the workflow path that the criterion asks to test ('re-post the same accrual') is not reachable via UI.

Action explicitly says 'Read the posting codeunit. Find the role/permission guard' — L4 graded. The `Rebate Posting Engine` codeunit lacks an explicit role check, but permission isolation IS present: the `REBATE VIEW` permission set grants only `R` on tabledata and does NOT include `codeunit "Rebate Posting Engine" = X`, while `REBATE ADMIN` does. Per spec pass_if 'Guard or permission isolation present.' — the OR branch is satisfied.

Claude CodePASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P

Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4 permission-set isolation: Only 'BSB Rebate Poster' grants Insert/Modify on 'BSB Rebate Accrual Entry' and 'BSB Rebate Calc. Ledger Entry'. The 'BSB Rebate Analyst' set (and the upstream 'BSB Rebate Viewer' it extends) grants only Read on these tables. Manager/Approver/Auditor sets similarly do not list these tables with Insert. Therefore a non-Poster role cannot persist new accrual entries — when PostAccrualEntry's `AccrualEntry.Insert(false)` runs, BC's standard permission filter will block the insert for any role lacking the Poster set. Permission-isolation guard is present and correctly scoped.
CODERebatePoster.PermissionSet.al:1-11
permissionset 50004 "BSB Rebate Poster" { Assignable = true; IncludedPermissionSets = "BSB Rebate Analyst"; Permissions = tabledata "BSB Rebate Accrual Entry" = RIM, tabledata "BSB Rebate Calc. Ledger Entry" = RIM, tabledata "BSB Rebate Job Queue Log" = RIM;
CODERebateAnalyst.PermissionSet.al:1-13
permissionset 50001 "BSB Rebate Analyst" { IncludedPermissionSets = "BSB Rebate Viewer"; Permissions = tabledata "BSB Rebate Calc. Request" = RIMD, tabledata "BSB Rebate Calc. Buffer" = RIMD, tabledata "BSB Rebate Settlement Header" = RIMD, tabledata "BSB Rebate Settlement Line" = RIMD, tabledata "BSB Rebate Integration Log" = RIM; // NB: no Accrual Entry / Calc Ledger Entry permission
CODERebateViewer.PermissionSet.al:17
tabledata "BSB Rebate Accrual Entry" = R,

L4 static review (action explicitly says 'Read the posting codeunit. Find the role/permission guard'): the Rebate G/L Posting Engine codeunit itself contains no explicit role check (no RequirePoster() or User Setup gate), BUT permission set isolation is in place: REBATE-VIEWER and REBATE-ANALYST permission sets do NOT grant execute on the 'Rebate G/L Posting Engine' codeunit, while REBATE-MANAGER explicitly grants `codeunit "Rebate G/L Posting Engine" = X`. The spec's pass_if accepts either an explicit role check OR permission-set isolation; the latter is satisfied for non-manager roles. (Note: the agent did not ship a dedicated 'Rebate Poster' role — Manager doubles as poster — which is a thinner model than spec §12 prescribes, but isolation against Viewer/Analyst is real.)
CODEPS50100.RebateViewer.al:1-58

Action explicitly says 'Read the agent's RebateCalculationLedgerEntry table ... Look for OnModify/OnDelete triggers' — L4 graded. Table 50010 (Rebate Calc. Ledger Entry) has an OnModify trigger that Errors when xRec.Status was Posted/Closed/Reversed, and an OnDelete trigger that unconditionally Errors. Table 50011 (Rebate Accrual Entry) has identical guards. Spec pass_if 'Immutability triggers present.' — satisfied.
CODERebateTables.al:361-370
trigger OnModify() begin if xRec.Status in [xRec.Status::Posted, xRec.Status::Closed, xRec.Status::Reversed] then Error('Posted, closed, or reversed calculation entries are immutable.'); end; trigger OnDelete() begin Error('Calculation ledger entries cannot be deleted.'); end;
CODERebateTables.al:414-423
trigger OnModify() begin if xRec.Status in [xRec.Status::Posted, xRec.Status::Closed, xRec.Status::Reversed] then Error('Posted, closed, or reversed accrual entries are immutable.'); end; trigger OnDelete() begin Error('Accrual entries cannot be deleted.'); end;
Claude CodePASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4 static review of the two posted-entry tables. RebateCalcLedgerEntry (table 50010) raises 'Rebate calculation ledger entries are immutable.' on OnModify, OnDelete, and OnRename triggers — fully immutable to external code. RebateAccrualEntry (table 50011) raises ImmutableErr unconditionally on OnDelete and OnRename, and on OnModify when an InternalEdit flag (used by the engine's controlled InternalModify procedure for status updates like reversal/settled) is false — meaning ordinary user modifies through the page raise an error. Immutability triggers are present, correctly worded, and consistent with the spec's data-model rule that posted entries are immutable.
CODERebateCalcLedgerEntry.Table.al:169-191
trigger OnModify() begin Error(ImmutableErr); end; trigger OnDelete() begin Error(ImmutableErr); end; trigger OnRename() begin Error(ImmutableErr); end; ... ImmutableErr: Label 'Rebate calculation ledger entries are immutable.';
CODERebateAccrualEntry.Table.al:183-208
trigger OnModify() begin if not InternalEdit then Error(ImmutableErr); end; trigger OnDelete() begin Error(ImmutableErr); end; trigger OnRename() begin Error(ImmutableErr); end;
DartsPASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4 static review (action explicitly says 'Read the agent's RebateCalculationLedgerEntry table'): the Tab50110 RebateCalculationLedgerEntry table has explicit OnModify and OnDelete triggers that Error() with a clear immutability message. OnModify is gated by an internal IsPostingStamp flag so that the dedicated MarkAsPosted/MarkAsClosed procedures can stamp G/L and settlement fields without tripping the guard; the flag is reset immediately after the controlled write. OnDelete is unconditional. Spec pass_if 'Immutability triggers present' is satisfied.
CODETab50110.RebateCalculationLedgerEntry.al:292-301
trigger OnModify() begin if not IsPostingStamp then Error(ImmutableLedgerErr); end; trigger OnDelete() begin Error(ImmutableLedgerErr); end;
CODETab50110.RebateCalculationLedgerEntry.al:333-335
var ImmutableLedgerErr: Label 'Rebate Calculation Ledger Entries are immutable and cannot be modified or deleted.'; IsPostingStamp: Boolean;

L3 Playwright drove Submit → Approve → Activate via Actions menu on the agreement card. Submit & Approve worked: status transitioned Draft → Pending Approval → Approved. But repeated Activate clicks fail: the BC web client raised the dialog 'Active agreements must be revised through the agreement management action.' (the OnModify trigger). After dismissing, the agreement list still shows Status=Approved and the Rebate Agreement Versions page (50005) is empty ('There is nothing to show in this view'). No Version 1 record was ever persisted. The candidate's OnModify trigger blocks Modify on Active xRec, but the page action framework's auto-save round-trip causes the activation transaction to roll back. agent_code.

procedure Activate(var Agreement: ...) begin if Agreement.Status <> Agreement.Status::Approved then Error(InvalidStatusErr, ...); Agreement.ValidateForActivation(); CreateVersion(Agreement, 'Activated'); Agreement.Status := Agreement.Status::Active; Agreement."Activated At" := CurrentDateTime();

L4: the Agreement Card definition (page 50124) contains no `actions { }` block. The Activate / SubmitAgreement / ApproveAgreement procedures exist in codeunits but are never wired to any page action. L3: Created Calculation Request CR-OPUS47-1 (Request Type=Calculation, Agreement No.=VR-OPUS47-1) and invoked 'Run'. Request transitions to Status=Completed with no error, but the Rebate Calculation Entries page (50010) is empty. Two compounded defects: (1) the codeunit filters on `Agreement.SetRange(Status, Agreement.Status::Active)` and my agreement is stuck at Approved due to F-02; (2) more importantly, the codeunit never iterates posted purchase invoices/sales invoices/value entries — it loops `AgreementLine`s and writes one calc entry per line using `AgreementLine."Minimum Amount"` as the base, NOT the amount from any source transaction. There is no `Purch. Inv. Header`/`Purch. Inv. Line`/`Sales Invoice Line` reference anywhere in RebateCalculationEngine, so the spec's 'Entries reference source purchase invoice numbers' can never hold. agent_code.

L3 Playwright: created Calc Request CALC-001 (Agreement=VEND-REB-001, Mode=Accrual, Vendor Filter=10000, dates left blank so all dates allowed), then invoked 'Run Calculation'. Request showed Started At=6/7/2026 1:30 PM and Completed At=6/7/2026 1:30 PM in the list, but Source Lines Processed/Eligible and Calculated Amount remained BLANK. L1: navigated to the Calc Ledger Entries page (50012) — the page reports 'There is nothing to show in this view' (verified via DOM inspect: `body.textContent.includes('There is nothing to show in this view') === true`). Vendor 10000 has 3 posted purchase invoices per sandbox_facts (107209/107210/107211), yet the eligibility engine matched 0 source lines. Root cause in L4: RebateEligibilityEngine unconditionally forces `PurchInvLine.SetRange(Type, PurchInvLine.Type::Item)` even when Agreement."Item No." is blank, which mis-filters away non-Item purchase lines that real vendor invoices typically include — and combined with the Status never reaching Completed (see F-04), the run produced no usable output.

attribution: agent_code
L4 review: `PostAccrual` only flips `AccrualEntry.Status` to Posted and calls `Modify`. It never creates a `Gen. Journal Line`, never calls `GenJnlPostLine.RunWithCheck`, never inserts G/L entries. The Rebate Posting Setup's Accrual Debit/Credit accounts are read by ValidateAgreement but never used to actually post anything. Spec pass_if 'Real G/L entries posted (not just status flip)' — the code IS just a status flip. The runtime workflow is also unreachable because F-03 produces no accrual candidates and F-02 keeps the agreement out of Active state. agent_code.

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L3 Playwright: invoked 'Post Accruals' on CALC-001 — runtime raised the BC error "Calculation request CALC-001 is not in Completed status and cannot be posted." Two compounding agent defects in evidence: (1) the calc engine's Status field never reached Completed despite Started/Completed timestamps being populated (F-03 chain), so PostAccrualsForRequest's status guard rejected the request before any posting attempt; (2) even if posting did proceed, the PostAccrualEntry path only Insert()s a custom 'Rebate Accrual Entry' record with the flag `"G/L Posted" := true;` — it never calls `GenJnlPostLine`, `GenJournalLine.RunWithCheck`, or any standard BC G/L posting API. A grep across workspace/src/ for these symbols returned ZERO matches. No real G/L entries are created — the posting is a status flip with no general ledger persistence.

attribution: agent_code
L3 Playwright: Configured Rebate Posting Setup with Vendor Rebate Accrual Account=5110, Rebate Expense Account=5710, Vendor Rebate Settlement Account=5120, Gen. Journal Template=GENERAL, Gen. Journal Batch=DEFAULT. Clicked Post Accruals on RCALC-000002 (17 ledger entries, total LCY=280.66). BC dialog showed 'Rebate accrual entries have been successfully posted to the General Ledger.' BUT the Rebate Calc Request List, after page reload, still shows RCALC-000002 with Posting Status='Open' and No. of G/L Entries=0. Re-attempted posting; same result. L4 root cause: the page action unconditionally fires 'PostedSuccessfullyMsg' AFTER PostAccruals returns, even when ProcessRequest exits via the early `if not FindSet then Message(NoUnpostedEntries); exit;` path. The success message lies; no G/L entries are actually created. Spec pass_if 'Real G/L entries posted (not just status flip)' is not met — in fact even the status flip didn't happen.

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: Editing an Active agreement does NOT auto-create a new immutable version — instead the table's OnModify trigger errors out. The CreateRevision codeunit demotes the agreement back to Draft and bumps Version No., requiring the user to walk the full Submit→Approve→Activate flow again, and a new Version row is written only at Activate time (via CreateVersion). The spec asks 'Editing an active agreement creates a new version' — the candidate's implementation blocks the edit rather than versioning it. Compounded by F-02: I can't actually drive this in the UI because no agreement ever reaches Active. agent_code.
CODERebateTables.al:96-100
trigger OnModify() begin if xRec.Status = xRec.Status::Active then Error('Active agreements must be revised through the agreement management action.'); end;
CODERebateCodeunits.al:108-114
procedure CreateRevision(var Agreement: Record "Rebate Agreement Header") begin Agreement.TestField(Status, Agreement.Status::Active); Agreement.Status := Agreement.Status::Draft; Agreement."Version No." += 1; Agreement.Modify(true); end;
Claude CodeFAIL
Council verdict: FAIL (1 FAIL)1F

Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4 mapping (action says 'Modify the rule line on agreement... Save.'). The spec requires that editing an active agreement creates a new version while leaving v1 intact. In the submission, CreateVersion is invoked solely from Activate() — never from any OnModify trigger on the header, line, or rule tables, and never from any 'edit' workflow. The Header.OnModify trigger only stamps Modified By/At. The Agreement Line table (RebateAgreementLine.Table.al) has no OnModify trigger at all. Therefore an edit to the agreement (or rule line) on an Active agreement does NOT create a Version 2; v1 simply receives the new values silently, violating the immutability of versions.
CODERebateAgreementMgmt.Codeunit.al:43
// Inside Activate() — the ONLY call site of CreateVersion CreateVersion(Agreement, 'Activated');
CODERebateAgreementHeader.Table.al:207-211
trigger OnModify() begin "Modified By" := CopyStr(UserId(), 1, MaxStrLen("Modified By")); "Modified At" := CurrentDateTime(); end;
BASHgrep -rn 'CreateVersion' workspace/src/
RebateAgreementMgmt.Codeunit.al:43 (call site) and 67 (definition) — only ever invoked from Activate

Council verdict: FAIL (1 FAIL)1F
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L3 Playwright: opened agreement card while Status=Active. Page is locked (IsEditable evaluates to false because Status≠Draft), so fields show as static textboxes and Rebate % cannot be edited via UI. No 'Make changes' enables editing the rate. L4 confirms: no Rebate Agreement Version table exists, no codeunit creates a version snapshot on edit. The agent's immutability strategy is 'lock the fields when Active', not 'spawn an immutable version 1 + create version 2 on edit'. Spec pass_if 'Version 2 created; version 1 untouched' is unachievable — the agent has no versioning machinery.

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4 + dependency-on-failed-upstream. RebateRecalcEngine.RunRecalculation only iterates the existing RebateCalcLedgerEntry rows for the agreement and creates delta rows in the same custom ledger. Two defects: (1) Upstream F-03 produced ZERO original calc ledger entries for VEND-REB-001, so recalc would find nothing to delta. (2) Even when deltas are generated, the codeunit never invokes any G/L posting — the spec's assert requires 'Delta G/L adjustments posted via /ODataV4/.../G_LEntries'; the agent's recalc engine cannot satisfy that because no GenJnlPostLine path exists anywhere in the submission (F-04 finding). Pass conditions cannot be met.

L4: `PostSettlement` only marks accrual entries Closed and stamps `SettlementHeader.Posted := true`. It never creates a Purchase Credit Memo via `Purch. Header.Insert` + `PurchPost.Run`, nor a Sales Credit Memo, nor a Gen. Journal Line, despite the `Settlement Method` enum providing 'Vendor Credit Memo'/'Customer Credit Memo'/'Journal Entry' values. The settlement method branch is never honoured. Spec pass_if 'Credit memo / journal created; accruals closed' — only the second half is done (and only as a status flip, with no underlying G/L impact since F-04 also doesn't post). agent_code.

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4 static review: RebateSettlementEngine.Post() only updates AccrualEntry.Status to Settled/'Partially Settled' and flips Settlement.Status to Posted. It does NOT instantiate any Purchase Header (Document Type::'Credit Memo'), Sales Header, or General Journal Line, and never invokes a posting codeunit. The spec requires a vendor credit memo / customer credit memo / journal entry to be generated — none of the standard BC posting APIs are referenced from the settlement engine. pass_if's 'Credit memo / journal created' cannot be satisfied by the shipped code.
CODERebateSettlementEngine.Codeunit.al:86-121
procedure Post(var Settlement: ...): SettlementLine.SetRange("Settlement No.", Settlement."No."); if SettlementLine.FindSet() then repeat if AccrualEntry.Get(...) then begin AccrualEntry."Settled Amount" += SettlementLine.Amount; ... AccrualEntry.Status := AccrualEntry.Status::Settled; AccrualEntry.InternalModify(); end; until SettlementLine.Next() = 0; Settlement.Status := Settlement.Status::Posted;

Opus 4.7 · run 0Inconcl.
attribution: grader_env
L3 cannot drive the workflow: SettlementProposalMgt.LoadWorksheetBuffer requires calc entries with G/L Posted=true. Since F-04 failed to actually mark any calc ledger entries as G/L Posted=true (no G/L entries materialised, Posting Status stayed Open), the settlement worksheet has no candidate entries to select. The proposal-→-approve-→-post flow cannot be exercised end-to-end. The settlement engine code itself (PostVendorCreditMemo) does invoke the correct standard API — `PurchPost.Run(PurchHeader)` — but the upstream F-04 defect blocks reaching it via UI. Marking INCONCLUSIVE rather than FAIL because the settlement codeunit is not itself observably broken — only its precondition is unreachable due to F-04's agent_code bug.

Opus 4.7 · run 0Inconcl.
attribution: grader_env
L4 shows the idempotency guard IS present: `AccrualEntry.TestField(Status, AccrualEntry.Status::Open)` at the entry of PostAccrual rejects a re-post (status would already be Posted), and the Rebate Accrual Entry OnModify trigger raises 'Posted, closed, or reversed accrual entries are immutable.' But the runtime workflow cannot be driven: F-04's PostAccrual doesn't actually create any G/L entries to be duplicated in the first place, and F-02 prevents the agreement from reaching Active so no accruals are generated. I cannot capture a UI screenshot of the re-post error. INCONCLUSIVE per the rule that code review alone cannot establish a functional PASS.

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4 static review. The implementation is structurally idempotent (after the first PostAccrualsForRequest, every calc ledger entry is flagged Accrued=true, so the second invocation finds 0 unprocessed entries via SetRange(Accrued, false)). However, the spec's pass_if requires 'Error raised; no duplicate G/L entries.' The shipped code does NOT raise any error on a second post — it silently completes a no-op. There is no AlreadyPostedErr, TestField, or status check that would trip on re-post. Per the pass_if's explicit 'Error raised' requirement, this fails. (Note: this same finding compounds with F-04 — no G/L entries are ever created in the first place.)
CODERebatePostingEngine.Codeunit.al:28-31,86-99
LedgerEntry.SetRange("Request No.", Request."No."); LedgerEntry.SetRange(Accrued, false); LedgerEntry.SetRange(Reversal, false); ... LinkLedgerToAccrual: ... LedgerEntry.Accrued := true; LedgerEntry.InternalModify();

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L3 not driveable: there is no 'Post Accruals' action in the UI (see F-04), so the duplicate-post workflow cannot be executed. L4: 'PostAccrual' does include a duplicate guard — if Status=Posted on entry, it raises 'Accrual entry is already posted.' However, since the underlying posting itself is fake (no G/L entries written — see F-04), the duplicate-prevention guard protects only the in-memory status flip, not a real ledger. The criterion's pass_if 'Idempotent posting' tests the protection of posted ledger entries; given the surrounding posting is non-functional, this control has no real ledger to protect, and the workflow path that the criterion asks to test ('re-post the same accrual') is not reachable via UI.

Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
Action explicitly says 'Read the posting codeunit. Find the role/permission guard' — L4 graded. The `Rebate Posting Engine` codeunit lacks an explicit role check, but permission isolation IS present: the `REBATE VIEW` permission set grants only `R` on tabledata and does NOT include `codeunit "Rebate Posting Engine" = X`, while `REBATE ADMIN` does. Per spec pass_if 'Guard or permission isolation present.' — the OR branch is satisfied.
CODERebateReportsAndPermissions.al:97-148
permissionset 50000 "REBATE VIEW" ... Permissions = tabledata "Rebate Setup" = R, ... (all tabledata = R; no codeunit "Rebate Posting Engine" granted)
CODERebateReportsAndPermissions.al:150-195
permissionset 50001 "REBATE ADMIN" ... Permissions = ... codeunit "Rebate Posting Engine" = X, codeunit "Rebate Settlement Engine" = X, codeunit "Rebate Accrual Engine" = X, codeunit "Rebate Calculation Engine" = X,
Claude CodePASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4 permission-set isolation: Only 'BSB Rebate Poster' grants Insert/Modify on 'BSB Rebate Accrual Entry' and 'BSB Rebate Calc. Ledger Entry'. The 'BSB Rebate Analyst' set (and the upstream 'BSB Rebate Viewer' it extends) grants only Read on these tables. Manager/Approver/Auditor sets similarly do not list these tables with Insert. Therefore a non-Poster role cannot persist new accrual entries — when PostAccrualEntry's `AccrualEntry.Insert(false)` runs, BC's standard permission filter will block the insert for any role lacking the Poster set. Permission-isolation guard is present and correctly scoped.
CODERebatePoster.PermissionSet.al:1-11
permissionset 50004 "BSB Rebate Poster" { Assignable = true; IncludedPermissionSets = "BSB Rebate Analyst"; Permissions = tabledata "BSB Rebate Accrual Entry" = RIM, tabledata "BSB Rebate Calc. Ledger Entry" = RIM, tabledata "BSB Rebate Job Queue Log" = RIM;
CODERebateAnalyst.PermissionSet.al:1-13
permissionset 50001 "BSB Rebate Analyst" { IncludedPermissionSets = "BSB Rebate Viewer"; Permissions = tabledata "BSB Rebate Calc. Request" = RIMD, tabledata "BSB Rebate Calc. Buffer" = RIMD, tabledata "BSB Rebate Settlement Header" = RIMD, tabledata "BSB Rebate Settlement Line" = RIMD, tabledata "BSB Rebate Integration Log" = RIM; // NB: no Accrual Entry / Calc Ledger Entry permission
CODERebateViewer.PermissionSet.al:17
tabledata "BSB Rebate Accrual Entry" = R,
DartsPASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P

Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4 static review (action explicitly says 'Read the posting codeunit. Find the role/permission guard'): the Rebate G/L Posting Engine codeunit itself contains no explicit role check (no RequirePoster() or User Setup gate), BUT permission set isolation is in place: REBATE-VIEWER and REBATE-ANALYST permission sets do NOT grant execute on the 'Rebate G/L Posting Engine' codeunit, while REBATE-MANAGER explicitly grants `codeunit "Rebate G/L Posting Engine" = X`. The spec's pass_if accepts either an explicit role check OR permission-set isolation; the latter is satisfied for non-manager roles. (Note: the agent did not ship a dedicated 'Rebate Poster' role — Manager doubles as poster — which is a thinner model than spec §12 prescribes, but isolation against Viewer/Analyst is real.)
CODEPS50100.RebateViewer.al:1-58
permissionset 50100 "REBATE-VIEWER" // Permissions list contains tabledata = R and page = X only; // NO 'codeunit "Rebate G/L Posting Engine" = X;' entry.
CODEPS50101.RebateAnalyst.al:1-22
permissionset 50101 "REBATE-ANALYST" IncludedPermissionSets = "REBATE-VIEWER"; // Permissions list has tabledata permissions only; // NO 'codeunit "Rebate G/L Posting Engine" = X;' entry.
CODEPS50102.RebateManager.al:22
codeunit "Rebate G/L Posting Engine" = X,

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: the spec calls for separate Viewer / Analyst / Manager / Approver / Poster / Administrator / Auditor roles (sec. 12). The agent ships a SINGLE 'RBT Permissions' set that grants RIMD on every rebate table and Execute on every rebate codeunit — no role separation at all. PostAccrual has no explicit role guard, no RequirePoster() check, and the only permission set in the extension does not restrict posting to a Poster role. Anyone with the single permission set can post.

Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
Action explicitly says 'Read the agent's RebateCalculationLedgerEntry table ... Look for OnModify/OnDelete triggers' — L4 graded. Table 50010 (Rebate Calc. Ledger Entry) has an OnModify trigger that Errors when xRec.Status was Posted/Closed/Reversed, and an OnDelete trigger that unconditionally Errors. Table 50011 (Rebate Accrual Entry) has identical guards. Spec pass_if 'Immutability triggers present.' — satisfied.
CODERebateTables.al:361-370
trigger OnModify() begin if xRec.Status in [xRec.Status::Posted, xRec.Status::Closed, xRec.Status::Reversed] then Error('Posted, closed, or reversed calculation entries are immutable.'); end; trigger OnDelete() begin Error('Calculation ledger entries cannot be deleted.'); end;
CODERebateTables.al:414-423
trigger OnModify() begin if xRec.Status in [xRec.Status::Posted, xRec.Status::Closed, xRec.Status::Reversed] then Error('Posted, closed, or reversed accrual entries are immutable.'); end; trigger OnDelete() begin Error('Accrual entries cannot be deleted.'); end;
Claude CodePASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4 static review of the two posted-entry tables. RebateCalcLedgerEntry (table 50010) raises 'Rebate calculation ledger entries are immutable.' on OnModify, OnDelete, and OnRename triggers — fully immutable to external code. RebateAccrualEntry (table 50011) raises ImmutableErr unconditionally on OnDelete and OnRename, and on OnModify when an InternalEdit flag (used by the engine's controlled InternalModify procedure for status updates like reversal/settled) is false — meaning ordinary user modifies through the page raise an error. Immutability triggers are present, correctly worded, and consistent with the spec's data-model rule that posted entries are immutable.
CODERebateCalcLedgerEntry.Table.al:169-191
trigger OnModify() begin Error(ImmutableErr); end; trigger OnDelete() begin Error(ImmutableErr); end; trigger OnRename() begin Error(ImmutableErr); end; ... ImmutableErr: Label 'Rebate calculation ledger entries are immutable.';
CODERebateAccrualEntry.Table.al:183-208
trigger OnModify() begin if not InternalEdit then Error(ImmutableErr); end; trigger OnDelete() begin Error(ImmutableErr); end; trigger OnRename() begin Error(ImmutableErr); end;
DartsPASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4 static review (action explicitly says 'Read the agent's RebateCalculationLedgerEntry table'): the Tab50110 RebateCalculationLedgerEntry table has explicit OnModify and OnDelete triggers that Error() with a clear immutability message. OnModify is gated by an internal IsPostingStamp flag so that the dedicated MarkAsPosted/MarkAsClosed procedures can stamp G/L and settlement fields without tripping the guard; the flag is reset immediately after the controlled write. OnDelete is unconditional. Spec pass_if 'Immutability triggers present' is satisfied.
CODETab50110.RebateCalculationLedgerEntry.al:292-301
trigger OnModify() begin if not IsPostingStamp then Error(ImmutableLedgerErr); end; trigger OnDelete() begin Error(ImmutableLedgerErr); end;
CODETab50110.RebateCalculationLedgerEntry.al:333-335
var ImmutableLedgerErr: Label 'Rebate Calculation Ledger Entries are immutable and cannot be modified or deleted.'; IsPostingStamp: Boolean;

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: both posted-entry tables — RBT Calc Ledger Entry (50012) and RBT Accrual Entry (50013) — have NO OnModify and NO OnDelete triggers. Worse, the permission set grants RIMD (read/insert/modify/delete) on these tables. There is no enforcement that posted ledger rows are immutable; they can be edited or deleted directly from a list page or via OData.
CODERebateCalculationLedgerEntry.Table.al:1-39
table 50012 "RBT Calc Ledger Entry" { DataClassification = CustomerContent; fields { field(1; "Entry No."; Integer) { AutoIncrement = true; ... } ... } keys { key(PK; "Entry No.") { Clustered = true; } } } // no trigger OnModify / OnDelete
CODERebateAccrualEntry.Table.al:1-35
table 50013 "RBT Accrual Entry" { ... fields {...} keys {...} } // no OnModify or OnDelete trigger
CODERebatePermissions.PermissionSet.al:20-21
tabledata "RBT Calc Ledger Entry" = RIMD, tabledata "RBT Accrual Entry" = RIMD,

Multi-Entity Intercompany Recharge

Enterprise intercompany recharge engine: shared-service cost allocation, multi-entity/multi-currency recharges, approvals, IC posting, inbox/outbox processing, reversals, reconciliation, audit trail, and retry-safe automation.

Positive path is implemented in code; negative path is demonstrated in UI.

F-02 · weight 3
Fixed-percentage / amount-based allocation produces lines that balance and never exceed the source amount.
Axis: Functional correctness · contributes to 40% of the total score
Codex (GPT-5 CLI)INCONCLUSIVE
Council verdict: INCONCLUSIVE (1 INCONCLUSIVE)1I
Council attribution: grader_env
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Inconcl.
attribution: grader_env
L4: Simulate iterates RequestLine and computes Allocation Amount = Source × Percent/100 with per-line Calculation Trace stored. However the engine REQUIRES the user to manually pre-enter allocation lines and percentages — there is no auto-proposal generation from source transactions (only re-balancing of pre-existing lines). L3 attempt to drive Simulate from UI was blocked because the ICR Request Lines subpage's New Line button stays disabled in the BC web client when the parent card is in default read-only mode and the lines aren't persisted across navigation. Without runtime proof of balanced lines + trace, and noting the spec asks for proposal generation from a basis (only re-balancing exists), the runtime cannot be confirmed via UI.
CODEICRechargeManagement.Codeunit.al:21-77
RequestLine."Allocation Amount" := Round(RequestHeader."Source Amount" * RequestLine."Allocation Percent" / 100, RoundingPrecision); RequestLine."Calculation Trace" := CopyStr(StrSubstNo('%1 percent of %2', RequestLine."Allocation Percent", RequestHeader."Source Amount"), 1, MaxStrLen(...));
CODEICRechargeRequest.Table.al:258-263
field(13; "Calculation Trace"; Text[250]) { Editable = false; }

attribution: grader_env
L3 blocked: needed a Recharge Line with IC Partner Code populated, but CRONUS IN ships with the BC IC framework un-provisioned (no IC Partners) so populating the Recharge Partner's IC Partner Code (TableRelation 'IC Partner') requires a multi-step IC Setup wizard that the agent's extension does not bootstrap. L1 OData: no custom API/web service exposed for RechargeHeader, so no programmatic alternative. L4: code shows allocation calc preserves Source Amount + Allocation % per line and writes Allocated Amount; lines store the calculation detail (basis, %, computed amount). Logic is sound but unverified at runtime.
CODECod50000.RechargeManagement.al:214-228
procedure CalculateAllocationLines(var RechargeHeader: Record "Recharge Header") ... if not RechargeLine."Manual Override" then begin RechargeLine."Allocated Amount" := Round(RechargeLine."Source Amount" * RechargeLine."Allocation %" / 100); RechargeLine.Modify();
CODETab50003.RechargeLine.al:80-92
field(31; "Allocation %"; Decimal) ... CalculateAllocation(); field(32; "Allocated Amount"; Decimal) ... trigger OnValidate begin ... "Manual Override" := true;

Council verdict: INCONCLUSIVE (1 INCONCLUSIVE)1I
Council attribution: grader_env
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Inconcl.
attribution: grader_env
L4: CalculateAllocations distributes Source Amount across user-entered lines using each line's Allocation %/Recharge Method, stores Allocation Calculation Note (e.g. rounding adjustment). Lines retain Allocation Basis, Allocation %, Source Amount, Recharge Amount and Exchange Rate for per-line traceability. However the spec asks for proposal/simulation generation FROM a selected basis or source transactions — the codeunit only recomputes amounts on lines the user has already added; there is no engine that GENERATES lines from a basis or selected source documents. UI workflow blocked because IC Partner master is unseeded in CRONUS IN and the candidate ships no auto-provisioning of IC Partner master / Recharge Request No. Series; cannot demonstrate proposal generation interactively.
CODECod50100.ICRechargeCalculation.al:24-118
procedure CalculateAllocations(var RechargeHeader: Record "IC Recharge Request Header") // per-line Recharge Amount + Allocation Calculation Note populated
CODETab50103.ICRechargeRequestLine.al:195-204
field(18; "Allocation Basis"; Text[100]) {...} field(19; "Allocation Calculation Note"; Text[250]) {...}

CODEICRechargeManagement.Codeunit.al:69-74
if TotalAmount > RequestHeader."Source Amount" then Error('Allocated amount %1 exceeds source amount %2 for request %3.', TotalAmount, RequestHeader."Source Amount", RequestHeader."No."); if RequestHeader."Allocation Basis" in [...::"Fixed Percentage", ...] then if Round(TotalPercent, 0.00001) <> 100 then Error('Allocation percent must total 100 for request %1...');

L3 blocked: cannot push the request through to allocation step without IC Partner master records (CRONUS IN ships without IC framework set up). L4: the ValidateAllocation routine sums Source vs Allocated and explicitly errors if TotalAllocated > TotalSource, called from ValidateRechargeRequest before status moves to Validated. The over-allocation guard is implemented correctly but cannot be exercised in this sandbox without IC partner provisioning.

procedure ValidateAllocation(...): Boolean ... if TotalAllocated > TotalSource then begin ErrorText := StrSubstNo('Total allocated amount %1 exceeds total source amount %2.', TotalAllocated, TotalSource); exit(false); ... Error(ErrorText)

attribution: grader_env
L4: ValidateAllocations and CalculateAllocations both guard sum(Fixed lines) > SourceAmt with TotalFixedExceedsSourceErr; ActualCostExceedsSourceErr similarly guards Actual Cost; Percentage allocations require sum to 100 ± 0.01. Logic is in place. UI walkthrough blocked because the IC Partner master / No. Series is not seeded in CRONUS IN — without an IC Partner record the candidate's recharge request can't reach a point where lines are validated end-to-end. Code-only PASS not permitted for a functional criterion.
CODECod50100.ICRechargeCalculation.al:50-51
if (SourceAmt > 0) and (TotalFixed > SourceAmt + GetRoundingPrecision()) then Error(TotalFixedExceedsSourceErr, TotalFixed, SourceAmt);
CODECod50100.ICRechargeCalculation.al:188-200
if HasFixedLines then begin if TotalFixed > SourceAmt + GetRoundingPrecision() then Error(TotalFixedExceedsSourceErr, TotalFixed, SourceAmt); if (not HasPctLines) and (not HasActualLines) then if Abs(TotalFixed - SourceAmt) > GetRoundingPrecision() then Error(FixedAmtNotBalancedErr, TotalFixed, SourceAmt);

F-04 · weight 3
Duplicate posting of the same recharge request is prevented.

Codex (GPT-5 CLI)FAIL
Council verdict: FAIL (1 FAIL)1F
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: ICR Posting Mgt.Post() inserts rows into the candidate's own ICR Posting Entry table and an ICR Processing Queue entry — but NEVER calls the BC standard intercompany posting APIs (no GenJnlPostLine, no ICOutboxJnlPost.Run, no real journal/IC document generation). The spec requires "creation of intercompany journals and/or intercompany documents" — the candidate fakes posting via custom ledger rows. Status field flips to Posted but no actual finance journal is produced. The header has no Editable=false on most fields, so 'locked' is only enforced by the codeunit's status check before re-posting (idempotency guard exists). This is a faked posting that doesn't produce real IC output as the spec requires.
CODEICRechargeManagement.Codeunit.al:285-339
PostingEntry.Init(); PostingEntry."Request No." := RequestHeader."No."; ... PostingEntry.Insert(true); // no GenJnlPostLine.RunWithCheck(...), no ICOutboxJnlPost.Run, no SalesPost
CODEICRechargeManagement.Codeunit.al:333-337
RequestHeader.Status := RequestHeader.Status::Posted; RequestHeader."Posted By" := CopyStr(UserId(), 1, ...); RequestHeader."Posted At" := CurrentDateTime();

L3 blocked: cannot post without IC framework provisioned in sandbox. L4: Recharge-Post codeunit verifies Status=Approved, copies into PostedRechargeHeader/Line, writes to standard 'IC Outbox Jnl. Line' table (native intercompany table), then transitions Status to Posted. Header field 'Status' is Editable=false and locked from edit; Recharge Header card group binds Posted Document No. (visible after posting). Logic is correct but runtime not driven.

if RechargeHeader.Status <> RechargeHeader.Status::Approved then Error(NotApprovedErr, ...); ... PostedRechargeHeader.Insert(true); ... CreateIntercompanyEntries(PostedRechargeHeader); RechargeHeader.Status := RechargeHeader.Status::Posted; RechargeHeader."Posted Document No." := PostedDocNo;

Jnl.-Post Batch" — standard BC routine posts the journal and creates IC Outbox entries when IC Partner Code is set. After posting, Posted flag + Status::Posted are stamped and TestStatusIsDraft locks edits. Implementation looks correct. Codex (GPT-5 CLI)INCONCLUSIVE

Council attribution: grader_env
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Inconcl.
attribution: grader_env
L4: Post() has three idempotency guards — status must be Approved, Posting Reference must be blank, and no existing posting entries. A second Post call on the same approved request will be rejected by the status check (no longer Approved after first post) AND the Posting Reference guard, AND the existence-of-entries guard. Code is correct. L3 runtime not reachable because the upstream lifecycle (Lines entry, Validate, Submit, Approve, Post) could not be completed in the UI due to the Lines subpage edit issue.
CODEICRechargeManagement.Codeunit.al:293-300
if RequestHeader.Status <> RequestHeader.Status::Approved then Error('Only approved recharge requests can be posted.'); if RequestHeader."Posting Reference" <> '' then Error('Recharge request %1 has already been posted.', RequestHeader."No."); PostingEntry.SetRange("Request No.", RequestHeader."No."); if not PostingEntry.IsEmpty() then Error('Posting entries already exist for request %1.', RequestHeader."No.");

L3 blocked (same IC env reason as F-04). L4: Recharge-Post.Code() guards on 'Posted Document No.' being already populated and raises DuplicatePostingErr. Background automation (Cod50003) also exposes CheckDuplicatePosting predicate guarding reruns. Idempotency design is present and correct.
CODECod50001.RechargePost.al:20-35
DuplicatePostingErr: Label 'Recharge request %1 has already been posted as %2.'; ... if RechargeHeader."Posted Document No." <> '' then Error(DuplicatePostingErr, RechargeHeader."No.", RechargeHeader."Posted Document No.");
CODECod50003.RechargeAutomation.al:61-67
procedure CheckDuplicatePosting(var RechargeHeader: Record "Recharge Header"): Boolean if RechargeHeader."Posted Document No." <> '' then exit(true);

Council verdict: INCONCLUSIVE (1 INCONCLUSIVE)1I

Opus 4.7 · run 0Inconcl.
attribution: grader_env
L4: duplicate-posting guard is enforced in two places — AdvanceStatus refuses to call post-management when Posted=true, and PostRechargeRequest itself re-checks the Posted flag and raises AlreadyPostedErr. The Posted boolean is set once at the end of a successful post and never cleared. Logic prevents duplicate output. UI workflow walkthrough blocked by grader_env (no IC Partner master / No. Series).
CODETab50102.ICRechargeRequestHeader.al:220-228
if "Posted" then Error(AlreadyPostedErr, "No."); ICRechargePostMgt.PostRechargeRequest(Rec);
CODECod50101.ICRechargePostMgt.al:23-25
if RechargeHeader."Posted" then Error(AlreadyPostedErr, RechargeHeader."No.");

attribution: grader_env
L4: Reverse() creates inverse-amount reversing entries with Original Entry No. linkage and Correction Entry = true, sets Reason Code on header, flips status to Reversed. Logic is sound, but the reversal entries are still in the candidate's CUSTOM ICR Posting Entry table, not real BC G/L entries — and the underlying Post never produced real entries either (see F-04). Reversal-of-fake-posting still has the linkage and reason-code structure the spec describes for its own data model. Runtime not reachable in UI.
CODEICRechargeManagement.Codeunit.al:376-423
ReversalEntry.Amount := -PostingEntry.Amount; ReversalEntry."Correction Entry" := true; ReversalEntry."Original Entry No." := PostingEntry."Entry No."; ... RequestHeader.Status := RequestHeader.Status::Reversed; RequestHeader."Correction Reason Code" := ReasonCode;

Implementation matches the spec.

if PostedRechargeHeader.Reversed then Error('Recharge document %1 has already been reversed.', ...); ... RechargeHeader."Source Amount" := -PostedRechargeLine."Source Amount"; ... StrSubstNo('Reversed via %1', ReversalDocNo), '', ReasonCode);

L4: Exchange Rate is persisted on the request header and on each posting entry. Simulate() errors if rates differ but rate is zero. However the implementation does NOT consult BC's standard Currency Exchange Rate table — it requires the user to manually enter the rate on the header. The spec says "storage of exchange rate used during processing" (✓) and "validation of unmapped accounts or dimension values before posting" — the rate guard satisfies that. Runtime not reachable in UI.
CODEICRechargeManagement.Codeunit.al:57-61
if RequestHeader."Target Currency Code" <> RequestHeader."Source Currency Code" then begin if RequestHeader."Exchange Rate" = 0 then Error('Exchange rate is required for request %1.', RequestHeader."No."); LineTargetAmount := Round(RequestLine."Allocation Amount" * RequestHeader."Exchange Rate", RoundingPrecision);
CODEICRechargeRequest.Table.al:68-73
field(12; "Exchange Rate"; Decimal) { DecimalPlaces = 0 : 10; InitValue = 1; }

attribution: grader_env
L3 blocked. L4: Recharge Line has a Currency Factor field which is intended to store the exchange rate used; TransferFields copies it to the Posted Recharge Line on posting. HOWEVER the posting codeunit does NOT call CurrencyExchangeRate.ExchangeRate(...) / CurrencyFactor() to compute/store the rate, and posting does NOT block when Currency Factor is zero/missing. The currency-rate storage and validation required by the spec is only partially implemented — the field exists but the rate is never computed or guarded, so a cross-currency posting would persist Currency Factor = 0. Without runtime verification I cannot definitively call this PASS or FAIL.
CODETab50003.RechargeLine.al:94-106
field(40; "Currency Code"; Code[10]) ... TableRelation = Currency; field(41; "Currency Factor"; Decimal) ... DecimalPlaces = 0 : 15;
CODECod50001.RechargePost.al:60-70
PostedRechargeLine.TransferFields(RechargeLine); PostedRechargeLine."Document No." := PostedDocNo; PostedRechargeLine.Insert(true);

attribution: grader_env
L4: ApplyCurrencyConversion resolves the partner Currency Rule, calls GetCurrencyExchangeRate which reads the standard Currency Exchange Rate table for the Posting Date and writes Exchange Rate + Exchange Rate Amount onto each line (read-only). MissingExchangeRateErr blocks posting when no rate is available; InvalidExchangeRateErr blocks zero rates. The exchange rate IS persisted on the line at processing time. UI walkthrough blocked — IC Partner / No. Series not seeded; cannot drive cross-currency posting interactively. L4 alone is insufficient for a functional PASS.
CODECod50100.ICRechargeCalculation.al:239-260
ExchRate := GetCurrencyExchangeRate(EffectiveCurrCode, PostingDate, ReqLine."Line No."); ReqLine."Currency Code" := EffectiveCurrCode; ReqLine."Exchange Rate" := ExchRate; ReqLine."Exchange Rate Amount" := Round(ReqLine."Recharge Amount" / ExchRate, GetRoundingPrecision());
CODECod50100.ICRechargeCalculation.al:306-323
if not CurrExchRate.FindLast() then Error(MissingExchangeRateErr, CurrencyCode, PostingDate, LineNo); if (ExchRateAmt = 0) or (RelationalAmt = 0) then Error(InvalidExchangeRateErr, CurrencyCode, PostingDate, LineNo);
CODETab50103.ICRechargeRequestLine.al:205-221
field(20; "Exchange Rate"; Decimal) { ... Editable = false; ... } field(21; "Exchange Rate Amount"; Decimal) { ... Editable = false; ... }

L3 blocked. L4: IC Partner Code is enforced via TestField in ValidateRechargeRequest (proven at runtime in F-01). However ValidateMappings uses Message (not Error), meaning unmapped dimensions only WARN and do not block posting — the Recharge-Post codeunit never calls ValidateMappings before posting. Posting is blocked for missing partner mapping but NOT blocked for missing dimension translation, contrary to the spec.
CODECod50000.RechargeManagement.al:17-25
RechargeLine.TestField("Recharge Partner Code"); RechargeLine.TestField("IC Partner Code"); RechargeLine.TestField("Source G/L Account No."); RechargeLine.TestField("Target IC G/L Account No.");
CODECod50004.RechargeReconciliation.al:54-76
procedure ValidateMappings(...) ... RechargeDimensionMapping.SetRange("Recharge Partner Code", RechargeLine."Recharge Partner Code"); if RechargeDimensionMapping.IsEmpty then UnmappedFound := true; if UnmappedFound then begin Message('Unmapped dimensions found for recharge %1.', RechargeHeader."No."); exit(false); end;

L4: posting partially guards mapping — TestField(IC Partner Code) prevents posting without a partner, and IC G/L Account is via standard TableRelation. The spec explicitly requires 'validation of unmapped accounts or dimension values before posting' and the agent's implementation doesn't translate dimensions across companies. Partial implementation = fail.

L4: Self-approval is blocked by user-id check. However the approval threshold only sets an 'Amount Requires Escalation' flag — it does NOT enforce any role-based routing or block the Approver based on permissions/role. Any authenticated user can call Approve(); there is no permission set or role gating because the extension ships NO permission sets (see S-04). The Analyst-cannot-approve-high-value half of the spec is not enforced — only segregation (self-approval) is. Partial implementation.
CODEICRechargeManagement.Codeunit.al:215-241
if RequestHeader.Status <> RequestHeader.Status::"Pending Approval" then Error('Only pending approval recharge requests can be approved.'); if RequestHeader."Submitted By" = UserId() then Error('Users cannot approve their own recharge request.');
CODEICRechargeManagement.Codeunit.al:194-201
Threshold := GetThreshold(RequestHeader."Partner Code"); ... ApprovalEntry."Amount Requires Escalation" := (Threshold <> 0) and (RequestHeader."Source Amount" > Threshold);

L3 blocked. L4: Permission sets gate the Analyst to read-only on Approval Entry (cannot approve) and exclude Recharge-Post codeunit from Analyst/Manager, so segregation-of-duties is enforced via table/codeunit permissions. Approval threshold logic is implemented. However the bespoke ApproveRequest procedure in Cod50000 does NOT check that the approver is different from the submitter — segregation is enforced only by permission sets at the codeunit/table level, not by the approval routing logic itself. The platform Approvals Management framework (which provides self-approval guards) is NOT used (see C-04). Partial coverage of the spec.
CODECod50000.RechargeManagement.al:143-173
local procedure CheckApprovalRequired(RechargeHeader: Record "Recharge Header"): Boolean ... Threshold := RechargeSetup."Default Approval Threshold"; ... if TotalAmount > Threshold then exit(true);

L4: the candidate's 'approval' is purely a status flip performed by any user with access to the page. AdvanceStatus moves Pending Approval → Approved with no role check, no approval threshold, no UserSetup substitute lookup, and no segregation-of-duties guard. There is no integration with the platform Approvals Management/Workflow framework. An Analyst can trivially click Advance Status past the approval step. The criterion (threshold-enforced approval routing + SoD-blocked self-approval) is unsatisfied.
CODETab50102.ICRechargeRequestHeader.al:205-234
// AdvanceStatus blindly flips Status::Validated → Pending Approval → Approved → Posted // with no approver lookup, no threshold check, no SoD guard Status::"Pending Approval": Validate(Status, Status::Approved);

attribution: agent_code
L4: Implementation uses a CUSTOM ICR Processing Queue table — it does NOT integrate with BC's standard IC Inbox/Outbox tables (no use of "IC Outbox Transaction", "IC Inbox Transaction", "ICInboxOutboxMgt", or "IC Setup" codeunits). The Accept/Reject actions only flip a status field on the custom queue table; no real intercompany handoff to a partner company occurs. Rejection routes to exception via the header's Exception Flag but there is no real partner-side processing. The spec says "transfer to intercompany outbox" and "receipt and processing through intercompany inbox" — both refer to BC's standard mechanics which the implementation bypasses.
CODEICRechargeManagement.Codeunit.al:341-371
procedure AcceptQueue(var ProcessingQueue: Record "ICR Processing Queue") ... ProcessingQueue.Status := ProcessingQueue.Status::Accepted; ProcessingQueue."Last Attempt At" := CurrentDateTime(); ProcessingQueue.Modify(true);
CODEICRechargeManagement.Codeunit.al:326-331
ProcessingQueue.Init(); ProcessingQueue."Request No." := RequestHeader."No."; ProcessingQueue.Direction := ProcessingQueue.Direction::Outbox; ProcessingQueue.Status := ProcessingQueue.Status::Pending; ProcessingQueue.Insert(true);

L3 blocked. L4: The posting codeunit writes to standard BC 'IC Outbox Jnl. Line', i.e. it correctly hands off to the native IC outbox mechanism. However NO custom code handles inbox-side acceptance, rejection, or correction routing within the extension — there is no Recharge-side inbox processor or rejection-routing codeunit (only the standard BC IC inbox would surface entries). The acceptance/rejection/resubmission lifecycle expected by the spec is not implemented in the extension.
CODECod50001.RechargePost.al:80-112

L4: the extension relies entirely on standard BC's IC engine — by writing IC Partner Code on Gen Jnl lines and posting via Gen. Jnl.-Post Batch, outbox entries are created. There is NO partner-side acceptance/rejection routing, NO correction workflow code, and NO routing of rejected inbox transactions to an exception queue. The spec specifically requires that 'a rejected inbox transaction without proper correction is not silently completed and routes to correction/exception handling' — this is missing.
CODECod50101.ICRechargePostMgt.al:65-130
// CreateICGeneralJournalLines sets IC Partner Code + IC Account No/Type on each Gen Jnl Line // then PostJournalBatch calls Codeunit::"Gen. Jnl.-Post Batch"

L4: Approval is implemented as a bespoke ICR Workflow Mgt. codeunit writing to a custom ICR Approval Entry table. C-07
Code is modular and upgrade-safe, separating setup, calculation, posting, and reconciliation concerns.
Axis: Code quality · contributes to 30% of the total score
Codex (GPT-5 CLI)PASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: Post() has three guards (status, posting reference, existing entries). Reverse() has two guards (status, already-reversed marker). Reversal entries link to Original Entry No. for traceability. The idempotency intent is clear — though the underlying 'posting' is fake (custom ledger). Re-running posting on a posted request will Error rather than create duplicate output.
CODEICRechargeManagement.Codeunit.al:293-300
if RequestHeader.Status <> RequestHeader.Status::Approved then Error(...); if RequestHeader."Posting Reference" <> '' then Error('Recharge request %1 has already been posted.', RequestHeader."No."); PostingEntry.SetRange("Request No.", RequestHeader."No."); if not PostingEntry.IsEmpty() then Error('Posting entries already exist for request %1.', RequestHeader."No.");
CODEICRechargeManagement.Codeunit.al:383-386
if RequestHeader.Status <> RequestHeader.Status::Posted then Error(...); if RequestHeader."Reversed By Request No." <> '' then Error('Recharge request %1 has already been reversed.', RequestHeader."No.");
Claude CodePASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: posting guards on Status=Approved, 'Posted Document No.' empty, and not Reversed — three idempotency checks. Reversal guards on PostedRechargeHeader.Reversed. Reversal correctly links Posted document to reversal document via 'Reversal Document No.' and back-links the original Recharge Header. Posted Recharge Header/Line tables have no exposed Modify actions on relevant pages (Editable = false on key fields), supporting immutability. Replay-safe by status guard.
CODECod50001.RechargePost.al:31-38
if RechargeHeader.Status <> RechargeHeader.Status::Approved then Error(NotApprovedErr, RechargeHeader."No."); if RechargeHeader."Posted Document No." <> '' then Error(DuplicatePostingErr, ...); if RechargeHeader.Reversed then Error(AlreadyReversedErr, RechargeHeader."No.");
CODECod50002.RechargeReversal.al:14-15,56-60
if PostedRechargeHeader.Reversed then Error('Recharge document %1 has already been reversed.', ...); ... PostedRechargeHeader.Reversed := true; PostedRechargeHeader."Reversal Document No." := ReversalDocNo;
CODECod50002.RechargeReversal.al:62-67
if RechargeHeader.Get(PostedRechargeHeader."Pre-Assigned No.") then begin RechargeHeader.Status := RechargeHeader.Status::Reversed; RechargeHeader."Reversal Document No." := ReversalDocNo;
DartsPASS
Council verdict: PASS (1 PASS)1P
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: posting is guarded by the Posted boolean (read-only, set once at end of successful post), AlreadyPostedErr is raised on rerun, and stale Gen Jnl Lines left over from a previous failed attempt are cleared before re-writing — that's a basic but real idempotency design. However, no replay-safety on correction (no reposting / linkage routine) since reversal isn't implemented. Idempotency on posting itself is adequate to PASS this code-quality criterion.
CODETab50102.ICRechargeRequestHeader.al:134-140
field(18; "Posted"; Boolean) { ... Editable = false; ToolTip = 'Indicates whether this IC Recharge Request has already been posted to the IC General Journal and IC Outbox. Prevents duplicate posting.'; }
CODECod50101.ICRechargePostMgt.al:23-25,42-47
if RechargeHeader."Posted" then Error(AlreadyPostedErr, RechargeHeader."No."); // also clears any leftover lines in the IC batch before re-writing GenJnlLine.DeleteAll(true);

The five-role boundary matrix the spec requires is entirely absent.

Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: All five roles are represented as Assignable=true permission sets. Boundaries enforced via tabledata permissions and codeunit access — Analyst lacks Recharge-Post (cannot post), Manager lacks Recharge-Post (cannot post final), Finance Poster has Posted tables RIMD + Recharge-Post X (can post but read-only on Recharge Partner), IC Admin manages setup/partners/dimensions, Auditor is read-only across all tables. Matches the role matrix.
CODEPermissionSet50000.RechargeAnalyst.al:1-26

L4: Audit log table exists and is populated by the Log() procedure with old/new status + user + action + message — sufficient as an audit trail. However: (1) there is NO reconciliation page or codeunit that ties source amount, allocation output, and posted entries together with filters by company/partner/period/status/currency as the spec requires, and (2) the audit log table is NOT immutable — fields are not Editable=false at the table level and no OnModify/OnDelete trigger blocks tampering. No export action is provided. Partial implementation.
CODEICRechargeLedger.Table.al:249-307
table 50009 "ICR Audit Log" { fields { Entry No., Request No., Action, User ID, Created At, Message, Old Status, New Status } keys { key(Request; "Request No.", "Created At") } }

L4: Recharge Audit Log table records action types Created/Modified/Validated/Submitted/Approved/Rejected/Posted/Reversed/Deleted with user, datetime, old/new values, source table/field. Audit log writes are AutoIncrement (no key allowed to be modified) and posted tables are not exposed for edit on pages, supporting immutability. Reconciliation codeunit compares source vs allocated vs posted totals. Reconciliation report exists. Sufficient for the spec.
CODETab50007.RechargeAuditLog.al:7-75
field(10; "Document No.") field(20; "Action Type"; Option) ... OptionMembers = Created,Modified,Validated,Submitted,Approved,Rejected,Posted,Reversed,Deleted; field(30; "User ID") field(40; "Date Time") field(50; Description) field(60; "Old Value") field(61; "New Value")

L4: ICR Processing Queue page exposes all 6 required states (Pending, Processing, Accepted, Rejected, Failed, Reversed). However there is no dedicated exception queue page (failures rely on the Exception Flag on the request header + Last Error text). No dashboard / role-center cue tile is wired up. The list page satisfies the visibility-of-states half but not the dedicated exception queue half.
