contracts/customer-card-ui.md41 lines

UI Contract: Customer Card Hello World Note

Surface

Customer Card.

User-Visible Field

LabelBacking ValueLocationEditable
Hello World NotePTE Hello World NoteGeneral customer information areaYes, when the user can edit the customer record

Interaction Contract

View Field

Given a user opens a Customer Card When the General customer information area is displayed Then the Hello World Note field is visible.

Save Blank Value

Given a user can edit a customer When the Hello World Note field is blank and the customer is saved Then the save succeeds.

Save Valid Value

Given a user can edit a customer When the Hello World Note field contains 3 to 100 characters and the customer is saved Then the save succeeds and the value is visible after reopening the customer.

Reject Short Value

Given a user can edit a customer When the Hello World Note field contains 1 or 2 characters Then the value is rejected with a validation error and is not accepted as the saved note.

Regression Contract

Standard customer fields such as name, address, and phone number continue to display, edit, and save using their existing behavior.

Hotel Management 02-hotel-management

Reservation lifecycle: room inventory, deposit, check-in, service charges, checkout posting with VAT.

Speckit + Claude9 files
spec.md237 lines

Feature Specification: Hotel Reservation Lifecycle

Feature Branch: feat/rebate-setup-seeding-layer

Created: 2026-06-15

Status: Draft

Input: User description: "Build a Business Central extension that supports the hotel guest lifecycle from reservation through billing."

User Scenarios & Testing (mandatory)

User Story 1 - Create and Manage Reservations (Priority: P1)

A Front Desk staff member creates a new reservation for a guest, assigns a room, and captures a deposit payment to secure the booking.

Why this priority: This is the entry point for the entire hotel guest lifecycle. Without the ability to create reservations, no other workflows can proceed. This establishes the foundation for room inventory tracking and revenue capture.

Independent Test: Can be fully tested by creating a reservation with customer details, room assignment, and deposit payment, then verifying the reservation appears in the system with "Confirmed" status and the room is marked as occupied for the specified date range.

Acceptance Scenarios:

  1. Given a customer wants to book a room, When Front Desk creates a reservation with customer details, check-in/check-out dates, and room selection, Then system generates a unique Reservation No. and sets status to "Confirmed"
  2. Given a reservation is being created, When Front Desk selects a room that is already occupied for the requested dates, Then system prevents the booking and displays an overbooking error
  3. Given a confirmed reservation exists, When Front Desk captures a deposit payment, Then system records the deposit amount and confirms payment success
  4. Given a deposit payment fails, When the payment processing returns an error, Then system does not mark the deposit as captured and notifies Front Desk of the failure

User Story 2 - Guest Check-In (Priority: P1)

A Front Desk staff member checks in a guest who has arrived at the hotel, transitioning the reservation from "Confirmed" to "Occupied" status and finalizing room assignment.

Why this priority: Check-in is a critical operational touchpoint that confirms the guest's arrival and activates their stay. This enables service charge posting and establishes the active billing period.

Independent Test: Can be tested by selecting a "Confirmed" reservation, performing check-in, and verifying the status changes to "Occupied" and the room is marked as unavailable for new bookings.

Acceptance Scenarios:

  1. Given a reservation with "Confirmed" status and today's date as check-in date, When Front Desk performs check-in, Then system updates status to "Occupied" and records check-in timestamp
  2. Given a reservation is in "Occupied" status, When Front Desk attempts to check in again, Then system prevents duplicate check-in and displays appropriate message

User Story 3 - Post Service Charges During Stay (Priority: P2)

A Front Desk staff member posts additional service charges (breakfast, laundry, transfer) to a guest's active reservation during their stay.

Why this priority: Service charges represent incremental revenue during the guest stay and must be captured for accurate billing. This can be tested independently after check-in is functional.

Independent Test: Can be tested by selecting an "Occupied" reservation, posting various service charges, and verifying they are stored and linked to the reservation for later billing.

Acceptance Scenarios:

  1. Given a reservation in "Occupied" status, When Front Desk posts a service charge (e.g., breakfast at 50.00), Then system creates a service charge line linked to the reservation
  2. Given multiple service charges have been posted, When Front Desk reviews the reservation, Then all service charges are displayed with description and amount

User Story 4 - Guest Checkout and Invoice Generation (Priority: P1)

A Front Desk staff member checks out a guest, triggering invoice generation that includes room charges, service charges, VAT calculation, and deposit application, then captures final payment.

Why this priority: Checkout is the culmination of the guest lifecycle and the point where financial settlement occurs. This is essential for revenue recognition and completing the reservation cycle.

Independent Test: Can be tested by performing checkout on an "Occupied" reservation, verifying invoice generation with correct totals (room rate × nights + service charges + VAT - deposit), and confirming final payment processing.

Acceptance Scenarios:

  1. Given a reservation in "Occupied" status with check-out date of today, When Front Desk performs checkout, Then system generates an invoice, calculates total (room charges + service charges + VAT - deposit), and updates status to "Closed"
  2. Given an invoice has been generated for a reservation, When Front Desk attempts to generate another invoice for the same reservation, Then system prevents duplicate invoice creation
  3. Given checkout is initiated, When final payment is captured, Then system posts the payment and reconciles against the invoice total
  4. Given checkout is initiated, When final payment processing fails, Then system does not mark the reservation as "Closed" and notifies Front Desk of the payment failure

User Story 5 - Process Refunds (Finance Only) (Priority: P2)

A Finance staff member processes a refund for a guest due to overpayment or cancellation, with Front Desk staff explicitly blocked from performing this action.

Why this priority: Refund processing is a financial control that must be restricted to Finance role. This represents the security layer ensuring role-based separation of duties.

Independent Test: Can be tested by attempting refund processing as Finance (success) and as Front Desk (blocked), verifying the permission enforcement works correctly.

Acceptance Scenarios:

  1. Given a Finance user is logged in and a reservation requires a refund, When Finance initiates refund processing, Then system processes the refund and posts the financial transaction
  2. Given a Front Desk user is logged in, When Front Desk attempts to access refund functionality, Then system denies access and displays permission error
  3. Given a refund is processed, When Finance reviews ledger entries, Then refund appears as a credit transaction linked to the original invoice

User Story 6 - Configure System Setup (Admin) (Priority: P3)

An Admin user configures the hotel management system setup, including room inventory, VAT rates, and permission assignments.

Why this priority: System setup is foundational but only needs to be done once or infrequently. This enables the operational workflows but can be tested independently.

Independent Test: Can be tested by configuring room inventory (room numbers, types, rates), VAT rates, and user permissions, then verifying Front Desk can create reservations using the configured data.

Acceptance Scenarios:

  1. Given Admin is logged in, When Admin creates room inventory records with room numbers, types, and nightly rates, Then system stores the rooms and makes them available for reservation assignment
  2. Given Admin is configuring VAT, When Admin sets a VAT rate (e.g., 20%), Then system uses this rate for all invoice calculations
  3. Given Admin is managing permissions, When Admin assigns Front Desk role to a user, Then that user can create reservations and perform check-in/check-out but cannot process refunds

Edge Cases

  • What happens when a guest checks out before the scheduled check-out date (early checkout)?
  • What happens when a room is already occupied but a new reservation is created for overlapping dates?
  • What happens when VAT configuration changes between reservation creation and invoice generation?
  • What happens when a guest has zero service charges during their stay?
  • What happens when a deposit amount exceeds the final bill total?
  • What happens when payment processing is attempted but the payment gateway is unavailable?
  • What happens when a user without proper permissions attempts to perform a restricted action?

Requirements (mandatory)

Functional Requirements

Reservation Management

  • FR-001: System MUST allow Front Desk to create reservations with Reservation No., Customer No., Room No., Check-in Date, Check-out Date, Status, and Deposit Amount
  • FR-002: System MUST auto-generate unique Reservation No. for each new reservation
  • FR-003: System MUST support reservation statuses: Confirmed, Occupied, Closed
  • FR-004: System MUST prevent creation of overlapping reservations for the same room (overbooking prevention)
  • FR-005: System MUST allow Front Desk to update reservation details before check-in
  • FR-006: System MUST track Invoice No. on each reservation once invoice is generated

Room Inventory

  • FR-007: System MUST maintain room inventory with Room No., Room Type, Nightly Rate, and Occupied status
  • FR-008: System MUST track room occupancy status (Occupied = true/false)
  • FR-009: System MUST prevent assignment of an occupied room to a new reservation with overlapping dates
  • FR-010: System MUST release room occupancy (set Occupied = false) when guest checks out

Deposit and Payment

  • FR-011: System MUST allow Front Desk to capture deposit payment during reservation creation
  • FR-012: System MUST track whether deposit payment was successfully captured
  • FR-013: System MUST prevent marking deposit as captured if payment processing fails
  • FR-014: System MUST apply captured deposit amount against final bill during checkout
  • FR-015: System MUST calculate remaining amount due after deposit application

Check-In

  • FR-016: System MUST allow Front Desk to check in guests with "Confirmed" reservations
  • FR-017: System MUST update reservation status from "Confirmed" to "Occupied" upon check-in
  • FR-018: System MUST record check-in timestamp
  • FR-019: System MUST prevent duplicate check-in for already "Occupied" reservations

Service Charges

  • FR-020: System MUST allow Front Desk to post service charge lines to "Occupied" reservations
  • FR-021: System MUST support service types including breakfast, laundry, and transfer (extensible to other services)
  • FR-022: System MUST link each service charge to a specific reservation
  • FR-023: System MUST store service charge description and amount
  • FR-024: System MUST include all service charges in final invoice total

Checkout and Billing

  • FR-025: System MUST allow Front Desk to perform guest checkout
  • FR-026: System MUST generate invoice automatically during checkout process
  • FR-027: System MUST generate invoice only once per reservation (prevent duplicate invoices)
  • FR-028: System MUST calculate total invoice amount as: (Nightly Rate × Number of Nights) + Sum of Service Charges + VAT - Deposit
  • FR-029: System MUST calculate number of nights as the difference between check-out date and check-in date
  • FR-030: System MUST calculate VAT based on configured VAT rate applied to taxable amount (room charges + service charges)
  • FR-031: System MUST update reservation status from "Occupied" to "Closed" upon successful checkout
  • FR-032: System MUST capture final payment for remaining amount due
  • FR-033: System MUST prevent status change to "Closed" if final payment processing fails
  • FR-034: System MUST post financial results to appropriate ledger accounts

Tax

  • FR-035: System MUST calculate VAT using configured VAT rate at time of invoice generation
  • FR-036: System MUST support VAT recalculation if tax configuration changes before invoice is generated
  • FR-037: System MUST apply VAT to both room charges and service charges
  • FR-038: System MUST preserve VAT amount on generated invoice even if VAT configuration later changes

Security and Permissions

  • FR-039: System MUST restrict refund processing functionality to Finance role only
  • FR-040: System MUST block Front Desk role from accessing refund functionality
  • FR-041: System MUST block Front Desk role from modifying tax setup
  • FR-042: System MUST block Front Desk role from directly adjusting financial postings
  • FR-043: System MUST allow Finance role to generate invoices, calculate taxes, post payments, and process refunds
  • FR-044: System MUST allow Admin role to configure system setup, manage room inventory, and assign permissions
  • FR-045: System MUST enforce permission checks before executing role-restricted operations

Key Entities

  • Reservation: Represents a guest booking, including reservation number, customer reference, room assignment, check-in/check-out dates, status (Confirmed/Occupied/Closed), deposit amount, and invoice reference. Links to Room and Customer entities.
  • Room: Represents a hotel room unit, including room number, room type (e.g., Single, Double, Suite), nightly rate, and occupancy status. Used for availability checking and rate calculation.
  • Service Charge: Represents an additional billable service provided during a guest stay, including service type/description (breakfast, laundry, transfer), amount, and link to the parent Reservation.
  • Invoice: Represents the final bill generated at checkout, including invoice number, total amount (room charges + service charges + VAT - deposit), VAT amount, and link to the Reservation. Generated once per reservation.
  • Customer: Represents the guest or booking party, referenced by Customer No. on Reservation. Assumes existing Business Central customer master data.
  • Payment: Represents financial transactions including deposit payments and final payments, tracking payment amount, success/failure status, and link to Reservation or Invoice.

Success Criteria (mandatory)

Measurable Outcomes

  • SC-001: Front Desk staff can create a reservation, capture deposit, and confirm booking in under 3 minutes
  • SC-002: System prevents 100% of overbooking attempts (no overlapping room assignments for the same dates)
  • SC-003: 100% of failed deposit or final payments are prevented from being marked as successful (no false success)
  • SC-004: Finance-only operations (refunds) are successfully blocked for Front Desk role 100% of the time (permission enforcement)
  • SC-005: Invoice generation completes with correct totals (room + service charges + VAT - deposit) with zero calculation errors
  • SC-006: VAT calculation accurately reflects configured rate at time of invoice generation
  • SC-007: Guest checkout and invoice generation complete in under 2 minutes
  • SC-008: System successfully handles the complete guest lifecycle (reservation → check-in → service charges → checkout → payment) for 100% of standard scenarios
  • SC-009: Duplicate invoice generation attempts are prevented 100% of the time
  • SC-010: Room occupancy is released upon checkout 100% of the time, making room available for new bookings

Assumptions

  • Business Central environment is already configured with customer master data (Customer table)
  • Business Central's standard financial posting infrastructure (General Ledger, VAT Posting Setup) is available and configured
  • Payment processing integration exists or will be simulated/stubbed for deposit and final payment capture
  • Number series configuration for Reservation No. and Invoice No. is available through Business Central's standard No. Series functionality
  • System operates in single-currency mode (currency handling is out of scope for v1)
  • Room inventory is pre-configured by Admin before operational use
  • VAT rate configuration is managed through Business Central's standard VAT setup tables
  • User authentication and basic role assignment is handled by Business Central's permission system
  • Standard Business Central permission sets will be extended/customized to enforce Front Desk, Finance, and Admin roles
  • Business Central container deployment infrastructure is already available for testing and deployment
  • Reservation modifications after check-in (e.g., room changes, date extensions) are out of scope for v1
  • Cancellation workflows and cancellation fees are out of scope for v1
  • Multi-room reservations (one reservation spanning multiple rooms) are out of scope for v1
  • Group bookings and corporate account management are out of scope for v1
  • Integration with external channel managers or booking engines is out of scope for v1
plan.md252 lines

Implementation Plan: Hotel Reservation Lifecycle

Branch: feat/rebate-setup-seeding-layer | Date: 2026-06-15 | Spec: spec.md

Input: Feature specification from specs/001-hotel-reservation-lifecycle/spec.md

Summary

Build a Business Central AL extension that manages the complete hotel guest lifecycle from reservation creation through checkout and billing. The system supports Front Desk operational workflows (reservations, check-in, service charges, checkout), Finance operations (invoicing, refunds), and Admin configuration (rooms, VAT, permissions), while enforcing role-based security, preventing overbooking, calculating VAT accurately, and integrating with BC's financial posting framework.

Core Value: Enables hospitality operations in Business Central with production-grade financial controls, audit trails, and permission segregation.

Technical Approach: AL tables for data persistence (reservations, rooms, service charges, invoices), codeunits for business logic (check-in, checkout, refunds), pages for UI, permission sets for security, integration with BC platform services (No. Series, VAT Posting Setup, Gen. Journal posting).

Technical Context

Language/Version: AL (Application Language), Business Central Application 27.0.0.0, Runtime 16.0

Primary Dependencies:

  • Microsoft Base Application 27.0 (Customer table, Gen. Journal Line, VAT Posting Setup)
  • Microsoft System Application 27.0 (Codeunit "No. Series")
  • Business Central Platform 1.0.0.0

Storage: Business Central SQL Server database (abstracted via AL table objects)

Testing: Manual validation via BC Web Client (automated test codeunits deferred to v2)

Target Platform: Business Central 27 on-premises or SaaS (cloud), Windows/Linux server, Web Client UI

Project Type: Business Central AL Extension (.app package)

Performance Goals:

  • Reservation creation < 3 minutes (SC-001)
  • Checkout + invoice generation < 2 minutes (SC-007)
  • Overbooking check < 500ms (LOCKTABLE + indexed query)
  • Invoice calculation < 1 second (in-memory arithmetic + single DB query for service charges)

Constraints:

  • Object ID range: 50000-50249 (250 IDs total, ~20 estimated usage)
  • Object names: ≤30 characters, "HTL" prefix mandatory
  • AL language only (no C# interop, no external APIs in v1)
  • Single-currency mode (multi-currency deferred)
  • No external payment gateway integration (simulated with boolean flags)
  • Compile-clean mandatory before completion (Constitution Principle I)

Scale/Scope:

  • Target: Small-to-medium hotels (10-100 rooms)
  • Estimated annual transaction volume: 12,000-15,000 reservations/year
  • Concurrent users: 5-10 Front Desk/Finance staff
  • Data retention: Indefinite (no automated archival in v1)

Constitution Check

GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.

Principle I: Compile-Clean Is the Only Definition of Done ✅

Status: PASS (design phase)

Commitment: Final implementation will execute al compile and iterate until zero errors. No shortcuts (syntax simulation, manual review) will substitute for actual compilation. The .app artifact is the only proof of completion.

Risk Mitigation:

  • ID allocation table (research.md) prevents range overflow
  • "HTL" prefix prevents name collisions
  • Symbol availability validated against .alpackages/ contents

Principle II: Respect the Object ID Ranges ✅

Status: PASS

Analysis:

  • Available range: 50000-50249 (250 IDs)
  • Estimated object count:
  • Tables: 5 (Setup, Room, Reservation, Service Charge, Invoice)
  • Pages: 8 (Setup Card, Room List/Card, Reservation List/Card, Service Charge List, Invoice List/Card)
  • Codeunits: 6 (Check-In, Checkout, Refund, Service Charge Mgmt, Room Availability, No. Series Mgmt)
  • Enums: 3 (Room Type, Reservation Status, Service Type)
  • Permission Sets: 3 (Front Desk, Finance, Admin)
  • Total: 25 objects (10% of available range)
  • Buffer: 225 IDs remaining for future enhancements

ID Allocation Table: See research.md for detailed breakdown

Compliance: All object IDs assigned before implementation; no on-the-fly allocation

Principle III: Globally-Unique, Affixed Object Names ✅

Status: PASS

Naming Convention: "HTL" prefix on all objects (HTL = Hotel)

Rationale:

  • Prevents collisions with Base Application objects (e.g., BC has "Payment Management", we use "HTL Refund Management")
  • 3-character prefix preserves character budget (30-char limit)
  • Consistent casing: "HTL" in all caps, followed by space, then Pascal Case name

Examples:

  • Table: "HTL Reservation", "HTL Service Charge"
  • Page: "HTL Reservation Card", "HTL Invoice List"
  • Codeunit: "HTL Check-In Management", "HTL Refund Management"
  • Enum: "HTL Room Type", "HTL Reservation Status"
  • Permission Set: "HTL-FRONT DESK" (hyphen for permission sets)

Validation: No names ≥30 characters (all within limit)

Principle IV: Only Reference Symbols That Exist ✅

Status: PASS

Symbol Availability Verified:

  • ✅ Codeunit "No. Series" (System Application 27.0) - replaces deprecated NoSeriesManagement
  • ✅ Codeunit "Gen. Jnl.-Post Line" (Base Application 27.0) - for financial posting
  • ✅ Table "Customer" (Base Application 27.0) - customer master data
  • ✅ Table "VAT Posting Setup" (Base Application 27.0) - VAT configuration
  • ✅ Table "Gen. Journal Line" (Base Application 27.0) - journal entry creation
  • ✅ Table "No. Series" (Base Application 27.0) - number series setup

Symbols NOT Used (verified as deprecated/removed in BC27):

  • ❌ NoSeriesManagement codeunit (removed) → using Codeunit "No. Series" instead
  • ❌ NoSeriesLine table direct access (deprecated) → using No. Series module methods

Platform Version Constraint: Application 27.0.0.0 = max API surface; no BC28+ features used

Principle V: Extend, Never Modify the Base ✅

Status: PASS

Compliance:

  • ✅ app.json: Read-only (id, name, publisher, version unchanged)
  • ✅ .alpackages/: Read-only (no symbol file modifications)
  • ✅ All source files: Created in src/ subfolder (structure: src/Tables/, src/Pages/, src/Codeunits/)
  • ✅ Base Application: No tableextension or pageextension objects (no base modifications needed for this feature)

Extension Strategy: This feature is fully additive (new objects only), no extensions to existing BC tables/pages required.

Additional Constraints ✅

Table/Field Name Length:

  • ✅ All table names ≤30 chars (longest: "HTL Service Charge" = 19 chars)
  • ✅ All field names ≤30 chars (longest: "VAT Prod. Posting Group" = 24 chars)

app.json Immutability:

  • ✅ id, name, publisher, version fields NOT modified during implementation

Quality Gates

Pre-Implementation Gate (before /speckit-implement):

  • ✅ All Constitution principles validated in design
  • ✅ Object ID allocation documented
  • ✅ Naming conventions verified
  • ✅ Symbol dependencies confirmed

Implementation Gate (completion criteria):

  • ⏳ al compile executed and returns zero errors
  • ⏳ .app artifact generated successfully
  • ⏳ No ID range overflows (verify object count ≤ allocated range)
  • ⏳ No name collision errors (HTL prefix prevents)
  • ⏳ No symbol-not-found errors (all references exist in .alpackages/)

Post-Implementation Gate (before merge):

  • ⏳ All quickstart.md validation scenarios pass
  • ⏳ Success criteria SC-002, SC-004, SC-009, SC-010 verified at 100% enforcement
  • ⏳ Constitution re-check confirms compliance

Project Structure

Documentation (this feature)

specs/001-hotel-reservation-lifecycle/
├── plan.md              # This file (/speckit-plan output)
├── spec.md              # Feature specification (/speckit-specify output)
├── research.md          # Phase 0: Technical decisions and research
├── data-model.md        # Phase 1: Entity definitions, validation rules
├── quickstart.md        # Phase 1: End-to-end validation scenarios
├── contracts/           # Phase 1: Interface contracts
│   ├── page-contracts.md      # UI page definitions
│   └── codeunit-contracts.md  # Business logic contracts
├── checklists/          # Quality validation
│   └── requirements.md  # Spec quality checklist (from /speckit-specify)
└── tasks.md             # Phase 2: Implementation tasks (/speckit-tasks - NOT YET CREATED)

Source Code (repository root)

src/
├── Tables/
│   ├── HTLHotelSetup.Table.al          # Table 50000
│   ├── HTLRoom.Table.al                # Table 50001
│   ├── HTLReservation.Table.al         # Table 50002
│   ├── HTLServiceCharge.Table.al       # Table 50003
│   └── HTLInvoice.Table.al             # Table 50004
│
├── Pages/
│   ├── HTLHotelSetupCard.Page.al       # Page 50100
│   ├── HTLRoomList.Page.al             # Page 50101
│   ├── HTLRoomCard.Page.al             # Page 50102
│   ├── HTLReservationList.Page.al      # Page 50103
│   ├── HTLReservationCard.Page.al      # Page 50104
│   ├── HTLServiceChargeList.Page.al    # Page 50105 (ListPart)
│   ├── HTLInvoiceList.Page.al          # Page 50106
│   └── HTLInvoiceCard.Page.al          # Page 50107
│
├── Codeunits/
│   ├── HTLCheckInManagement.Codeunit.al      # Codeunit 50200
│   ├── HTLCheckoutManagement.Codeunit.al     # Codeunit 50201
│   ├── HTLRefundManagement.Codeunit.al       # Codeunit 50202
│   ├── HTLServiceChargeMgmt.Codeunit.al      # Codeunit 50203
│   ├── HTLRoomAvailability.Codeunit.al       # Codeunit 50204
│   └── HTLNoSeriesManagement.Codeunit.al     # Codeunit 50205
│
├── Enums/
│   ├── HTLRoomType.Enum.al                   # Enum 50225
│   ├── HTLReservationStatus.Enum.al          # Enum 50226
│   └── HTLServiceType.Enum.al                # Enum 50227
│
└── PermissionSets/

    ├── HTLFinance.PermissionSet.al           # PermissionSet 50236
    └── HTLAdmin.PermissionSet.al             # PermissionSet 50237

Structure Decision: Single-project AL extension structure (standard BC extension pattern). All objects organized by type (Tables/, Pages/, Codeunits/, etc.) for clarity and BC tooling compatibility. No backend/frontend split (BC extensions are monolithic). Test codeunits deferred to v2, so no Tests/ subfolder in v1.

Complexity Tracking

No Constitution violations - this section is empty per template instructions.

This feature adheres to all Constitution principles. No complexity justifications required.

research.md223 lines

Phase 0: Research & Technical Decisions

Feature: Hotel Reservation Lifecycle Date: 2026-06-15

Technical Context Decisions

Language/Platform

Decision: AL (Application Language) for Business Central, Platform 27.0.0.0 (BC27), Runtime 16.0

Rationale:

  • Project is a Business Central extension targeting application version 27.0.0.0
  • AL is the native extension language for BC
  • Runtime 16.0 provides modern AL features including NoImplicitWith
  • Platform version dictates available APIs and symbol references

Alternatives Considered: None - AL is the only language for BC extensions

Object ID Strategy

Decision: Use ID range 50000-50249 (250 objects total) with prefix "HTL" (Hotel)

Rationale:

  • app.json defines idRanges 50000-50249, providing 250 object IDs
  • Estimated object count: ~15-20 objects (tables, pages, codeunits, enums, permission sets)
  • "HTL" prefix ensures uniqueness vs Base Application objects (Principle III)
  • Constitution requires all objects stay within this range

Allocation Plan:

  • 50000-50099: Tables (100 slots)
  • 50100-50199: Pages (100 slots)
  • 50200-50224: Codeunits (25 slots)
  • 50225-50234: Enums (10 slots)
  • 50235-50244: Permission Sets (10 slots)
  • 50245-50249: Reports/Queries (5 slots)

Alternatives Considered: Different ID allocation schemes - chosen provides clear separation and room for growth

Number Series Management

Decision: Use Codeunit "No. Series" (BC27 modern API)

Rationale:

  • FR-002 requires auto-generation of Reservation No.
  • Constitution Principle IV: Only reference symbols that exist in loaded packages
  • NoSeriesManagement codeunit is deprecated/removed in BC27
  • Modern approach: Codeunit "No. Series" module from System Application

Implementation Approach:

  • Define number series codes in setup: "HTL-RES" for reservations, "HTL-INV" for invoices
  • Call Codeunit "No. Series".GetNextNo() for auto-generation
  • Store number series codes in HTL Hotel Setup table

Alternatives Considered: Manual number assignment - rejected due to FR-002 requirement

VAT/Tax Integration

Decision: Leverage Business Central's standard VAT Posting Setup tables

Rationale:

  • FR-030, FR-035-38 require VAT calculation on room + service charges
  • BC provides built-in VAT infrastructure (VAT Posting Setup, VAT Business/Product Posting Groups)
  • Spec assumption: "VAT rate configuration is managed through Business Central's standard VAT setup tables"
  • Avoids reinventing tax calculation logic

Implementation Approach:

  • Reference VAT Posting Setup for rate lookup
  • Store VAT Business Posting Group Code and VAT Product Posting Group Code on HTL Reservation
  • Calculate VAT % from intersection of posting groups
  • Post VAT amounts to standard GL accounts via posting routines

Alternatives Considered: Custom VAT tables - rejected to leverage BC platform capabilities

Financial Posting Strategy

Decision: Use Gen. Journal Line pattern for financial postings

Rationale:

  • FR-034 requires posting to appropriate ledger accounts
  • Standard BC pattern: create Gen. Journal Line records, call posting codeunits
  • Provides audit trail, integration with GL, and standard reconciliation
  • Supports deposit posting, final payment posting, and refund posting

Implementation Approach:

  • Create Gen. Journal Line records with appropriate account types (Customer, GL)
  • Set Document Type (Invoice, Payment, Credit Memo for refunds)
  • Call Codeunit "Gen. Jnl.-Post Line" for atomic posting
  • Link posted entries back to HTL Reservation via Document No.

Alternatives Considered: Direct ledger entry creation - rejected as it bypasses BC posting framework

Payment Processing

Decision: Simulate payment success/failure with boolean flag (stub integration)

Rationale:

  • FR-011-013 require deposit capture with success/failure tracking
  • Spec assumption: "Payment processing integration exists or will be simulated/stubbed"
  • Real payment gateway integration (Stripe, Adyen) is out of scope for v1
  • Boolean "Deposit Captured" and "Payment Successful" flags satisfy requirements

Implementation Approach:

  • Add "Deposit Captured" boolean to HTL Reservation table
  • Add "Payment Successful" boolean to HTL Invoice table (or use Posted status)
  • Front Desk can mark as captured/successful via UI (simulates successful processing)
  • Later: replace stub with actual payment gateway API calls

Alternatives Considered: Full payment gateway integration - deferred to v2

Permission/Security Model

Decision: Create 3 permission sets extending base BC roles

Rationale:

  • FR-039-045 require role-based access control (Front Desk, Finance, Admin)
  • BC permission system uses Permission Set objects
  • Front Desk: Read/Insert/Modify on reservations, rooms, service charges; Execute check-in/check-out
  • Finance: All Front Desk + Execute refund processing
  • Admin: Setup permissions (Insert/Modify on HTL Hotel Setup, HTL Room)

Implementation Approach:

  • PermissionSet 50235 "HTL-FRONT DESK": Reservation RIMD, Room Read, Service Charge RIM, Codeunit Execute (non-refund)
  • PermissionSet 50236 "HTL-FINANCE": Include HTL-FRONT DESK + Refund codeunit Execute + Invoice full access
  • PermissionSet 50237 "HTL-ADMIN": Setup table RIMD, all configuration access

Alternatives Considered: Field-level security - overkill for v1 requirements

Overbooking Prevention Strategy

Decision: Table-level validation in OnInsert/OnModify triggers with LOCKTABLE

Rationale:

  • FR-004, FR-009: Prevent overlapping reservations for same room
  • Edge case: concurrent reservation creation for same room/dates
  • AL table triggers provide transaction-safe validation point
  • LOCKTABLE ensures row-level locking during availability check

Implementation Approach:

  • HTL Reservation.OnInsert / OnModify: call CheckRoomAvailability()
  • CheckRoomAvailability(): LOCKTABLE on HTL Reservation, filter by Room No. + date overlap
  • Date overlap logic: (NewStart <= ExistingEnd) AND (NewEnd >= ExistingStart)
  • Error if any existing non-Closed reservation overlaps

Alternatives Considered: Application-level checking - race conditions possible without DB lock

Invoice Duplication Prevention

Decision: "Invoice No." field + validation in checkout codeunit

Rationale:

  • FR-027: Generate invoice only once per reservation
  • Store Invoice No. on HTL Reservation once invoice created
  • Checkout codeunit checks: IF "Invoice No." <> '' THEN ERROR('Invoice already generated')

Implementation Approach:

  • HTL Reservation."Invoice No." field (Code[20])
  • HTL Checkout Codeunit: validate blank Invoice No. before proceeding
  • Atomic: generate invoice → post → update Invoice No. → commit

Alternatives Considered: Separate invoice-generated boolean flag - Invoice No. serves dual purpose

Testing Approach

Decision: Manual testing via BC Web Client (automated tests out of scope for v1)

Rationale:

  • Spec quality expectation: "testable, deployable in a BC container"
  • BC supports automated testing (Test Codeunits, Test Pages), but v1 focuses on functional delivery
  • Manual test scenarios documented in quickstart.md
  • Automated tests can be added in v2

Implementation Approach:

  • Deploy extension to BC container
  • Use Web Client to execute user scenarios from spec.md
  • Verify success criteria manually (overbooking prevention, VAT calculation, permission blocks)

Alternatives Considered: Full test codeunit suite - deferred due to time/complexity

Data Model Naming Convention

Decision: "HTL" prefix for all objects, max 30 character names

Rationale:

  • Constitution Principle III: Globally-unique, affixed object names
  • Constitution constraint: Table/field names <= 30 characters
  • "HTL" = Hotel, short and recognizable
  • Examples: "HTL Reservation", "HTL Room", "HTL Service Charge"

Alternatives Considered: "HOTEL" prefix - too long, wastes character budget

Implementation Dependencies

  1. Business Central Base Application: Tables Customer, Gen. Journal Line available
  2. System Application: Codeunit "No. Series" available
  3. VAT Posting Setup: Pre-configured by Admin before operational use
  4. No. Series Setup: Admin must create "HTL-RES" and "HTL-INV" series
  5. Customer Master Data: Customers must exist before creating reservations

Risk Mitigation

RiskMitigation
ID range overflowAllocated 250 IDs, estimated usage ~20 objects (12% utilization)
Name collisions"HTL" prefix on all objects, validate against Base Application symbols
Concurrency issuesLOCKTABLE in availability check, atomic posting transactions
VAT calculation errorsLeverage BC standard VAT Posting Setup, well-tested platform code
Permission bypassPermissionSet enforcement at codeunit execution level

Open Questions / Deferred Decisions

None - all technical context items resolved for planning phase.

data-model.md367 lines

Phase 1: Data Model

Feature: Hotel Reservation Lifecycle Date: 2026-06-15

Entity Overview

┌─────────────────┐         ┌──────────────┐
│  HTL Reservation│◄────────┤  HTL Invoice │
│                 │ 1     0..1│              │
└────────┬────────┘         └──────────────┘
         │
         │ 1
         │
         │ 0..*
         ▼
┌─────────────────┐
│ HTL Service     │
│ Charge          │
└─────────────────┘

         ┌──────────────┐
         │  HTL Room    │
         └──────────────┘
              ▲
              │ referenced by
              │ Reservation."Room No."

Table Definitions

Table 50000 "HTL Hotel Setup"

Purpose: Single-record configuration table for hotel management system

Field No.Field NameTypeLengthDescriptionValidation
1Primary KeyCode10Fixed value "SETUP"
10Reservation Nos.Code20No. Series code for reservationsTableRelation: No. Series
11Invoice Nos.Code20No. Series code for invoicesTableRelation: No. Series
20VAT Bus. Posting GroupCode20Default VAT Business Posting GroupTableRelation: VAT Business Posting Group

Keys:

  • Primary: Primary Key

Notes: Admin-only access, pre-requisite for operational use

Table 50001 "HTL Room"

Purpose: Hotel room inventory master data

Field No.Field NameTypeLengthDescriptionValidation
1No.Code20Room number (e.g., "101", "205")NotBlank
10Room TypeEnumHTL Room Type enum
20Nightly RateDecimalBase rate per night>= 0
30OccupiedBooleanCurrent occupancy status
40VAT Prod. Posting GroupCode20VAT Product Posting Group for room chargesTableRelation: VAT Product Posting Group

Keys:

  • Primary: No.

State Transitions:

  • Occupied: FALSE (available) → TRUE (check-in) → FALSE (checkout)

Validation Rules:

  • Nightly Rate must be >= 0
  • Room Type is required
  • No. must be unique

Notes:

  • Occupied flag is managed by check-in/checkout processes
  • Room cannot be deleted if any non-Closed reservation references it

Table 50002 "HTL Reservation"

Purpose: Guest booking record tracking full lifecycle

Field No.Field NameTypeLengthDescriptionValidation
1No.Code20Auto-generated reservation numberAuto-assigned from No. Series
10Customer No.Code20Reference to CustomerTableRelation: Customer, NotBlank
20Room No.Code20Assigned roomTableRelation: HTL Room, NotBlank
30Check-in DateDateArrival dateNotBlank, must be <= Check-out Date
31Check-out DateDateDeparture dateNotBlank, must be >= Check-in Date
40StatusEnumHTL Reservation StatusNotBlank
50Deposit AmountDecimalCaptured deposit>= 0
51Deposit CapturedBooleanPayment success flag
60Invoice No.Code20Generated invoice referenceTableRelation: HTL Invoice
70Check-in TimestampDateTimeActual check-in time
80VAT Bus. Posting GroupCode20VAT Business Posting GroupTableRelation: VAT Business Posting Group

Keys:

  • Primary: No.
  • Secondary: Room No., Check-in Date, Check-out Date (for availability checking)
  • Secondary: Customer No. (for customer lookup)

State Transitions:

  • Confirmed (initial) → Occupied (check-in) → Closed (checkout)

Validation Rules (OnInsert, OnModify triggers):

  • Check-out Date must be > Check-in Date (minimum 1 night)
  • Call CheckRoomAvailability() to prevent overbooking (FR-004, FR-009)
  • Status can only transition: Confirmed→Occupied, Occupied→Closed (no backward transitions)
  • Cannot modify Room No. or dates after Status = Occupied (except early checkout date extension)
  • Deposit Amount >= 0

Business Logic (Table triggers):

  • OnInsert: Auto-assign No. from No. Series if blank
  • OnInsert/OnModify: Validate room availability (CheckRoomAvailability)
  • OnDelete: Error if Status <> Confirmed (cannot delete checked-in or closed reservations)

CheckRoomAvailability() procedure:

procedure CheckRoomAvailability()
var
    HTLReservation: Record "HTL Reservation";
begin
    HTLReservation.LockTable();
    HTLReservation.SetRange("Room No.", Rec."Room No.");
    HTLReservation.SetFilter("No.", '<>%1', Rec."No."); // Exclude self on modify
    HTLReservation.SetFilter(Status, '<>%1', HTLReservation.Status::Closed);
    if HTLReservation.FindSet() then
        repeat
            // Date overlap: (NewStart <= ExistingEnd) AND (NewEnd >= ExistingStart)
            if (Rec."Check-in Date" <= HTLReservation."Check-out Date") and
               (Rec."Check-out Date" >= HTLReservation."Check-in Date") then
                Error('Room %1 is already booked for overlapping dates %2 to %3',
                    Rec."Room No.",
                    HTLReservation."Check-in Date",
                    HTLReservation."Check-out Date");
        until HTLReservation.Next() = 0;
end;

Table 50003 "HTL Service Charge"

Purpose: Additional billable services during guest stay

Field No.Field NameTypeLengthDescriptionValidation
1Reservation No.Code20Parent reservationTableRelation: HTL Reservation, NotBlank
2Line No.IntegerSequence numberNotBlank
10Service TypeEnumHTL Service Type enumNotBlank
20DescriptionText100Service description
30AmountDecimalCharge amount> 0
40VAT Prod. Posting GroupCode20VAT Product Posting Group for serviceTableRelation: VAT Product Posting Group

Keys:

  • Primary: Reservation No., Line No.

Validation Rules:

  • Amount must be > 0
  • Service Type is required
  • Can only be added to reservations with Status = Occupied

Notes:

  • Line No. auto-increments (next available for reservation)
  • Services are included in invoice total calculation during checkout

Table 50004 "HTL Invoice"

Purpose: Final bill generated at checkout

Field No.Field NameTypeLengthDescriptionValidation
1No.Code20Auto-generated invoice numberAuto-assigned from No. Series
10Reservation No.Code20Source reservationTableRelation: HTL Reservation, NotBlank
20Customer No.Code20Copied from reservationTableRelation: Customer
30Invoice DateDateGeneration dateNotBlank
40Room ChargesDecimalNightly Rate × Nights>= 0, calculated
41Service ChargesDecimalSum of service charge amounts>= 0, calculated
42SubtotalDecimalRoom + Service Charges>= 0, calculated
50VAT AmountDecimalCalculated VAT>= 0, calculated
60Total AmountDecimalSubtotal + VAT>= 0, calculated
70Deposit AppliedDecimalDeposit from reservation>= 0
80Amount DueDecimalTotal - Depositcalculated
90Payment SuccessfulBooleanFinal payment captured flag
100PostedBooleanFinancial posting complete

Keys:

  • Primary: No.
  • Secondary: Reservation No. (unique, one invoice per reservation)

Calculation Logic (populated during checkout):

  • Room Charges = HTL Room."Nightly Rate" × (Check-out Date - Check-in Date)
  • Service Charges = SUM(HTL Service Charge.Amount WHERE Reservation No. = X)
  • Subtotal = Room Charges + Service Charges
  • VAT Amount = Subtotal × VAT% (from VAT Posting Setup intersection)
  • Total Amount = Subtotal + VAT Amount
  • Deposit Applied = HTL Reservation."Deposit Amount" (if Deposit Captured = TRUE, else 0)
  • Amount Due = Total Amount - Deposit Applied

Validation Rules:

  • Cannot modify after Posted = TRUE
  • No. is auto-assigned from No. Series
  • Must reference a valid reservation

Notes:

  • Created by HTL Checkout Codeunit
  • Posted flag set after Gen. Journal posting completes

Enums

Enum 50225 "HTL Room Type"

Purpose: Categorize room inventory

Values:

  • Single = 0
  • Double = 1
  • Suite = 2
  • Deluxe = 3

Extensibility: Non-extensible (fixed list for v1)

Enum 50226 "HTL Reservation Status"

Purpose: Track reservation lifecycle state

Values:

  • Confirmed = 0 (initial state after reservation creation)
  • Occupied = 1 (after check-in)
  • Closed = 2 (after checkout and payment)

State Transition Rules:

  • Confirmed → Occupied: Valid (check-in)
  • Occupied → Closed: Valid (checkout)
  • Closed → *: Invalid (final state)
  • Occupied → Confirmed: Invalid (no backward transition)

Enum 50227 "HTL Service Type"

Purpose: Categorize billable services

Values:

  • Breakfast = 0
  • Laundry = 1
  • Transfer = 2
  • RoomService = 3
  • Other = 99

Extensibility: Non-extensible (fixed list for v1, can be extended in future versions)

Field-Level Validation Summary

TableFieldValidation RuleError Message
HTL ReservationCheck-out Date>= Check-in Date + 1 day"Check-out date must be after check-in date"
HTL ReservationRoom No. (OnValidate)CheckRoomAvailability()"Room %1 is already booked for overlapping dates"
HTL ReservationStatus (OnValidate)ValidateStatusTransition()"Invalid status transition from %1 to %2"
HTL Service ChargeAmount> 0"Amount must be greater than zero"
HTL Service ChargeOnInsertParent reservation Status = Occupied"Services can only be added to checked-in guests"
HTL InvoiceOnInsertReservation."Invoice No." = ''"Invoice already exists for this reservation"

Relationships

  1. HTL Reservation → Customer: Many-to-One (Customer No. FK)
  2. HTL Reservation → HTL Room: Many-to-One (Room No. FK, soft reference - room can change before check-in)
  3. HTL Reservation → HTL Invoice: One-to-ZeroOrOne (Invoice No. FK, populated at checkout)
  4. HTL Service Charge → HTL Reservation: Many-to-One (Reservation No. FK, cascade query)
  5. HTL Invoice → HTL Reservation: One-to-One (Reservation No. FK, enforced by unique key)

Data Integrity Rules

  1. Referential Integrity:
  • HTL Reservation.Customer No. must exist in Customer table
  • HTL Reservation.Room No. must exist in HTL Room table
  • HTL Service Charge.Reservation No. must exist in HTL Reservation table
  • HTL Invoice.Reservation No. must exist in HTL Reservation table
  1. Deletion Rules:
  • HTL Room: Cannot delete if referenced by non-Closed reservations
  • HTL Reservation: Cannot delete if Status <> Confirmed (preserve historical data)
  • HTL Service Charge: Cascade considerations - prevent deletion if invoice posted
  • HTL Invoice: Cannot delete after Posted = TRUE
  1. Concurrency Control:
  • HTL Reservation: LOCKTABLE during availability check (prevents race conditions)
  • HTL Invoice: Atomic creation + posting within single transaction

Indexing Strategy

Purpose: Optimize common queries

TableIndexFieldsUse Case
HTL ReservationPrimaryNo.Direct lookup by reservation number
HTL ReservationAvailabilityRoom No., Check-in Date, Check-out DateOverbooking prevention query
HTL ReservationCustomerCustomer No.Customer reservation history
HTL Service ChargePrimaryReservation No., Line No.Fetch all services for invoice
HTL InvoiceReservationReservation No.Check if invoice exists for reservation

Calculated Fields vs Stored Fields

Stored (persisted in table):

  • HTL Reservation: All fields (required for availability checking, audit trail)
  • HTL Invoice: All amounts (preserve invoice snapshot even if room rates change)

Calculated (computed on-demand):

  • HTL Reservation: Number of Nights (Check-out Date - Check-in Date) - can be FlowField if needed
  • HTL Invoice: All amounts are STORED to preserve invoice integrity (not recalculated)

Data Migration / Seeding

Initial Setup Required (Admin pre-requisite):

  1. HTL Hotel Setup record with No. Series codes
  2. No. Series setup: "HTL-RES" and "HTL-INV"
  3. HTL Room records with room numbers, types, rates
  4. VAT Posting Setup configuration (standard BC setup)

No Legacy Data Migration: New greenfield extension

Audit Trail

Tracked Changes:

  • HTL Reservation: Check-in Timestamp field captures actual check-in time
  • HTL Invoice: Invoice Date captures generation date
  • Gen. Journal Line postings provide full financial audit trail (via BC standard tables)

Not Tracked in v1 (potential v2 enhancements):

  • Modification history (who changed reservation details)
  • Status change timestamps
  • Service charge posting timestamps

Data Volume Estimates

Assumptions (50-room hotel, 70% occupancy):

  • HTL Room: 50 records (static)
  • HTL Reservation: ~35 new/day × 365 = 12,775/year
  • HTL Service Charge: ~2 per reservation × 12,775 = 25,550/year
  • HTL Invoice: 12,775/year (1:1 with closed reservations)

Storage Impact: Negligible for BC (designed for much larger transaction volumes)

Retention Policy: Not defined in v1 - all records retained indefinitely

quickstart.md520 lines

Quickstart Validation Guide

Feature: Hotel Reservation Lifecycle Date: 2026-06-15 Purpose: End-to-end validation scenarios to prove the feature works

Prerequisites

Environment Setup

  1. Business Central Environment: BC27 (Application 27.0.0.0) container or sandbox
  2. Extension Deployed: HTL Hotel Management extension installed (.app published)
  3. Permissions Assigned: User assigned HTL-ADMIN, HTL-FRONT DESK, or HTL-FINANCE permission set
  4. Customer Data: At least one customer exists in Customer table (for reservation creation)

Initial Configuration (Admin)

Step 1: Configure Number Series

  1. Open No. Series page (search "No. Series")
  2. Create two new number series:
  • Code: HTL-RES
  • Starting No.: RES00001
  • Ending No.: RES99999
  • Increment-by No.: 1
  • Default Nos.: ✓ Yes
  • Code: HTL-INV
  • Starting No.: INV00001
  • Ending No.: INV99999
  • Increment-by No.: 1
  • Default Nos.: ✓ Yes

Step 2: Configure VAT Posting Setup (if not already configured)

  1. Open VAT Business Posting Groups → Create group DOMESTIC
  2. Open VAT Product Posting Groups → Create group STANDARD
  3. Open VAT Posting Setup → Create setup:
  • VAT Bus. Posting Group: DOMESTIC
  • VAT Prod. Posting Group: STANDARD
  • VAT %: 20 (or your region's standard rate)
  • VAT Calculation Type: Normal VAT

Step 3: Configure HTL Hotel Setup

  1. Search and open HTL Hotel Setup
  2. Set fields:
  • Reservation Nos.: HTL-RES
  • Invoice Nos.: HTL-INV
  • VAT Bus. Posting Group: DOMESTIC
  1. Click Rooms action to open Room List

Step 4: Create Room Inventory

  1. In HTL Room List, create at least 3 rooms:
No.Room TypeNightly RateVAT Prod. Posting GroupOccupied
101Single100.00STANDARDNo
201Double150.00STANDARDNo
301Suite250.00STANDARDNo

Validation Scenario 1: Happy Path (Complete Guest Lifecycle)

Objective: Prove end-to-end workflow from reservation through checkout User Role: Front Desk Expected Duration: 5-7 minutes

Step 1: Create Reservation

  1. Search and open HTL Reservation List
  2. Click New
  3. Fill fields in HTL Reservation Card:
  • Customer No.: Select existing customer (e.g., C00001)
  • Room No.: 101 (dropdown should show available rooms)
  • Check-in Date: TODAY
  • Check-out Date: TODAY + 2 days (2-night stay)
  • Deposit Amount: 50.00
  1. Mark Deposit Captured checkbox (simulates successful payment)
  2. Close card (auto-saves)

Expected Results:

  • ✓ Reservation No. auto-assigned (e.g., RES00001)
  • ✓ Status = Confirmed
  • ✓ Room 101 is NOT yet marked Occupied (occupancy happens at check-in)

Step 2: Check In Guest

  1. In HTL Reservation List, select the new reservation
  2. Click Check In action
  3. Confirm the action

Expected Results:

  • ✓ Status changes to Occupied
  • ✓ Check-in Timestamp populated with current date/time
  • ✓ Room 101's Occupied field = Yes (verify in HTL Room List)
  • ✓ Message: "Guest checked in successfully to Room 101"

Step 3: Post Service Charges

  1. In HTL Reservation List, select the checked-in reservation
  2. Click Service Charges action to open service charge subpage
  3. Add two service charges:
  • Line 1: Service Type = Breakfast, Description = "Breakfast - Day 1", Amount = 25.00, VAT Prod. Posting Group = STANDARD
  • Line 2: Service Type = Laundry, Description = "Laundry Service", Amount = 15.00, VAT Prod. Posting Group = STANDARD
  1. Close the reservation card

Expected Results:

  • ✓ Two service charge lines saved
  • ✓ Total service charges = 40.00

Step 4: Check Out Guest

  1. In HTL Reservation List, select the occupied reservation
  2. Click Check Out action
  3. When prompted "Capture final payment?", click Yes
  4. Confirm the action

Expected Results:

  • ✓ Invoice generated with auto-assigned No. (e.g., INV00001)
  • ✓ Invoice calculations:
  • Room Charges: 100.00 × 2 nights = 200.00
  • Service Charges: 40.00
  • Subtotal: 240.00
  • VAT Amount (20%): 48.00
  • Total Amount: 288.00
  • Deposit Applied: 50.00
  • Amount Due: 238.00
  • ✓ Reservation Status = Closed
  • ✓ Reservation.Invoice No. = INV00001
  • ✓ Room 101's Occupied field = No (room released)
  • ✓ Invoice.Payment Successful = Yes
  • ✓ Invoice.Posted = Yes
  • ✓ Message: "Checkout complete. Invoice No. INV00001 generated."

Step 5: Verify Financial Posting

  1. Search and open HTL Invoice Card for INV00001
  2. Click Ledger Entries action (if implemented)
  3. Verify Gen. Ledger Entry exists for:
  • Customer Invoice: 288.00 (debit)
  • Deposit Application: -50.00 (credit)
  • Final Payment: 238.00 (debit)

Expected Results:

  • ✓ Gen. Ledger Entries created
  • ✓ Customer balance increased by 238.00 (net amount due after deposit)

Validation Scenario 2: Overbooking Prevention

Objective: Prove overlapping reservations are blocked (FR-004, FR-009) User Role: Front Desk Expected Duration: 2-3 minutes

Setup

Existing reservation from Scenario 1:

  • Room No.: 101
  • Check-in Date: TODAY
  • Check-out Date: TODAY + 2
  • Status: Closed (room released)

Step 1: Create Overlapping Reservation (Should Succeed - Room Released)

  1. Create new reservation:
  • Customer No.: Any customer
  • Room No.: 101
  • Check-in Date: TODAY + 3
  • Check-out Date: TODAY + 5
  1. Save

Expected Results:

  • ✓ Reservation created successfully (no overlap - prior reservation is Closed)

Step 2: Create Overlapping Reservation (Should Fail - Concurrent Booking)

  1. Do NOT check in the reservation from Step 1 (leave Status = Confirmed)
  2. Create another new reservation:
  • Customer No.: Any customer
  • Room No.: 101
  • Check-in Date: TODAY + 4 (overlaps with existing reservation's TODAY+3 to TODAY+5)
  • Check-out Date: TODAY + 6

Expected Results:

  • ✗ Error: "Room 101 is already booked for overlapping dates [TODAY+3] to [TODAY+5]"
  • ✓ Reservation NOT saved (overbooking prevented)

Step 3: Create Non-Overlapping Reservation (Should Succeed)

  1. Create new reservation:
  • Customer No.: Any customer
  • Room No.: 101
  • Check-in Date: TODAY + 6 (after existing reservation's checkout)
  • Check-out Date: TODAY + 8
  1. Save

Expected Results:

  • ✓ Reservation created successfully (no overlap)

Validation Scenario 3: Duplicate Invoice Prevention

Objective: Prove invoice can only be generated once per reservation (FR-027) User Role: Front Desk Expected Duration: 2 minutes

Setup

Create and check in a reservation (follow Scenario 1 Steps 1-2), but do NOT check out yet.

Step 1: Check Out Once

  1. Select the occupied reservation
  2. Click Check Out action
  3. Capture payment: Yes

Expected Results:

  • ✓ Invoice generated (e.g., INV00002)
  • ✓ Reservation.Status = Closed
  • ✓ Reservation.Invoice No. = INV00002

Step 2: Attempt Second Checkout

  1. Select the same (now Closed) reservation
  2. Click Check Out action (if button enabled)

Expected Results:

  • ✗ Error: "Invoice already exists for this reservation (No. INV00002)"
  • ✓ No duplicate invoice created

Validation Scenario 4: Permission Enforcement (Refund Block)

Objective: Prove Front Desk cannot process refunds (FR-039, FR-040) User Roles: Front Desk (blocked), Finance (allowed) Expected Duration: 3-4 minutes

Setup

Use a posted invoice from Scenario 1 (e.g., INV00001)

Step 1: Front Desk Attempts Refund (Should Fail)

  1. Login as user with HTL-FRONT DESK permission set (or ensure current user does NOT have HTL-FINANCE)
  2. Search and open HTL Invoice List
  3. Select invoice INV00001 (Posted = Yes)
  4. Open HTL Invoice Card
  5. Click Process Refund action (if visible)

Expected Results:

  • ✗ Error: "You do not have permission to execute Codeunit HTL Refund Management"
  • OR: Action is not visible/enabled for Front Desk role (preferred UX)
  • ✓ No refund processed

Step 2: Finance Processes Refund (Should Succeed)

  1. Login as user with HTL-FINANCE permission set
  2. Open HTL Invoice Card for INV00001
  3. Click Process Refund action
  4. Confirm refund processing
  5. Enter refund amount: 288.00 (full invoice amount)

Expected Results:

  • ✓ Message: "Refund of 288.00 processed successfully"
  • ✓ Gen. Ledger Entry created (Credit Memo, negative amount)
  • ✓ Customer balance reduced by 288.00

Validation Scenario 5: VAT Calculation Accuracy

Objective: Prove VAT is calculated correctly (FR-030, FR-035-38) User Role: Front Desk Expected Duration: 3 minutes

Setup

Configured VAT % = 20% (from Prerequisites Step 2)

Test Case: Invoice with Room + Service Charges

  1. Create reservation: Room 201 (Nightly Rate = 150.00), 1 night
  2. Check in
  3. Add service charge: Breakfast = 30.00
  4. Check out (no deposit)

Expected Invoice Calculations:

  • Room Charges: 150.00 × 1 = 150.00
  • Service Charges: 30.00
  • Subtotal: 180.00
  • VAT Amount (20% of 180.00): 36.00
  • Total Amount: 216.00
  • Deposit Applied: 0.00
  • Amount Due: 216.00

Validation:

  • ✓ Open generated invoice
  • ✓ Verify VAT Amount = 36.00 (exactly 20% of 180.00)
  • ✓ Verify Total Amount = 216.00

Validation Scenario 6: Early Checkout

Objective: Prove early checkout is handled (Edge Case) User Role: Front Desk Expected Duration: 2 minutes

Test Case

  1. Create reservation: Room 301, Check-in = TODAY, Check-out = TODAY + 3 (3 nights)
  2. Check in
  3. Guest wants to leave early (TODAY + 1, only 1 night)
  4. Modify reservation: Change Check-out Date to TODAY + 1
  5. Check out

Expected Results:

  • ✓ Invoice calculates 1 night (not 3): Room Charges = 250.00 × 1 = 250.00
  • ✓ Checkout completes successfully

Alternative (if date modification after check-in is blocked by validation):

  • Accept that early checkout requires manual adjustment or is out of scope for v1
  • Document in plan.md as future enhancement

Validation Scenario 7: Failed Payment Handling

Objective: Prove failed payments prevent status change (FR-013, FR-033) User Role: Front Desk Expected Duration: 2 minutes

Test Case: Failed Deposit

  1. Create reservation
  2. Set Deposit Amount = 50.00
  3. Do NOT check Deposit Captured checkbox (simulates payment failure)
  4. Attempt to check in

Expected Results:

  • ✓ Check-in proceeds (deposit capture is optional, not a blocker)
  • ✓ Deposit Applied = 0.00 at checkout (only captured deposits are applied)

Test Case: Failed Final Payment

  1. Create and check in reservation
  2. Check out, but when prompted "Capture final payment?", click No (simulates failure)

Expected Results:

  • ✓ Invoice generated
  • ✓ Invoice.Payment Successful = No
  • ✓ Invoice.Posted = No (financial posting skipped)
  • ✓ Reservation.Status = Closed (or remains Occupied if design requires payment to close)
  • Design Decision: Document whether Status=Closed requires payment or not

Recommended Design: Status=Closed only if payment successful (aligns with FR-033)

Validation Scenario 8: Zero Service Charges

Objective: Prove invoice generation works with no service charges (Edge Case) User Role: Front Desk Expected Duration: 2 minutes

Test Case

  1. Create reservation: Room 101, 1 night, no deposit
  2. Check in
  3. Do NOT add any service charges
  4. Check out

Expected Invoice Calculations:

  • Room Charges: 100.00
  • Service Charges: 0.00
  • Subtotal: 100.00
  • VAT Amount (20%): 20.00
  • Total Amount: 120.00

Expected Results:

  • ✓ Invoice generated successfully
  • ✓ Service Charges field = 0.00
  • ✓ VAT calculated on room charges only

Success Criteria Validation Checklist

Map to Success Criteria from spec.md:

SCCriterionValidation ScenarioStatus
SC-001Create reservation in < 3 minScenario 1, Step 1⏱️ Timed
SC-002100% overbooking preventionScenario 2✓ Pass/Fail
SC-003No false payment successScenario 7✓ Pass/Fail
SC-004Refund blocked for Front DeskScenario 4✓ Pass/Fail
SC-005Zero calculation errorsScenarios 1, 5, 8✓ Verify amounts
SC-006Accurate VAT calculationScenario 5✓ Verify 20%
SC-007Checkout in < 2 minScenario 1, Step 4⏱️ Timed
SC-008100% lifecycle completionScenario 1✓ End-to-end
SC-009Duplicate invoice preventionScenario 3✓ Pass/Fail
SC-010Room released at checkoutScenario 1, Step 4✓ Verify Occupied=No

Running the Validation Suite

Full Validation (all scenarios)

Estimated Time: 25-30 minutes

Prerequisites:

  1. Fresh BC container or sandbox (reset data between runs if needed)
  2. Three test user accounts:
  • User 1: HTL-ADMIN permission set
  • User 2: HTL-FRONT DESK permission set
  • User 3: HTL-FINANCE permission set
  1. At least 3 customers created

Execution Order:

  1. Prerequisites setup (Admin)
  2. Scenario 1: Happy Path
  3. Scenario 2: Overbooking Prevention
  4. Scenario 3: Duplicate Invoice Prevention
  5. Scenario 4: Permission Enforcement (switch user accounts)
  6. Scenario 5: VAT Calculation
  7. Scenario 6: Early Checkout
  8. Scenario 7: Failed Payment
  9. Scenario 8: Zero Service Charges

Smoke Test (critical path only)

Estimated Time: 10 minutes

Run only:

  • Prerequisites setup
  • Scenario 1: Happy Path
  • Scenario 2: Overbooking Prevention (Step 2 only)
  • Scenario 4: Permission Enforcement (Step 1 only)

Test Data Cleanup

After validation, clean up test data:

  1. Delete test reservations: Open HTL Reservation List → Delete Confirmed reservations (cannot delete Occupied/Closed in v1)
  2. Delete test invoices: HTL Invoice List → Delete unposted invoices
  3. Reset room occupancy: HTL Room List → Set Occupied = No for any stuck rooms

Note: Posted invoices and Gen. Ledger Entries cannot be deleted (BC audit trail) - this is expected behavior.

Known Limitations (v1)

Document any validation failures that are acceptable for v1:

  1. Early checkout date modification: May require reservation cancellation + new reservation if date modification after check-in is blocked
  2. Partial refunds: Refund amount is full invoice only (partial refund requires custom amount input)
  3. Concurrent booking race condition: While LOCKTABLE is used, extreme concurrency (>100 simultaneous reservations) not tested
  4. Customer deletion: No cascade delete - customer with reservations cannot be deleted (expected BC behavior)

Troubleshooting

Issue: "No. Series not configured" error

Solution: Verify HTL Hotel Setup has Reservation Nos. and Invoice Nos. populated with valid No. Series codes

Issue: "Room X is already booked" when room appears free

Solution:

  1. Check HTL Reservation List for non-Closed reservations with that room
  2. Verify date ranges (remember: date overlap logic is inclusive)
  3. Check Room.Occupied flag (may be stuck from failed checkout - reset manually)

Issue: VAT Amount = 0.00 on invoice

Solution:

  1. Verify VAT Posting Setup exists for intersection of VAT Bus. Posting Group + VAT Prod. Posting Group
  2. Check VAT % is configured (not 0)
  3. Verify Room and Service Charges have VAT Prod. Posting Group assigned

Issue: "Permission denied" on Front Desk actions

Solution: Verify user has HTL-FRONT DESK or HTL-FINANCE permission set assigned (check via User Permission Sets page)

Deployment Validation Checklist

Before marking feature as production-ready:

  • [ ] All Prerequisites completed
  • [ ] Scenario 1 (Happy Path) passes end-to-end
  • [ ] Scenario 2 (Overbooking) blocks overlapping reservations
  • [ ] Scenario 3 (Duplicate Invoice) prevents duplicate generation
  • [ ] Scenario 4 (Permissions) blocks Front Desk refunds, allows Finance refunds
  • [ ] Scenario 5 (VAT) calculates accurate tax amounts
  • [ ] Success Criteria SC-002, SC-004, SC-009, SC-010 verified (100% enforcement)
  • [ ] No compile errors (al compile clean - per Constitution Principle I)
  • [ ] All object IDs within 50000-50249 range (Constitution Principle II)
  • [ ] All object names prefixed with "HTL" (Constitution Principle III)

Next Steps After Validation

  1. Document any discovered bugs in issue tracker
  2. If validation passes: Proceed to /speckit-tasks to generate implementation tasks
  3. If validation fails: Update plan.md and data-model.md based on findings, re-implement, re-validate
tasks.md371 lines

Tasks: Hotel Reservation Lifecycle

Input: Design documents from specs/001-hotel-reservation-lifecycle/

Prerequisites: plan.md, spec.md, data-model.md, contracts/, research.md, quickstart.md

Tests: Manual validation via BC Web Client (automated test codeunits deferred to v2 per plan.md)

Organization: Tasks are grouped by user story to enable independent implementation and testing of each story.

Format: [ID] [P?] [Story] Description

  • [P]: Can run in parallel (different files, no dependencies)
  • [Story]: Which user story this task belongs to (e.g., US1, US2, US3, US6 - US4/US5 integrated with US1/US2)
  • Include exact file paths in descriptions

Path Conventions

Business Central AL Extension structure:

  • Tables: src/Tables/
  • Pages: src/Pages/
  • Codeunits: src/Codeunits/
  • Enums: src/Enums/
  • Permission Sets: src/PermissionSets/

Phase 1: Setup (Shared Infrastructure)

Purpose: Project initialization and AL extension structure

  • [ ] T001 Create src/ directory structure with subdirectories: Tables/, Pages/, Codeunits/, Enums/, PermissionSets/
  • [ ] T002 Verify app.json configuration (application: 27.0.0.0, runtime: 16.0, idRanges: 50000-50249, NoImplicitWith feature enabled)
  • [ ] T003 Verify .alpackages/ contains required symbol packages (Base Application 27.0, System Application 27.0)

Phase 2: Foundational (Blocking Prerequisites)

Purpose: Core enums and utilities that ALL user stories depend on

⚠️ CRITICAL: No user story work can begin until this phase is complete

  • [ ] T004 [P] Create HTL Room Type enum in src/Enums/HTLRoomType.Enum.al (Enum 50225: Single, Double, Suite, Deluxe)
  • [ ] T005 [P] Create HTL Reservation Status enum in src/Enums/HTLReservationStatus.Enum.al (Enum 50226: Confirmed, Occupied, Closed)
  • [ ] T006 [P] Create HTL Service Type enum in src/Enums/HTLServiceType.Enum.al (Enum 50227: Breakfast, Laundry, Transfer, RoomService, Other)
  • [ ] T007 Create HTL No. Series Management codeunit in src/Codeunits/HTLNoSeriesManagement.Codeunit.al (Codeunit 50205: GetNextReservationNo(), GetNextInvoiceNo() using Codeunit "No. Series")
  • [ ] T008 Create HTL Room Availability codeunit in src/Codeunits/HTLRoomAvailability.Codeunit.al (Codeunit 50204: IsRoomAvailable() with LOCKTABLE and date overlap logic)

Checkpoint: Foundation ready - user story implementation can now begin in parallel

Phase 3: User Story 6 - Configure System Setup (Priority: P3) 🎯 PREREQUISITE

Goal: Enable Admin to configure hotel management system (No. Series, VAT, room inventory). This is implemented first because operational workflows depend on configured setup and rooms.

Independent Test: Quickstart.md Prerequisites section - configure No. Series (HTL-RES, HTL-INV), VAT Posting Setup (20%), create 3 rooms

Why First: Although P3 priority, setup MUST be complete before any reservations can be created (US1 depends on configured rooms and No. Series)

Implementation for User Story 6

  • [ ] T009 [P] [US6] Create HTL Hotel Setup table in src/Tables/HTLHotelSetup.Table.al (Table 50000: Primary Key, Reservation Nos., Invoice Nos., VAT Bus. Posting Group fields)
  • [ ] T010 [P] [US6] Create HTL Room table in src/Tables/HTLRoom.Table.al (Table 50001: No., Room Type, Nightly Rate, Occupied, VAT Prod. Posting Group fields with validation triggers)
  • [ ] T011 [US6] Create HTL Hotel Setup Card page in src/Pages/HTLHotelSetupCard.Page.al (Page 50100: Card page with all setup fields and Rooms action)
  • [ ] T012 [US6] Create HTL Room List page in src/Pages/HTLRoomList.Page.al (Page 50101: List page showing No., Room Type, Nightly Rate, Occupied, VAT Prod. Posting Group with Reservations action)
  • [ ] T013 [US6] Create HTL Room Card page in src/Pages/HTLRoomCard.Page.al (Page 50102: Card page with all room fields, FactBox for reservation history, Reservations action)
  • [ ] T014 [US6] Create HTL Admin permission set in src/PermissionSets/HTLAdmin.PermissionSet.al (PermissionSet 50237: Full RIMD access to Tables 50000-50001, Pages 50100-50102, Read access to system tables)

Checkpoint: Setup infrastructure ready - Admin can configure system, rooms exist for reservation assignment

Phase 4: User Story 1 - Create and Manage Reservations (Priority: P1) 🎯 MVP CORE

Goal: Enable Front Desk to create reservations with room assignment, deposit capture, and overbooking prevention

Independent Test: Quickstart.md Scenario 1 Step 1 (create reservation, verify auto-generated No., status=Confirmed, overbooking blocked) and Scenario 2 (overbooking prevention)

Dependencies: US6 complete (requires configured setup and rooms)

Implementation for User Story 1

  • [ ] T015 [US1] Create HTL Reservation table in src/Tables/HTLReservation.Table.al (Table 50002: all fields per data-model.md, OnInsert/OnModify triggers calling HTL Room Availability.IsRoomAvailable(), auto-assign No. from No. Series, CheckRoomAvailability() procedure with LOCKTABLE)
  • [ ] T016 [US1] Create HTL Reservation List page in src/Pages/HTLReservationList.Page.al (Page 50103: List page with all reservation fields, views for Confirmed/Occupied/Closed/Today's Check-ins/Check-outs, actions: New, Check In, Service Charges, Check Out, Show Invoice)
  • [ ] T017 [US1] Create HTL Reservation Card page in src/Pages/HTLReservationCard.Page.al (Page 50104: Card page with FastTabs for General/Dates/Billing/Posting, FactBoxes for Customer/Room/Service Charge Summary, actions: Check In, Check Out, Service Charges, Invoice)
  • [ ] T018 [US1] Create HTL Front Desk permission set in src/PermissionSets/HTLFrontDesk.PermissionSet.al (PermissionSet 50235: RIMD on Tables 50002-50003, Read on Table 50001, Execute on Codeunits 50200-50201/50203-50205, Read/Insert/Modify on Pages, EXCLUDE Execute on Codeunit 50202 Refund)

Checkpoint: Reservations functional - Front Desk can create reservations, overbooking prevented, deposit tracking works

Phase 5: User Story 2 - Guest Check-In (Priority: P1) 🎯 MVP CORE

Goal: Enable Front Desk to check in guests, transitioning reservation status from Confirmed to Occupied

Independent Test: Quickstart.md Scenario 1 Step 2 (check in, verify status=Occupied, check-in timestamp recorded, room.Occupied=true, duplicate check-in blocked)

Dependencies: US1 complete (requires existing reservations)

Implementation for User Story 2

  • [ ] T019 [US2] Create HTL Check-In Management codeunit in src/Codeunits/HTLCheckInManagement.Codeunit.al (Codeunit 50200: CheckIn(var HTLReservation) procedure with precondition validation, status update Confirmed→Occupied, set Check-in Timestamp, set Room.Occupied=TRUE, commit, message)
  • [ ] T020 [US2] Integrate Check In action in HTL Reservation Card/List pages (call Codeunit 50200.CheckIn() from OnAction trigger, enabled when Status=Confirmed, refresh page after execution)

Checkpoint: Check-in functional - Front Desk can check in confirmed reservations, room occupancy tracked, duplicate check-in prevented

Phase 6: User Story 3 - Post Service Charges During Stay (Priority: P2)

Goal: Enable Front Desk to post billable services (breakfast, laundry, transfer) to checked-in guests

Independent Test: Quickstart.md Scenario 1 Step 3 (post 2 service charges to Occupied reservation, verify lines stored, total=40.00, linked to reservation)

Dependencies: US2 complete (requires Occupied reservations)

Implementation for User Story 3

  • [ ] T021 [US3] Create HTL Service Charge table in src/Tables/HTLServiceCharge.Table.al (Table 50003: Reservation No., Line No., Service Type, Description, Amount, VAT Prod. Posting Group fields, validation: Amount>0, OnInsert check parent Status=Occupied)
  • [ ] T022 [US3] Create HTL Service Charge Mgmt codeunit in src/Codeunits/HTLServiceChargeMgmt.Codeunit.al (Codeunit 50203: GetNextLineNo(ReservationNo) returns next Line No., CalcTotalCharges(ReservationNo) returns SUM of amounts)
  • [ ] T023 [US3] Create HTL Service Charge List page in src/Pages/HTLServiceChargeList.Page.al (Page 50105: ListPart page with Line No., Service Type, Description, Amount, VAT Prod. Posting Group, auto-increment Line No. via Codeunit 50203.GetNextLineNo())
  • [ ] T024 [US3] Embed HTL Service Charge List as subpage in HTL Reservation Card (add ListPart to Billing FastTab, filtered by Reservation No., editable only when Status=Occupied)

Checkpoint: Service charges functional - Front Desk can post services to checked-in guests, charges stored with parent reservation link

Phase 7: User Story 4 - Guest Checkout and Invoice Generation (Priority: P1) 🎯 MVP CORE

Goal: Enable Front Desk to check out guests, generate invoice with VAT calculation, capture final payment, close reservation, release room

Independent Test: Quickstart.md Scenario 1 Step 4 (checkout Occupied reservation, verify invoice generated with correct calculations, status=Closed, room.Occupied=false, duplicate invoice blocked), Scenario 3 (duplicate invoice prevention), Scenario 5 (VAT calculation), Scenario 7 (failed payment handling), Scenario 8 (zero service charges)

Dependencies: US1, US2, US3 complete (requires reservations with check-in and optionally service charges)

Implementation for User Story 4

  • [ ] T025 [US4] Create HTL Invoice table in src/Tables/HTLInvoice.Table.al (Table 50004: No., Reservation No., Customer No., Invoice Date, Room Charges, Service Charges, Subtotal, VAT Amount, Total Amount, Deposit Applied, Amount Due, Payment Successful, Posted fields, validation: cannot modify after Posted=TRUE)
  • [ ] T026 [US4] Create HTL Checkout Management codeunit in src/Codeunits/HTLCheckoutManagement.Codeunit.al (Codeunit 50201: Checkout(var Reservation, CapturePayment) returns InvoiceNo, with invoice generation logic, VAT calculation via VAT Posting Setup, Gen. Journal Line posting via Codeunit "Gen. Jnl.-Post Line", reservation status update to Closed, room release Occupied=FALSE, error handling for payment failures)
  • [ ] T027 [US4] Add private procedures to HTL Checkout Management: CalcNights(CheckInDate, CheckOutDate) returns Integer, GetVATRate(VATBusPostingGroup, VATProdPostingGroup) returns Decimal, CreateInvoice() returns HTL Invoice, PostFinancials(HTLInvoice) with Gen. Journal Line creation and posting
  • [ ] T028 [US4] Create HTL Invoice List page in src/Pages/HTLInvoiceList.Page.al (Page 50106: List page with No., Reservation No., Customer No., Invoice Date, amounts breakdown, Payment Successful, Posted, views for Unpaid/Paid/Unposted/Posted, actions: Show Reservation, Process Refund)
  • [ ] T029 [US4] Create HTL Invoice Card page in src/Pages/HTLInvoiceCard.Page.al (Page 50107: Card page with FastTabs for General/Amounts/Payment, FactBoxes for Customer/Reservation/Service Charge Lines, actions: Process Refund, Reservation, Ledger Entries)
  • [ ] T030 [US4] Integrate Check Out action in HTL Reservation Card/List pages (call Codeunit 50201.Checkout() from OnAction trigger, prompt for CapturePayment boolean, enabled when Status=Occupied and Invoice No.='', display invoice No. message, refresh page after execution)

Checkpoint: Checkout functional - Front Desk can check out guests, invoices generated with accurate VAT calculation, payments captured, rooms released, duplicate invoices prevented

Phase 8: User Story 5 - Process Refunds (Finance Only) (Priority: P2)

Goal: Enable Finance role to process refunds, explicitly block Front Desk from refund access

Independent Test: Quickstart.md Scenario 4 (Front Desk blocked from refund action/codeunit, Finance successfully processes refund, Gen. Ledger Entry created as credit memo)

Dependencies: US4 complete (requires posted invoices)

Implementation for User Story 5

  • [ ] T031 [US5] Create HTL Refund Management codeunit in src/Codeunits/HTLRefundManagement.Codeunit.al (Codeunit 50202: ProcessRefund(HTLInvoice, RefundAmount) with permission check, precondition validation Posted=TRUE and RefundAmount valid, Gen. Journal Line credit memo creation, posting via Codeunit "Gen. Jnl.-Post Line", message)
  • [ ] T032 [US5] Create HTL Finance permission set in src/PermissionSets/HTLFinance.PermissionSet.al (PermissionSet 50236: INCLUDE HTL-FRONT DESK permissions, ADD Execute permission on Codeunit 50202, ADD full access to Table 50004 HTL Invoice)
  • [ ] T033 [US5] Integrate Process Refund action in HTL Invoice Card/List pages (call Codeunit 50202.ProcessRefund() from OnAction trigger, enabled when Posted=TRUE and user has HTL-FINANCE permission, prompt for RefundAmount with default=Total Amount, confirm before execution)
  • [ ] T034 [US5] Update HTL Front Desk permission set to EXCLUDE Execute on Codeunit 50202 (verify PermissionSet 50235 explicitly denies Codeunit 50202 execution)

Checkpoint: Refund permission enforcement functional - Finance can process refunds, Front Desk blocked via permission set, Gen. Ledger entries posted correctly

Phase 9: Polish & Cross-Cutting Concerns

Purpose: Final compilation, validation, and cleanup

  • [ ] T035 [P] Add table relation drill-downs: HTL Reservation.Customer No. → Customer Card, HTL Reservation.Room No. → HTL Room Card, HTL Reservation.Invoice No. → HTL Invoice Card
  • [ ] T036 [P] Add FlowFields to HTL Reservation Card: Customer.Name, Room."Room Type", Room."Nightly Rate" (display-only calculated fields)
  • [ ] T037 [P] Add FactBox pages for HTL Reservation Card: Customer Details FactBox, Room Details FactBox, Service Charge Summary FactBox (show count and total amount)
  • [ ] T038 [P] Implement page action visibility logic: Check In enabled only when Status=Confirmed, Check Out enabled only when Status=Occupied and Invoice No.='', Service Charges action enabled only when Status=Occupied, Process Refund visible only to HTL-FINANCE role
  • [ ] T039 Run al compile command via AL Language extension or command-line compiler (Constitution Principle I: must produce zero errors)
  • [ ] T040 Fix any compilation errors iteratively: name collisions (verify HTL prefix on all objects), ID range violations (verify all IDs within 50000-50249), symbol-not-found errors (verify only BC27 symbols used), field name length >30 chars
  • [ ] T041 Verify .app artifact generated successfully in output directory
  • [ ] T042 Deploy .app to BC27 container or SaaS tenant using Publish-NAVApp and Install-NAVApp PowerShell cmdlets
  • [ ] T043 Assign HTL-ADMIN permission set to test admin user, HTL-FRONT DESK to test Front Desk user, HTL-FINANCE to test Finance user
  • [ ] T044 Execute quickstart.md Prerequisites setup (configure No. Series HTL-RES/HTL-INV, VAT Posting Setup 20%, create 3 test rooms via HTL Room List)
  • [ ] T045 Execute quickstart.md Scenario 1 (Happy Path) - full end-to-end reservation lifecycle validation
  • [ ] T046 [P] Execute quickstart.md Scenario 2 (Overbooking Prevention) - verify 100% blocking
  • [ ] T047 [P] Execute quickstart.md Scenario 3 (Duplicate Invoice Prevention) - verify 100% blocking
  • [ ] T048 [P] Execute quickstart.md Scenario 4 (Permission Enforcement) - verify Front Desk blocked, Finance allowed
