  • [ ] T049 [P] Execute quickstart.md Scenario 5 (VAT Calculation) - verify 20% VAT accuracy
  • [ ] T050 [P] Execute quickstart.md Scenario 6 (Early Checkout) - verify date modification and invoice calculation
  • [ ] T051 [P] Execute quickstart.md Scenario 7 (Failed Payment) - verify deposit and payment handling
  • [ ] T052 [P] Execute quickstart.md Scenario 8 (Zero Service Charges) - verify invoice with no services
  • [ ] T053 Verify Success Criteria SC-002 (100% overbooking prevention), SC-004 (100% refund blocking), SC-009 (100% duplicate invoice prevention), SC-010 (100% room release on checkout)
  • [ ] T054 Re-run al compile to confirm zero errors after any validation fixes (Constitution Principle I final gate)
  • [ ] T055 Document any known limitations in plan.md (e.g., early checkout handling, partial refunds, concurrency edge cases)

Dependencies & Execution Order

Phase Dependencies

  • Setup (Phase 1): No dependencies - can start immediately
  • Foundational (Phase 2): Depends on Setup (Phase 1) completion - BLOCKS all user stories
  • User Story 6 - Setup (Phase 3): Depends on Foundational (Phase 2) - BLOCKS US1 (reservations need configured rooms)
  • User Story 1 - Reservations (Phase 4): Depends on US6 (Phase 3) completion (requires rooms and No. Series)
  • User Story 2 - Check-In (Phase 5): Depends on US1 (Phase 4) completion (requires existing reservations)
  • User Story 3 - Service Charges (Phase 6): Depends on US2 (Phase 5) completion (requires Occupied reservations)
  • User Story 4 - Checkout (Phase 7): Depends on US1, US2, US3 (Phases 4-6) completion (full lifecycle integration)
  • User Story 5 - Refunds (Phase 8): Depends on US4 (Phase 7) completion (requires posted invoices)
  • Polish (Phase 9): Depends on all desired user stories being complete

User Story Dependencies

Phase 1: Setup → Phase 2: Foundational
                      ↓
Phase 3: US6 (Admin Setup - P3 but FIRST due to blocking nature)
                      ↓
Phase 4: US1 (Reservations - P1) ← MVP Entry Point
                      ↓
Phase 5: US2 (Check-In - P1)
                      ↓
Phase 6: US3 (Service Charges - P2) [Can be skipped for minimal MVP]
                      ↓
Phase 7: US4 (Checkout - P1) ← MVP Exit Point (integrates US1+US2+US3)
                      ↓
Phase 8: US5 (Refunds - P2) [Can be added post-MVP]
                      ↓
Phase 9: Polish & Validation

Critical Path for MVP: Setup → Foundational → US6 → US1 → US2 → US4 → Compile & Validate

Optional for Enhanced MVP: Add US3 (Service Charges) between US2 and US4

Post-MVP Enhancement: Add US5 (Refunds) after US4

Within Each User Story

  • Enums before tables (foundational types used in table fields)
  • Tables before codeunits (codeunits operate on tables)
  • Codeunits before pages (pages call codeunit actions)
  • Permission sets after all objects defined (reference tables/codeunits/pages)
  • Integration tasks last within each story

Parallel Opportunities

  • Phase 1 (Setup): All tasks sequential (directory creation)
  • Phase 2 (Foundational): T004, T005, T006 can run in parallel (different enum files), T007 and T008 can run in parallel (different codeunit files)
  • Phase 3 (US6): T009 and T010 can run in parallel (different table files), T011/T012/T013 can run in parallel after tables complete (different page files)
  • Phase 4 (US1): T016 and T017 can run in parallel after T015 completes (different page files)
  • Phase 6 (US3): T021 and T022 parallel (table and codeunit), T023 after both complete
  • Phase 7 (US4): T025 and T026/T027 parallel (table and codeunit), T028/T029 parallel after T025/T026 complete, T030 last
  • Phase 8 (US5): T031 and T032 parallel (codeunit and permission set), T033/T034 after both complete
  • Phase 9 (Polish): T035/T036/T037/T038 can run in parallel (enhancements to different aspects), validation scenarios T046-T052 can run in parallel after deployment T042-T044 complete

Parallel Example: Phase 7 (User Story 4 - Checkout)

# Launch table and core codeunit together (different files):
Task T025: "Create HTL Invoice table in src/Tables/HTLInvoice.Table.al"
Task T026: "Create HTL Checkout Management codeunit in src/Codeunits/HTLCheckoutManagement.Codeunit.al"

# After both complete, launch both page implementations together:
Task T028: "Create HTL Invoice List page in src/Pages/HTLInvoiceList.Page.al"
Task T029: "Create HTL Invoice Card page in src/Pages/HTLInvoiceCard.Page.al"

Implementation Strategy

Minimal MVP (Core Lifecycle Only)

Goal: Reservation → Check-In → Checkout (no service charges, no refunds)

  1. Complete Phase 1: Setup (T001-T003)
  2. Complete Phase 2: Foundational (T004-T008)
  3. Complete Phase 3: US6 Setup (T009-T014)
  4. Complete Phase 4: US1 Reservations (T015-T018)
  5. Complete Phase 5: US2 Check-In (T019-T020)
  6. SKIP Phase 6: US3 Service Charges (defer to v1.1)
  7. Complete Phase 7: US4 Checkout (T025-T030) - note: checkout calculates zero service charges
  8. SKIP Phase 8: US5 Refunds (defer to v1.1)
  9. Complete Phase 9: Compile + Validate (T039-T054, run Scenarios 1, 2, 3, 8 only)
  10. STOP and VALIDATE: MVP ready - can create reservations, check in, check out with invoice

Estimated Tasks: ~35 tasks (Phases 1-2-3-4-5-7-9 partial)

Enhanced MVP (With Service Charges)

Goal: Full operational lifecycle with service charges, defer refunds

  1. Complete Phases 1-5 (same as Minimal MVP)
  2. Complete Phase 6: US3 Service Charges (T021-T024)
  3. Complete Phase 7: US4 Checkout (T025-T030) - checkout includes service charges in calculation
  4. SKIP Phase 8: US5 Refunds (defer to v1.1)
  5. Complete Phase 9: Compile + Validate (T039-T054, run all scenarios except Scenario 4 Step 2 - Finance refund)
  6. STOP and VALIDATE: Enhanced MVP ready - full guest lifecycle with services

Estimated Tasks: ~45 tasks (Phases 1-2-3-4-5-6-7-9 partial)

Full v1.0 (All Features)

Goal: Complete specification including refund permission enforcement

  1. Complete Phases 1-7 (same as Enhanced MVP)
  2. Complete Phase 8: US5 Refunds (T031-T034)
  3. Complete Phase 9: Compile + Validate (T039-T054, run ALL scenarios)
  4. STOP and VALIDATE: v1.0 ready - full specification with permission enforcement

Estimated Tasks: 55 tasks (all phases)

Incremental Delivery Path

  1. Milestone 1: Setup + Foundational → Foundation deployed (Admin can configure setup, no end-user features yet)
  2. Milestone 2: Add US6 → Admin can manage rooms (Room List/Card functional)
  3. Milestone 3: Add US1 → Front Desk can create reservations (MVP entry point, no check-in yet)
  4. Milestone 4: Add US2 → Check-in functional (status transitions work)
  5. Milestone 5: Add US3 → Service charges functional (optional - can skip for faster MVP)
  6. Milestone 6: Add US4 → Checkout functional (invoicing complete, MVP ready)
  7. Milestone 7: Add US5 → Refunds functional (permission enforcement complete, v1.0 ready)

Each milestone adds value independently and is deployable/testable.

Parallel Team Strategy

With 2 developers after Foundational phase completes:

  • Developer A: US6 (Setup) → US1 (Reservations) → US4 part 1 (Invoice table/codeunit)
  • Developer B: US2 (Check-In) → US3 (Service Charges) → US4 part 2 (Invoice pages/integration)
  • Merge Point: US4 completion, then proceed to US5 together or assign to one developer
  • Final: Both developers run validation scenarios in parallel (split Scenarios 1-4 vs 5-8)

Constitution Compliance Checklist

Before marking feature as complete, verify:

  • [ ] All 25 objects created with IDs within 50000-50249 range (10% of available 250 IDs used)
  • [ ] All object names have "HTL" prefix (Tables: "HTL Reservation", Pages: "HTL Reservation Card", Codeunits: "HTL Check-In Management", Enums: "HTL Room Type", PermissionSets: "HTL-FRONT DESK")
  • [ ] All table and field names ≤30 characters (longest: "HTL Service Charge" = 19 chars, "VAT Prod. Posting Group" = 24 chars)
  • [ ] Only BC27 symbols referenced: Codeunit "No. Series", Codeunit "Gen. Jnl.-Post Line", Table "Customer", Table "VAT Posting Setup", Table "Gen. Journal Line"
  • [ ] No deprecated symbols used: NoSeriesManagement codeunit NOT referenced
  • [ ] app.json unchanged: id, name, publisher, version fields untouched
  • [ ] .alpackages/ unchanged: no symbol file modifications
  • [ ] All source files in src/ subdirectories: Tables/, Pages/, Codeunits/, Enums/, PermissionSets/
  • [ ] al compile executed and returns ZERO errors (.app artifact generated)
  • [ ] All quickstart.md validation scenarios pass
  • [ ] Success Criteria SC-002, SC-004, SC-009, SC-010 verified at 100% enforcement

Notes

  • [P] tasks = different files, no dependencies, can run concurrently
  • [Story] label maps task to specific user story (US1-US6) for traceability
  • US6 is P3 priority but implemented FIRST due to blocking dependencies (setup prerequisite)
  • US4 integrates US1+US2+US3 (checkout depends on full lifecycle)
  • US5 enhances US4 (refunds require posted invoices from checkout)
  • Each user story should be independently testable per quickstart.md scenarios
  • Commit after each task or logical group
  • Stop at any checkpoint to validate story independently before proceeding
  • Constitution Principle I: al compile clean is the ONLY definition of done - no shortcuts
  • Avoid: Object ID duplicates, name collisions (ensure HTL prefix), >30 char names, BC28+ APIs
  • Test validation requires BC27 container/SaaS tenant deployment and manual Web Client testing
checklists/requirements.md49 lines

Specification Quality Checklist: Hotel Reservation Lifecycle

Purpose: Validate specification completeness and quality before proceeding to planning

Created: 2026-06-15

Feature: spec.md

Content Quality

  • [x] No implementation details (languages, frameworks, APIs)
  • [x] Focused on user value and business needs
  • [x] Written for non-technical stakeholders
  • [x] All mandatory sections completed

Requirement Completeness

  • [x] No [NEEDS CLARIFICATION] markers remain
  • [x] Requirements are testable and unambiguous
  • [x] Success criteria are measurable
  • [x] Success criteria are technology-agnostic (no implementation details)
  • [x] All acceptance scenarios are defined
  • [x] Edge cases are identified
  • [x] Scope is clearly bounded
  • [x] Dependencies and assumptions identified

Feature Readiness

  • [x] All functional requirements have clear acceptance criteria
  • [x] User scenarios cover primary flows
  • [x] Feature meets measurable outcomes defined in Success Criteria
  • [x] No implementation details leak into specification

Notes

Validation Results: ✅ All quality checks passed

Specification Quality Assessment:

  • Specification is complete with 45 functional requirements organized by domain
  • 6 prioritized user stories with independent test scenarios covering full lifecycle
  • 10 measurable success criteria with quantitative targets (time, percentage, error rates)
  • Comprehensive edge cases identified (early checkout, overbooking, VAT changes, payment failures)
  • Clear role-based security requirements (Front Desk, Finance, Admin)
  • Assumptions documented for scope boundaries and dependencies
  • No implementation details present - focus maintained on WHAT and WHY, not HOW
  • All requirements are testable and unambiguous
  • Ready for planning phase

No issues found - Specification meets all quality gates for proceeding to /speckit-plan

contracts/codeunit-contracts.md473 lines

Codeunit Contracts

Feature: Hotel Reservation Lifecycle Date: 2026-06-15

Overview

This document defines the business logic contracts (codeunits) that implement the reservation lifecycle workflows. These codeunits encapsulate complex operations and enforce business rules.

Codeunit 50200 "HTL Check-In Management"

Purpose: Handle guest check-in process

Public Procedures

CheckIn(var HTLReservation: Record "HTL Reservation")

Description: Transition reservation from Confirmed to Occupied status

Parameters:

  • HTLReservation (VAR): Reservation record to check in (modified in place)

Preconditions:

  • Reservation.Status = Confirmed
  • Reservation."Check-in Date" <= TODAY (cannot check in future reservations)
  • Reservation."Room No." is assigned

Process:

  1. Validate preconditions (error if violated)
  2. Update HTL Room.Occupied = TRUE for assigned room
  3. Set Reservation.Status = Occupied
  4. Set Reservation."Check-in Timestamp" = CURRENTDATETIME
  5. Commit changes

Postconditions:

  • Reservation.Status = Occupied
  • Reservation."Check-in Timestamp" populated
  • HTL Room.Occupied = TRUE
  • Message: "Guest checked in successfully to Room %1"

Error Conditions:

  • Status <> Confirmed: "Reservation must be in Confirmed status to check in"
  • Check-in Date > TODAY: "Cannot check in before scheduled check-in date"
  • Room No. blank: "Room must be assigned before check-in"

Permissions Required: HTL-FRONT DESK

Example Usage (from Page 50104 action):

trigger OnAction()
var
    HTLCheckIn: Codeunit "HTL Check-In Management";
begin
    HTLCheckIn.CheckIn(Rec);
    CurrPage.Update(false);
end;

Codeunit 50201 "HTL Checkout Management"

Purpose: Handle guest checkout, invoice generation, and payment

Public Procedures

Checkout(var HTLReservation: Record "HTL Reservation"; CapturePayment: Boolean): Code[20]

Description: Complete checkout workflow - generate invoice, post financials, capture payment

Parameters:

  • HTLReservation (VAR): Reservation record to check out (modified in place)
  • CapturePayment (Boolean): TRUE to mark payment as captured (simulates payment gateway success)

Returns: Invoice No. (Code[20]) of generated invoice

Preconditions:

  • Reservation.Status = Occupied
  • Reservation."Check-out Date" <= TODAY (cannot checkout future reservations, but allow early checkout)
  • Reservation."Invoice No." = '' (invoice not already generated)

Process:

  1. Validate preconditions
  2. Generate Invoice:
  • Create HTL Invoice record
  • Auto-assign Invoice No. from No. Series
  • Calculate Room Charges = Room."Nightly Rate" × CalcNights()
  • Calculate Service Charges = SUM(HTL Service Charge.Amount)
  • Calculate VAT Amount = (Room Charges + Service Charges) × GetVATRate()
  • Calculate Total Amount = Room Charges + Service Charges + VAT Amount
  • Calculate Deposit Applied = IF(Reservation."Deposit Captured", Reservation."Deposit Amount", 0)
  • Calculate Amount Due = Total Amount - Deposit Applied
  • Set Payment Successful = CapturePayment parameter
  1. Post Financial Transactions (if CapturePayment = TRUE):
  • Create Gen. Journal Line for Customer Invoice (Total Amount)
  • Create Gen. Journal Line for Deposit application (negative, if captured)
  • Create Gen. Journal Line for Final Payment (Amount Due)
  • Post via Codeunit "Gen. Jnl.-Post Line"
  • Set Invoice.Posted = TRUE
  1. Update Reservation:
  • Set Reservation."Invoice No." = new invoice number
  • Set Reservation.Status = Closed
  1. Release Room:
  • Set HTL Room.Occupied = FALSE
  1. Commit changes
  2. Return Invoice No.

Postconditions (if successful):

  • HTL Invoice created with all amounts calculated
  • Reservation.Status = Closed
  • Reservation."Invoice No." populated
  • HTL Room.Occupied = FALSE
  • Gen. Ledger Entries posted (if CapturePayment = TRUE)
  • Invoice.Posted = TRUE (if CapturePayment = TRUE)

Error Conditions:

  • Status <> Occupied: "Reservation must be checked in before checkout"
  • Invoice No. <> '': "Invoice already exists for this reservation (No. %1)"
  • Payment posting failure: "Payment processing failed - reservation not closed" (Status remains Occupied, Invoice.Posted = FALSE)

Permissions Required: HTL-FRONT DESK

Example Usage (from Page 50104 action):

trigger OnAction()
var
    HTLCheckout: Codeunit "HTL Checkout Management";
    InvoiceNo: Code[20];
    CapturePayment: Boolean;
begin
    CapturePayment := Confirm('Capture final payment?', true);
    InvoiceNo := HTLCheckout.Checkout(Rec, CapturePayment);
    Message('Checkout complete. Invoice No. %1 generated.', InvoiceNo);
    CurrPage.Update(false);
end;

Private Procedures (internal helpers)

CalcNights(CheckInDate: Date; CheckOutDate: Date): Integer

Description: Calculate number of nights for room charge calculation

Logic: CheckOutDate - CheckInDate (minimum 1)

GetVATRate(VATBusPostingGroup: Code[20]; VATProdPostingGroup: Code[20]): Decimal

Description: Lookup VAT % from VAT Posting Setup

Returns: VAT % (e.g., 20.00 for 20%)

Fallback: Error if VAT Posting Setup not found (setup prerequisite)

CreateInvoice(HTLReservation: Record "HTL Reservation"; RoomCharges: Decimal; ServiceCharges: Decimal; VATAmount: Decimal): Record "HTL Invoice"

Description: Initialize and insert HTL Invoice record with calculated amounts

PostFinancials(HTLInvoice: Record "HTL Invoice")

Description: Create and post Gen. Journal Lines for invoice

Transaction Scope: Commits only if all postings succeed

Codeunit 50202 "HTL Refund Management"

Purpose: Process refunds for posted invoices (Finance-only)

Public Procedures

ProcessRefund(HTLInvoice: Record "HTL Invoice"; RefundAmount: Decimal)

Description: Create credit memo and post refund transaction

Parameters:

  • HTLInvoice: Invoice to refund (read-only)
  • RefundAmount: Amount to refund (must be <= Invoice."Total Amount")

Preconditions:

  • Invoice.Posted = TRUE (can only refund posted invoices)
  • RefundAmount > 0 AND RefundAmount <= Invoice."Total Amount"
  • User has HTL-FINANCE permission set (enforced via TableData permissions)

Process:

  1. Permission Check:
  • Verify current user has HTL-FINANCE permission
  • Error if Front Desk user attempts execution (FR-039, FR-040)
  1. Validate preconditions
  2. Create Credit Memo:
  • Create Gen. Journal Line with Document Type = Credit Memo
  • Account Type = Customer, Account No. = Invoice."Customer No."
  • Amount = -RefundAmount (negative)
  • Document No. = Next No. from Invoice Nos. series
  1. Post Refund:
  • Post via Codeunit "Gen. Jnl.-Post Line"
  • Link to original invoice via "Applies-to Doc. No." = Invoice."No."
  1. Update Invoice (optional tracking):
  • Add "Refunded" boolean field to HTL Invoice (if needed for reporting)
  1. Commit changes
  2. Message: "Refund of %1 processed successfully"

Postconditions:

  • Gen. Ledger Entry created (Credit Memo posted)
  • Customer balance reduced by RefundAmount

Error Conditions:

  • User not in HTL-FINANCE: "You do not have permission to process refunds" (enforced by permission set)
  • Invoice not posted: "Can only refund posted invoices"
  • RefundAmount invalid: "Refund amount must be between 0 and %1"
  • Posting failure: "Refund posting failed: %1" (rollback transaction)

Permissions Required: HTL-FINANCE (permission set blocks Front Desk via TableData/Codeunit permissions)

Security Enforcement:

  • Permission set HTL-FRONT DESK excludes Execute permission on Codeunit 50202
  • Permission set HTL-FINANCE includes Execute permission on Codeunit 50202

Example Usage (from Page 50107 action):

trigger OnAction()
var
    HTLRefund: Codeunit "HTL Refund Management";
    RefundAmount: Decimal;
begin
    if not Confirm('Process refund for this invoice?', false) then
        exit;
    
    RefundAmount := Rec."Total Amount"; // Or prompt for partial refund
    HTLRefund.ProcessRefund(Rec, RefundAmount);
end;

Codeunit 50203 "HTL Service Charge Mgmt"

Purpose: Helper functions for service charge operations

Public Procedures

GetNextLineNo(ReservationNo: Code[20]): Integer

Description: Calculate next available Line No. for new service charge

Parameters:

  • ReservationNo: Parent reservation number

Returns: Next Line No. (increment of 10000, e.g., 10000, 20000, 30000)

Logic:

HTLServiceCharge.SetRange("Reservation No.", ReservationNo);
if HTLServiceCharge.FindLast() then
    exit(HTLServiceCharge."Line No." + 10000)
else
    exit(10000);

CalcTotalCharges(ReservationNo: Code[20]): Decimal

Description: Sum all service charge amounts for a reservation

Parameters:

  • ReservationNo: Parent reservation number

Returns: Total service charge amount

Usage: Called by Checkout codeunit during invoice generation

Codeunit 50204 "HTL Room Availability"

Purpose: Centralized room availability checking logic

Public Procedures

IsRoomAvailable(RoomNo: Code[20]; CheckInDate: Date; CheckOutDate: Date; ExcludeReservationNo: Code[20]): Boolean

Description: Check if room is available for given date range

Parameters:

  • RoomNo: Room to check
  • CheckInDate: Requested arrival date
  • CheckOutDate: Requested departure date
  • ExcludeReservationNo: Reservation No. to exclude (for modify scenario, use '' for new)

Returns:

  • TRUE if room is available
  • FALSE if room has overlapping booking

Logic:

HTLReservation.LockTable(); // Prevent race conditions
HTLReservation.SetRange("Room No.", RoomNo);
HTLReservation.SetFilter("No.", '<>%1', ExcludeReservationNo);
HTLReservation.SetFilter(Status, '<>%1', HTLReservation.Status::Closed);
if HTLReservation.FindSet() then
    repeat
        // Date overlap: (NewStart <= ExistingEnd) AND (NewEnd >= ExistingStart)
        if (CheckInDate <= HTLReservation."Check-out Date") and
           (CheckOutDate >= HTLReservation."Check-in Date") then
            exit(false); // Overlapping reservation found
    until HTLReservation.Next() = 0;
exit(true); // No overlap, room is available

Usage: Called from HTL Reservation table OnInsert/OnModify triggers

Codeunit 50205 "HTL No. Series Management"

Purpose: Centralized number series operations

Public Procedures

GetNextReservationNo(): Code[20]

Description: Get next reservation number from configured series

Returns: Next Reservation No.

Logic:

HTLHotelSetup.Get();
HTLHotelSetup.TestField("Reservation Nos.");
exit(NoSeries.GetNextNo(HTLHotelSetup."Reservation Nos."));

GetNextInvoiceNo(): Code[20]

Description: Get next invoice number from configured series

Returns: Next Invoice No.

Codeunit Contract Summary

CodeunitIDPurposeEntry PointsPermissions
HTL Check-In Management50200Guest check-inCheckIn()HTL-FRONT DESK
HTL Checkout Management50201Checkout + invoice generationCheckout()HTL-FRONT DESK
HTL Refund Management50202Refund processingProcessRefund()HTL-FINANCE only
HTL Service Charge Mgmt50203Service charge utilitiesGetNextLineNo(), CalcTotalCharges()HTL-FRONT DESK
HTL Room Availability50204Availability checkingIsRoomAvailable()All (called by table triggers)
HTL No. Series Management50205Number series utilitiesGetNextReservationNo(), GetNextInvoiceNo()All (called by table triggers)

Transaction Boundaries

Atomic Operations (all-or-nothing):

  • Checkout: Invoice creation + financial posting + reservation status update + room release
  • Refund: Credit memo creation + posting
  • Check-in: Room occupancy update + reservation status update

Rollback Behavior:

  • If any step fails, entire transaction rolls back
  • User sees error message with specific failure reason
  • No partial state changes (e.g., invoice created but not posted)

Error Handling Strategy

Validation Errors (user-correctable):

  • TestField() for required setup (e.g., No. Series configured)
  • Error() for business rule violations (e.g., wrong status, duplicate invoice)
  • Confirm() for destructive actions (e.g., refund processing)

System Errors (unexpected):

  • Database errors propagate to user (BC standard behavior)
  • Posting errors from Gen. Jnl.-Post Line surface with details

Error Messages (user-friendly):

  • Include context: field name, current value, expected value
  • Actionable: "Configure No. Series in HTL Hotel Setup" vs "No. Series missing"

Integration Points

Business Central Platform

IntegrationBC ComponentUsage
Number SeriesCodeunit "No. Series"Auto-assign Reservation No., Invoice No.
Financial PostingCodeunit "Gen. Jnl.-Post Line"Post invoices, payments, refunds
VAT CalculationTable "VAT Posting Setup"Lookup VAT % for invoice
Customer LedgerTable "Cust. Ledger Entry"Customer invoice/payment tracking

Extension Points (Future)

  • Event: OnBeforeCheckIn, OnAfterCheckIn (allow other extensions to hook into check-in)
  • Event: OnBeforeCheckout, OnAfterCheckout (e.g., loyalty points, email notifications)
  • Event: OnBeforeCalculateInvoice (custom pricing rules)

Not Implemented in v1 - standard AL events can be added in v2 without breaking changes

Performance Considerations

Database Locks:

  • LOCKTABLE used in IsRoomAvailable() to prevent overbooking race conditions
  • Minimize lock duration (only during availability check, not entire reservation creation)

Batch Operations:

  • Not applicable - operations are single-record (one reservation at a time)
  • Future: bulk check-in for group bookings

Caching:

  • No caching needed (BC handles record caching)
  • No. Series caching handled by BC platform

Testing Contracts

Unit Test Scenarios (manual testing in v1)

HTL Check-In Management:

  • ✓ Check in Confirmed reservation (success)
  • ✓ Attempt check-in on Occupied reservation (error)
  • ✓ Attempt check-in on future date (error)

HTL Checkout Management:

  • ✓ Checkout with payment capture (success, invoice posted)
  • ✓ Checkout without payment (success, invoice unposted)
  • ✓ Checkout with service charges (verify invoice total)
  • ✓ Attempt duplicate checkout (error)
  • ✓ Verify room released after checkout

HTL Refund Management:

  • ✓ Finance user processes refund (success)
  • ✓ Front Desk attempts refund (permission denied)
  • ✓ Refund unposted invoice (error)
  • ✓ Refund amount > total (error)

HTL Room Availability:

  • ✓ Check available room (returns TRUE)
  • ✓ Check overlapping dates (returns FALSE)
  • ✓ Concurrent reservation creation (only one succeeds)

Contract Versioning

Version: 1.0 Breaking Changes: Not applicable (initial release)

Future signature changes:

  • Adding optional parameters: non-breaking (provide defaults)
  • Adding return values to void procedures: breaking (require major version bump)
  • Renaming procedures: breaking
  • Adding new procedures: non-breaking
contracts/page-contracts.md453 lines

Page Contracts

Feature: Hotel Reservation Lifecycle Date: 2026-06-15

Overview

This document defines the UI contracts (pages) exposed by the Hotel Reservation Lifecycle extension. These pages provide the primary user interface for Front Desk, Finance, and Admin roles.

Page 50100 "HTL Hotel Setup Card"

Type: Card Page Source Table: HTL Hotel Setup (Table 50000) Target Users: Admin Purpose: Configure system-wide settings

Fields Displayed

Field CaptionSource FieldEditableNotes
Reservation Nos.Reservation Nos.YesNo. Series lookup
Invoice Nos.Invoice Nos.YesNo. Series lookup
VAT Bus. Posting GroupVAT Bus. Posting GroupYesLookup to VAT Business Posting Group

Actions

Action CaptionTypeFunction
RoomsNavigateOpen HTL Room List

Usage Pattern

  1. Admin opens HTL Hotel Setup from search
  2. Configures No. Series codes (prerequisite: No. Series must exist)
  3. Sets default VAT Business Posting Group
  4. Navigates to Rooms to configure inventory

Permissions Required

  • HTL-ADMIN permission set
  • Read/Modify access to Table 50000

Page 50101 "HTL Room List"

Type: List Page Source Table: HTL Room (Table 50001) Target Users: Admin (edit), Front Desk (read for room selection) Purpose: Manage room inventory

Fields Displayed

Field CaptionSource FieldEditableNotes
No.No.Yes (insert)Primary key
Room TypeRoom TypeYesEnum dropdown
Nightly RateNightly RateYesDecimal
OccupiedOccupiedNoSystem-managed
VAT Prod. Posting GroupVAT Prod. Posting GroupYesLookup

Actions

Action CaptionTypeFunction
ReservationsNavigateShow reservations for selected room

Usage Pattern

  1. Admin creates new room records (No., Type, Rate, VAT Product Posting Group)
  2. Front Desk views list when selecting room for new reservation
  3. Occupied flag updates automatically during check-in/checkout (read-only)

Permissions Required

  • HTL-ADMIN: Full RIMD access
  • HTL-FRONT DESK: Read access only

Page 50102 "HTL Room Card"

Type: Card Page Source Table: HTL Room (Table 50001) Target Users: Admin Purpose: Detailed room configuration

Fields Displayed

Field CaptionSource FieldEditableNotes
No.No.Yes (insert only)Primary key
Room TypeRoom TypeYesEnum
Nightly RateNightly RateYesDecimal
OccupiedOccupiedNoSystem-managed
VAT Prod. Posting GroupVAT Prod. Posting GroupYesLookup

FactBoxes

  • Reservation History (shows past/current reservations for this room)

Actions

Action CaptionTypeFunction
ReservationsNavigateHTL Reservation List filtered by Room No.

Permissions Required

  • HTL-ADMIN: Full RIMD access

Page 50103 "HTL Reservation List"

Type: List Page Source Table: HTL Reservation (Table 50002) Target Users: Front Desk, Finance Purpose: View and manage all reservations

Fields Displayed

Field CaptionSource FieldEditableNotes
No.No.NoAuto-generated
Customer No.Customer No.Yes (before Occupied)Lookup to Customer
Customer NameCustomer.NameNoFlowField
Room No.Room No.Yes (before Occupied)Lookup to HTL Room (filter: not Occupied)
Check-in DateCheck-in DateYes (before Occupied)Date picker
Check-out DateCheck-out DateYes (before Occupied)Date picker
StatusStatusNoSystem-managed via actions
Deposit AmountDeposit AmountYesDecimal
Deposit CapturedDeposit CapturedYesCheckbox
Invoice No.Invoice No.NoPopulated at checkout

Views (Filters)

  • All Reservations (default)
  • Confirmed (Status = Confirmed)
  • Checked In (Status = Occupied)
  • Closed (Status = Closed)
  • Today's Check-ins (Check-in Date = TODAY)
  • Today's Check-outs (Check-out Date = TODAY)

Actions

Action CaptionTypeFunctionPermissions
NewStandardCreate new reservationHTL-FRONT DESK
Check InProcessRun HTL Check-In CodeunitHTL-FRONT DESK, Status=Confirmed
Service ChargesNavigateOpen HTL Service Charge List for reservationHTL-FRONT DESK, Status=Occupied
Check OutProcessRun HTL Checkout CodeunitHTL-FRONT DESK, Status=Occupied
Show InvoiceNavigateOpen HTL Invoice CardAll, if Invoice No. exists

Usage Pattern

  1. Front Desk creates new reservation (auto-assign No., select customer/room/dates)
  2. Front Desk marks Deposit Captured checkbox after payment success
  3. Front Desk uses "Check In" action when guest arrives (Status → Occupied)
  4. Front Desk posts service charges during stay
  5. Front Desk uses "Check Out" action to generate invoice and capture final payment
  6. Finance reviews closed reservations and invoices

Permissions Required

  • HTL-FRONT DESK: RIMD on Confirmed/Occupied reservations, Execute check-in/checkout
  • HTL-FINANCE: Same as Front Desk + access to refund actions

Page 50104 "HTL Reservation Card"

Type: Card Page Source Table: HTL Reservation (Table 50002) Target Users: Front Desk, Finance Purpose: Detailed reservation view and editing

FastTabs

General:

  • No. (display only)
  • Customer No. (editable before Occupied)
  • Customer Name (FlowField display)
  • Room No. (editable before Occupied)
  • Room Type (FlowField display)
  • Status (display only)

Dates:

  • Check-in Date (editable before Occupied)
  • Check-out Date (editable before Occupied)
  • Check-in Timestamp (display only, populated at check-in)

Billing:

  • Nightly Rate (FlowField from Room, display only)
  • Deposit Amount (editable)
  • Deposit Captured (checkbox)
  • Invoice No. (display only)

Posting:

  • VAT Bus. Posting Group (editable before Occupied)

FactBoxes

  • Customer Details (Customer card fields)
  • Room Details (Room card fields)
  • Service Charge Summary (count, total amount)

Actions

Action GroupAction CaptionFunctionEnabled When
ProcessCheck InRun HTL Check-In CodeunitStatus = Confirmed
ProcessCheck OutRun HTL Checkout CodeunitStatus = Occupied
NavigateService ChargesOpen HTL Service Charge ListStatus = Occupied
NavigateInvoiceOpen HTL Invoice CardInvoice No. <> ''

Permissions Required

  • HTL-FRONT DESK: RIMD before checkout
  • HTL-FINANCE: Read all, execute refunds

Page 50105 "HTL Service Charge List"

Type: List Page (Part) Source Table: HTL Service Charge (Table 50003) Target Users: Front Desk Purpose: Post billable services to reservation

Fields Displayed

Field CaptionSource FieldEditableNotes
Line No.Line No.NoAuto-increment
Service TypeService TypeYesEnum dropdown
DescriptionDescriptionYesText
AmountAmountYesDecimal
VAT Prod. Posting GroupVAT Prod. Posting GroupYesLookup

Usage Pattern

  1. Embedded in HTL Reservation Card page as ListPart
  2. Front Desk adds new lines while guest is checked in (Status = Occupied)
  3. Service charges automatically included in invoice calculation at checkout

Validation

  • Can only add lines if parent reservation Status = Occupied
  • Amount must be > 0
  • Service Type is required

Permissions Required

  • HTL-FRONT DESK: RIM access (no Delete to preserve audit trail)

Page 50106 "HTL Invoice List"

Type: List Page Source Table: HTL Invoice (Table 50004) Target Users: Finance, Front Desk (read-only) Purpose: Review generated invoices

Fields Displayed

Field CaptionSource FieldEditableNotes
No.No.NoAuto-generated
Reservation No.Reservation No.NoLink
Customer No.Customer No.NoCopied from reservation
Customer NameCustomer.NameNoFlowField
Invoice DateInvoice DateNoGeneration date
Room ChargesRoom ChargesNoCalculated
Service ChargesService ChargesNoCalculated
VAT AmountVAT AmountNoCalculated
Total AmountTotal AmountNoCalculated
Deposit AppliedDeposit AppliedNoFrom reservation
Amount DueAmount DueNoCalculated
Payment SuccessfulPayment SuccessfulNoCheckout result
PostedPostedNoSystem flag

Views (Filters)

  • All Invoices (default)
  • Unpaid (Payment Successful = FALSE)
  • Paid (Payment Successful = TRUE)
  • Unposted (Posted = FALSE)
  • Posted (Posted = TRUE)

Actions

Action CaptionTypeFunctionPermissions
Show ReservationNavigateOpen HTL Reservation CardAll
Process RefundProcessRun HTL Refund CodeunitHTL-FINANCE only

Permissions Required

  • HTL-FRONT DESK: Read access
  • HTL-FINANCE: Read access + Execute refund codeunit

Page 50107 "HTL Invoice Card"

Type: Card Page Source Table: HTL Invoice (Table 50004) Target Users: Finance, Front Desk (read-only) Purpose: Detailed invoice view

FastTabs

General:

  • No. (display only)
  • Reservation No. (display only, drill-down to reservation)
  • Customer No. (display only)
  • Customer Name (FlowField display)
  • Invoice Date (display only)

Amounts:

  • Room Charges (display only)
  • Service Charges (display only)
  • Subtotal (display only)
  • VAT Amount (display only)
  • Total Amount (display only)
  • Deposit Applied (display only)
  • Amount Due (display only)

Payment:

  • Payment Successful (display only)
  • Posted (display only)

FactBoxes

  • Customer Details
  • Reservation Details
  • Service Charge Lines (embedded list)

Actions

Action GroupAction CaptionFunctionEnabled When
ProcessProcess RefundRun HTL Refund CodeunitHTL-FINANCE, Posted = TRUE
NavigateReservationOpen HTL Reservation CardAlways
NavigateLedger EntriesShow Gen. Ledger Entries for Document No.Posted = TRUE

Permissions Required

  • HTL-FRONT DESK: Read access
  • HTL-FINANCE: Read access + Execute refund

Page Flow Diagrams

Reservation Lifecycle (Front Desk)

HTL Reservation List (Page 50103)
    ↓ [New]
HTL Reservation Card (Page 50104)
    ↓ [Fill details, mark Deposit Captured]
    ↓ [Check In action]
HTL Reservation Card (Status = Occupied)
    ↓ [Service Charges action]
HTL Service Charge List (Page 50105 - embedded)
    ↓ [Add breakfast, laundry, etc.]
    ↓ [Return to Reservation Card]
    ↓ [Check Out action]
HTL Checkout Process (Codeunit)
    ↓ [Generate invoice, capture payment]
HTL Invoice Card (Page 50107) - read-only view

Refund Processing (Finance)

HTL Invoice List (Page 50106)
    ↓ [Filter: Posted = TRUE]
    ↓ [Select invoice requiring refund]
HTL Invoice Card (Page 50107)
    ↓ [Process Refund action]
HTL Refund Codeunit (permission check → Finance only)
    ↓ [Create credit memo, post reversal]
Gen. Ledger Entries (BC standard page)

Setup (Admin)

HTL Hotel Setup Card (Page 50100)
    ↓ [Configure No. Series, VAT]
    ↓ [Rooms action]
HTL Room List (Page 50101)
    ↓ [New]
HTL Room Card (Page 50102)
    ↓ [Fill room details]

UI Validation Rules

PageFieldValidationError Message
HTL Reservation CardRoom No.Table trigger CheckRoomAvailability()"Room X is already booked..."
HTL Reservation CardCheck-out Date>= Check-in Date + 1"Check-out must be after check-in"
HTL Service Charge ListAmount> 0"Amount must be greater than zero"
HTL Service Charge ListOnInsertRecordParent Status = Occupied"Services can only be added to checked-in guests"

Accessibility & Usability

  • All pages support keyboard navigation (standard BC behavior)
  • Required fields marked with red asterisk (standard BC behavior)
  • Lookups provide search/filter capabilities (Customer No., Room No.)
  • FactBoxes provide context without leaving page
  • Color coding (future enhancement): Status badges (Confirmed=Yellow, Occupied=Green, Closed=Gray)

Mobile/Responsive Considerations

  • Card pages automatically adapt to BC mobile client
  • List pages support swipe gestures on mobile
  • Promote key fields for mobile rendering: No., Customer Name, Room No., Status, Check-in/out Dates

API Surface (Future - Out of Scope for v1)

Not exposed as web services in v1. Potential v2 APIs:

  • API Page for HTL Reservation (RESTful reservation creation from external booking engine)
  • API Page for HTL Room (room availability queries)

Contract Versioning

Version: 1.0 Breaking Changes: Not applicable (initial release) Deprecations: None

Future changes must maintain backward compatibility:

  • New fields: append to end of field list
  • Field removals: deprecate first, remove in major version
  • Enum additions: safe (non-breaking)
  • Enum removals: breaking change

spec.md151 lines

Feature Specification: Hotel Reservation Lifecycle

Feature Branch: 001-hotel-reservation-lifecycle

Created: 2026-06-13

Status: Draft

Input: User description: "Build a hospitality reservation lifecycle capability for customer reservations, room assignment, deposits, check-in, service charges, checkout, invoice generation, VAT calculation, final payment, and unauthorized refund prevention."

User Scenarios & Testing (mandatory)

User Story 1 - Manage Reservation Through Check-In (Priority: P1)

A front desk user creates or updates a reservation, assigns an available room for the stay dates, records the required deposit outcome, and checks the guest in when the room is ready.

Why this priority: Reservation capture, room allocation, and check-in are the core operational flow; without this, the hotel cannot accept guests reliably.

Independent Test: Can be tested by creating a reservation for a customer, assigning an unoccupied room for non-overlapping dates, capturing a successful deposit, and moving the reservation to occupied status.

Acceptance Scenarios:

  1. Given an available room and a known customer, When front desk creates a reservation with check-in and check-out dates, Then the reservation is stored with a confirmed status and the minimum reservation data.
  2. Given a confirmed reservation with an available room, When front desk assigns the room, Then the room is linked to the reservation and cannot be assigned to another overlapping reservation.
  3. Given a confirmed reservation with a successfully captured deposit, When front desk checks in the guest, Then the reservation status becomes occupied and the room is marked occupied.
  4. Given a reservation deposit attempt fails, When front desk reviews the reservation, Then the reservation clearly shows the deposit was not captured and the failed payment is not treated as successful.

User Story 2 - Add Services and Complete Checkout (Priority: P2)

A front desk user records billable services during the stay and checks out the guest, releasing the room and preparing the reservation for final billing.

Why this priority: Hotels need to capture stay-related charges and release room availability promptly after departure.

Independent Test: Can be tested by adding service charges to an occupied reservation, checking out the guest, verifying the room is released, and confirming service amounts remain available for billing.

Acceptance Scenarios:

  1. Given an occupied reservation, When front desk posts breakfast, laundry, or transfer charges, Then each service charge is linked to the reservation with a billable amount.
  2. Given an occupied reservation with service charges, When front desk checks out the guest, Then the reservation becomes closed for occupancy purposes and the room is no longer marked occupied.
  3. Given a closed reservation, When additional room assignment is attempted for the completed stay, Then the system prevents changes that would compromise the final bill or occupancy history.

User Story 3 - Invoice and Settle Guest Balance (Priority: P3)

A finance user generates exactly one invoice for the reservation, validates VAT, applies the deposit, records final payment, and verifies the financial outcome.

Why this priority: Billing accuracy and financial reconciliation are required for revenue recognition and auditability.

Independent Test: Can be tested by generating an invoice for a checked-out reservation with room nights, service charges, a deposit, VAT, and a remaining balance, then posting final payment.

Acceptance Scenarios:

  1. Given a checked-out reservation without an invoice, When finance generates the invoice, Then room charges, service charges, deposit application, VAT, and remaining amount due are included.
  2. Given an invoice already exists for a reservation, When invoice generation is requested again, Then the duplicate invoice is prevented and the original invoice reference remains unchanged.
  3. Given tax configuration changes before invoicing, When finance generates or recalculates the invoice before posting, Then VAT uses the current configured rate and produces a verifiable tax amount.
  4. Given an invoiced reservation with an outstanding balance, When finance records final payment, Then the remaining amount due is settled and the ledger outcome can be reviewed.

User Story 4 - Enforce Refund and Finance Separation (Priority: P4)

The system restricts refund processing and finance-sensitive actions to finance users while allowing front desk users to complete operational tasks.

Why this priority: Unauthorized refunds and direct finance adjustments create material financial and audit risk.

Independent Test: Can be tested by attempting refund and finance actions as front desk, finance, and admin users and verifying only authorized roles can complete them.

Acceptance Scenarios:

  1. Given a front desk user, When the user attempts to process a refund, Then the action is blocked and no financial refund result is created.
  2. Given a finance user, When the user processes an approved refund, Then the refund is recorded with traceable reservation and invoice context.
  3. Given a front desk user, When the user attempts to modify tax setup or directly adjust finance postings, Then the action is blocked.
  4. Given an admin user, When the user manages setup, room inventory master data, or permissions, Then setup changes are allowed according to admin responsibilities.

Edge Cases

  • A room cannot be assigned to two reservations with overlapping stay dates.
  • A reservation cannot be checked in without a valid room assignment.
  • A failed deposit or final payment attempt cannot update the reservation or invoice as paid.
  • Checkout cannot release the wrong room or leave the assigned room marked occupied.
  • Invoice generation cannot run more than once for the same reservation.
  • VAT must be recalculated from current configuration before invoice posting if tax setup changes after reservation creation.
  • Refund attempts by front desk users must leave no partial refund, ledger, or settlement effect.
  • Reservations with invalid date ranges, such as check-out on or before check-in, must be rejected.

Requirements (mandatory)

Functional Requirements

  • FR-001: The system MUST allow authorized front desk users to create and update reservations containing reservation number, customer number, room number when assigned, check-in date, check-out date, status, deposit amount, and invoice number when invoiced.
  • FR-002: The system MUST support reservation statuses of Confirmed, Occupied, and Closed, and allow status changes only when the required preceding business conditions are met.
  • FR-003: The system MUST maintain room inventory with room number, room type, nightly rate, and current occupancy indicator.
  • FR-004: The system MUST prevent assigning a room to any reservation whose stay dates overlap an existing active reservation for the same room.
  • FR-005: The system MUST reject reservations where the check-out date is not later than the check-in date.
  • FR-006: The system MUST allow authorized front desk users to capture deposit payment results for a reservation.
  • FR-007: The system MUST store whether a deposit was successfully captured and MUST NOT treat a failed payment attempt as a successful deposit.
  • FR-008: The system MUST apply successfully captured deposit amounts against the final guest bill.
  • FR-009: The system MUST allow authorized front desk users to record billable service charge lines linked to a reservation, including services such as breakfast, laundry, and transfer.
  • FR-010: The system MUST include all billable service charges linked to a reservation in the final bill.
  • FR-011: The system MUST allow authorized front desk users to check in guests only for confirmed reservations with an assigned available room and valid deposit state.
  • FR-012: The system MUST mark the reservation as occupied and the assigned room as occupied when check-in completes.
  • FR-013: The system MUST allow authorized front desk users to check out occupied guests and release the assigned room from occupancy.
  • FR-014: The system MUST allow authorized finance users to generate an invoice for a checked-out reservation.
  • FR-015: The system MUST prevent more than one invoice from being generated for the same reservation and preserve the first invoice reference.
  • FR-016: The system MUST calculate VAT on invoiceable amounts using the configured VAT rate effective at invoice calculation time.
  • FR-017: The system MUST support VAT recalculation before invoice posting when tax configuration changes after reservation creation.
  • FR-018: The system MUST include room-night charges, service charges, VAT, deposit application, and remaining amount due in the final invoice calculation.
  • FR-019: The system MUST allow authorized finance users to post and reconcile final payments against the remaining amount due.
  • FR-020: The system MUST allow authorized finance users to review reservation, invoice, payment, refund, and ledger outcomes for audit and reconciliation.
  • FR-021: The system MUST restrict refund processing to finance users.
  • FR-022: The system MUST block front desk users from processing refunds, modifying tax setup, or directly adjusting finance postings.
  • FR-023: The system MUST allow admin users to configure setup, manage room inventory master data, and manage permissions.
  • FR-024: The system MUST preserve traceability from reservation to assigned room, deposit result, service charges, invoice, payments, refunds, and final status.

Key Entities

  • Reservation: Represents a guest stay request and lifecycle state; includes reservation number, customer number, assigned room number, check-in date, check-out date, status, deposit amount, deposit result, invoice number, and related billing state.
  • Room: Represents a room available for sale; includes room number, room type, nightly rate, and occupancy state.
  • Service Charge: Represents a billable service consumed during a stay; includes reservation link, service type, amount, and billing inclusion state.
  • Invoice: Represents the final bill for a reservation; includes room charges, service charges, VAT, deposit application, remaining amount due, and invoice reference.
  • Payment: Represents deposit or final settlement activity; includes reservation or invoice context, amount, outcome, and reconciliation state.
  • Refund: Represents a finance-authorized return of funds; includes reservation and invoice context, amount, authorization role, and resulting financial effect.
  • VAT Configuration: Represents the active tax rate used to calculate VAT before invoice posting.
  • User Role: Represents operational, finance, and administrative permissions for front desk, finance, and admin users.

Success Criteria (mandatory)

Measurable Outcomes

  • SC-001: Front desk users can create a valid reservation, assign an available room, capture deposit outcome, and complete check-in in under 3 minutes during standard operation.
  • SC-002: In 100% of tested overlapping-date cases, the same room cannot be assigned to more than one active reservation.
  • SC-003: In 100% of failed payment simulations, failed deposits and final payments do not mark the reservation or invoice as paid.
  • SC-004: Finance users can generate a complete invoice for a checked-out reservation in under 2 minutes, including room charges, service charges, VAT, deposit application, and remaining balance.
  • SC-005: In 100% of duplicate invoice attempts, the system prevents a second invoice for the same reservation.
  • SC-006: VAT amounts on generated invoices match the configured rate at invoice calculation time in 100% of tax validation test cases.
  • SC-007: Front desk refund attempts are blocked in 100% of authorization tests and create no financial effect.
  • SC-008: At least 95% of pilot operational users can complete their assigned reservation, checkout, billing, or payment tasks without support after standard training.

Assumptions

  • A reservation requires an existing customer before it can be confirmed.
  • Room charges are calculated from nightly rate multiplied by the number of stay nights.
  • Confirmed and occupied reservations are treated as active for overbooking prevention; closed reservations preserve history but no longer reserve occupancy.
  • Deposit capture is recorded as an outcome from an approved payment process, with failed attempts retained for visibility but not applied to the bill.
  • Service charges are entered as positive billable amounts and are included unless explicitly corrected before invoicing by an authorized user.
  • VAT is calculated before invoice posting, and posted invoices preserve their financial result after posting.
  • Refunds require an existing billed or paid financial context and are performed only by finance users.
  • Admin users manage setup and master data but do not replace finance authorization for refunds or financial reconciliation.
plan.md118 lines

Implementation Plan: Hotel Reservation Lifecycle

Branch: 001-hotel-reservation-lifecycle | Date: 2026-06-13 | Spec: spec.md

Input: Feature specification from specs/001-hotel-reservation-lifecycle/spec.md

Summary

Build a Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central AL extension for the hotel reservation lifecycle: reservations, room assignment, deposits, check-in, service charges, checkout, invoice generation, VAT calculation, final payment, and refund authorization. The implementation will add hotel-specific tables and pages under src/, orchestrate business rules through AL codeunits, integrate billing through standard sales invoice and VAT behavior, and enforce role separation through permission sets and action-level validation.

Technical Context

Language/Version: AL, runtime 16.0, application 27.0.0.0

Primary Dependencies: Business Central base/application symbols from .alpackages/: Microsoft_Application_27.0.38460.40691.app, Microsoft_Base Application_27.0.38460.40691.app, Microsoft_Business Foundation_27.0.38460.40586.app, Microsoft_System Application_27.0.38460.40586.app, System.app

Storage: Business Central tenant database through AL tables owned by this extension, with standard customer, sales, VAT, and ledger records referenced where available in the loaded symbols

Testing: AL test codeunits and Business Central Test Tool scenarios for reservation lifecycle, overbooking, duplicate invoice prevention, VAT calculation, payment failure handling, and role-based authorization

Target Platform: Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central application 27.0.0.0 / runtime 16.0

Project Type: Business Central AL extension

Performance Goals: Reservation entry and check-in complete within 3 minutes for trained front desk users; invoice generation completes within 2 minutes for standard reservations; overlapping room checks complete before assignment is committed

Constraints: Use object IDs only in 50000..50249; do not modify app.json or .alpackages/; place all AL source under src/; extend Microsoft base objects only with tableextension or pageextension; do not use syntax or APIs newer than runtime 16.0 and application 27.0.0.0

Scale/Scope: Single extension module covering hotel setup, room inventory, reservation lifecycle, service charges, sales invoice integration, payment/refund tracking, and permissions for Front Desk, Finance, and Admin roles

Constitution Check

GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.

The constitution file contains only template placeholders and defines no enforceable project principles, quality gates, or workflow constraints. Gate status: PASS.

Post-design re-check: PASS. The design remains within the supplied AL project constraints, keeps all planned source files under src/, avoids modifying preconfigured metadata and symbol packages, and uses only object IDs in the configured range.

Project Structure
