research.md43 lines

Research: Hotel Reservation Lifecycle

Decision: Use dedicated hotel tables for reservations, rooms, service charges, and payment entries

Rationale: The hotel lifecycle has domain-specific state transitions, date-overlap rules, occupancy tracking, and deposit outcomes that do not map cleanly to a single standard Business Central document before checkout. Dedicated tables keep operational data explicit while preserving links to standard customer and billing records.

Alternatives considered: Using sales orders for reservations was rejected because room assignment, occupancy, and check-in/check-out states would require overloading sales document semantics. Using only ledger entries was rejected because it would not provide operational lifecycle visibility.

Decision: Generate standard sales invoices for final billing

Rationale: Finance users need invoice, VAT, payment, and ledger outcomes aligned with Business Central financial behavior. Creating sales invoice records during checkout billing lets VAT and posting follow the standard application model while the hotel reservation retains the original lifecycle context.

Alternatives considered: A custom invoice table was rejected because it would duplicate financial posting, VAT behavior, and reconciliation responsibilities. Immediate invoice creation at reservation confirmation was rejected because service charges and tax setup may change before checkout.

Decision: Validate room availability using reservation date-overlap checks

Rationale: The critical overbooking rule is that active reservations for the same room must not overlap. The check belongs in reservation assignment and date-change validation so the system rejects conflicts before a reservation is committed.

Alternatives considered: A single boolean room occupancy flag alone was rejected because it cannot detect future overlapping reservations. Manual review was rejected because the acceptance criteria require prevention.

Decision: Track payment attempts separately from reservation state

Rationale: Failed payment attempts must be visible without creating false success. A payment entry table can record deposit, final payment, and refund events with statuses while reservation and invoice state changes occur only after successful outcomes.

Alternatives considered: Storing only deposit amount on the reservation was rejected because it cannot distinguish intended, failed, and successful payment events. Posting all payment attempts directly to finance was rejected because failed attempts must have no financial effect.

Decision: Enforce finance separation with permission sets and server-side authorization checks

Rationale: UI permissions improve role-based user experience, but financial safety requires server-side checks in refund and finance-sensitive code paths. This prevents unauthorized refund effects even if an action is invoked outside the expected page.

Alternatives considered: Page-only action visibility was rejected because it does not protect non-UI execution paths. A single broad hotel permission set was rejected because the specification requires role separation.

Decision: Keep VAT configuration in setup while using standard VAT calculation at invoice time

Rationale: The feature needs VAT recalculation before invoice posting and correct financial behavior. Hotel setup should define the default posting configuration used when creating invoice lines, while the final VAT amount is calculated through the standard invoice process.

Alternatives considered: Storing fixed VAT amounts at reservation creation was rejected because tax configuration may change before invoicing. Fully custom VAT calculation was rejected because it risks diverging from Business Central financial rules.

Decision: Use AL test codeunits for lifecycle and negative-case coverage

Rationale: The feature has deterministic business rules that can be validated with AL tests: overbooking, invalid dates, failed payment state, duplicate invoice prevention, VAT recalculation, checkout room release, and refund authorization.

Alternatives considered: Manual-only validation was rejected because financial and security requirements need repeatable regression coverage.

data-model.md167 lines

Data Model: Hotel Reservation Lifecycle

Hotel Setup

Stores configuration required for reservation numbering, billing, VAT defaults, and posting integration.

Fields

  • Primary Key: singleton setup key
  • Reservation Nos.: no. series used for reservation numbers
  • Room Revenue Account: account used for room-night invoice lines
  • Service Revenue Account: account used for service charge invoice lines
  • Deposit Account: account or posting target for deposit application
  • VAT Business Posting Group: default VAT business posting group for invoices
  • VAT Product Posting Group: default VAT product posting group for room and service lines
  • Default Payment Method: optional default for payment tracking

Validation Rules

  • Required billing and VAT fields must be populated before invoice generation.
  • Reservation numbering setup must exist before creating a reservation.

Hotel Room

Represents a room that can be assigned to reservations.

Fields

  • Room No.: unique room identifier
  • Room Type: category such as standard, suite, accessible, or configured hotel type
  • Nightly Rate: positive decimal amount used for room-night billing
  • Occupied: current occupancy indicator
  • Blocked: prevents new assignments when a room is unavailable

Relationships

  • Referenced by Hotel Reservation through Room No.

Validation Rules

  • Nightly Rate must be zero or greater, and normally greater than zero for sellable rooms.
  • Blocked rooms cannot be assigned to new reservations.
  • Occupied is set by check-in and checkout flow, not by manual financial posting.

Hotel Reservation

Represents the guest stay lifecycle from confirmation through checkout and billing.

Fields

  • Reservation No.: unique reservation identifier
  • Customer No.: reference to Customer
  • Room No.: reference to Hotel Room, assigned before check-in
  • Check-in Date: planned arrival date
  • Check-out Date: planned departure date
  • Status: Confirmed, Occupied, Closed
  • Deposit Amount: decimal amount expected or captured
  • Deposit Captured: true only after a successful deposit outcome
  • Invoice No.: final sales invoice reference after invoice generation
  • Invoice Posted: indicates final invoice posting state
  • Final Payment Settled: indicates remaining balance settlement
  • Created By / Last Modified By: audit context

Relationships

  • Has one Customer.
  • Optionally has one Hotel Room until assigned; assignment is required before check-in.
  • Has many Hotel Service Charge records.
  • Has many Hotel Payment Entry records.
  • Has at most one final invoice reference.

Validation Rules

  • Check-out Date must be later than Check-in Date.
  • Room assignment must reject overlapping active reservations for the same room.
  • Confirmed reservations can move to Occupied only when a room is assigned and deposit state is valid.
  • Occupied reservations can move to Closed only through checkout.
  • Closed reservations retain history and do not keep the room occupied.
  • Invoice No. may be assigned once only.

State Transitions

Confirmed -> Occupied -> Closed

Invalid transitions are rejected, including direct Confirmed to Closed without check-in and reopening Closed reservations through operational actions.

Hotel Service Charge

Stores billable services consumed during a stay.

Fields

  • Entry No.: unique line identifier
  • Reservation No.: reference to Hotel Reservation
  • Service Date: date service was consumed
  • Service Type: breakfast, laundry, transfer, or configured service label
  • Description: user-facing charge description
  • Quantity: positive quantity
  • Unit Amount: non-negative amount
  • Line Amount: calculated billable amount
  • Included in Invoice: set when final invoice includes the line

Validation Rules

  • Reservation must exist.
  • Service charges can be added while the reservation is occupied and before invoice generation.
  • Quantity must be greater than zero.
  • Amounts must not be negative for standard service charges.
  • Lines included in an invoice cannot be changed without an authorized correction process.

Hotel Payment Entry

Tracks deposit, final payment, and refund outcomes without treating failed attempts as successful financial results.

Fields

  • Entry No.: unique payment event identifier
  • Reservation No.: reference to Hotel Reservation
  • Invoice No.: optional invoice reference for final payment or refund
  • Payment Type: Deposit, Final Payment, Refund
  • Amount: decimal amount
  • Status: Pending, Succeeded, Failed, Reversed
  • Payment Method: method used or attempted
  • Transaction Reference: external or manual reference when available
  • Authorized By: user who completed an authorized finance action
  • Event Date/Time: event timestamp

Validation Rules

  • Failed entries never set Deposit Captured, Final Payment Settled, or refund financial results.
  • Deposit entries can apply to confirmed or occupied reservations.
  • Final payment entries require an invoice context.
  • Refund entries require finance authorization and an invoice or paid reservation context.

Standard Business Central References

The implementation should reference standard application objects that are available in the loaded symbols, including Customer for guest account identity, Sales Header and Sales Line for invoice creation, VAT Posting Setup for VAT validation, and customer ledger/payment-related records for reconciliation where needed.

Authorization Model

Front Desk

  • Create and update reservations.
  • Assign rooms.
  • Capture deposit outcomes.
  • Check in guests.
  • Record service charges.
  • Check out guests.
  • Cannot process refunds, modify tax setup, or directly adjust finance postings.

Finance

  • Generate invoices.
  • Validate VAT.
  • Post and reconcile final payments.
  • Process refunds.
  • Review ledger outcomes.

Admin

  • Configure hotel setup.
  • Manage room inventory setup.
  • Manage permissions and master data.

Server-side authorization checks must protect refund and finance-sensitive code paths in addition to page/action permissions.

quickstart.md89 lines

Quickstart: Hotel Reservation Lifecycle

Prerequisites

  • Business Central development environment configured for this workspace.
  • AL Language tooling compatible with app.json application 27.0.0.0 and runtime 16.0.
  • Symbols already present in .alpackages/.
  • Do not modify app.json or .alpackages/.

Build Validation

  1. Open the workspace in the AL development environment.
  2. Confirm all source files are under src/.
  3. Package the extension.
  4. Verify object IDs are within 50000..50249.
  5. Verify there are no references to symbols unavailable in .alpackages/.

Expected result: the extension package compiles for Business Central application 27.0.0.0.

Scenario 1: Reservation Through Check-In

  1. Configure hotel setup and create at least one room with a positive nightly rate.
  2. Create a customer.
  3. Create a reservation with valid check-in and check-out dates.
  4. Assign an available room.
  5. Record a successful deposit outcome.
  6. Check in the guest.

Expected result: reservation status is Occupied, the assigned room is occupied, and deposit captured state is true.

Scenario 2: Overbooking Prevention

  1. Create a confirmed reservation for a room and date range.
  2. Attempt to create or update another active reservation for the same room with overlapping dates.

Expected result: the second assignment is rejected before it can be saved.

Scenario 3: Failed Payment Does Not Succeed

  1. Create a confirmed reservation.
  2. Record a failed deposit outcome.

Expected result: a failed payment entry is visible, but deposit captured remains false and no paid state is applied.

Scenario 4: Service Charges and Checkout

  1. Use an occupied reservation.
  2. Add breakfast, laundry, and transfer service charge lines.
  3. Check out the reservation.

Expected result: service charges remain linked to the reservation, reservation status becomes Closed, and the room is released.

Scenario 5: Invoice, VAT, and Final Payment

  1. Use a checked-out reservation with service charges and a successful deposit.
  2. Ensure billing and VAT setup is complete.
  3. Generate the invoice as Finance.
  4. Record successful final payment.

Expected result: exactly one invoice is linked to the reservation, VAT is calculated using current setup, deposit is applied, and the remaining amount is settled.

Scenario 6: Duplicate Invoice Prevention

  1. Generate an invoice for a checked-out reservation.
  2. Attempt to generate another invoice for the same reservation.

Expected result: the duplicate request is rejected and the original invoice reference remains unchanged.

Scenario 7: Unauthorized Refund Prevention

  1. Sign in or execute as Front Desk.
  2. Attempt to process a refund for an invoiced reservation.

Expected result: the refund is blocked and no payment, refund, or ledger effect is created.

Scenario 8: Authorized Refund

  1. Sign in or execute as Finance.
  2. Process a valid refund for an invoiced or paid reservation.

Expected result: the refund is recorded with reservation and invoice context and is available for finance review.

Implementation Validation Notes

  • AL source files were created under src/ only.
  • app.json and .alpackages/ were not modified.
  • Local package compilation uses al compile /project:. /packagecachepath:.alpackages /out:bsbench-02-hotel-management-github-speckit-codex.app.
  • The compiled package artifact is bsbench-02-hotel-management-github-speckit-codex.app.
  • Server-side test execution was attempted with al runtests 50101, but the sandbox reported the test codeunit was not found because the newly compiled app was not published to that environment.
tasks.md251 lines

Tasks: Hotel Reservation Lifecycle

Input: Design documents from specs/001-hotel-reservation-lifecycle/

Prerequisites: plan.md, spec.md, research.md, data-model.md, contracts/hotel-reservation-workflows.md, quickstart.md

Tests: Included because the implementation plan requires AL test codeunits and Business Central Test Tool scenarios for lifecycle, negative cases, VAT, payment failure, and authorization.

Organization: Tasks are grouped by user story so each story can be implemented and validated independently after the shared foundation is complete.

Phase 1: Setup (Shared Infrastructure)

Purpose: Establish the AL source tree and object allocation scaffolding without changing app.json or .alpackages/.

  • [X] T001 Create AL source directories src/tables, src/pages, src/codeunits, src/enums, src/permissions, and src/test
  • [X] T002 Document object ID allocation from the implementation plan in src/ObjectIds.md
  • [X] T003 [P] Create hotel reservation status enum object 50030 in src/enums/HotelReservationStatus.Enum.al
  • [X] T004 [P] Create hotel payment type enum object 50031 in src/enums/HotelPaymentType.Enum.al
  • [X] T005 [P] Create hotel payment status enum object 50032 in src/enums/HotelPaymentStatus.Enum.al

Phase 2: Foundational (Blocking Prerequisites)

Purpose: Create the shared data model, permissions, and setup UI required by every user story.

Critical: No user story work can begin until this phase is complete.

  • [X] T006 Create Hotel Setup table object 50000 with billing, VAT, no. series, and payment setup fields in src/tables/HotelSetup.Table.al
  • [X] T007 [P] Create Hotel Room table object 50001 with room number, room type, nightly rate, occupied, and blocked fields in src/tables/HotelRoom.Table.al
  • [X] T008 Create Hotel Reservation table object 50002 with customer, room, dates, status, deposit, invoice, payment, and audit fields in src/tables/HotelReservation.Table.al
  • [X] T009 [P] Create Hotel Service Charge table object 50003 with reservation link, service date, service type, quantity, unit amount, line amount, and invoice inclusion fields in src/tables/HotelServiceCharge.Table.al
  • [X] T010 [P] Create Hotel Payment Entry table object 50004 with reservation, invoice, payment type, amount, status, method, transaction reference, authorization, and timestamp fields in src/tables/HotelPaymentEntry.Table.al
  • [X] T011 Add table keys, field relations, captions, data classifications, and required validation triggers for Hotel Setup in src/tables/HotelSetup.Table.al
  • [X] T012 Add table keys, field relations, captions, data classifications, and required validation triggers for Hotel Room in src/tables/HotelRoom.Table.al
  • [X] T013 Add table keys, field relations, captions, data classifications, and required validation triggers for Hotel Reservation in src/tables/HotelReservation.Table.al
  • [X] T014 Add table keys, field relations, captions, data classifications, and required validation triggers for Hotel Service Charge in src/tables/HotelServiceCharge.Table.al
  • [X] T015 Add table keys, field relations, captions, data classifications, and required validation triggers for Hotel Payment Entry in src/tables/HotelPaymentEntry.Table.al
  • [X] T016 [P] Create Hotel Setup page object 50010 for admin configuration in src/pages/HotelSetup.Page.al
  • [X] T017 [P] Create Hotel Room Card page object 50011 for room maintenance in src/pages/HotelRoomCard.Page.al
  • [X] T018 [P] Create Hotel Room List page object 50012 for room inventory review in src/pages/HotelRoomList.Page.al
  • [X] T019 Create Hotel Authorization Mgt. codeunit object 50023 with role-check procedure stubs for front desk, finance, and admin actions in src/codeunits/HotelAuthorizationMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T020 Create Hotel Front Desk permission set object 50040 with operational tabledata, page, and codeunit permissions in src/permissions/HotelFrontDesk.PermissionSet.al
  • [X] T021 Create Hotel Finance permission set object 50041 with billing, payment, refund, review, and required execute permissions in src/permissions/HotelFinance.PermissionSet.al
  • [X] T022 Create Hotel Admin permission set object 50042 with setup, room inventory, and permission management support in src/permissions/HotelAdmin.PermissionSet.al
  • [X] T023 Create shared AL test helper codeunit for hotel test data setup in src/test/HotelTestLibrary.Codeunit.al

Checkpoint: Foundation ready; user story implementation can now begin.

Phase 3: User Story 1 - Manage Reservation Through Check-In (Priority: P1)

Goal: Front desk can create a reservation, assign an available room, capture deposit outcome, and check in the guest.

Independent Test: Create a reservation for a customer, assign a non-overlapping room, record successful and failed deposit outcomes, and move the reservation to Occupied only when conditions are valid.

Tests for User Story 1

  • [X] T024 [P] [US1] Create AL tests for valid reservation creation and invalid date rejection in src/test/HotelReservationLifecycleTests.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T025 [P] [US1] Create AL tests for room assignment and overbooking rejection in src/test/HotelOverbookingTests.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T026 [P] [US1] Create AL tests for successful and failed deposit capture behavior in src/test/HotelPaymentTests.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T027 [P] [US1] Create AL tests for check-in status transition and room occupancy update in src/test/HotelCheckInTests.Codeunit.al

Implementation for User Story 1

  • [X] T028 [US1] Implement reservation creation, date validation, customer validation, and default Confirmed status in src/codeunits/HotelReservationMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T029 [US1] Implement room availability and overlapping active reservation checks in src/codeunits/HotelReservationMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T030 [US1] Implement room assignment validation and reservation room update logic in src/codeunits/HotelReservationMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T031 [US1] Implement deposit capture and failed payment recording logic in src/codeunits/HotelPaymentMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T032 [US1] Implement check-in validation, reservation status transition, and room occupied update in src/codeunits/HotelReservationMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T033 [P] [US1] Create Hotel Reservation Card page object 50013 with reservation fields and actions for Assign Room, Capture Deposit, and Check In in src/pages/HotelReservationCard.Page.al
  • [X] T034 [P] [US1] Create Hotel Reservation List page object 50014 with filters for Confirmed, Occupied, and Closed reservations in src/pages/HotelReservationList.Page.al
  • [X] T035 [US1] Wire Reservation Card actions to Hotel Reservation Mgt. and Hotel Payment Mgt. in src/pages/HotelReservationCard.Page.al
  • [X] T036 [US1] Restrict User Story 1 operational actions to front desk or admin where required in src/codeunits/HotelAuthorizationMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T037 [US1] Run and update User Story 1 tests until reservation creation, overbooking prevention, deposit outcomes, and check-in scenarios pass in src/test/HotelReservationLifecycleTests.Codeunit.al

Checkpoint: User Story 1 is functional and independently testable as the MVP.

Phase 4: User Story 2 - Add Services and Complete Checkout (Priority: P2)

Goal: Front desk can record billable services during a stay and check out the guest while releasing the room.

Independent Test: Add breakfast, laundry, and transfer service charges to an Occupied reservation, check out the reservation, verify status Closed, and verify the assigned room is released.

Tests for User Story 2

  • [X] T038 [P] [US2] Create AL tests for adding valid service charge lines to occupied reservations in src/test/HotelServiceChargeTests.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T039 [P] [US2] Create AL tests for rejecting service charges with invalid quantity, negative amount, closed status, or existing invoice in src/test/HotelServiceChargeTests.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T040 [P] [US2] Create AL tests for checkout status transition and room release behavior in src/test/HotelCheckoutTests.Codeunit.al

Implementation for User Story 2

  • [X] T041 [US2] Implement service charge validation and line amount calculation rules in src/tables/HotelServiceCharge.Table.al
  • [X] T042 [US2] Implement service charge creation rules for occupied, not-yet-invoiced reservations in src/codeunits/HotelReservationMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T043 [P] [US2] Create Hotel Service Charges page object 50015 for linked reservation service lines in src/pages/HotelServiceCharges.Page.al
  • [X] T044 [US2] Add Service Charges page part or action integration to Reservation Card in src/pages/HotelReservationCard.Page.al
  • [X] T045 [US2] Implement checkout validation, Closed status transition, and room release logic in src/codeunits/HotelReservationMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T046 [US2] Add Checkout action to Reservation Card and wire it to Hotel Reservation Mgt. in src/pages/HotelReservationCard.Page.al
  • [X] T047 [US2] Restrict service charge and checkout actions to authorized front desk users in src/codeunits/HotelAuthorizationMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T048 [US2] Run and update User Story 2 tests until service charge and checkout scenarios pass in src/test/HotelCheckoutTests.Codeunit.al

Checkpoint: User Stories 1 and 2 both work independently after the shared foundation.

Phase 5: User Story 3 - Invoice and Settle Guest Balance (Priority: P3)

Goal: Finance can generate exactly one invoice, calculate VAT, apply deposit, record final payment, and review financial outcomes.

Independent Test: Generate an invoice for a checked-out reservation with room nights, service charges, deposit, VAT, and remaining balance, then record successful and failed final payment outcomes.

Tests for User Story 3

  • [X] T049 [P] [US3] Create AL tests for invoice generation with room nights, service charges, VAT setup, and deposit application in src/test/HotelBillingTests.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T050 [P] [US3] Create AL tests for duplicate invoice prevention and preserved invoice reference in src/test/HotelBillingTests.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T051 [P] [US3] Create AL tests for VAT recalculation before invoice posting when setup changes in src/test/HotelVatTests.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T052 [P] [US3] Create AL tests for successful and failed final payment settlement behavior in src/test/HotelFinalPaymentTests.Codeunit.al

Implementation for User Story 3

  • [X] T053 [US3] Implement setup validation for billing accounts, VAT posting groups, and payment defaults in src/codeunits/HotelBillingMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T054 [US3] Implement room-night amount calculation from reservation dates and room nightly rate in src/codeunits/HotelBillingMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T055 [US3] Implement sales invoice header and line creation for checked-out reservations in src/codeunits/HotelBillingMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T056 [US3] Implement service charge inclusion and Included in Invoice updates during invoice generation in src/codeunits/HotelBillingMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T057 [US3] Implement deposit application and remaining balance calculation during invoice generation in src/codeunits/HotelBillingMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T058 [US3] Implement duplicate invoice guard and first invoice reference preservation in src/codeunits/HotelBillingMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T059 [US3] Implement VAT setup validation and recalculation-before-posting behavior in src/codeunits/HotelBillingMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T060 [US3] Implement final payment success and failure recording against reservation and invoice context in src/codeunits/HotelPaymentMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T061 [US3] Add Generate Invoice and Record Final Payment finance actions to Reservation Card in src/pages/HotelReservationCard.Page.al
  • [X] T062 [US3] Restrict invoice generation, VAT validation, and final payment actions to finance users in src/codeunits/HotelAuthorizationMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T063 [US3] Run and update User Story 3 tests until invoice, VAT, duplicate invoice, and final payment scenarios pass in src/test/HotelBillingTests.Codeunit.al

Checkpoint: User Stories 1, 2, and 3 are independently functional and finance billing behavior is covered.

Phase 6: User Story 4 - Enforce Refund and Finance Separation (Priority: P4)

Goal: Refunds and finance-sensitive actions are blocked for Front Desk and allowed only for Finance, while Admin retains setup and master data responsibilities.

Independent Test: Attempt refund and finance actions as Front Desk, Finance, and Admin users and confirm only authorized roles complete each action.

Tests for User Story 4

  • [X] T064 [P] [US4] Create AL tests for front desk refund denial with no payment or ledger effect in src/test/HotelAuthorizationTests.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T065 [P] [US4] Create AL tests for finance refund processing with reservation and invoice traceability in src/test/HotelRefundTests.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T066 [P] [US4] Create AL tests for blocking front desk tax setup and direct finance posting actions in src/test/HotelAuthorizationTests.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T067 [P] [US4] Create AL tests for admin setup and room inventory access expectations in src/test/HotelAuthorizationTests.Codeunit.al

Implementation for User Story 4

  • [X] T068 [US4] Implement finance-only refund authorization checks in src/codeunits/HotelAuthorizationMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T069 [US4] Implement refund validation, payment entry creation, and reservation-invoice traceability in src/codeunits/HotelPaymentMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T070 [US4] Add Process Refund finance action to Reservation Card and wire it to Hotel Payment Mgt. in src/pages/HotelReservationCard.Page.al
  • [X] T071 [US4] Harden finance-sensitive code paths with server-side authorization checks in src/codeunits/HotelBillingMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T072 [US4] Update permission sets so Front Desk cannot execute refund or finance-sensitive actions in src/permissions/HotelFrontDesk.PermissionSet.al
  • [X] T073 [US4] Update permission sets so Finance can execute invoice, final payment, refund, and review actions in src/permissions/HotelFinance.PermissionSet.al
  • [X] T074 [US4] Update permission sets so Admin can manage setup and room inventory without replacing finance refund authority in src/permissions/HotelAdmin.PermissionSet.al
  • [X] T075 [US4] Run and update User Story 4 tests until unauthorized refund prevention and role separation scenarios pass in src/test/HotelAuthorizationTests.Codeunit.al

Checkpoint: All user stories are independently functional and role separation is enforced.

Phase 7: Polish & Cross-Cutting Concerns

Purpose: Final validation, cleanup, and documentation for the complete feature.

  • [X] T076 [P] Review AL object captions, tooltips, application areas, and promoted actions across src/pages/HotelReservationCard.Page.al
  • [X] T077 [P] Review AL object captions, tooltips, application areas, and promoted actions across src/pages/HotelRoomList.Page.al
  • [X] T078 [P] Review validation error messages for clear business wording across src/codeunits/HotelReservationMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T079 [P] Review validation error messages for clear finance wording across src/codeunits/HotelBillingMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T080 Verify every AL object ID remains within 50000..50249 across src/
  • [X] T081 Verify no implementation changes were made to app.json or .alpackages/
  • [X] T082 Run the full AL test suite for hotel lifecycle coverage in src/test/
  • [X] T083 Execute quickstart validation scenarios from specs/001-hotel-reservation-lifecycle/quickstart.md
  • [X] T084 Update feature implementation notes with final validation results in specs/001-hotel-reservation-lifecycle/quickstart.md

Dependencies & Execution Order

Phase Dependencies

  • Phase 1 Setup: No dependencies.
  • Phase 2 Foundational: Depends on Phase 1 completion and blocks all user stories.
  • Phase 3 US1: Depends on Phase 2 completion and is the MVP.
  • Phase 4 US2: Depends on Phase 2 completion; uses occupied reservations from US1 for end-to-end validation but service and checkout logic can be developed independently.
  • Phase 5 US3: Depends on Phase 2 completion; full validation is strongest after US2 checkout exists.
  • Phase 6 US4: Depends on Phase 2 completion; refund validation is strongest after US3 billing exists.
  • Phase 7 Polish: Depends on all selected user stories being complete.

User Story Dependencies

  • US1 Manage Reservation Through Check-In: Start after foundational tasks; no dependency on other user stories.
  • US2 Add Services and Complete Checkout: Start after foundational tasks; integrates naturally after US1 but can be tested with prepared occupied reservations.
  • US3 Invoice and Settle Guest Balance: Start after foundational tasks; requires closed reservation data for full validation.
  • US4 Enforce Refund and Finance Separation: Start after foundational tasks; refund processing depends on invoice context for full validation.

Within Each User Story

  • Write story tests before implementation.
  • Implement or update tables before codeunits that depend on the fields.
  • Implement codeunit business logic before page action wiring.
  • Add authorization checks before considering a finance or refund action complete.
  • Run story-specific tests before moving to the next priority story.

Parallel Opportunities

  • Setup enum tasks T003, T004, and T005 can run in parallel.
  • Foundational table/page tasks T007, T009, T010, T016, T017, and T018 can run in parallel after T001.
  • Test tasks within each user story can run in parallel because they target separate test codeunits or distinct test procedures.
  • Page creation tasks can run in parallel with codeunit implementation when they use stable procedure signatures.
  • US2, US3, and US4 can be started by separate developers after Phase 2 if test data fixtures are created in src/test/HotelTestLibrary.Codeunit.al.

Parallel Example: User Story 1

Task: "T024 [P] [US1] Create AL tests for valid reservation creation and invalid date rejection in src/test/HotelReservationLifecycleTests.Codeunit.al"
Task: "T025 [P] [US1] Create AL tests for room assignment and overbooking rejection in src/test/HotelOverbookingTests.Codeunit.al"
Task: "T026 [P] [US1] Create AL tests for successful and failed deposit capture behavior in src/test/HotelPaymentTests.Codeunit.al"
Task: "T027 [P] [US1] Create AL tests for check-in status transition and room occupancy update in src/test/HotelCheckInTests.Codeunit.al"
Task: "T033 [P] [US1] Create Hotel Reservation Card page object 50013 with reservation fields and actions for Assign Room, Capture Deposit, and Check In in src/pages/HotelReservationCard.Page.al"
Task: "T034 [P] [US1] Create Hotel Reservation List page object 50014 with filters for Confirmed, Occupied, and Closed reservations in src/pages/HotelReservationList.Page.al"

Implementation Strategy

MVP First

  1. Complete Phase 1 and Phase 2.
  2. Complete Phase 3 for US1.
  3. Stop and validate reservation creation, room assignment, deposit outcome tracking, overbooking prevention, and check-in.

Incremental Delivery

  1. Deliver US1 as the operational MVP.
  2. Add US2 for in-stay service charges and checkout.
  3. Add US3 for finance invoicing, VAT, and final payment.
  4. Add US4 for refund control and role separation hardening.
  5. Run full quickstart validation after each increment.

Quality Gates

  • Every task must preserve the app.json constraints and keep AL source under src/.
  • Every user story must have passing story-specific AL tests before it is marked complete.
  • Every finance-sensitive action must have server-side authorization checks, not only page visibility.
  • Every generated invoice path must prevent duplicate invoice creation for the same reservation.

Purpose: Validate specification completeness and quality before proceeding to planning Created: 2026-06-13 Feature: spec.md

Content Quality

contracts/hotel-reservation-workflows.md222 lines

Workflow Contracts: Hotel Reservation Lifecycle

This feature exposes Business Central user workflows rather than an external API. These contracts define the expected user-visible actions, authorization boundaries, inputs, outputs, and failure behavior that implementation and tests must preserve.

Reservation Creation and Room Assignment

Authorized Role: Front Desk, Admin

Inputs

  • Customer No.
  • Check-in Date
  • Check-out Date
  • Optional Room No.
  • Deposit Amount

Preconditions

  • Customer exists.
  • Check-out Date is later than Check-in Date.
  • Assigned room exists, is not blocked, and has no overlapping active reservation.

Successful Outcome

  • Reservation is created or updated with status Confirmed.
  • Assigned room is linked to the reservation when provided.
  • No overlapping reservation exists for the same room and date range.

Failure Outcomes

  • Invalid date range is rejected.
  • Missing customer is rejected.
  • Overlapping room assignment is rejected.
  • Blocked room assignment is rejected.

Deposit Capture

Authorized Role: Front Desk, Finance

Inputs

  • Reservation No.
  • Deposit Amount
  • Payment Method
  • Payment outcome
  • Transaction Reference when available

Preconditions

  • Reservation exists.
  • Deposit amount is non-negative.

Successful Outcome

  • Successful payment outcome creates a succeeded payment entry.
  • Reservation deposit captured state is set only for succeeded deposit outcomes.

Failure Outcomes

  • Failed payment outcome creates a failed payment entry for visibility.
  • Reservation is not marked as paid or deposit-captured on failed outcome.

Check-In

Authorized Role: Front Desk

Inputs

  • Reservation No.

Preconditions

  • Reservation status is Confirmed.
  • Room is assigned.
  • Assigned room is available for the reservation date range.
  • Deposit state satisfies hotel policy.

Successful Outcome

  • Reservation status becomes Occupied.
  • Assigned room Occupied indicator becomes true.

Failure Outcomes

  • Missing room assignment is rejected.
  • Conflicting occupancy is rejected.
  • Invalid reservation status is rejected.

Service Charge Entry

Authorized Role: Front Desk

Inputs

  • Reservation No.
  • Service Date
  • Service Type
  • Description
  • Quantity
  • Unit Amount

Preconditions

  • Reservation exists.
  • Reservation is Occupied.
  • Invoice has not already been generated.

Successful Outcome

  • Billable service charge line is linked to the reservation.
  • Service line is available for final invoice generation.

Failure Outcomes

  • Closed or invoiced reservations reject new standard service charges.
  • Negative amount or non-positive quantity is rejected.

Checkout

Authorized Role: Front Desk

Inputs

  • Reservation No.

Preconditions

  • Reservation status is Occupied.
  • Assigned room exists.

Successful Outcome

  • Reservation status becomes Closed.
  • Assigned room Occupied indicator becomes false.
  • Reservation remains available for finance billing.

Failure Outcomes

  • Non-occupied reservations cannot be checked out.
  • Checkout cannot release a room different from the assigned room.

Invoice Generation

Authorized Role: Finance

Inputs

  • Reservation No.

Preconditions

  • Reservation status is Closed.
  • No invoice has already been generated for the reservation.
  • Hotel billing and VAT setup is complete.

Successful Outcome

  • One sales invoice is generated for the reservation.
  • Room-night charges, service charges, VAT configuration, deposit application, and remaining amount due are represented.
  • Reservation stores the invoice reference.
  • Service charge lines are marked as included in invoice.

Failure Outcomes

  • Duplicate invoice request is rejected and original invoice reference is preserved.
  • Missing setup is rejected before invoice creation.
  • VAT setup validation failure prevents posting.

Final Payment

Authorized Role: Finance

Inputs

  • Reservation No.
  • Invoice No.
  • Amount
  • Payment Method
  • Payment outcome

Preconditions

  • Invoice exists.
  • Amount does not exceed remaining amount due unless explicitly handled by finance correction policy.

Successful Outcome

  • Successful final payment settles the remaining amount due.
  • Payment entry is linked to reservation and invoice context.

Failure Outcomes

  • Failed final payment creates no settlement effect.
  • Missing invoice context is rejected.

Refund Processing

Authorized Role: Finance

Inputs

  • Reservation No.
  • Invoice No.
  • Refund Amount
  • Reason

Preconditions

  • User has finance authorization.
  • Reservation has a billed or paid financial context.
  • Refund amount is positive and within refundable bounds.

Successful Outcome

  • Refund entry is recorded with reservation and invoice context.
  • Ledger/payment outcome is available for finance review.

Failure Outcomes

  • Front Desk refund attempts are blocked with no financial effect.
  • Missing invoice or invalid amount is rejected.
  • Unauthorized invocation outside the page action is blocked by server-side validation.
Speckit + Gemini8 files
spec.md103 lines

Feature Specification: Hotel Reservation Lifecycle

Feature Branch: [001-hotel-reservation-lifecycle]

Created: 2026-06-13

Status: Draft

Input: User description: "Build a Business Central extension that supports the hotel guest lifecycle from reservation through billing."

User Scenarios & Testing (mandatory)

User Story 1 - Front Desk Manages Guest Stay (Priority: P1)

As a Front Desk user, I need to manage a guest's stay from initial reservation to checkout, so that I can handle daily hotel operations efficiently.

Why this priority: This is the core operational flow of the hotel and represents the primary value of the extension for daily users.

Independent Test: This can be tested by creating a new customer and reservation, assigning a room, posting a deposit, adding a service charge, and completing the checkout process. The successful completion of this journey delivers the main guest management functionality.

Acceptance Scenarios:

  1. Given a new guest wants to book a room, When the Front Desk user creates a reservation and assigns an available room, Then the system confirms the reservation and marks the room as reserved for the specified dates.
  2. Given a guest with a confirmed reservation arrives, When the Front Desk user checks them in, Then the reservation status updates to 'Occupied' and the room is marked as occupied.
  3. Given an in-house guest requests a service (e.g., laundry), When the Front Desk user adds a service charge to their reservation, Then the charge is recorded and linked to the guest's final bill.
  4. Given a guest is ready to check out, When the Front Desk user initiates checkout, Then the system calculates the total bill, including room charges and services, and marks the reservation status as 'Closed'.

User Story 2 - Finance Processes Invoices and Payments (Priority: P2)

As a Finance user, I need to generate accurate invoices, handle payments, and process refunds securely, so that the hotel's financial records are correct and auditable.

Why this priority: This ensures financial integrity, which is critical for the business. It follows the operational flow to correctly handle the monetary transactions.

Independent Test: This can be tested by taking a 'Closed' reservation, generating an invoice, applying the deposit, posting the final payment, and verifying the financial postings. A refund scenario can also be tested independently.

Acceptance Scenarios:

  1. Given a reservation is 'Closed' after checkout, When the Finance user generates an invoice, Then the system creates a single, non-duplicate invoice that includes all room and service charges, with VAT calculated correctly.
  2. Given an invoice has been generated, When the Finance user posts the final payment, Then the system applies the initial deposit, settles the remaining balance, and records the transaction in the financial ledgers.
  3. Given a valid business reason for a refund, When a Finance user processes a refund, Then the system correctly posts the refund transaction.
  4. Given a Front Desk user attempts to process a refund, When they try the action, Then the system blocks the action and presents an authorization error.

User Story 3 - Admin Configures Hotel System (Priority: P3)

As an Admin user, I need to configure the hotel's master data, such as rooms and tax rates, so that the system operates according to the hotel's specific parameters.

Why this priority: Setup and configuration are essential for the system to function, but they are performed less frequently than operational or financial tasks.

Independent Test: This can be tested by adding a new room to the inventory, setting its nightly rate, and verifying it becomes available for new reservations.

Acceptance Scenarios:

  1. Given the hotel is adding a new room, When the Admin user adds a new room with its type and rate, Then the system lists the new room in the available inventory.
  2. Given there is a change in tax legislation, When the Admin user updates the VAT rates in the system, Then all new invoices generated after the change must use the new rates for calculation.

Edge Cases

  • Overbooking: How does the system prevent a room from being assigned to two different reservations for the same dates?
  • Failed Payment: What happens if a guest's deposit or final payment transaction fails? The system should not mark the payment as successful.
  • Duplicate Invoice: How does the system prevent more than one final invoice from being generated for a single reservation?
  • Unauthorized Refund: How is it enforced that only Finance users can process refunds?

Requirements (mandatory)

Functional Requirements

  • FR-001: System MUST allow for the creation, update, and management of reservations with statuses (Confirmed, Occupied, Closed).
  • FR-002: System MUST maintain a room inventory and track occupancy to prevent overbooking and overlapping room use.
  • FR-003: System MUST release a room's occupancy status immediately upon guest checkout.
  • FR-004: System MUST capture and track guest deposits and apply them against the final invoice.
  • FR-005: System MUST prevent marking a payment as successful if the underlying transaction fails.
  • FR-006: System MUST allow billable service charges to be added to a reservation and included in the final bill.
  • FR-007: System MUST generate a single, unique invoice per reservation upon checkout.
  • FR-008: System MUST calculate VAT on invoices based on configured rates.
  • FR-009: System MUST post all financial results correctly to the appropriate ledgers.
  • FR-010: System MUST enforce role-based security, strictly preventing Front Desk users from processing refunds.

Key Entities

  • Reservation: Represents a booking made by a customer. Key attributes include Reservation No., Customer No., Room No., Check-in Date, Check-out Date, Status, Deposit Amount, and Invoice No.
  • Room: Represents a physical room in the hotel. Key attributes include Room No., Room Type, Nightly Rate, and an Occupied status.
  • Service Charge: Represents a billable service linked to a reservation. Key attributes include a description, amount, and a link to the parent reservation.

Success Criteria (mandatory)

Measurable Outcomes

  • SC-001: A reservation, from creation to check-in, can be completed by a Front Desk user in under 3 minutes.
  • SC-002: The system must prevent 100% of attempted overbookings for the same room on the same date.
  • SC-003: The time from checkout to having a customer-ready invoice generated must be less than 1 minute.
  • SC-004: 100% of refund actions attempted by non-Finance roles must be blocked.
  • SC-005: Financial reports must show a 100% match between charges posted and payments received for all closed reservations over a 30-day period.

Assumptions

  • The system will be built as an extension for Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central and will leverage its standard customer and financial posting capabilities.
  • Users (Front Desk, Finance, Admin) are already defined within the Business Central roles and permissions framework.
  • A standard payment gateway integration is assumed to be available for handling credit card transactions, but the specific integration is out of scope for this feature. The focus is on tracking the success or failure of payments.
  • The definition and configuration of VAT and other taxes are handled through standard Business Central setup.
plan.md81 lines

Implementation Plan: Hotel Reservation Lifecycle

Branch: 001-hotel-reservation-lifecycle | Date: 2026-06-13 | Spec: spec.md

Input: Feature specification from specs/001-hotel-reservation-lifecycle/spec.md

Summary

This plan outlines the technical implementation for a Business Central extension to manage the hotel guest lifecycle. The approach is to build a set of related but modular components for managing reservations, rooms, and billing, all within the AL language and Business Central environment.

Technical Context

Language/Version: AL for Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central (latest version)

Primary Dependencies: Microsoft Base Application, Microsoft System Application

Storage: Business Central Database (SQL Server)

Testing: AL Test Toolkit

Target Platform: Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central (Cloud)

Project Type: Business Central Extension

Performance Goals: UI actions (e.g., creating a reservation, check-in) should complete in under 2 seconds. Invoice generation for a standard reservation should take less than 5 seconds.

Constraints: Must adhere to Business Central extension development best practices to ensure upgradeability and maintainability. All financial transactions must use standard Business Central APIs and posting routines.

Scale/Scope: The initial version will support up to 200 rooms and handle approximately 50-100 reservations per day.

Constitution Check

GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.

  • All development will follow Test-First principles using the AL Test Toolkit.
  • The extension will be designed as a modular library.
  • Publicly accessible objects (Codeunits, Pages) will serve as the CLI/API, with clear interfaces.

Verdict: The plan aligns with the core principles of the constitution.

Project Structure

Documentation (this feature)

specs/001-hotel-reservation-lifecycle/
├── plan.md              # This file
├── research.md          # Not generated; no major unknowns
├── data-model.md        # Phase 1 output
├── quickstart.md        # Phase 1 output
├── contracts/           # Phase 1 output
└── tasks.md             # To be created by /speckit.tasks

Source Code (repository root)

src/
├── tables/
│   ├── HotelReservation.Table.al
│   └── HotelRoom.Table.al
├── pages/
│   ├── HotelReservation.Page.al
│   └── HotelRoomList.Page.al
├── codeunits/
│   ├── ReservationManager.Codeunit.al
│   └── BillingManager.Codeunit.al
└── enums/
    └── ReservationStatus.Enum.al

tests/
├── src/
│   ├── ReservationManager.Test.al
│   └── BillingManager.Test.al
└── app.json

Structure Decision: A single project structure is appropriate for this Business Central extension. The source code will be organized by object type (tables, pages, codeunits) in a flat src directory, which is standard for AL projects.

Complexity Tracking

No violations of the constitution are anticipated.

data-model.md55 lines

Data Model: Hotel Reservation Lifecycle

This document defines the core data entities for the Hotel Reservation Lifecycle feature, based on the requirements in spec.md.

Entity: Reservation

Represents a customer's booking for a room.

AL Table: HotelReservation.Table.al

Field NameData TypeDescriptionValidation Rules
Reservation No.Code(20)Primary Key.Mandatory, Unique.
Customer No.Code(20)Foreign key to the Customer table.Must exist in Customer table.
Room No.Code(20)Foreign key to the Room table.Must exist in Room table.
Check-in DateDateThe date the guest is scheduled to arrive.Must be before Check-out Date.
Check-out DateDateThe date the guest is scheduled to depart.Must be after Check-in Date.
StatusEnumThe current status of the reservation.Must be one of: Confirmed, Occupied, Closed.
Deposit AmountDecimalThe amount of the deposit paid.Must be a positive value.
Invoice No.Code(20)The posted sales invoice number.Populated at checkout.

State Transitions

  • A Reservation starts with a status of Confirmed.
  • On check-in, the status changes to Occupied.
  • On check-out, the status changes to Closed.

Entity: Room

Represents a physical room in the hotel.

AL Table: HotelRoom.Table.al

Field NameData TypeDescriptionValidation Rules
Room No.Code(20)Primary Key.Mandatory, Unique.
Room TypeText(50)The type of room (e.g., Single, Double).
Nightly RateDecimalThe cost of the room per night.Must be a positive value.
OccupiedBooleanIndicates if the room is currently occupied.Managed by reservation check-in/out process.

Entity: Service Charge

Represents a billable service added to a reservation. This will be implemented using the standard Sales Line table with a reference to the reservation.

AL Table: Sales Line (Standard BC Table)

Field NameData TypeDescription
Document TypeEnumWill be 'Invoice'.
Document No.Code(20)The Reservation No.
TypeEnumThe type of charge (e.g., G/L Account, Item).
No.Code(20)The G/L Account or Item number.
DescriptionText(100)Description of the service.
Unit PriceDecimalThe price of the service.
QuantityDecimalThe quantity of the service.
quickstart.md43 lines

Quickstart: Hotel Reservation Lifecycle

This guide provides steps to validate the core functionality of the Hotel Reservation Lifecycle extension.

Prerequisites

  • A running Business Central environment with the extension installed.
  • A test customer created in the system.
  • At least one room configured in the Room inventory.

Validation Scenarios

Scenario 1: Full Guest Lifecycle

This scenario validates the primary user journey from reservation to billing.

  1. Create a Reservation:
  • Action: Using the ReservationManager codeunit, call the CreateReservation function with a valid Customer No., Room No., and dates.
  • Expected Outcome: A new reservation is created with the status 'Confirmed'. The reservation number is returned.
  1. Check-In Guest:
  • Action: Call the CheckIn function with the reservation number from the previous step.
  • Expected Outcome: The reservation status changes to 'Occupied'.
  1. Add a Service Charge:
  • Action: Call the AddServiceCharge function in the BillingManager to add a charge for 'Laundry' to the reservation.
  • Expected Outcome: A new line is added to the reservation's billing details.
  1. Check-Out Guest:
  • Action: Call the CheckOut function with the reservation number.
  • Expected Outcome: The reservation status changes to 'Closed'.
  1. Generate Invoice:
  • Action: Call the GenerateInvoice function in the BillingManager with the reservation number.
  • Expected Outcome: A new, posted sales invoice is created, and the invoice number is returned. The invoice should contain lines for the room charges and the laundry service.

Scenario 2: Security - Unauthorized Refund

This scenario validates the security constraint preventing the Front Desk role from processing refunds.

  1. Attempt Refund:
  • Action: As a user with the 'Front Desk' permission set, attempt to run the standard 'Create Credit Memo' action on the invoice generated in Scenario 1.
  • Expected Outcome: The system should display a permission error, blocking the action.
tasks.md158 lines

Tasks: Hotel Reservation Lifecycle

Input: Design documents from specs/001-hotel-reservation-lifecycle/

Prerequisites: plan.md, spec.md, data-model.md, contracts/

Tests: Test tasks are included as per the project's Test-First constitution.

Organization: Tasks are grouped by user story to enable independent implementation and testing.

Format: [ID] [P?] [Story] Description

  • [P]: Can run in parallel (different files, no dependencies)
  • [Story]: Which user story this task belongs to (e.g., US1, US2, US3)
  • Include exact file paths in descriptions

Path Conventions

  • Paths are relative to the project root.

Phase 1: Setup (Shared Infrastructure)

Purpose: Configure the AL project and base structure.

  • [ ] T001 Verify and configure the main app.json for the extension.
  • [ ] T002 Configure debugger settings in .vscode/launch.json.

Phase 2: Foundational (Blocking Prerequisites)

Purpose: Core data structures that MUST be complete before ANY user story can be implemented.

  • [ ] T003 Create the ReservationStatus.Enum.al in src/enums/.
  • [X] T004 [P] Create the HotelRoom.Table.al definition in src/tables/.
  • [ ] T005 [P] Create the HotelReservation.Table.al definition in src/tables/.

Checkpoint: Foundation ready - user story implementation can now begin.

Phase 3: User Story 1 - Front Desk Manages Guest Stay (Priority: P1) 🎯 MVP

Goal: Implement the core operational flow for managing a guest's stay, from reservation to checkout.

Independent Test: Create a reservation, check a guest in, add a service charge, and check the guest out. Verify the status changes and financial entries are correct.

Tests for User Story 1 ⚠️

NOTE: Write these tests FIRST, ensure they FAIL before implementation
  • [ ] T006 [P] [US1] Create test file ReservationManager.Test.al in tests/src/ with failing tests for the CreateReservation, CheckIn, and CheckOut functions.
  • [ ] T007 [P] [US1] Create test file BillingManager.Test.al in tests/src/ with a failing test for the AddServiceCharge function.

Implementation for User Story 1

  • [ ] T008 [US1] Implement ReservationManager.Codeunit.al in src/codeunits/ with the CreateReservation, CheckIn, and CheckOut functions to pass tests from T006.
  • [ ] T009 [US1] Implement BillingManager.Codeunit.al in src/codeunits/ with the AddServiceCharge function to pass the test from T007.
  • [ ] T010 [P] [US1] Create the HotelRoomList.Page.al in src/pages/ to display the list of hotel rooms.
  • [X] T011 [US1] Create the HotelReservation.Page.al in src/pages/ to create and manage individual reservations.

Checkpoint: At this point, User Story 1 should be fully functional and testable independently.

Phase 4: User Story 2 - Finance Processes Invoices and Payments (Priority: P2)

Goal: Implement the financial processing part of the lifecycle, including invoicing and security.

Independent Test: Take a reservation with a 'Closed' status, generate an invoice, and verify its correctness. As a 'Front Desk' user, attempt to issue a refund and confirm it is blocked.

Tests for User Story 2 ⚠️

  • [ ] T012 [US2] Add a failing test to BillingManager.Test.al in tests/src/ for the GenerateInvoice function.
  • [ ] T013 [US2] Add a failing security test to BillingManager.Test.al in tests/src/ to verify that a user without 'Finance' permissions cannot process a refund/credit memo.

Implementation for User Story 2

  • [ ] T014 [US2] Extend the BillingManager.Codeunit.al in src/codeunits/ to implement the GenerateInvoice function, ensuring it passes the test from T012.
  • [X] T015 [US2] Implement the necessary permission checks in the system to block unauthorized refund attempts, ensuring the security test from T013 passes.

Checkpoint: At this point, User Stories 1 AND 2 should both work independently.

Phase 5: User Story 3 - Admin Configures Hotel System (Priority: P3)

Goal: Allow an administrator to configure master data for the hotel.

Independent Test: Create a new room via the UI, then verify it is available for a new reservation.

Tests for User Story 3 ⚠️

  • [ ] T016 [US3] Add a test to tests/src/ that programmatically creates a new room and then verifies it can be assigned to a new reservation.

Implementation for User Story 3

  • [X] T017 [US3] Ensure the HotelRoomList.Page.al in src/pages/ has actions to create, edit, and delete rooms, and that the underlying HotelRoom.Table.al supports these operations.

Checkpoint: All user stories should now be independently functional.

Phase 6: Polish & Cross-Cutting Concerns

Purpose: Final improvements and documentation.

  • [ ] T018 [P] Review and add comments to all new AL files (.al) for clarity.
  • [ ] T019 [P] Perform a final review of all code against AL best practices.
  • [X] T020 Run all validation scenarios listed in specs/001-hotel-reservation-lifecycle/quickstart.md to ensure end-to-end functionality.

Dependencies & Execution Order

Phase Dependencies

  • Setup (Phase 1): Must be completed first.
  • Foundational (Phase 2): Depends on Setup. Blocks all user story phases.
  • User Stories (Phase 3-5): Depend on Foundational.
  • US2 depends on US1 because invoicing requires a completed reservation.
  • US3 is independent of US1 and US2 and could be done after the Foundational phase.
  • Polish (Phase 6): Depends on all user stories being complete.

Recommended Execution Flow

  1. Phase 1 (Setup)
  2. Phase 2 (Foundational)
  3. Phase 3 (User Story 1)
  4. Phase 4 (User Story 2)
  5. Phase 5 (User Story 3)
  6. Phase 6 (Polish)

Parallel Opportunities

  • Within Phase 2, T004 and T005 can be done in parallel.
  • Within Phase 3 (US1), T006 and T007 (test creation) can be done in parallel. After tests are written, T008 and T009 (codeunit implementation) can be worked on in parallel. UI tasks T010 and T011 can also be done in parallel with the codeunit work, as long as the underlying tables exist.
  • Once the foundational phase is done, User Story 3 can be developed in parallel with User Story 1 and 2.

Implementation Strategy

MVP First (User Story 1 Only)

  1. Complete Phase 1: Setup
  2. Complete Phase 2: Foundational
  3. Complete Phase 3: User Story 1
  4. STOP and VALIDATE: Test the full Front Desk flow independently. This provides the core value of the extension.

Incremental Delivery

  1. Deliver MVP (US1).
  2. Add User Story 2 for financial processing.
  3. Add User Story 3 for administrative configuration.
  4. Complete the Polish phase.
checklists/requirements.md34 lines

Specification Quality Checklist: Hotel Reservation Lifecycle

Purpose: Validate specification completeness and quality before proceeding to planning Created: 2026-06-13 Feature: spec.md

Content Quality

  • [x] No implementation details (languages, frameworks, APIs)
  • [x] Focused on user value and business needs
  • [x] Written for non-technical stakeholders
  • [x] All mandatory sections completed

Requirement Completeness

  • [x] No [NEEDS CLARIFICATION] markers remain
  • [x] Requirements are testable and unambiguous
  • [x] Success criteria are measurable
  • [x] Success criteria are technology-agnostic (no implementation details)
  • [x] All acceptance scenarios are defined
  • [x] Edge cases are identified
  • [x] Scope is clearly bounded
  • [x] Dependencies and assumptions identified

Feature Readiness

  • [x] All functional requirements have clear acceptance criteria
  • [x] User scenarios cover primary flows
  • [x] Feature meets measurable outcomes defined in Success Criteria
  • [x] No implementation details leak into specification

Notes

  • All validation checks passed. The specification is ready for the planning phase.
contracts/billing.md28 lines

Contract: Billing Management

AL Codeunit: BillingManager.Codeunit.al

This codeunit handles the financial aspects of a reservation, including invoicing and payments.

Public Functions

GenerateInvoice(ReservationNo: Code[20]): Code[20]

  • Description: Generates a sales invoice for a 'Closed' reservation. It compiles all room charges and service charges.
  • Parameters:
  • ReservationNo: The reservation to generate an invoice for.
  • Returns: The Invoice No. of the newly created and posted sales invoice.
  • Events:
  • OnAfterGenerateInvoice(ReservationNo: Code[20]; InvoiceNo: Code[20])

AddServiceCharge(ReservationNo: Code[20]; ServiceType: Enum; ServiceNo: Code[20]; Description: Text; Amount: Decimal)

  • Description: Adds a billable service to a reservation.
  • Parameters:
  • ReservationNo: The reservation to add the charge to.
  • ServiceType: The type of service (e.g., G/L Account).
  • ServiceNo: The number of the service (e.g., G/L Account No.).
  • Description: A description of the service.
  • Amount: The cost of the service.
  • Events:
  • OnAfterAddServiceCharge(ReservationNo: Code[20])
contracts/reservations.md35 lines

Contract: Reservation Management

AL Codeunit: ReservationManager.Codeunit.al

This codeunit exposes the primary business logic for managing reservations.

Public Functions

CreateReservation(CustomerNo: Code[20]; RoomNo: Code[20]; CheckinDate: Date; CheckoutDate: Date): Code[20]

  • Description: Creates a new reservation with a 'Confirmed' status.
  • Parameters:
  • CustomerNo: The customer who is making the reservation.
  • RoomNo: The room being reserved.
  • CheckinDate: The check-in date.
  • CheckoutDate: The check-out date.
  • Returns: The Reservation No. of the newly created reservation.
  • Events:
  • OnAfterCreateReservation(ReservationNo: Code[20])

CheckIn(ReservationNo: Code[20])

  • Description: Checks in a guest for a confirmed reservation, changing the status to 'Occupied'.
  • Parameters:
  • ReservationNo: The reservation to check in.
  • Events:
  • OnAfterCheckIn(ReservationNo: Code[20])

CheckOut(ReservationNo: Code[20])

  • Description: Checks out a guest, changing the reservation status to 'Closed'. This will trigger the billing process.
  • Parameters:
  • ReservationNo: The reservation to check out.
  • Events:
  • OnAfterCheckOut(ReservationNo: Code[20])
Openspec + Claude10 files
proposal.md47 lines

Why

Hospitality operations using Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central currently lack integrated support for hotel guest lifecycle management. This extension addresses the need for a production-ready hospitality module that supports operational workflows from reservation through billing while ensuring financial correctness and role-based security controls.

What Changes

  • NEW: Complete hotel reservation management system supporting reservation creation, updates, room assignment, and status tracking with overbooking prevention
  • NEW: Room inventory management with occupancy tracking and conflict prevention
  • NEW: Deposit capture and payment processing with failure detection and application to final billing
  • NEW: Service charge tracking for billable items (breakfast, laundry, transfers) linked to reservations
  • NEW: Checkout workflow with automated invoice generation, VAT calculation, and financial posting
  • NEW: Role-based security enforcement with Front Desk, Finance, and Admin permission separation
  • BREAKING: Introduces new permission sets that must be assigned to users for proper access control

Capabilities

New Capabilities

  • reservation-management: Hotel reservation lifecycle including creation, room assignment, check-in, and status transitions
  • room-inventory: Room tracking with availability management and occupancy conflict prevention
  • deposit-payment: Deposit capture with payment validation and application to final billing
  • service-charges: Service charge line tracking for billable items linked to reservations
  • checkout-billing: Guest checkout with invoice generation, VAT calculation, and financial posting
  • tax-calculation: VAT calculation based on configured rates with support for pre-invoice recalculation
  • security-rbac: Role-based access control enforcing Front Desk, Finance, and Admin permission boundaries

Modified Capabilities

<!-- No existing capabilities are being modified -->

Impact

Code Areas:

  • New AL extension with tables, pages, codeunits for reservation and room management
  • New permission sets for role-based access control
  • Integration with Business Central Customer, Invoice, and G/L Posting modules

APIs:

  • No external API changes; internal AL procedures for reservation lifecycle operations

Dependencies:

  • Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central base application
  • Standard BC financial posting framework
  • BC VAT calculation engine

Systems:

  • Requires BC container deployment environment
  • Affects front desk operational workflows
  • Impacts finance invoice generation and payment reconciliation processes
specs/checkout-billing/spec.md74 lines

ADDED Requirements

Requirement: Support Guest Checkout

The system SHALL allow authorized users to complete guest checkout for an occupied reservation.

Scenario: Checkout occupied reservation

  • WHEN user initiates checkout for a reservation with status "Occupied"
  • THEN system processes checkout and transitions status to "Closed"

Scenario: Prevent checkout of non-occupied reservation

  • WHEN user attempts to checkout a reservation with status "Confirmed"
  • THEN system rejects checkout with validation error

Requirement: Generate Invoice Once Per Reservation

The system SHALL generate exactly one invoice per reservation during checkout.

Scenario: Generate invoice on first checkout

  • WHEN user completes checkout for a reservation without existing invoice
  • THEN system generates new invoice and stores Invoice No. on reservation

Scenario: Prevent duplicate invoice generation

  • WHEN user attempts checkout for a reservation that already has an Invoice No.
  • THEN system rejects checkout with duplicate invoice error

Requirement: Calculate Invoice Total

The system SHALL calculate invoice total including room charges and service charges.

Scenario: Calculate total with room and service charges

  • WHEN checkout processes reservation with 3 nights at 200.00/night and service charges of 90.00
  • THEN invoice subtotal is 690.00 (600.00 + 90.00)

Scenario: Calculate total for stay duration

  • WHEN reservation has Check-in Date Jan 5 and Check-out Date Jan 8
  • THEN system calculates 3 nights of room charges

Requirement: Calculate VAT

The system SHALL calculate and apply VAT to the invoice total based on configured tax rates.

Scenario: Apply VAT to invoice

  • WHEN invoice subtotal is 690.00 and VAT rate is 20%
  • THEN system calculates VAT amount of 138.00 and total of 828.00

Scenario: Handle zero VAT rate

  • WHEN VAT rate is configured as 0%
  • THEN system sets VAT amount to 0.00 and total equals subtotal

Requirement: Post Financial Results

The system SHALL post invoice amounts to the general ledger.

Scenario: Post invoice to G/L

  • WHEN invoice is generated with total 828.00
  • THEN system creates G/L entries for revenue and VAT liability

Scenario: Post customer receivable

  • WHEN invoice is generated for Customer No. C-001
  • THEN system creates customer ledger entry for amount due

Requirement: Settle Remaining Amount Due

The system SHALL calculate and process payment for the remaining balance after applying deposit.

Scenario: Settle balance after deposit

  • WHEN invoice total is 828.00 and deposit was 500.00
  • THEN system processes payment for remaining 328.00

Scenario: Handle fully paid via deposit

  • WHEN deposit amount equals or exceeds invoice total
  • THEN system marks invoice as fully paid with no additional payment required

Requirement: Release Room on Checkout

The system SHALL release the room for new reservations when checkout completes.

Scenario: Free room after successful checkout

  • WHEN checkout completes for Room 101
  • THEN system sets room Occupied flag to false
specs/deposit-payment/spec.md56 lines

ADDED Requirements

Requirement: Capture Deposit

The system SHALL allow authorized users to capture a deposit payment for a reservation.

Scenario: Capture deposit for confirmed reservation

  • WHEN user captures a deposit of 500.00 for a reservation
  • THEN system records the deposit amount on the reservation

Scenario: Prevent deposit capture without reservation

  • WHEN user attempts to capture deposit for non-existent Reservation No.
  • THEN system rejects the operation with validation error

Requirement: Track Deposit Success

The system SHALL track whether deposit payment was successfully captured.

Scenario: Mark deposit as captured on successful payment

  • WHEN payment processor confirms deposit capture
  • THEN system records Deposit Amount and marks deposit as successfully captured

Scenario: Detect failed deposit capture

  • WHEN payment processor reports deposit failure
  • THEN system does NOT record deposit amount and marks capture as failed

Requirement: Apply Deposit to Final Bill

The system SHALL deduct the captured deposit from the final bill amount during checkout.

Scenario: Apply deposit to invoice total

  • WHEN checkout generates invoice with total 1200.00 and deposit was 500.00
  • THEN system calculates remaining amount due as 700.00

Scenario: Handle zero remaining after deposit

  • WHEN deposit amount equals or exceeds total invoice amount
  • THEN system sets remaining amount due to zero

Requirement: Prevent False Success on Payment Failure

The system SHALL NOT mark deposit as captured if payment processing fails.

Scenario: Reject deposit on payment gateway timeout

  • WHEN payment gateway times out during deposit capture
  • THEN system marks deposit as failed and does NOT store Deposit Amount

Scenario: Reject deposit on insufficient funds

  • WHEN payment processor returns insufficient funds error
  • THEN system marks deposit as failed and alerts user

Requirement: Validate Deposit Amount

The system SHALL validate that deposit amount is positive and within acceptable limits.

Scenario: Accept valid deposit amount

  • WHEN user enters deposit amount of 250.00
  • THEN system accepts and processes the deposit

Scenario: Reject negative deposit amount

  • WHEN user attempts to enter deposit amount of -100.00
  • THEN system rejects with validation error
specs/reservation-management/spec.md67 lines

ADDED Requirements

Requirement: Create Reservation

The system SHALL allow authorized users to create a new hotel reservation with required guest and stay details.

Scenario: Create reservation with valid data

  • WHEN user creates a reservation with Customer No., Room No., Check-in Date, Check-out Date
  • THEN system generates unique Reservation No. and sets status to "Confirmed"

Scenario: Prevent reservation without required fields

  • WHEN user attempts to create reservation without Customer No.
  • THEN system rejects creation with validation error

Requirement: Update Reservation

The system SHALL allow modification of reservation details before check-in.

Scenario: Update check-in date for confirmed reservation

  • WHEN user updates Check-in Date on a "Confirmed" reservation
  • THEN system validates new date and updates reservation if room is available

Scenario: Prevent update of occupied reservation

  • WHEN user attempts to update Room No. on an "Occupied" reservation
  • THEN system rejects the modification

Requirement: Assign Room

The system SHALL allow assignment of a specific room to a reservation.

Scenario: Assign available room

  • WHEN user assigns an available room to a confirmed reservation
  • THEN system updates Room No. on reservation and marks room as occupied

Scenario: Prevent double-booking

  • WHEN user attempts to assign a room already occupied during the requested dates
  • THEN system rejects assignment with overbooking error

Requirement: Manage Reservation Status

The system SHALL track reservation lifecycle through status transitions.

Scenario: Transition from Confirmed to Occupied on check-in

  • WHEN user checks in a guest with status "Confirmed"
  • THEN system updates status to "Occupied"

Scenario: Transition from Occupied to Closed on checkout

  • WHEN user completes checkout for an "Occupied" reservation
  • THEN system updates status to "Closed"

Scenario: Prevent invalid status transition

  • WHEN user attempts to check in a reservation already in "Closed" status
  • THEN system rejects the operation

Requirement: Prevent Overbooking

The system SHALL prevent assignment of the same room to overlapping reservation dates.

Scenario: Detect date overlap for same room

  • WHEN user attempts to create reservation for Room 101 from Jan 5-10 while another reservation exists for Room 101 from Jan 8-12
  • THEN system rejects the reservation with overbooking error

Scenario: Allow non-overlapping reservations for same room

  • WHEN user creates reservation for Room 101 from Jan 5-10 and another reservation for Room 101 from Jan 11-15
  • THEN system accepts both reservations

Requirement: Track Reservation Data

The system SHALL maintain all required reservation data fields.

Scenario: Store complete reservation record

  • WHEN reservation is created
  • THEN system stores Reservation No., Customer No., Room No., Check-in Date, Check-out Date, Status, Deposit Amount, and Invoice No.
specs/room-inventory/spec.md48 lines

ADDED Requirements

Requirement: Maintain Room Inventory

The system SHALL maintain a master list of hotel rooms with their attributes.

Scenario: Create room record

  • WHEN admin creates a new room with Room No., Room Type, and Nightly Rate
  • THEN system stores the room record with Occupied flag set to false

Scenario: Prevent duplicate room numbers

  • WHEN admin attempts to create a room with an existing Room No.
  • THEN system rejects creation with duplicate key error

Requirement: Track Room Occupancy

The system SHALL track whether each room is currently occupied.

Scenario: Mark room as occupied on check-in

  • WHEN guest checks in and is assigned to a room
  • THEN system sets the room's Occupied flag to true

Scenario: Mark room as available on checkout

  • WHEN guest checks out from a room
  • THEN system sets the room's Occupied flag to false

Requirement: Prevent Overlapping Room Use

The system SHALL prevent assigning the same room to multiple guests during overlapping date ranges.

Scenario: Reject overlapping assignment

  • WHEN system attempts to assign Room 201 to a reservation from Feb 1-5 while Room 201 is already assigned from Jan 30-Feb 3
  • THEN system rejects the assignment with conflict error

Scenario: Allow sequential room assignments

  • WHEN system assigns Room 201 to a reservation from Feb 1-5 and another reservation starts on Feb 5
  • THEN system accepts both assignments (same-day turnover allowed)

Requirement: Release Room on Checkout

The system SHALL automatically release room occupancy when checkout is completed.

Scenario: Release room after checkout

  • WHEN checkout process completes for a room
  • THEN system sets room Occupied flag to false and allows new assignment

Requirement: Store Room Attributes

The system SHALL store essential room data including Room No., Room Type, Nightly Rate, and Occupied status.

Scenario: Retrieve room details

  • WHEN user queries a room by Room No.
  • THEN system returns Room Type, Nightly Rate, and current Occupied status
specs/security-rbac/spec.md82 lines

ADDED Requirements

Requirement: Restrict Refund Processing to Finance Role

The system SHALL allow only users with Finance role to process refunds.

Scenario: Finance user processes refund

  • WHEN user with Finance permission set initiates refund
  • THEN system allows refund processing

Scenario: Block Front Desk from refund

  • WHEN user with Front Desk permission set attempts refund
  • THEN system rejects operation with insufficient permissions error

Requirement: Block Front Desk from Refund Actions

The system SHALL prevent Front Desk users from accessing refund functionality.

Scenario: Hide refund option from Front Desk

  • WHEN Front Desk user views payment options
  • THEN refund action is not visible or accessible

Scenario: Reject direct refund attempt by Front Desk

  • WHEN Front Desk user attempts to execute refund via API or direct action
  • THEN system rejects with authorization error

Requirement: Enforce Role-Based Separation

The system SHALL separate operational tasks (Front Desk) from financial tasks (Finance) through permission sets.

Scenario: Front Desk creates reservation

  • WHEN user has Front Desk permission set
  • THEN system allows reservation creation, room assignment, check-in, checkout

Scenario: Finance processes payments

  • WHEN user has Finance permission set
  • THEN system allows invoice generation, payment posting, refund processing

Scenario: Prevent Front Desk from tax setup

  • WHEN Front Desk user attempts to modify VAT configuration
  • THEN system rejects with insufficient permissions error

Requirement: Admin Configuration Access

The system SHALL restrict setup and master data configuration to Admin role.

Scenario: Admin configures room inventory

  • WHEN user with Admin permission set creates or modifies room records
  • THEN system allows the operation

Scenario: Block non-Admin from setup

  • WHEN Front Desk or Finance user attempts to modify setup tables
  • THEN system rejects with insufficient permissions error

Requirement: Prevent Unauthorized Finance Posting Modification

The system SHALL prevent Front Desk users from directly modifying financial postings.

Scenario: Block G/L entry modification

  • WHEN Front Desk user attempts to modify general ledger entries
  • THEN system rejects with permission error

Scenario: Block customer ledger adjustment

  • WHEN Front Desk user attempts to adjust customer ledger entries
  • THEN system rejects with permission error

Requirement: Define Permission Sets

The system SHALL provide distinct permission sets for Front Desk, Finance, and Admin roles.

Scenario: Assign Front Desk permission set

  • WHEN admin assigns Front Desk permission set to user
  • THEN user gains access to reservation, check-in, checkout operations only
