Scenario: Assign Finance permission set

  • WHEN admin assigns Finance permission set to user
  • THEN user gains access to invoicing, payment posting, and refund operations

Scenario: Assign Admin permission set

  • WHEN admin assigns Admin permission set to user
  • THEN user gains full access to setup and master data configuration

Requirement: Log Permission Violations

The system SHALL log unauthorized access attempts for security auditing.

Scenario: Log refund attempt by Front Desk

  • WHEN Front Desk user attempts unauthorized refund
  • THEN system logs the attempt with user ID, timestamp, and action blocked
specs/service-charges/spec.md67 lines

ADDED Requirements

Requirement: Store Service Charge Lines

The system SHALL store service charge line items linked to a reservation.

Scenario: Add service charge to reservation

  • WHEN user adds a breakfast service charge of 25.00 to a reservation
  • THEN system creates a service charge line linked to that reservation

Scenario: Store multiple service charges

  • WHEN user adds breakfast (25.00), laundry (15.00), and transfer (50.00) charges
  • THEN system stores all three service charge lines for the reservation

Requirement: Support Billable Service Types

The system SHALL support common billable service types including breakfast, laundry, and transfer services.

Scenario: Record breakfast service

  • WHEN user posts a breakfast charge with description "Breakfast" and amount 25.00
  • THEN system stores service charge with type Breakfast

Scenario: Record laundry service

  • WHEN user posts a laundry charge with description "Laundry" and amount 15.00
  • THEN system stores service charge with type Laundry

Scenario: Record transfer service

  • WHEN user posts a transfer charge with description "Airport Transfer" and amount 50.00
  • THEN system stores service charge with type Transfer

Requirement: Link Service Charges to Reservation

The system SHALL associate each service charge with a specific reservation number.

Scenario: Query service charges by reservation

  • WHEN user queries service charges for Reservation No. RES-001
  • THEN system returns all service charge lines linked to RES-001

Scenario: Prevent orphaned service charges

  • WHEN user attempts to create service charge without valid Reservation No.
  • THEN system rejects the operation with validation error

Requirement: Include Service Charges in Final Billing

The system SHALL include all service charge amounts in the final invoice total.

Scenario: Calculate total with service charges

  • WHEN checkout generates invoice for reservation with room charges 1000.00 and service charges totaling 90.00
  • THEN invoice subtotal includes 1090.00 before tax

Scenario: Handle reservation with no service charges

  • WHEN checkout generates invoice for reservation with no service charges
  • THEN invoice subtotal includes only room charges

Requirement: Track Service Charge Details

The system SHALL store description, amount, and date for each service charge.

Scenario: Record complete service charge data

  • WHEN user posts service charge on Jan 15, 2026 with description "Room Service" and amount 35.00
  • THEN system stores all three attributes for that charge line

Requirement: Allow Service Charge Modification Before Checkout

The system SHALL allow authorized users to modify or remove service charges before checkout is completed.

Scenario: Remove incorrect service charge

  • WHEN user deletes a service charge line before checkout
  • THEN system removes the charge and excludes it from final bill

Scenario: Prevent modification after checkout

  • WHEN user attempts to modify service charge on a closed reservation
  • THEN system rejects the modification
specs/tax-calculation/spec.md63 lines

ADDED Requirements

Requirement: Calculate VAT Based on Configured Rates

The system SHALL calculate VAT amount by applying the configured tax rate to the invoice subtotal.

Scenario: Calculate VAT at standard rate

  • WHEN invoice subtotal is 1000.00 and configured VAT rate is 20%
  • THEN system calculates VAT amount as 200.00

Scenario: Calculate VAT at reduced rate

  • WHEN invoice subtotal is 1000.00 and configured VAT rate is 10%
  • THEN system calculates VAT amount as 100.00

Requirement: Support VAT Rate Configuration

The system SHALL retrieve VAT rate from Business Central tax configuration.

Scenario: Use default VAT rate

  • WHEN no specific VAT rate is configured for hotel services
  • THEN system uses the default VAT business posting group rate

Scenario: Use service-specific VAT rate

  • WHEN hotel services have a specific VAT product posting group configured
  • THEN system uses the rate from that posting group

Requirement: Recalculate VAT When Configuration Changes

The system SHALL support recalculation of VAT if tax configuration changes before invoice is generated.

Scenario: Recalculate after rate change

  • WHEN VAT rate changes from 20% to 15% before invoice generation
  • THEN system applies the new 15% rate to the invoice

Scenario: Preserve VAT on posted invoice

  • WHEN VAT rate changes after invoice has been posted
  • THEN system does NOT recalculate VAT on the existing invoice

Requirement: Preserve Financial Posting Correctness

The system SHALL ensure VAT amounts are correctly split between revenue and tax liability in G/L posting.

Scenario: Post VAT liability correctly

  • WHEN invoice with VAT 200.00 is posted
  • THEN system credits VAT liability account with 200.00

Scenario: Post revenue net of VAT

  • WHEN invoice has subtotal 1000.00 and VAT 200.00
  • THEN system credits revenue account with 1000.00 and VAT account with 200.00

Requirement: Handle Zero VAT Scenarios

The system SHALL correctly handle cases where VAT rate is zero or exempt.

Scenario: Calculate with zero VAT

  • WHEN VAT rate is 0% and invoice subtotal is 1000.00
  • THEN system sets VAT amount to 0.00 and invoice total to 1000.00

Scenario: Handle VAT-exempt transactions

  • WHEN service is marked as VAT-exempt
  • THEN system applies 0% VAT rate

Requirement: Validate VAT Calculation Accuracy

The system SHALL ensure VAT calculations are mathematically accurate to two decimal places.

Scenario: Round VAT to two decimals

  • WHEN invoice subtotal is 33.33 and VAT rate is 20%
  • THEN system calculates VAT as 6.67 (rounded to nearest cent)
design.md210 lines

Context

Business Central currently lacks native hospitality functionality. This extension fills that gap by implementing a complete hotel reservation lifecycle system integrated with BC's financial posting framework. The design must balance operational workflow requirements (fast check-in, room assignments) with financial correctness (accurate VAT, audit trails) and security (role-based access control preventing unauthorized refunds).

Current State: No existing hotel management functionality in BC base application.

Constraints:

  • Must use BC's standard posting framework for financial transactions
  • Must integrate with existing Customer, Invoice, and G/L modules
  • Must be deployable as an AL extension (upgrade-safe, no base app modifications)
  • Performance: Front desk operations must complete within 2 seconds
  • Data integrity: No orphaned financial postings or inventory inconsistencies

Stakeholders:

  • Front Desk: Primary users for reservation and guest operations
  • Finance: Responsible for billing accuracy and payment reconciliation
  • Admin: Manages system setup and permissions
  • Auditors: Require complete financial audit trails

Goals / Non-Goals

Goals:

  • Provide complete reservation-to-billing lifecycle in a single extension
  • Prevent overbooking through date-based room conflict detection
  • Ensure deposit amounts are correctly applied to final invoices
  • Enforce strict role-based access control with Finance-only refund processing
  • Support standard BC VAT calculation and G/L posting patterns
  • Enable testability through isolated codeunits and clear data validation

Non-Goals:

  • Integration with third-party booking platforms (APIs can be added later)
  • Advanced yield management or dynamic pricing
  • Multi-property management (single hotel scope)
  • Guest loyalty programs or CRM features
  • Real-time room availability dashboards (basic list pages sufficient)
  • Payment gateway integration (simulated payment success/failure for MVP)

Decisions

Decision 1: Use BC Table Extension Pattern with New Tables

Choice: Create new tables (Reservation, Room, Service Charge) rather than extending existing BC tables.

Rationale: Hotel reservation data does not fit into existing BC entities (Sales Order, Item, Resource). New tables provide:

  • Clear domain model aligned with hospitality concepts
  • Simpler upgrade path (no dependencies on base table schema changes)
  • Isolated data for easier testing and deployment

Alternatives Considered:

  • Sales Order approach: Treating reservations as sales orders would force unnatural mapping (room as item, dates as shipment dates) and complicate service charge handling.
  • Custom fields on existing tables: Would require complex logic to differentiate hotel transactions from other BC operations.

Decision 2: Date-Based Overbooking Prevention Logic

Choice: Implement overbooking check as procedural validation comparing date ranges for the same room.

Implementation: Before assigning a room, query existing reservations for that room where (new_checkin < existing_checkout) AND (new_checkout > existing_checkin).

Rationale:

  • Simple, deterministic logic suitable for unit testing
  • Performs well with indexed Room No. and date fields
  • Aligns with standard interval overlap detection pattern

Alternatives Considered:

  • Reservation calendar table: Pre-populating a daily calendar would consume excessive storage and complicate updates.
  • External scheduling service: Adds unnecessary external dependency for a core business rule.

Decision 3: Deposit Handling with Explicit Success Flag

Choice: Store both Deposit Amount and Deposit Captured boolean flag on Reservation table.

Rationale:

  • Prevents false success: If payment processing fails, amount remains 0 and flag stays false
  • Enables retry: User can reattempt deposit capture without data corruption
  • Clear audit trail: Explicit flag makes successful vs. attempted deposits unambiguous

Alternatives Considered:

  • Amount-only approach: Zero amount could mean "no deposit" or "failed capture" - ambiguous.
  • Separate payment transaction table: Overkill for MVP; standard BC payment journal serves for actual financial posting.

Decision 4: Invoice Generation Idempotency Check

Choice: Reject checkout if Invoice No. field is already populated on reservation.

Rationale:

  • Prevents duplicate G/L postings which would corrupt financial records
  • Simple implementation: single field check before invoice creation
  • Clear error message for users attempting re-checkout

Alternatives Considered:

  • Allow re-generation with void: Adds complexity of reversing postings; error-prone.
  • Silent skip: User confusion if checkout appears to succeed but does nothing.

Decision 5: VAT Calculation via BC Standard Posting Groups

Choice: Use BC's existing VAT Business Posting Group and VAT Product Posting Group mechanism.

Rationale:

  • Leverages BC's tested VAT engine (handles rate changes, exemptions, rounding)
  • Ensures correct G/L account mapping per jurisdiction
  • Standard BC finance team workflow (no custom tax logic to maintain)

Alternatives Considered:

  • Custom VAT calculation: Would duplicate BC functionality and risk compliance errors.
  • Fixed tax rate in setup: Insufficient for multi-jurisdiction deployments.

Decision 6: Role-Based Security via Permission Sets

Choice: Define three permission sets (Front Desk, Finance, Admin) with object-level permissions.

Implementation:

  • Front Desk: Read/Write on Reservation, Room, Service Charge tables; Execute on Check-in/Checkout codeunits; No access to Refund codeunit
  • Finance: Read on Reservation; Execute on Invoice Generation, Payment Posting, Refund codeunits
  • Admin: Full access to setup tables (Room master, VAT configuration)

Rationale:

  • BC's permission framework enforces at runtime (not bypassable via code)
  • Standard BC security model familiar to admins
  • Meets audit requirement for separation of duties

Alternatives Considered:

  • Code-based permission checks: Redundant with BC framework; harder to audit.
  • Single permission set with feature flags: Does not enforce separation of duties.

Decision 7: Service Charge Storage as Line Table

Choice: Create Service Charge Line table with foreign key to Reservation No.

Rationale:

  • Supports multiple charges per reservation (breakfast + laundry + transfer)
  • Natural relational model for line-item data
  • Easy aggregation via SUM() for invoice total calculation

Alternatives Considered:

  • Fixed fields on Reservation table: Inflexible; can't handle varying number of services.
  • JSON blob: Poor query performance and validation difficulty.

Risks / Trade-offs

Risk: Race Condition in Overbooking Check

Description: Two users might assign the same room simultaneously between read and write operations.

Mitigation:

  • Use database-level unique constraint on (Room No., Date Range) if BC supports temporal constraints.
  • Fallback: Wrap room assignment in transaction with SERIALIZABLE isolation level.
  • Document as known limitation if neither is feasible; occurrence probability is low in typical hotel operations.

Risk: Deposit Payment Simulation Inconsistency

Description: MVP simulates payment success/failure without real gateway integration; behavior may differ from production payment processor.

Mitigation:

  • Design Capture Deposit codeunit with clear interface (ProcessPayment(Amount): Boolean)
  • Implement as separate codeunit to isolate for future replacement
  • Document expected gateway behavior in code comments
  • Add integration test scenarios for both success and failure paths

Risk: VAT Rate Change Timing

Description: If VAT rate changes between reservation creation and checkout, invoice might use unexpected rate.

Trade-off: System uses rate at checkout time (correct for financial posting) but may differ from guest's expectation at booking.

Mitigation:

  • Document behavior: "VAT calculated at checkout using current configuration"
  • Consider storing "Quoted VAT Rate" on reservation for display (non-binding)
  • Finance team can manually adjust invoice if regulatory requirement exists

Risk: Performance with Large Reservation Volume

Description: Overbooking check requires querying all reservations for a room across date range; could slow down with thousands of historical records.

Mitigation:

  • Add index on (Room No., Check-in Date, Check-out Date)
  • Filter query to active reservations only (exclude Closed status older than 1 year)
  • Monitor query performance; add date range constraints if needed

Trade-off: Single Invoice Per Reservation

Description: Design enforces one invoice per reservation; cannot split billing (e.g., room to company, services to guest).

Rationale: Simplifies MVP implementation and covers 90% of use cases.

Future Enhancement: Add "Split Billing" feature post-MVP if customer demand exists.

Trade-off: No Reservation Cancellation Workflow

Description: Design does not include formal cancellation status or refund triggering.

Rationale: Refund processing exists (Finance role) but cancellation business rules vary by property (cancellation fees, notice periods).

Mitigation: Front Desk can delete reservation before check-in; Finance processes refund separately per property policy.

Migration Plan

Initial Deployment:

  1. Install AL extension in BC container
  2. Admin configures Room Inventory (master data)
  3. Admin assigns permission sets to users (Front Desk, Finance, Admin roles)
  4. Admin configures VAT Posting Groups for hotel services
  5. Finance validates test reservation end-to-end before go-live

Rollback Strategy:

  • Extension can be uninstalled if no financial postings have occurred (reservations exist only in extension tables)
  • If G/L postings exist, must reverse invoices before uninstalling to maintain ledger integrity
  • Backup reservation data before uninstall (export to Excel or XML)

Data Migration:

  • Not applicable for MVP (greenfield deployment)
  • Future: If migrating from external system, provide data import wizard for Reservations and Rooms

Open Questions

  1. Payment Gateway Selection: Which payment processor will be used in production? Design assumes boolean success/failure response; need actual API contract for final implementation.
  1. Room Turnover Policy: Should same-day checkout/check-in be allowed (Check-out Date = Next Check-in Date)? Current design allows this; confirm with hotel operations.
  1. Historical Data Retention: How long should Closed reservations be retained? Impacts overbooking query performance and storage. Suggest 2-year retention with archival process.
  1. Multi-Currency Support: Does hotel accept bookings in multiple currencies? Current design assumes single currency; BC supports multi-currency but requires additional posting group configuration.
  1. Service Charge Tax Treatment: Are all service charges subject to same VAT rate as room charges, or do some (e.g., minibar) have different rates? May need VAT Product Posting Group per service type.
tasks.md117 lines

1. Extension Setup and Data Model

  • [x] 1.1 Create AL extension project structure with app.json and manifest
  • [x] 1.2 Define Reservation table with fields: Reservation No., Customer No., Room No., Check-in Date, Check-out Date, Status, Deposit Amount, Deposit Captured, Invoice No.
  • [x] 1.3 Define Room table with fields: Room No., Room Type, Nightly Rate, Occupied
  • [x] 1.4 Define Service Charge Line table with fields: Reservation No., Line No., Description, Service Type, Amount, Date
  • [x] 1.5 Add primary keys and indexes on Reservation (Reservation No.), Room (Room No.), Service Charge (Reservation No. + Line No.)
  • [x] 1.6 Add index on Room for (Room No., Check-in Date, Check-out Date) for overbooking queries

2. Reservation Management Implementation

  • [x] 2.1 Create Reservation List page showing all reservations with filters for status
  • [x] 2.2 Create Reservation Card page with editable fields and validation
  • [x] 2.3 Implement CreateReservation codeunit with required field validation
  • [x] 2.4 Implement UpdateReservation codeunit with status-based modification rules
  • [x] 2.5 Add OnValidate triggers for Check-in Date and Check-out Date to ensure check-out > check-in
  • [x] 2.6 Implement status transition logic (Confirmed → Occupied → Closed)

3. Room Inventory and Overbooking Prevention

  • [x] 3.1 Create Room List page for room master data management
  • [x] 3.2 Create Room Card page with Room No., Room Type, Nightly Rate, Occupied flag
  • [x] 3.3 Implement CheckRoomAvailability codeunit with date range overlap query
  • [x] 3.4 Add overbooking validation on room assignment: reject if (new_checkin < existing_checkout) AND (new_checkout > existing_checkin)
  • [x] 3.5 Implement AssignRoom procedure to set Room No. on reservation and mark room as Occupied
  • [x] 3.6 Implement ReleaseRoom procedure to set Occupied flag to false on checkout

4. Deposit Payment Processing

  • [x] 4.1 Create CaptureDeposit codeunit with ProcessPayment interface (returns Boolean)
  • [x] 4.2 Implement simulated payment processing with configurable success/failure for testing
  • [x] 4.3 Add deposit capture action on Reservation Card page
  • [x] 4.4 On successful payment: set Deposit Amount and Deposit Captured = true
  • [x] 4.5 On failed payment: set Deposit Captured = false and show error message
  • [x] 4.6 Add validation to prevent negative deposit amounts

5. Service Charges

  • [x] 5.1 Create Service Charge Lines subpage for Reservation Card
  • [x] 5.2 Implement AddServiceCharge procedure with Reservation No. validation
  • [x] 5.3 Add service type enum or option field (Breakfast, Laundry, Transfer, Other)
  • [x] 5.4 Implement validation to prevent service charge creation for non-existent reservations
  • [x] 5.5 Add delete protection for service charges on closed reservations
  • [x] 5.6 Create CalculateTotalServiceCharges function to sum all charges for a reservation

6. Check-in Process

  • [x] 6.1 Create CheckIn codeunit to transition reservation from Confirmed to Occupied
  • [x] 6.2 Add check-in action on Reservation Card (enabled only for Confirmed status)
  • [x] 6.3 On check-in: validate room is assigned, update status to Occupied, set room Occupied flag to true
  • [x] 6.4 Add check-in date/time tracking if needed for audit trail

7. Checkout and Invoice Generation

  • [x] 7.1 Create Checkout codeunit with invoice generation logic
  • [x] 7.2 Implement invoice duplicate check: reject if Invoice No. is already populated
  • [x] 7.3 Calculate invoice subtotal: (nights × nightly rate) + sum of service charges
  • [x] 7.4 Calculate nights: DATEDIFF(Check-out Date, Check-in Date)
  • [x] 7.5 Create Sales Invoice with Customer No. from reservation
  • [x] 7.6 Add invoice line for room charges with description and amount
  • [x] 7.7 Add invoice lines for each service charge
  • [x] 7.8 Store generated Invoice No. back to Reservation record

8. VAT Calculation and Financial Posting

  • [x] 8.1 Configure VAT Product Posting Group for hotel services
  • [x] 8.2 Apply VAT Business/Product Posting Groups to invoice lines
  • [x] 8.3 Verify BC standard VAT calculation applies to invoice total
  • [x] 8.4 Implement ApplyDeposit logic: reduce invoice total by Deposit Amount if Deposit Captured = true
  • [x] 8.5 Calculate remaining amount due: invoice total - deposit amount
  • [x] 8.6 Post invoice to create G/L entries using BC standard posting codeunit
  • [x] 8.7 Create customer ledger entry for amount due

9. Payment and Room Release on Checkout

  • [x] 9.1 Add payment processing for remaining balance (simulated for MVP)
  • [x] 9.2 On successful payment: mark invoice as paid
  • [x] 9.3 Update reservation status to Closed
  • [x] 9.4 Call ReleaseRoom to set room Occupied flag to false
  • [x] 9.5 Add checkout action to Reservation Card (enabled only for Occupied status)

10. Security and Permission Sets

  • [x] 10.1 Create Front Desk permission set with Read/Write on Reservation, Room, Service Charge tables
  • [x] 10.2 Grant Execute permission on CreateReservation, CheckIn, Checkout, CaptureDeposit codeunits to Front Desk
  • [x] 10.3 Create Finance permission set with Read on Reservation and Execute on Invoice Generation, Payment Posting
  • [x] 10.4 Create ProcessRefund codeunit (stub implementation for MVP)
  • [x] 10.5 Grant Execute permission on ProcessRefund codeunit ONLY to Finance permission set
  • [x] 10.6 Create Admin permission set with full access to Room setup and VAT configuration
  • [x] 10.7 Add permission validation logging for refund attempts by non-Finance users

11. Negative Case Handling

  • [x] 11.1 Add error handling for overbooking: display clear message with conflicting reservation details
  • [x] 11.2 Add error handling for failed payment: rollback deposit capture and show payment failure reason
  • [x] 11.3 Add validation to prevent duplicate invoice: display existing Invoice No. in error message
  • [x] 11.4 Add authorization check in ProcessRefund: throw permission error if user not in Finance role
  • [x] 11.5 Test all negative scenarios: overbooking, payment failure, duplicate invoice, unauthorized refund

12. Testing and Validation

  • [x] 12.1 Create test codeunit for reservation lifecycle: create, update, check-in, checkout
  • [x] 12.2 Create test codeunit for overbooking prevention with overlapping and non-overlapping dates
  • [x] 12.3 Create test codeunit for deposit capture success and failure scenarios
  • [x] 12.4 Create test codeunit for service charge aggregation in invoice
  • [x] 12.5 Create test codeunit for VAT calculation accuracy
  • [x] 12.6 Create test codeunit for permission set enforcement (Front Desk blocked from refund)
  • [x] 12.7 Test complete end-to-end workflow: reservation → deposit → check-in → service charges → checkout → invoice → payment

13. Documentation and Deployment

  • [x] 13.1 Document setup steps: room inventory configuration, permission set assignment, VAT posting groups
  • [x] 13.2 Create deployment guide for BC container installation
  • [x] 13.3 Document known limitations: simulated payment, single invoice per reservation, no cancellation workflow
  • [x] 13.4 Create user guide for Front Desk operations
  • [x] 13.5 Create user guide for Finance operations
  • [x] 13.6 Package AL extension for deployment
Openspec + Codex6 files
proposal.md31 lines

Why

Hospitality operations need a controlled reservation-to-billing flow in Business Central so front desk activity, room occupancy, tax calculation, invoicing, payment, and refunds remain financially correct. This change introduces a production-shaped hotel lifecycle module with clear separation between operational and finance responsibilities.

What Changes

  • Add reservation records with customer, room, stay dates, lifecycle status, deposit amount, and invoice linkage.
  • Add room inventory records with room type, nightly rate, and occupancy tracking.
  • Enforce room availability and prevent overlapping reservations for the same room.
  • Support deposit capture with explicit success tracking and failed-payment handling.
  • Add service charge lines linked to reservations for billable hotel services.
  • Support check-in, checkout, one-time invoice generation, VAT calculation, and final payment settlement.
  • Restrict refund processing to Finance users and block Front Desk users from refund actions.
  • Add role-oriented pages, actions, posting logic, permissions, and tests for positive and negative lifecycle paths.

Capabilities

New Capabilities

  • hotel-reservations: Reservation creation, updates, room assignment, status transitions, overbooking prevention, and checkout occupancy release.
  • hotel-billing: Deposit capture, service charges, VAT calculation, invoice generation, final payment settlement, and duplicate invoice prevention.
  • hotel-security: Role-based separation for front desk, finance, and admin responsibilities, including unauthorized refund prevention.

Modified Capabilities

  • None.

Impact

  • New AL source under src/ using object IDs from the configured app.json range.
  • New tables, pages, enums, codeunits, permission sets, and tests for hotel reservation, billing, and security workflows.
  • Integration with standard Business Central customer, sales invoice, VAT, payment, and ledger behavior where appropriate.
  • No changes to app.json, .alpackages/, or Microsoft base objects.
specs/hotel-billing/spec.md56 lines

ADDED Requirements

Requirement: Deposit capture

The system SHALL capture and track reservation deposits, including whether the deposit was successfully captured.

Scenario: Successful deposit

  • WHEN a front desk user captures a deposit and the payment operation succeeds
  • THEN the system records the deposit amount and marks the deposit as captured.

Scenario: Failed deposit

  • WHEN a front desk user captures a deposit and the payment operation fails
  • THEN the system does not mark the deposit as captured and does not treat the deposit as available for final billing.

Requirement: Service charge lines

The system SHALL store service charge lines linked to a reservation and include billable services in final billing.

Scenario: Add service charge

  • WHEN a front desk user posts a breakfast, laundry, transfer, or other billable service charge to a reservation
  • THEN the system stores the charge line with amount and reservation linkage.

Scenario: Include service charges in billing

  • WHEN finance generates the final invoice for a reservation with service charge lines
  • THEN the invoice includes the service charge amounts in addition to room charges.

Requirement: Invoice generation

The system SHALL generate a sales invoice once per reservation and link the generated invoice number to the reservation.

Scenario: Generate invoice

  • WHEN finance generates an invoice for a reservation that has not already been invoiced
  • THEN the system creates the invoice, includes room nights and service charges, applies eligible deposit amounts, and stores the invoice number on the reservation.

Scenario: Prevent duplicate invoice

  • WHEN finance attempts to generate an invoice for a reservation that already has an invoice number
  • THEN the system rejects the duplicate invoice request.

Requirement: VAT calculation

The system SHALL calculate VAT for the final invoice based on configured Business Central VAT rates at invoice generation time.

Scenario: Calculate VAT

  • WHEN finance generates an invoice for taxable room and service charges
  • THEN the system calculates VAT using the configured VAT posting setup.

Scenario: Recalculate VAT before invoicing

  • WHEN tax configuration changes after reservation creation but before invoice generation
  • THEN the generated invoice uses the current tax configuration rather than stale reservation-time values.

Requirement: Final payment settlement

The system SHALL settle the remaining amount due after invoice generation and eligible deposit application.

Scenario: Post final payment

  • WHEN finance posts a successful final payment for the remaining reservation balance
  • THEN the system records the settlement and preserves the reservation-to-invoice financial linkage.

Scenario: Reject failed final payment

  • WHEN finance posts a final payment and the payment operation fails
  • THEN the system does not mark the remaining balance as settled.
specs/hotel-reservations/spec.md49 lines

ADDED Requirements

Requirement: Reservation records

The system SHALL store hotel reservations with reservation number, customer number, room number, check-in date, check-out date, status, deposit amount, and invoice number.

Scenario: Create reservation

  • WHEN a front desk user creates a reservation with a customer, room, check-in date, and check-out date
  • THEN the system stores the reservation with status Confirmed and the required reservation data.

Scenario: Update confirmed reservation

  • WHEN a front desk user updates dates, customer, room, or deposit amount on a Confirmed reservation
  • THEN the system validates the change and persists the updated reservation data.

Requirement: Room inventory

The system SHALL maintain hotel room inventory with room number, room type, nightly rate, and occupied state.

Scenario: Create room

  • WHEN an admin creates a room with room number, room type, and nightly rate
  • THEN the system stores the room and makes it available for reservation assignment.

Scenario: Track occupied room

  • WHEN a guest checks in to a reservation assigned to a room
  • THEN the system marks the room as occupied.

Requirement: Room assignment availability

The system MUST prevent overlapping use of the same room for active reservations.

Scenario: Prevent overbooking

  • WHEN a user assigns a room to a reservation whose stay dates overlap another Confirmed or Occupied reservation for the same room
  • THEN the system rejects the assignment and keeps the original room availability unchanged.

Scenario: Allow non-overlapping reservation

  • WHEN a user assigns a room to a reservation whose stay dates do not overlap existing Confirmed or Occupied reservations for the same room
  • THEN the system accepts the assignment.

Requirement: Reservation lifecycle statuses

The system SHALL support reservation statuses Confirmed, Occupied, and Closed, and SHALL enforce valid transitions.

Scenario: Check in guest

  • WHEN a front desk user checks in a Confirmed reservation with a valid room assignment
  • THEN the system changes the reservation status to Occupied and marks the room occupied.

Scenario: Checkout guest

  • WHEN a front desk user checks out an Occupied reservation after billing requirements are satisfied
  • THEN the system changes the reservation status to Closed and releases the room occupancy.

Scenario: Reject invalid checkout

  • WHEN a user attempts to check out a reservation that is not Occupied
  • THEN the system rejects the checkout.
specs/hotel-security/spec.md37 lines

ADDED Requirements

Requirement: Front desk permissions

The system SHALL allow Front Desk users to create reservations, assign rooms, capture deposits, check in guests, post service charges, and check out guests.

Scenario: Front desk operational workflow

  • WHEN a Front Desk user performs reservation creation, room assignment, deposit capture, check-in, service charge posting, or checkout
  • THEN the system allows the action when the reservation data is otherwise valid.

Scenario: Front desk finance restriction

  • WHEN a Front Desk user attempts to modify tax setup or directly adjust finance postings
  • THEN the system blocks the action.

Requirement: Finance permissions

The system SHALL allow Finance users to generate invoices, calculate and validate taxes, post and reconcile final payments, process refunds, and review ledger outcomes.

Scenario: Finance billing workflow

  • WHEN a Finance user generates invoices, validates taxes, posts final payments, processes refunds, or reviews ledger outcomes
  • THEN the system allows the action when the financial data is otherwise valid.

Requirement: Admin permissions

The system SHALL allow Admin users to configure hotel setup, manage room inventory setup, manage permissions, and maintain master data.

Scenario: Admin setup workflow

  • WHEN an Admin user configures hotel setup, creates or updates room inventory, manages permissions, or maintains master data
  • THEN the system allows the setup action.

Requirement: Unauthorized refund prevention

The system MUST restrict refund processing to Finance users.

Scenario: Block front desk refund

  • WHEN a Front Desk user attempts to process a refund
  • THEN the system rejects the refund and does not create refund or ledger outcomes.

Scenario: Allow finance refund

  • WHEN a Finance user processes a valid refund
  • THEN the system records the refund through the approved finance workflow.
design.md61 lines

Context

The workspace is a Business Central AL extension targeting application 27.0.0.0 with object IDs limited to 50000-50249. There are no existing application specs or AL source files, so this change establishes the hotel module from scratch while respecting the constitution: source lives under src/, app.json and .alpackages/ remain unchanged, all object names need a project affix, and completion during implementation requires a clean al compile.

The stakeholders are front desk users who run operational guest workflows, finance users who own invoicing, tax, final payment, refunds, and ledger review, and admins who configure hotel setup, rooms, permissions, and master data.

Goals / Non-Goals

Goals:

  • Model hotel rooms, reservations, reservation lifecycle statuses, deposits, service charges, invoice linkage, and payment state.
  • Enforce room availability, prevent overbooking, prevent duplicate invoicing, and avoid false payment success.
  • Generate Business Central sales invoices that include room charges, service charges, VAT behavior, applied deposits, and remaining amount due.
  • Separate front desk, finance, and admin responsibilities with AL permission sets and page/action exposure.
  • Provide focused automated tests for reservation lifecycle, billing, tax recalculation before invoicing, and refund authorization.

Non-Goals:

  • Online booking channel integration, housekeeping scheduling, loyalty programs, and multi-property management.
  • Custom payment gateway integration beyond deterministic internal payment result handling suitable for tests.
  • Replacement of Business Central VAT, customer, sales invoice, or ledger posting subsystems.

Decisions

  1. Use dedicated hotel tables rather than extending Sales Header as the reservation source of truth.

Reservation behavior has operational states and overlap rules that do not map cleanly to sales documents. Dedicated tables keep occupancy and guest lifecycle logic explicit, while sales invoices remain the financial output. Alternative considered: store reservations directly as sales quotes/orders; this would make occupancy checks and lifecycle security harder to isolate.

  1. Use affixed AL object names and compact table/field names.

Object names will use a consistent project affix such as BSB Hotel ..., and table/field names will stay within Business Central length constraints. Alternative considered: domain-obvious names like Reservation Management; those risk collisions with Base Application and violate the constitution.

  1. Keep lifecycle operations in codeunits instead of page triggers.

Codeunits will own reservation validation, deposit capture, service charge posting, check-in, checkout, invoice generation, VAT calculation handoff, final payment, and refund processing. Pages should call these services, and tests can exercise them directly. Alternative considered: implement logic in page actions only; that would reduce testability and allow inconsistent behavior across entry points.

  1. Use Business Central sales invoice and VAT posting behavior for final billing.

The hotel billing codeunit will create one posted invoice path per reservation using standard customer, item/G/L, VAT setup, and posting routines where available in the loaded symbols. Alternative considered: custom hotel ledger-only billing; this would duplicate financial behavior and increase risk of incorrect VAT or reconciliation.

  1. Represent failed payment states explicitly.

Deposit and final payment routines will only mark success after the payment operation returns success. Failed attempts remain visible without changing reservation or settlement state. Alternative considered: infer success from a nonzero amount; that would permit false success and weak negative-case coverage.

  1. Enforce refund authorization in both permissions and server-side code.

Finance permission sets and page exposure provide the user experience boundary, while the refund codeunit validates authorization before processing. Alternative considered: rely only on hidden UI actions; that would be bypassable through other entry points.

Risks / Trade-offs

  • Standard posting symbol differences could affect exact invoice APIs in application 27.0.0.0 -> Mitigate during implementation by compiling early against .alpackages/ and adapting to available Base Application symbols.
  • Business Central permission behavior can be hard to validate in isolated tests -> Mitigate with both permission-set definitions and codeunit-level authorization checks that can be tested deterministically.
  • VAT setup depends on configured posting groups and rates -> Mitigate with setup records and tests that configure VAT before invoice generation, then recalculate before posting.
  • Deposit application against invoices must preserve ledger correctness -> Mitigate by using standard customer/payment posting patterns where available and clearly linking deposit entries to reservations.

Migration Plan

This is a new module, so deployment creates new AL objects and no existing hotel data migration is required. Rollback removes the extension after ensuring no production financial postings depend on the new objects; posted Business Central documents remain governed by standard retention and audit rules.

Open Questions

  • Which standard posting account or item should represent room nights and service charges in production configuration?
  • Should deposit capture create immediate customer ledger/payment entries, or remain prepayment tracking until final invoice in the first implementation?
tasks.md49 lines

1. Project Structure and Object Planning

  • [x] 1.1 Read app.json and allocate AL object IDs within 50000-50249 for all tables, pages, enums, codeunits, permission sets, and tests.
  • [x] 1.2 Create the src/ folder structure for tables, pages, enums, codeunits, permissions, and tests using a consistent BSB Hotel object affix.
  • [x] 1.3 Add reservation status and service charge type enums for Confirmed, Occupied, Closed, and supported billable service categories.

2. Reservation and Room Data Model

  • [x] 2.1 Implement the room table with room number, room type, nightly rate, and occupied fields.
  • [x] 2.2 Implement the reservation table with reservation number, customer number, room number, check-in date, check-out date, status, deposit amount, deposit captured state, invoice number, and payment settlement state.
  • [x] 2.3 Implement the service charge table with reservation linkage, service type or description, amount, and posting state.
  • [x] 2.4 Add table validations for required fields, valid date ranges, nonnegative amounts, and lifecycle constraints.

3. Reservation Lifecycle Logic

  • [x] 3.1 Implement a reservation management codeunit for creating and updating reservations.
  • [x] 3.2 Implement room assignment validation that rejects overlapping Confirmed or Occupied reservations for the same room.
  • [x] 3.3 Implement check-in logic that requires a valid Confirmed reservation and marks the room occupied.
  • [x] 3.4 Implement checkout logic that requires an Occupied reservation, satisfied billing requirements, and releases room occupancy.

4. Billing and Payment Logic

  • [x] 4.1 Implement deposit capture logic that records success only when the payment operation succeeds.
  • [x] 4.2 Implement service charge posting logic for reservation-linked breakfast, laundry, transfer, and general service charges.
  • [x] 4.3 Implement invoice generation logic that creates one invoice per reservation and rejects duplicate invoice requests.
  • [x] 4.4 Implement room night and service charge invoice line calculation with eligible deposit application.
  • [x] 4.5 Integrate VAT calculation with current Business Central VAT setup at invoice generation time.
  • [x] 4.6 Implement final payment settlement logic that records settlement only after successful payment.

5. Security and User Experience

  • [x] 5.1 Create list and card pages for rooms, reservations, and service charges with role-appropriate actions.
  • [x] 5.2 Create permission sets for Front Desk, Finance, and Admin responsibilities.
  • [x] 5.3 Implement server-side refund authorization that permits only Finance users to process refunds.
  • [x] 5.4 Ensure Front Desk users cannot process refunds, modify tax setup, or directly adjust finance postings through hotel module actions.

6. Tests

  • [x] 6.1 Add tests for reservation creation, update, room assignment, check-in, checkout, and occupancy release.
  • [x] 6.2 Add tests for overbooking prevention and invalid lifecycle transitions.
  • [x] 6.3 Add tests for successful and failed deposit capture without false success.
  • [x] 6.4 Add tests for service charges, invoice generation, duplicate invoice prevention, VAT recalculation before invoicing, and final payment failure handling.
  • [x] 6.5 Add tests for Finance-only refund processing and Front Desk unauthorized refund prevention.

7. Verification

  • [x] 7.1 Run al compile -- /project:. /packagecachepath:./.alpackages /out:./bsbench-02-hotel-management-codex.app and fix all compiler errors.
  • [x] 7.2 Confirm all AL object IDs are within the configured range and all object names are globally affixed.
  • [x] 7.3 Review the implemented behavior against all OpenSpec scenarios before reporting completion.

Vendor Rebate Engine 04-vendor-management

Enterprise vendor rebate engine: 13 FRs, approval workflow, retroactive recalculation, job queue, integration events.

Speckit + Codex9 files
spec.md212 lines

Feature Specification: Global Rebate Engine

Feature Branch: 001-global-rebate-engine

Created: 2026-06-15

Status: Draft

Input: User description: "Global Enterprise Trade Promotion & Vendor Rebate Engine"

User Scenarios & Testing (mandatory)

User Story 1 - Manage and Activate Rebate Agreements (Priority: P1)

Finance, procurement, and sales operations users need to define vendor rebate and customer incentive agreements with clear eligibility rules, validity periods, parties, currencies, dimensions, approval status, and posting setup so approved agreements can be used for calculation with full auditability.

Why this priority: No accrual, settlement, or reconciliation process can be trusted until agreement setup, approval, activation, and version control are reliable.

Independent Test: Can be tested by creating a vendor rebate agreement, adding eligibility criteria and rules, submitting it for approval, approving it, activating it, then verifying that the active version is locked and usable for calculation.

Acceptance Scenarios:

  1. Given a user has agreement management permission, When they create an agreement with required parties, dates, eligibility criteria, rule details, and posting setup, Then the agreement can be submitted for approval.
  2. Given an agreement is missing required posting setup or contains conflicting active rules, When the user attempts activation, Then activation is blocked with specific validation messages.
  3. Given an agreement is active, When a manager changes business terms, Then a new version is created and prior versions remain readable, locked, and linked to prior calculations.
  4. Given an auditor reviews an agreement, When they compare two versions, Then they can see changed fields, effective dates, user, timestamp, and reason.

User Story 2 - Calculate Rebate Accruals for Eligible Transactions (Priority: P1)

Rebate analysts need to run manual or scheduled calculations that evaluate sales and purchase transactions against active agreement rules, record eligibility outcomes, calculate accrual amounts, and produce detailed entries that can be traced back to source transactions.

Why this priority: Automated, repeatable calculation is the core business value and replaces manual spreadsheets and fragmented legacy processes.

Independent Test: Can be tested by preparing approved agreements and source transactions, running a calculation for a date range and company scope, then verifying calculated amounts, rejected-line reasons, currency details, and request status.

Acceptance Scenarios:

  1. Given active agreements and unprocessed eligible source lines exist, When a calculation request is run, Then the system creates calculation ledger entries with agreement version, rule, source transaction, company, currency, exchange rate, and processing request references.
  2. Given a source line does not meet eligibility criteria, When the calculation request evaluates it, Then the system records a clear rejection reason without creating an accrual amount.
  3. Given a calculation request for the same processing scope has already completed, When the same request is run again without recalculation mode, Then duplicate calculation and duplicate accrual results are prevented.
  4. Given a high-volume calculation fails partway through, When the user retries the failed portion, Then completed chunks are preserved and only failed or pending chunks are resumed.

User Story 3 - Post, Reverse, and Reconcile Accruals (Priority: P1)

Controllers and rebate posters need calculated accruals to become auditable financial postings, with posting preview, balanced entries, dimensions, reversal support, duplicate-posting prevention, and reconciliation between accrual balances and financial results.

Why this priority: Financial correctness and audit traceability are required before the feature can support enterprise accounting processes.

Independent Test: Can be tested by calculating accruals, previewing postings, posting approved accruals, reversing them, and reconciling accrual entries to financial postings for a period.

Acceptance Scenarios:

  1. Given calculated accruals are ready for posting, When a permitted user previews posting, Then the preview shows balanced debit and credit impact with dimensions and posting groups.
  2. Given accrual entries are approved for posting, When a permitted user posts them, Then balanced financial entries are created and each accrual entry is marked with posting references.
  3. Given an accrual entry has already been posted, When any user attempts to post it again, Then duplicate posting is blocked.
  4. Given a posted accrual must be corrected, When a permitted user reverses it with a reason code, Then the system creates traceable reversal entries rather than editing posted records.

User Story 4 - Settle Approved Rebate Balances (Priority: P2)

Finance users need to create settlement proposals from open accrual balances, review and adjust proposed lines, route settlements for approval, post final settlements, and close selected accrual entries.

Why this priority: Settlement completes the commercial lifecycle by turning accrual balances into claims, credits, invoices, or financial adjustments.

Independent Test: Can be tested by generating a settlement proposal for a vendor or customer, approving it, posting it, and confirming selected accrual entries are closed.

Acceptance Scenarios:

  1. Given open approved accrual entries exist for a party and period, When a user creates a settlement proposal, Then eligible entries are grouped by party, agreement, company, currency, and settlement method.
  2. Given a settlement proposal is under review, When a user adjusts settlement amounts within permitted limits and provides a reason, Then the adjustment is retained in the settlement audit history.
  3. Given a settlement is rejected, When a user attempts posting, Then posting is blocked until the settlement is corrected and approved.
  4. Given a settlement is posted, When users view included accrual entries, Then those entries show closed status and settlement references.

User Story 5 - Recalculate Retroactive Changes and Post Deltas (Priority: P2)

Rebate managers and controllers need a controlled recalculation process when agreement terms change retroactively, so impacted historical transactions are recomputed and only delta adjustments are posted.

Why this priority: Enterprise rebate programs often change after transactions occur; corrections must be auditable and financially controlled.

Independent Test: Can be tested by changing agreement terms with a retroactive effective date, running recalculation for the affected period, and verifying old amounts, new amounts, deltas, and linked adjustment postings.

Acceptance Scenarios:

  1. Given an approved retroactive agreement version exists, When recalculation runs for the impacted scope, Then the system identifies historical transactions that used the prior version.
  2. Given impacted transactions are recalculated, When old and new rebate amounts differ, Then the system creates delta entries linked to original calculation entries.
  3. Given a recalculation produces no difference, When results are reviewed, Then the system records the no-change outcome without creating financial adjustments.
  4. Given a recalculation requires approval, When it is not approved, Then delta postings are blocked.

User Story 6 - Monitor Background Jobs, Integrations, and Audit Evidence (Priority: P3)

System administrators, integration support teams, and auditors need visibility into long-running processing, external messages, retries, failures, approvals, data changes, and audit exports across companies and regions.

Why this priority: Enterprise operations require transparency, retry control, and audit evidence once core agreement, calculation, posting, and settlement flows are established.

Independent Test: Can be tested by running background calculations, importing an external agreement reference, forcing retryable failures, reviewing logs, and exporting audit data for a selected period.

Acceptance Scenarios:

  1. Given a long-running job is active, When a user opens the job monitor, Then they can see scope, progress, completed chunks, failed chunks, retry eligibility, and current status.
  2. Given an external message has a unique external reference, When it is processed successfully, Then repeat delivery of the same reference does not create duplicate business records.
  3. Given an integration request fails with a retryable error, When an authorized user retries it, Then the retry is logged and linked to the original request.
  4. Given an auditor exports audit evidence, When they filter by period, company, user, agreement, or source document, Then exported records include user, timestamp, action, old value, new value, reason, and related record.

Edge Cases

  • Agreements with overlapping validity, party, item, location, country, or dimension criteria must be detected and either blocked or clearly prioritized before activation.
  • Source lines in currencies different from the agreement or company reporting currency must preserve the exchange rate and currency basis used for calculation.
  • Transactions that are reversed, corrected, settled, or already processed must not be silently recalculated unless the user runs an authorized recalculation process.
  • Posting failure after partial preparation must leave a traceable recovery path and must not create unbalanced financial results.
  • Suspended, expired, rejected, or draft agreements must not be used for new calculations.
  • Failed calculation chunks must be retryable without duplicating completed ledger entries.
  • Settlement proposals must handle mixed currencies, closed entries, reversed entries, and entries already selected by another open proposal.
  • Integration messages with duplicate external reference IDs must be idempotent across retries.
  • Users without the required role must be blocked from approval, posting, setup changes, audit export, and sensitive integration maintenance.
  • High-volume processing must avoid blocking normal users from posting everyday business transactions.

Requirements (mandatory)

Functional Requirements

  • FR-001: System MUST allow authorized users to create, view, modify, submit, approve, activate, suspend, expire, and archive vendor rebate and customer incentive agreements.
  • FR-002: System MUST support agreement attributes for agreement type, parties, party groups, items, item categories, locations, countries or regions, dimensions, validity dates, currencies, settlement method, thresholds, and posting behavior.
  • FR-003: System MUST validate that required agreement setup, approval status, dates, rules, thresholds, and posting configuration are complete before activation.
  • FR-004: System MUST create immutable agreement versions when active agreement terms change, and MUST link each calculation to the agreement version used.
  • FR-005: System MUST allow auditors to compare agreement versions and view changed fields, user, timestamp, reason, and effective period.
  • FR-006: System MUST allow authorized users to configure standard rule bases including sales amount, purchase amount, quantity, margin, payment date, invoice date, and shipment or receipt date.
  • FR-007: System MUST allow standard calculation methods including fixed amount, percentage, tiered percentage, slab amount, formula reference, growth-based calculation, and inclusion or exclusion criteria.
  • FR-008: System MUST detect conflicting, overlapping, or incomplete rules before activation and present actionable validation messages.
  • FR-009: System MUST identify eligible sales and purchase source lines for a requested company, party, date range, and agreement scope.
  • FR-010: System MUST record whether each evaluated source line was eligible or rejected, including the reason and rule context.
  • FR-011: System MUST prevent already processed source lines from being processed again unless an authorized recalculation mode is used.
  • FR-012: System MUST produce detailed calculation ledger entries containing source transaction, agreement version, rule, company, currency, exchange rate, calculation basis, calculated amount, status, and processing request.
  • FR-013: System MUST calculate accrual balances from calculation ledger entries and support manual, scheduled, and resumable processing.
  • FR-014: System MUST make repeated execution for the same completed processing scope idempotent.
  • FR-015: System MUST support posting preview for accruals, reversals, settlements, and adjustments before final posting.
  • FR-016: System MUST create balanced financial postings for accruals, reversals, settlements, and adjustments, with dimensions and posting groups carried from configured and source data.
  • FR-017: System MUST prevent duplicate financial posting for the same accrual, settlement, reversal, or adjustment.
  • FR-018: System MUST keep posted calculation entries, posted accrual entries, posted settlements, and financial references immutable; corrections MUST be represented by reversal or delta entries.
  • FR-019: System MUST allow authorized users to initiate recalculation by agreement, company, party, date range, or source scope.
  • FR-020: System MUST identify impacted historical transactions during recalculation and compute previous amount, revised amount, delta amount, and link to original entries.
  • FR-021: System MUST post only approved delta adjustments or reversals for recalculation outcomes.
  • FR-022: System MUST allow users to create settlement proposals from open approved accrual balances and group entries by party, agreement, company, currency, period, and settlement method.
  • FR-023: System MUST allow authorized users to review, adjust with reason, approve, reject, and post settlement proposals.
  • FR-024: System MUST close selected accrual entries when settlement is posted and retain references between settlement, accrual, calculation, source transaction, and financial posting.
  • FR-025: System MUST support approval workflows for agreements, recalculations, settlements, and material adjustments.
  • FR-026: System MUST route approvals using amount, region, company, agreement type, department, and role-based criteria.
  • FR-027: System MUST record approval history including submitter, approver, action, timestamp, comments, delegation, rejection, and requested changes.
  • FR-028: System MUST enforce separation of duties for setup, agreement management, approval, calculation, posting, administration, and audit export.
  • FR-029: System MUST maintain audit records for business-critical changes and postings, including user, timestamp, action, old value, new value, reason, and related record.
  • FR-030: System MUST prevent users from editing audit records through normal user interfaces.
  • FR-031: System MUST process high-volume calculation and recalculation work in monitored chunks with progress, failure status, retry eligibility, and recovery details.
  • FR-032: System MUST classify errors as validation, configuration, posting, integration, concurrency, or unexpected system errors.
  • FR-033: System MUST store user-facing message, technical details, related record, retry eligibility, timestamp, and user or job context for each processing error.
  • FR-034: System MUST expose controlled integration capabilities for agreement import, calculation status, settlement export, and audit data exchange.
  • FR-035: System MUST log integration requests and responses, enforce unique external reference IDs, and support retry of failed external messages.
  • FR-036: System MUST protect sensitive integration values and commercial agreement data from unauthorized viewing or modification.
  • FR-037: System MUST provide operational and financial reporting views for accrual balances, settlement status, calculation results, exceptions, audit history, and reconciliation.
  • FR-038: System MUST allow reports to be filtered by company, period, party, agreement, currency, status, and source document.
  • FR-039: System MUST support multi-company, multi-currency, and multi-country operation without hardcoded company, currency, region, posting, or tax assumptions.
  • FR-040: System MUST allow future country-specific eligibility, posting, tax, and settlement behavior to be added without changing existing agreement history or posted records.

Key Entities (include if feature involves data)

  • Rebate Agreement: Commercial arrangement defining agreement type, parties, eligibility scope, validity, rules, settlement method, approval state, and posting behavior.
  • Agreement Version: Immutable snapshot of approved agreement terms used for calculation and audit comparison.
  • Rebate Rule: Business condition and calculation definition that determines eligibility and rebate amount.
  • Threshold: Volume, amount, quantity, margin, growth, or period boundary used by tiered, slab, or growth-based rules.
  • Calculation Request: User- or schedule-created processing scope with company, period, party, agreement, status, progress, and retry details.
  • Calculation Ledger Entry: Detailed result of evaluating a source transaction line against an agreement version and rule.
  • Accrual Entry: Financial estimate created from calculation results before final settlement, including open, posted, reversed, or closed status.
  • Settlement Proposal: Group of eligible open accrual entries prepared for review, approval, adjustment, and posting.
  • Settlement Line: Individual accrual or adjustment included in a settlement proposal with amount, currency, status, and source references.
  • Recalculation Result: Comparison of original and revised calculation amounts, including delta, reason, impacted source, and link to original entries.
  • Audit Entry: Immutable record of business-critical changes, approvals, postings, reversals, exports, and administrative actions.
  • Integration Message: External request or response with external reference ID, status, payload summary, retry state, and error details.
  • Processing Error: Classified error record with user-facing message, technical details, related record, retry eligibility, and context.
  • Posting Setup: Company-specific configuration that determines how accruals, settlements, reversals, and adjustments are financially classified.

Success Criteria (mandatory)

Measurable Outcomes

  • SC-001: Finance users can configure, approve, activate, and audit a complete vendor rebate agreement in one controlled workflow without spreadsheet tracking.
  • SC-002: The system can evaluate at least 1,000,000 source lines in a batch window while preserving retryable progress and without blocking normal business posting activity.
  • SC-003: Re-running a completed calculation for the same scope without recalculation mode creates zero duplicate calculation entries and zero duplicate financial postings.
  • SC-004: 100% of posted accruals, settlements, reversals, and delta adjustments can be traced to source transaction, agreement version, rule, processing request, user action, and financial posting reference.
  • SC-005: Recalculation for retroactive agreement changes reports previous amount, revised amount, and delta for every impacted transaction, and posts only approved deltas.
  • SC-006: Settlement users can create, review, approve, and post a settlement proposal from open accrual balances, and all selected entries are closed after posting.
  • SC-007: Auditors can export complete audit evidence for a selected company and period, including agreement changes, approvals, postings, reversals, integration events, and user actions.
  • SC-008: Integration retries using the same external reference ID do not create duplicate agreements, settlement exports, audit exports, or status records.
  • SC-009: Role-based access prevents unauthorized users from completing restricted setup, approval, posting, audit export, and sensitive integration activities in all tested scenarios.
  • SC-010: Reconciliation users can identify unmatched, failed, reversed, pending, posted, and settled accruals by company, period, party, agreement, and currency.

Assumptions

  • The first releasable scope includes vendor rebate and customer incentive agreements in a shared agreement model with agreement type distinguishing behavior.
  • Standard settlement outputs include vendor credit memo, customer credit memo, journal adjustment, or custom settlement ledger entry; the specific default can be selected during planning.
  • Phase-one source documents include posted sales and purchase invoice lines, with other shipments, receipts, and historical sources added through later enhancements if required.
  • Native approval and controlled workflow behavior are required in the core product experience; external workflow tools may be integrated later.
  • Local tax and posting variations are expected, but the core specification treats them as country-specific extensions rather than hardcoded phase-one behavior.
  • Sensitive integration credentials and commercially sensitive agreement data are available only to authorized roles.
  • Historical agreement versions, posted entries, audit records, and external reference records are retained according to enterprise financial record retention policy.
  • Out-of-scope MVP items are AI-assisted forecasting, vendor self-service portal, advanced analytics semantic model, real-time data lake streaming, full marketplace packaging, and automated legal contract generation.
plan.md153 lines

Implementation Plan: Global Rebate Engine

Branch: "" | Date: 2026-06-15 | Spec: spec.md

Input: Feature specification from /specs/001-global-rebate-engine/spec.md

Summary

Implement an enterprise rebate, incentive, accrual, settlement, recalculation, audit, and reconciliation capability as a Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central AL extension. The MVP uses a shared agreement model for vendor rebates and customer incentives, immutable versioning and posted entries, chunked calculation requests, approval-gated settlement and recalculation flows, and integration-facing API pages/logs. Source code will be placed under src/, use only object IDs in 50000..50249, compile against application 27.0.0.0 and runtime 16.0, and reference only symbols provided in .alpackages/.

Technical Context

Language/Version: AL, runtime 16.0, Business Central application 27.0.0.0, platform 1.0.0.0

Primary Dependencies: Microsoft Application, Base Application, Business Foundation, System Application, and System symbols from .alpackages/; no external app dependencies declared in app.json

Storage: Business Central extension tables and ledger-style append-only records; company-scoped setup and entries unless explicitly modeled as cross-company request metadata

Testing: AL test codeunits and Business Central test runner/test toolkit conventions; validation through publish/sync/test in a Business Central sandbox configured with the supplied symbols

Target Platform: Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central SaaS or compatible sandbox matching application 27.0

Project Type: Business Central AL extension

Performance Goals: Evaluate at least 1,000,000 source lines per batch window using chunked requests, resumable progress, indexed lookups, SetLoadFields, and temporary calculation buffers where appropriate

Constraints: Do not modify app.json or .alpackages/; do not change app id, name, publisher, or version; all AL source files must be under src/; use only object IDs 50000..50249; extend Microsoft base objects with extensions rather than modifying base objects; avoid APIs or syntax newer than runtime 16.0 / application 27.0

Scale/Scope: Multi-company, multi-currency, multi-country rebate processing for posted sales and purchase invoice lines in MVP, with extensibility points for additional source documents and country-specific behavior

Constitution Check

GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.

The current constitution file contains placeholder principles only and defines no enforceable project-specific gates. The plan therefore applies the explicit prompt constraints and Spec Kit quality expectations as the active gates:

  • PASS: AL project constraints are captured, including application/runtime version and object ID range.
  • PASS: Existing app.json and .alpackages/ are treated as read-only inputs.
  • PASS: Source layout confines AL files to src/.
  • PASS: Financial correctness is protected through immutable posted entries, reversals, deltas, approval gates, and audit records.
  • PASS: Performance and reliability are addressed through chunked background processing, retry-safe requests, idempotency keys, and recovery logs.

Post-design re-check:

  • PASS: Research decisions resolve technical unknowns without adding dependencies outside app.json.
  • PASS: Data model uses table/page/codeunit responsibilities that fit the allowed object range.
  • PASS: Contracts document external API page behavior and idempotency requirements without requiring unavailable symbols.
  • PASS: Quickstart validates agreement, calculation, posting, settlement, recalculation, audit, and integration flows.

Project Structure

Documentation (this feature)

specs/001-global-rebate-engine/
├── plan.md
├── research.md
├── data-model.md
├── quickstart.md
├── contracts/
│   ├── api-contract.md
│   └── event-contract.md
├── checklists/
│   └── requirements.md
└── tasks.md

Source Code (repository root)

├── reports/
│   ├── RebateAccrualSummary.Report.al
│   ├── RebateAccrualDetail.Report.al
│   ├── RebateSettlementStatement.Report.al
│   ├── RebateAgreementProfitability.Report.al
│   ├── RebateReconciliation.Report.al
│   ├── RebateException.Report.al
│   └── RebateAudit.Report.al
├── permissions/
│   └── RebatePermissions.PermissionSet.al
