    ├── RebateAgreementTests.Codeunit.al
    ├── RebateCalculationTests.Codeunit.al
    ├── RebatePostingTests.Codeunit.al
    ├── RebateSettlementTests.Codeunit.al
    ├── RebateRecalculationTests.Codeunit.al
    ├── RebateIntegrationTests.Codeunit.al
    └── RebatePerformanceHarness.Codeunit.al

Structure Decision: Use a single Business Central AL extension with domain folders under src/. The folder split reflects AL object types and keeps generated task work easy to assign while preserving the requirement that all source files live under src/.

Complexity Tracking

No constitution violations are present. The design is broad because the feature is enterprise-scale, but it remains a single extension and uses standard Business Central extension concepts.

research.md71 lines

Research: Global Rebate Engine

Decision: Build as a single AL extension first

Rationale: app.json is preconfigured as one AL app with no external dependencies and object IDs 50000..50249. A single extension keeps the MVP deployable, testable, and easier to publish in the current repository while still allowing later separation into finance, workflow, integration, localization, or analytics apps.

Alternatives considered: Multiple extensions matching the proposed business app split. Rejected for MVP because it would require dependency boundaries, separate app manifests, and more object allocation before core behavior is validated.

Decision: Shared agreement model for vendor rebates and customer incentives

Rationale: The specification assumes a shared model distinguished by agreement type. This reduces duplicated setup, versioning, rules, approval, audit, and calculation code. Type-specific behavior can be handled by enum values, posting setup, and integration events.

Alternatives considered: Separate vendor and customer agreement tables. Rejected for MVP because rule, version, threshold, audit, and calculation requirements are largely identical and duplication would increase reconciliation risk.

Decision: MVP source scope is posted sales and purchase invoice lines

Rationale: The spec assumptions identify posted sales and purchase invoice lines as phase-one source documents. These provide stable posted financial source data and are the most direct path to accrual and settlement validation.

Alternatives considered: Include shipments, receipts, orders, and all historical documents immediately. Rejected for MVP because each source introduces different lifecycle and correction behavior. Additional source types should be added through source evaluator events after the invoice-line flow is stable.

Decision: Immutable ledger-style entries for calculations, accruals, settlements, and audit

Rationale: Financial correctness requires traceability and non-destructive correction. Posted calculation entries, accrual entries, settlement references, and audit records should not be edited after posting; corrections use reversals or delta entries linked to originals.

Alternatives considered: Updating prior entries in place. Rejected because it breaks auditability, undermines reconciliation, and conflicts with the specification.

Decision: Chunked calculation requests with idempotency keys

Rationale: The feature must process up to 1,000,000 source lines per batch window and resume after failure. Calculation requests should track scope, chunk number, status, retries, and a deterministic processing key so completed work is not duplicated.

Alternatives considered: Single long-running transaction. Rejected because it risks locks, timeouts, and unrecoverable partial work.

Decision: Posting through controlled journal/document preparation and preview

Rationale: Accruals, reversals, settlements, and adjustments require balanced financial impact, dimensions, posting groups, and posting preview. The posting engine should prepare posting buffers and persist references only after successful posting.

Alternatives considered: Directly creating financial ledger entries. Rejected because Business Central financial posting must respect posting routines, validation, dimensions, and audit controls available in the base application.

Decision: Native approval state tracking for MVP

Rationale: The spec requires approval for agreements, recalculations, and settlements, and the prompt calls for the core product experience. MVP can implement explicit statuses, approval history, and role checks while leaving workflow integration extensible.

Alternatives considered: Rely exclusively on external approval automation. Rejected because it would make core financial controls dependent on integration availability and would not prove the native lifecycle.

Decision: API pages plus integration log for external interfaces

Rationale: Business Central AL supports API pages as a natural external interface pattern. Each inbound or outbound message needs an external reference ID, status, retry metadata, and error details for idempotency and audit.

Alternatives considered: File-only import/export. Rejected for enterprise integration because it does not satisfy calculation status, settlement export, retry, and external reference requirements as cleanly.

Decision: Use integration events for country-specific and custom rule logic

Rationale: The feature must support future localization and customer-specific eligibility without changing the core engine. Integration events before and after validation, eligibility, calculation, posting preparation, settlement grouping, and recalculation provide stable extension points.

Alternatives considered: Hardcoded country branches in the core engine. Rejected because this conflicts with multi-country extensibility and upgrade safety.

Decision: Object allocation by domain within 50000..50249

Rationale: The allowed ID range is limited and must cover tables, enums, codeunits, pages, API pages, reports, permission sets, and test objects. Reserve contiguous subranges by object category and leave gaps for additions.

Alternatives considered: Allocate IDs only when implementing. Rejected because this feature spans many objects and needs early collision control.

Recommended allocation:

  • Tables: 50000..50049
  • Enums: 50050..50074
  • Codeunits: 50075..50119
  • Pages and API pages: 50120..50169
  • Reports: 50170..50194
  • Permission sets and queries/XMLports if needed: 50195..50209
  • Tests and harness objects: 50210..50249
data-model.md232 lines

Data Model: Global Rebate Engine

Rebate Agreement Header

Represents the commercial agreement header shared by vendor rebates and customer incentives.

Fields: Agreement No., Agreement Type, Description, Status, Vendor No., Customer No., Customer Group Code, Currency Code, Starting Date, Ending Date, Country/Region Code, Location Code, Global Dimension 1 Code, Global Dimension 2 Code, Settlement Method, Posting Setup Code, Current Version No., Created By, Created DateTime, Last Modified By, Last Modified DateTime.

Relationships: Has many Agreement Lines, Rule Headers, Agreement Versions, Calculation Requests, Calculation Ledger Entries, Accrual Entries, and Settlement Lines.

Validation Rules:

  • Agreement Type must be Vendor Rebate or Customer Incentive.
  • Vendor No. is required for vendor rebate agreements unless a vendor group is used.
  • Customer No. or Customer Group Code is required for customer incentive agreements.
  • Starting Date must be on or before Ending Date.
  • Posting Setup Code is required before activation.
  • Active date and eligibility overlap must be validated against other active agreements and rules.

State Transitions: Draft -> Pending Approval -> Approved -> Active -> Suspended -> Expired. Rejected returns to Draft through controlled revision.

Rebate Agreement Line

Stores eligibility dimensions and filters below the agreement header.

Fields: Agreement No., Line No., Type, Item No., Item Category Code, Vendor No., Customer No., Customer Group Code, Location Code, Country/Region Code, Dimension Code, Dimension Value Code, Include/Exclude, Starting Date, Ending Date.

Relationships: Belongs to Rebate Agreement Header.

Validation Rules:

  • Include/exclude criteria must not create unresolved conflicts for the same scope.
  • Line dates must fall within agreement dates unless blank and inherited from header.

Rebate Agreement Version

Immutable approved snapshot of agreement header, lines, rules, thresholds, posting setup, and effective period.

Fields: Agreement No., Version No., Effective From, Effective To, Status, Snapshot DateTime, Snapshot By, Change Reason Code, Previous Version No., Snapshot Payload Summary.

Relationships: Belongs to Agreement Header; referenced by Calculation Ledger Entries and Recalculation Results.

Validation Rules:

  • Versions used by calculations cannot be deleted or edited.
  • Version effective periods for the same agreement must not conflict.

Rebate Rule Header

Represents a rule group under an agreement.

Fields: Agreement No., Rule No., Description, Rule Basis, Calculation Method, Priority, Starting Date, Ending Date, Status, Formula Reference, Stop Further Processing, Currency Code.

Relationships: Belongs to Agreement Header; has many Rule Lines and Thresholds; referenced by Calculation Ledger Entries.

Validation Rules:

  • Rule Basis and Calculation Method are required.
  • Active rules must be valid for the agreement date range.
  • Priority must be deterministic when multiple rules can apply.

Rebate Rule Line

Defines conditions for rule eligibility.

Fields: Agreement No., Rule No., Line No., Field Type, Operator, Value, Include/Exclude, Required, Rejection Reason Code.

Relationships: Belongs to Rule Header.

Validation Rules:

  • Operator must be valid for the selected field type.
  • Required criteria must have values.

Rebate Threshold

Defines tier, slab, growth, quantity, amount, or margin boundaries.

Fields: Agreement No., Rule No., Threshold No., Minimum Value, Maximum Value, Rate Percent, Fixed Amount, Currency Code, Applies To Increment Only.

Relationships: Belongs to Rule Header.

Validation Rules:

  • Minimum Value must be less than or equal to Maximum Value when Maximum Value is present.
  • Threshold ranges for the same rule must not overlap unless explicitly configured as cumulative tiers.

Rebate Calculation Request

Represents a manual or scheduled processing request.

Fields: Request No., Request Type, Agreement No., Company Name, Vendor No., Customer No., Starting Date, Ending Date, Recalculation Mode, Status, Total Chunks, Completed Chunks, Failed Chunks, Created By, Created DateTime, Started DateTime, Completed DateTime, Idempotency Key, Last Error No.

Relationships: Has many Calculation Ledger Entries, Job Queue Logs, Processing Errors, and Recalculation Results.

Validation Rules:

  • Date range is required.
  • Idempotency Key must be unique for completed non-recalculation scope.
  • Recalculation Mode requires authorization and reason.

State Transitions: Draft -> Ready -> Processing -> Completed. Processing -> Failed -> Ready is allowed for retry. Processing -> Cancelled is allowed before posting.

Rebate Calculation Ledger Entry

Detailed immutable result of source-line evaluation.

Fields: Entry No., Request No., Company Name, Agreement No., Agreement Version No., Rule No., Source Document Type, Source Document No., Source Line No., Source Posting Date, Source Party No., Source Item No., Source Quantity, Source Amount, Source Currency Code, Exchange Rate, Calculation Basis Amount, Rebate Amount, Rebate Currency Code, Eligibility Status, Rejection Reason, Processing Key, Original Entry No.

Relationships: Belongs to Calculation Request; references Agreement Version, Rule Header, Source Transaction, Accrual Entry, and Recalculation Result.

Validation Rules:

  • Processing Key must prevent duplicate entries for the same scope and source line.
  • Posted or accrued entries are immutable.

Rebate Accrual Entry

Represents accrual balance created from calculation results.

Fields: Entry No., Calculation Entry No., Request No., Agreement No., Agreement Version No., Company Name, Party Type, Party No., Posting Date, Period Code, Amount, Currency Code, Amount LCY, Status, Posting Reference, Reversal Entry No., Settlement No., Closed, Reason Code.

Relationships: Created from Calculation Ledger Entry; referenced by Settlement Lines and Reversal/Delta entries.

Validation Rules:

  • Posted entries cannot be edited.
  • Closed entries cannot be selected for new settlement proposals.
  • Reversals must link to original entry.

State Transitions: Open -> Posted -> Selected for Settlement -> Settled/Closed. Posted -> Reversed through reversal entry.

Rebate Settlement Header

Represents settlement proposal and posted settlement.

Fields: Settlement No., Settlement Type, Status, Company Name, Party Type, Party No., Agreement No., Currency Code, Period Start, Period End, Total Amount, Approval Status, Posting Date, Posted, Posted By, Posted DateTime, External Reference ID.

Relationships: Has many Settlement Lines; references Accrual Entries, Approval History, Audit Entries, and Integration Logs.

Validation Rules:

  • Settlement must include at least one open eligible accrual entry.
  • Rejected or unapproved settlements cannot be posted.
  • Posted settlements are immutable.

State Transitions: Draft -> Pending Approval -> Approved -> Posted. Pending Approval -> Rejected -> Draft is allowed through correction.

Rebate Settlement Line

Represents accrual entry, adjustment, or settlement detail.

Fields: Settlement No., Line No., Accrual Entry No., Agreement No., Calculation Entry No., Description, Amount, Currency Code, Adjustment Amount, Reason Code, Closed.

Relationships: Belongs to Settlement Header; references Accrual Entry.

Validation Rules:

  • Adjustment Amount requires reason code.
  • Same open accrual entry cannot be selected by multiple open settlements.

Recalculation Result

Stores comparison between original and revised calculation.

Fields: Result No., Request No., Original Calculation Entry No., New Calculation Entry No., Agreement No., Original Version No., Revised Version No., Original Amount, Revised Amount, Delta Amount, Status, Delta Accrual Entry No., Reason Code.

Relationships: Belongs to Calculation Request; references Calculation Ledger Entries and Accrual Entries.

Validation Rules:

  • Delta posting requires approval.
  • Zero-delta results are recorded but do not create postings.

Rebate Audit Entry

Immutable evidence for business-critical activity.

Fields: Entry No., Source Table ID, Source Record ID, Source Document No., Action, User ID, DateTime, Field No., Field Caption, Old Value, New Value, Reason Code, Company Name, Exported, External Reference ID.

Relationships: References any rebate source record by record ID/document number.

Validation Rules:

  • Audit entries cannot be edited from user pages.
  • Critical changes must include reason code where configured.

Rebate Integration Log

Tracks inbound and outbound external messages.

Fields: Entry No., Direction, Interface Type, External Reference ID, Status, Request Summary, Response Summary, Retry Count, Retry Eligible, Last Error No., Created DateTime, Last Attempt DateTime, Related Record ID.

Relationships: References Agreements, Calculation Requests, Settlements, Audit Exports, and Processing Errors.

Validation Rules:

  • External Reference ID must be unique for the same direction and interface type.
  • Retry count must be capped by setup.

Rebate Job Queue Log

Tracks chunked background work.

Fields: Entry No., Request No., Job Type, Chunk No., Status, Started DateTime, Completed DateTime, Records Scanned, Records Processed, Records Failed, Retry Count, Last Error No.

Relationships: Belongs to Calculation Request or Recalculation Request.

Validation Rules:

  • Completed chunks cannot be processed again unless explicitly reset by authorized recovery.

Rebate Setup

Company-level setup for processing behavior.

Fields: Primary Key, Default Chunk Size, Max Retry Count, Enable Auto Posting, Require Reason Codes, Audit Export Retention, Default Settlement Method, Integration Enabled.

Relationships: Used by calculation, posting, settlement, integration, and audit engines.

Rebate Posting Setup

Company-level posting classification.

Fields: Posting Setup Code, Agreement Type, Party Type, Accrual Account, Expense Account, Receivable Account, Payable Account, Settlement Account, Reversal Account, Dimension Policy, Active.

Relationships: Referenced by Agreement Header and Posting Engine.

Validation Rules:

  • Required accounts must be present before agreements using the setup can activate.
  • Inactive posting setup cannot be used for new activation.
quickstart.md88 lines

Quickstart: Global Rebate Engine

This guide defines validation scenarios for the implementation phase. It assumes a Business Central sandbox compatible with application 27.0.0.0, runtime 16.0, and the symbols already present in .alpackages/.

Prerequisites

  1. Keep app.json and .alpackages/ unchanged.
  2. Place all AL source files under src/.
  3. Use only object IDs from 50000..50249.
  4. Publish and sync the extension to a sandbox after implementation.
  5. Assign rebate roles for setup, manager, analyst, approver, poster, administrator, and auditor scenarios.

Validation Scenario 1: Agreement Activation

  1. Create Rebate Setup and Rebate Posting Setup for a test company.
  2. Create a vendor rebate agreement with dates, vendor, currency, item or item category eligibility, and posting setup.
  3. Add a percentage rule and threshold.
  4. Submit the agreement for approval.
  5. Approve and activate the agreement.

Expected outcome: Agreement status becomes Active, version 1 is immutable, audit entries exist, and activation is blocked if posting setup is removed.

Validation Scenario 2: Calculation and Idempotency

  1. Post or prepare representative posted purchase invoice lines that match the active agreement.
  2. Create a calculation request for the agreement and posting date range.
  3. Run calculation manually.
  4. Run the same completed request scope again without recalculation mode.

Expected outcome: Calculation ledger entries are created once, rejected lines have reasons, currency and exchange-rate details are recorded, and the second run creates no duplicates.

Validation Scenario 3: Accrual Posting and Reversal

  1. Review calculated accrual balances.
  2. Run posting preview.
  3. Post accrual entries as a user with posting permission.
  4. Attempt to post the same accruals again.
  5. Reverse one posted accrual with a reason code.

Expected outcome: Financial impact is balanced, dimensions and posting setup are applied, duplicate posting is blocked, and reversal creates linked entries without editing posted records.

Validation Scenario 4: Settlement

  1. Create a settlement proposal from open approved accrual entries.
  2. Review proposal lines and adjust one line with a reason code if permitted.
  3. Submit, approve, and post the settlement.

Expected outcome: Settlement closes selected accrual entries, posted settlement is immutable, rejected settlements cannot post, and settlement references are visible from accrual entries.

Validation Scenario 5: Recalculation

  1. Create a revised agreement version with a retroactive effective date.
  2. Create a recalculation request for the impacted agreement and date range.
  3. Run recalculation and review old amount, new amount, and delta.
  4. Approve and post delta adjustments.

Expected outcome: Original calculation entries remain unchanged, delta entries link to originals, zero-delta rows do not post, and only approved deltas affect financial balances.

Validation Scenario 6: Background Retry

  1. Create a calculation request with a chunk size small enough to produce multiple chunks.
  2. Force or simulate a retryable validation/configuration failure in one chunk.
  3. Correct the issue and retry failed chunks.

Expected outcome: Completed chunks are not duplicated, failed chunks retry successfully, job progress is visible, and recovery details remain in logs.

Validation Scenario 7: Integration Idempotency

  1. Send an agreement import payload with a unique external reference ID.
  2. Send the same payload again with the same external reference ID.
  3. Query calculation status and settlement export using integration-facing pages.

Expected outcome: Duplicate import does not create a duplicate agreement, integration logs retain request/response status, and status/export responses honor authorization.

Validation Scenario 8: Audit and Security

  1. Change agreement setup, approve records, post accruals, post a settlement, reverse an accrual, and export audit entries.
  2. Repeat restricted actions with a user lacking the required role.

Expected outcome: Audit entries include user, timestamp, action, old value, new value, reason, and related record. Unauthorized setup, approval, posting, export, and sensitive integration actions are blocked.

Validation Scenario 9: Performance Harness

  1. Generate or load representative source data for 100,000 lines.
  2. Run calculation and record processing time, chunk progress, failures, and retries.
  3. Repeat with 1,000,000 source lines when the environment can support the data volume.

Expected outcome: Processing remains chunked and resumable, normal posting activity is not blocked, and the observed timing supports the batch-window target.

tasks.md353 lines

Tasks: Global Rebate Engine

Input: Design documents from /specs/001-global-rebate-engine/

Prerequisites: plan.md, spec.md, research.md, data-model.md, contracts/

Tests: Included because the specification explicitly requires unit, integration, performance, and regression testing.

Organization: Tasks are grouped by user story to enable independent implementation and testing.

Format: [ID] [P?] [Story] Description

  • [P]: Can run in parallel because it targets different files and has no dependency on incomplete tasks in the same phase
  • [Story]: Maps to a user story from spec.md
  • Every task includes concrete file paths

Phase 1: Setup (Shared Infrastructure)

Purpose: Establish the AL source layout, object allocation discipline, and shared test structure.

  • [X] T001 Create source directories src/tables, src/enums, src/codeunits, src/pages, src/api, src/reports, src/permissions, and src/tests
  • [X] T002 Create object allocation reference in src/ObjectIdAllocation.md covering IDs 50000..50249 from app.json
  • [X] T003 [P] Create agreement and status enums in src/enums/RebateAgreementType.Enum.al and src/enums/RebateAgreementStatus.Enum.al
  • [X] T004 [P] Create calculation enums in src/enums/RebateCalculationMethod.Enum.al, src/enums/RebateRuleBasis.Enum.al, and src/enums/RebateProcessingStatus.Enum.al
  • [X] T005 [P] Create settlement and error enums in src/enums/RebateSettlementMethod.Enum.al and src/enums/RebateErrorCategory.Enum.al
  • [X] T006 Create base permission set shell in src/permissions/RebatePermissions.PermissionSet.al

Phase 2: Foundational (Blocking Prerequisites)

Purpose: Core setup, logging, audit, permissions, and shared services that all user stories depend on.

Critical: No user story work should begin until this phase is complete.

  • [X] T007 [P] Create company processing setup table in src/tables/RebateSetup.Table.al
  • [X] T008 [P] Create posting setup table in src/tables/RebatePostingSetup.Table.al
  • [X] T009 [P] Create audit entry table in src/tables/RebateAuditEntry.Table.al
  • [X] T010 [P] Create integration log table in src/tables/RebateIntegrationLog.Table.al
  • [X] T011 [P] Create job queue log table in src/tables/RebateJobQueueLog.Table.al
  • [X] T012 [P] Create setup pages in src/pages/RebateSetup.Page.al and src/pages/RebatePostingSetup.Page.al
  • [X] T013 [P] Create audit entries page in src/pages/RebateAuditEntries.Page.al
  • [X] T014 Implement audit manager with immutable audit insert helpers in src/codeunits/RebateAuditMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T015 Implement error handler for categorized validation, configuration, posting, integration, concurrency, and unexpected errors in src/codeunits/RebateErrorHandler.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T016 Implement integration manager idempotency helpers in src/codeunits/RebateIntegrationMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T017 Implement job dispatcher chunk status helpers in src/codeunits/RebateJobDispatcher.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T018 Update src/permissions/RebatePermissions.PermissionSet.al with viewer, analyst, manager, approver, poster, administrator, and auditor access to foundational objects
  • [X] T019 [P] Add unit tests for setup validation in src/tests/RebateSetupTests.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T020 [P] Add unit tests for audit immutability in src/tests/RebateAuditTests.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T021 [P] Add unit tests for integration idempotency helpers in src/tests/RebateIntegrationTests.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T022 [P] Add unit tests for error classification in src/tests/RebateErrorHandlerTests.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T023 Verify foundational compile readiness against application 27.0.0.0 symbols using src/**/*.al

Checkpoint: Foundation ready. User stories can now be implemented.

Phase 3: User Story 1 - Manage and Activate Rebate Agreements (Priority: P1)

Goal: Finance, procurement, and sales operations users can create, validate, approve, activate, version, and audit agreements.

Independent Test: Create a vendor rebate agreement, add eligibility and rule details, submit and approve it, activate it, and verify the active version is locked and auditable.

Tests for User Story 1

  • [X] T024 [P] [US1] Add agreement validation tests in src/tests/RebateAgreementTests.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T025 [P] [US1] Add rule validation tests in src/tests/RebateRuleTests.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T026 [P] [US1] Add agreement approval/versioning integration tests in src/tests/RebateAgreementWorkflowTests.Codeunit.al

Implementation for User Story 1

  • [X] T027 [P] [US1] Create agreement header table in src/tables/RebateAgreementHeader.Table.al
  • [X] T028 [P] [US1] Create agreement line table in src/tables/RebateAgreementLine.Table.al
  • [X] T029 [P] [US1] Create agreement version table in src/tables/RebateAgreementVersion.Table.al
  • [X] T030 [P] [US1] Create rule header table in src/tables/RebateRuleHeader.Table.al
  • [X] T031 [P] [US1] Create rule line table in src/tables/RebateRuleLine.Table.al
  • [X] T032 [P] [US1] Create threshold table in src/tables/RebateThreshold.Table.al
  • [X] T033 [US1] Implement agreement creation, status transitions, active-version locking, and revision creation in src/codeunits/RebateAgreementMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T034 [US1] Implement posting setup, overlap, threshold, and conflicting-rule validation in src/codeunits/RebateRuleValidator.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T035 [US1] Implement agreement approval status checks in src/codeunits/RebateWorkflowMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T036 [P] [US1] Create agreement list page in src/pages/RebateAgreementList.Page.al
  • [X] T037 [P] [US1] Create agreement card page in src/pages/RebateAgreementCard.Page.al
  • [X] T038 [P] [US1] Create rule worksheet page in src/pages/RebateRuleWorksheet.Page.al
  • [X] T039 [US1] Add audit calls for agreement create, edit, approve, activate, suspend, expire, and version compare in src/codeunits/RebateAgreementMgt.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T040 [US1] Update src/permissions/RebatePermissions.PermissionSet.al for agreement manager, approver, viewer, and auditor behavior

Checkpoint: User Story 1 is independently functional and testable.

Phase 4: User Story 2 - Calculate Rebate Accruals for Eligible Transactions (Priority: P1)

Goal: Rebate analysts can run deterministic manual or scheduled calculations for eligible posted sales and purchase invoice lines.

Independent Test: Prepare approved agreements and source transactions, run a calculation for a company/date scope, and verify calculated entries, rejected-line reasons, currency details, and idempotency.

Tests for User Story 2

  • [X] T041 [P] [US2] Add eligibility engine unit tests in src/tests/RebateEligibilityTests.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T042 [P] [US2] Add calculation engine unit tests in src/tests/RebateCalculationTests.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T043 [P] [US2] Add calculation idempotency integration tests in src/tests/RebateCalculationIdempotencyTests.Codeunit.al

Implementation for User Story 2

  • [X] T044 [P] [US2] Create calculation request table in src/tables/RebateCalculationRequest.Table.al

  • [X] T046 [US2] Implement source line eligibility for posted sales and purchase invoice lines in src/codeunits/RebateEligibilityEngine.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T047 [US2] Implement fixed, percentage, tiered, slab, and growth calculation dispatch in src/codeunits/RebateCalculationEngine.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T048 [US2] Implement request idempotency keys, chunk progression, retry-safe status updates, and resumable processing in src/codeunits/RebateCalculationEngine.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T049 [US2] Implement calculation request dispatch through job queue helpers in src/codeunits/RebateJobDispatcher.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T050 [P] [US2] Create calculation request page in src/pages/RebateCalculationRequests.Page.al

  • [X] T052 [US2] Add integration events for eligibility and calculation extension points in src/codeunits/RebateEligibilityEngine.Codeunit.al and src/codeunits/RebateCalculationEngine.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T053 [US2] Update src/permissions/RebatePermissions.PermissionSet.al for analyst execution and viewer/auditor read access

Checkpoint: User Story 2 is independently functional and testable.

Phase 5: User Story 3 - Post, Reverse, and Reconcile Accruals (Priority: P1)

Goal: Controllers and rebate posters can preview, post, reverse, and reconcile accruals with balanced financial impact.

Independent Test: Calculate accruals, preview posting, post approved accruals, prevent duplicate posting, reverse one accrual, and reconcile accrual entries to financial references.

Tests for User Story 3

  • [X] T059 [US3] Implement posting preview, balanced posting preparation, dimension propagation, and duplicate-posting prevention in src/codeunits/RebatePostingEngine.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T060 [US3] Implement reversal entry creation with reason code and original entry links in src/codeunits/RebatePostingEngine.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T061 [P] [US3] Create accrual entries page in src/pages/RebateAccrualEntries.Page.al

  • [X] T065 [US3] Update src/permissions/RebatePermissions.PermissionSet.al for poster, controller, viewer, and auditor behavior

Checkpoint: User Story 3 is independently functional and testable.

Phase 6: User Story 4 - Settle Approved Rebate Balances (Priority: P2)

Goal: Finance users can generate, review, adjust, approve, post, and audit settlement proposals from open accrual balances.

Independent Test: Generate a settlement proposal, adjust with reason, approve and post it, and confirm selected accrual entries are closed.

Tests for User Story 4

  • [X] T076 [US4] Update src/permissions/RebatePermissions.PermissionSet.al for settlement analyst, approver, poster, viewer, and auditor behavior

Phase 7: User Story 5 - Recalculate Retroactive Changes and Post Deltas (Priority: P2)

Goal: Rebate managers and controllers can recalculate historical transactions for retroactive agreement changes and post only approved deltas.

Independent Test: Create a retroactive agreement version, run recalculation, review original/revised/delta amounts, approve deltas, and post linked adjustments.

Tests for User Story 5

  • [X] T078 [P] [US5] Add delta posting and zero-delta regression tests in src/tests/RebateDeltaPostingTests.Codeunit.al

Checkpoint: User Story 5 is independently functional and testable.

Phase 8: User Story 6 - Monitor Background Jobs, Integrations, and Audit Evidence (Priority: P3)

Goal: Administrators, integration support teams, and auditors can monitor jobs, retry integrations, export audit data, and inspect exceptions.

Independent Test: Run chunked processing, process duplicate external references, retry a failed integration, review job logs, and export audit evidence.

Tests for User Story 6

Implementation for User Story 6

  • [X] T094 [P] [US6] Create settlement export API page in src/api/RebateSettlementExportApi.Page.al
  • [X] T095 [P] [US6] Create audit export API page in src/api/RebateAuditExportApi.Page.al

  • [X] T098 [P] [US6] Create audit report in src/reports/RebateAudit.Report.al

Phase 9: Polish & Cross-Cutting Concerns

Purpose: Hardening that spans user stories after desired scope is implemented.

  • [X] T100 [P] Add performance harness for 100,000 and 1,000,000 source-line calculation scenarios in src/tests/RebatePerformanceHarness.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T101 [P] Add upgrade safety tests for immutable posted entries and agreement version compatibility in src/tests/RebateUpgradeSafetyTests.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T102 [P] Add source comments for public integration events in src/codeunits/RebateEligibilityEngine.Codeunit.al, src/codeunits/RebateCalculationEngine.Codeunit.al, src/codeunits/RebatePostingEngine.Codeunit.al, and src/codeunits/RebateSettlementEngine.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T103 Review all table keys and secondary indexes for calculation, settlement, reconciliation, and idempotency lookups in src/tables/*.Table.al
  • [X] T104 Review all loops for SetLoadFields, temporary buffers, and avoidable FlowField calculation in src/codeunits/*.Codeunit.al
  • [X] T105 Run quickstart validation scenarios from specs/001-global-rebate-engine/quickstart.md
  • [X] T106 Update implementation notes and task completion evidence in specs/001-global-rebate-engine/tasks.md
  • [X] T107 Verify final compile and tests against symbols in .alpackages/ without modifying app.json or .alpackages/

Dependencies & Execution Order

Phase Dependencies

  • Phase 1 Setup: No dependencies.
  • Phase 2 Foundational: Depends on Phase 1 and blocks all user stories.
  • Phase 3 US1: Depends on Phase 2.
  • Phase 4 US2: Depends on Phase 2 and can be developed after mock/fixture agreements exist, but full acceptance needs US1 activation.
  • Phase 5 US3: Depends on Phase 2 and calculation entries from US2.
  • Phase 6 US4: Depends on Phase 2 and open accrual entries from US3.
  • Phase 7 US5: Depends on Phase 2, agreement versions from US1, calculation entries from US2, and posting support from US3.
  • Phase 8 US6: Depends on Phase 2; API and monitoring tasks can start after related underlying story objects exist.
  • Phase 9 Polish: Depends on all implemented target stories.

User Story Completion Order

  1. US1 Manage and Activate Rebate Agreements: MVP setup and agreement control.
  2. US2 Calculate Rebate Accruals: Core calculation value.
  3. US3 Post, Reverse, and Reconcile Accruals: Financial correctness.
  4. US4 Settle Approved Rebate Balances: Commercial lifecycle completion.
  5. US5 Recalculate Retroactive Changes: Controlled correction and delta posting.
  6. US6 Monitor Background Jobs, Integrations, and Audit Evidence: Operationalization and external connectivity.

Parallel Opportunities

  • Setup enum tasks T003, T004, and T005 can run in parallel.
  • Foundational table/page/test tasks T007 through T013 and T019 through T022 can run in parallel.
  • Within each story, tests marked [P] can be authored before implementation in parallel.
  • Table/page/report/API tasks marked [P] can run in parallel within each story.
  • US1 and US2 can overlap after Phase 2 if US2 uses test fixtures for active agreements, then later integrates with US1.
  • US6 API page shell tasks can overlap with US4 and US5 once integration log foundations exist.

Parallel Execution Examples

User Story 1

Task: T024 Add agreement validation tests in src/tests/RebateAgreementTests.Codeunit.al
Task: T025 Add rule validation tests in src/tests/RebateRuleTests.Codeunit.al
Task: T027 Create agreement header table in src/tables/RebateAgreementHeader.Table.al
Task: T030 Create rule header table in src/tables/RebateRuleHeader.Table.al

User Story 2

Task: T041 Add eligibility engine unit tests in src/tests/RebateEligibilityTests.Codeunit.al
Task: T042 Add calculation engine unit tests in src/tests/RebateCalculationTests.Codeunit.al
Task: T044 Create calculation request table in src/tables/RebateCalculationRequest.Table.al
Task: T050 Create calculation request page in src/pages/RebateCalculationRequests.Page.al

User Story 3

Task: T054 Add accrual generation tests in src/tests/RebateAccrualTests.Codeunit.al
Task: T057 Create accrual entry table in src/tables/RebateAccrualEntry.Table.al
Task: T062 Create accrual summary report in src/reports/RebateAccrualSummary.Report.al
Task: T064 Create reconciliation report in src/reports/RebateReconciliation.Report.al

User Story 6

Task: T088 Add API idempotency contract tests in src/tests/RebateApiContractTests.Codeunit.al
Task: T092 Create agreement import API page in src/api/RebateAgreementApi.Page.al
Task: T093 Create calculation status API page in src/api/RebateCalculationStatusApi.Page.al
Task: T095 Create audit export API page in src/api/RebateAuditExportApi.Page.al

Implementation Strategy

MVP First

  1. Complete Phase 1 and Phase 2.
  2. Complete Phase 3 for agreement setup, approval, activation, versioning, and audit.
  3. Validate US1 independently using its tests and quickstart scenario.
  4. Continue with US2 and US3 to reach the first financially useful rebate lifecycle.

Incremental Delivery

  1. Agreement lifecycle.
  2. Calculation lifecycle.
  3. Accrual posting and reversal lifecycle.
  4. Settlement lifecycle.
  5. Recalculation and delta lifecycle.
  6. Monitoring, APIs, audit export, and performance hardening.

Quality Gates

  • Tests for a story should be written before its implementation tasks and initially fail.
  • Posted calculation, accrual, settlement, audit, and delta records must remain immutable.
  • All AL files must remain under src/.
  • No implementation may modify app.json or .alpackages/.
  • Object IDs must remain within 50000..50249.

Implementation Evidence

  • Generated AL source under src/ for tables, enums, codeunits, pages, API pages, reports, permissions, and test codeunits.
  • Verified compile with l compile /project:. /packagecachepath:.alpackages /out:./bsbench-04-vendor-management-github-speckit-codex.app on 2026-06-15.
  • Compiler completed with zero errors and produced sbench-04-vendor-management-github-speckit-codex.app.

Specification Quality Checklist: Global Rebate Engine

Purpose: Validate specification completeness and quality before proceeding to planning Created: 2026-06-15 Feature: spec.md

Content Quality

  • [x] No implementation details (languages, frameworks, APIs)
  • [x] Focused on user value and business needs
  • [x] Written for non-technical stakeholders
  • [x] All mandatory sections completed

Requirement Completeness

  • [x] No [NEEDS CLARIFICATION] markers remain
  • [x] Requirements are testable and unambiguous
  • [x] Success criteria are measurable
  • [x] Success criteria are technology-agnostic (no implementation details)
  • [x] All acceptance scenarios are defined
  • [x] Edge cases are identified
  • [x] Scope is clearly bounded
  • [x] Dependencies and assumptions identified

Feature Readiness

  • [x] All functional requirements have clear acceptance criteria
  • [x] User scenarios cover primary flows
  • [x] Feature meets measurable outcomes defined in Success Criteria
  • [x] No implementation details leak into specification

Notes

  • Validation passed. The source request included implementation and platform details, but this specification intentionally expresses stakeholder-facing behavior, business rules, controls, and measurable outcomes. Technical platform and architecture decisions are deferred to planning.
contracts/api-contract.md146 lines

API Contract: Global Rebate Engine

These contracts define the external interface behavior for AL API pages and integration logs. Exact page object names and IDs will be assigned during implementation from the allowed 50000..50249 object range.

Common Rules

  • Every inbound request must include externalReferenceId.
  • externalReferenceId must be unique per interface type and direction.
  • Repeated delivery with the same externalReferenceId must return the original result or current status without creating duplicate business records.
  • All requests and responses must create or update a Rebate Integration Log entry.
  • Sensitive credentials are not accepted in business payloads.
  • Errors return a user-facing message, technical correlation value, error category, retry eligibility, and related record reference when available.

Agreement Import

Purpose: Create or update draft rebate agreements from an external enterprise platform.

Operation: Upsert agreement import request.

Request fields:

  • externalReferenceId
  • agreementType
  • description
  • vendorNo
  • customerNo
  • customerGroupCode
  • currencyCode
  • startingDate
  • endingDate
  • countryRegionCode
  • locationCode
  • dimensions
  • settlementMethod
  • postingSetupCode
  • lines
  • rules
  • thresholds

Response fields:

  • externalReferenceId
  • agreementNo
  • status
  • validationMessages
  • integrationLogEntryNo

Acceptance contract:

  • Valid imports create or update draft agreements only.
  • Active agreements are not overwritten; changes create a controlled revision path.
  • Invalid imports do not partially activate agreements.

Calculation Status

Purpose: Allow external systems to query calculation or recalculation progress.

Operation: Read calculation request status by request number or external reference.

Response fields:

  • requestNo
  • externalReferenceId
  • requestType
  • status
  • totalChunks
  • completedChunks
  • failedChunks
  • recordsProcessed
  • recordsFailed
  • startedDateTime
  • completedDateTime
  • lastErrorCategory
  • lastErrorMessage
  • retryEligible

Acceptance contract:

  • Status is readable while processing is active.
  • Failed chunks and retry eligibility are visible.
  • Completed requests expose summary totals without exposing sensitive commercial setup unless caller is authorized.

Settlement Export

Purpose: Export approved or posted settlement details for external finance, vendor, customer, or reconciliation systems.

Operation: Read settlement header and lines by settlement number, party, period, or external reference.

Response fields:

  • settlementNo
  • externalReferenceId
  • status
  • companyName
  • partyType
  • partyNo
  • agreementNo
  • currencyCode
  • totalAmount
  • postingDate
  • postingReference
  • lines
  • auditReferences

Acceptance contract:

  • Settlement exports include only settlements the caller is authorized to view.
  • Repeated export requests with the same external reference do not create duplicate export logs.
  • Posted settlement lines include accrual entry references and closure status.

Audit Export

Purpose: Provide external audit evidence for selected companies, periods, users, agreements, documents, or action types.

Operation: Read audit entries by filter.

Request filters:

  • companyName
  • startingDateTime
  • endingDateTime
  • userId
  • agreementNo
  • sourceDocumentNo
  • action
  • externalReferenceId

Response fields:

  • auditEntryNo
  • companyName
  • dateTime
  • userId
  • action
  • sourceRecord
  • fieldCaption
  • oldValue
  • newValue
  • reasonCode
  • externalReferenceId

Acceptance contract:

  • Audit export is read-only.
  • Audit entries are not modified by export except for optional exported metadata if configured.
  • Unauthorized users cannot export audit records.
contracts/event-contract.md91 lines

Event Contract: Global Rebate Engine

The core engine must publish integration events so localization, customer-specific rules, and future apps can extend behavior without modifying posted history or core code.

Agreement and Rule Validation Events

  • OnBeforeValidateAgreementActivation
  • OnAfterValidateAgreementActivation
  • OnBeforeValidateRule
  • OnAfterValidateRule

Purpose: Add country-specific validation, posting setup checks, conflict detection, and controlled bypasses where permitted.

Rules:

  • Subscribers must not activate agreements directly.
  • Subscribers may add validation messages or block activation through the provided validation result pattern.
  • Validation must be deterministic for the same agreement version.

Eligibility Events

  • OnBeforeEvaluateSourceLine
  • OnAfterEvaluateSourceLine
  • OnBeforeRejectSourceLine

Purpose: Add or override eligibility details for country, party, source document, item, dimension, or campaign-specific rules.

Rules:

  • Subscribers must not mutate posted source transactions.
  • Subscribers must provide rejection reasons when blocking eligibility.
  • Subscribers must preserve idempotency keys for repeated processing.

Calculation Events

  • OnBeforeCalculateRuleAmount
  • OnAfterCalculateRuleAmount
  • OnBeforeCreateCalculationEntry
  • OnAfterCreateCalculationEntry

Purpose: Support custom formulas, growth calculations, rounding behavior, and local currency treatment.

Rules:

  • Subscribers must return deterministic amounts for the same inputs.
  • Subscribers must not edit posted calculation entries.
  • Custom calculation output must include basis, currency, and trace details.

Posting Events

  • OnBeforePrepareAccrualPosting
  • OnAfterPrepareAccrualPosting
  • OnBeforePrepareSettlementPosting
  • OnAfterPrepareSettlementPosting
  • OnBeforePrepareDeltaPosting

Purpose: Add country-specific tax, account, dimension, or settlement document behavior.

Rules:

  • Subscribers must preserve balanced posting output.
  • Subscribers must not bypass posting preview or duplicate-posting checks.
  • Subscribers must not edit posted accrual or settlement entries.

Settlement Events

  • OnBeforeBuildSettlementProposal
  • OnAfterBuildSettlementProposal
  • OnBeforeCloseAccrualEntry
  • OnAfterCloseAccrualEntry

Purpose: Customize grouping, settlement document type, minimum claim thresholds, and closure behavior.

Rules:

  • Subscribers must not select closed accrual entries.
  • Subscribers must respect approval status before posting.

Integration and Audit Events

  • OnBeforeLogIntegrationRequest
  • OnAfterLogIntegrationRequest
  • OnBeforeWriteAuditEntry
  • OnAfterWriteAuditEntry

Purpose: Enrich external logs, support additional correlation IDs, and add audit metadata.

Rules:

  • Subscribers must not suppress required audit records.
  • Subscribers must not expose sensitive data to unauthorized users.

Three verdicts per criterion

  • PASS — evidence proves the agent’s submission meets the spec.
  • FAIL — evidence proves it does not. Attribution is always agent_code.
  • INCONCLUSIVE — grader exhausted the verification hierarchy without reaching a verdict. Attribution: bc_platform, grader_env, or spec_ambiguous. Excluded from the axis denominator so the agent is not penalised for environmental issues.

Verification hierarchy (L1 → L4)

Real Business Central consultants verify in this order; the grader does too:

  • L1 — Find Entries / OData state. The database is the source of truth. After any posting action, query G_LEntries, Customer_Ledger_Entry, posted invoices.
  • L2 — Test codeunits with Library Assert. If the agent shipped tests, the grader runs them.
  • L3 — UI walkthrough via Playwright. User-realistic; captures screenshots for evidence.
  • L4 — Static AL review. Always available backstop. PASS if code invokes the right standard codeunits (e.g. SalesPost.Run, NoSeriesMgt.GetNextNo); FAIL if implementation is a stub, fabricates posting, or reinvents BC built-ins.

Cross-validation

Every task is graded by a council of 2–4 independent grader runs (Claude Opus 4.7 + GPT-5.5 ensemble). Second runs of each model are added on tasks where the first-run pair disagreed. The per-criterion verdict is the council majority; the axis score is then computed deterministically from PASS / FAIL counts. Within-model variance is dampened by criterion-level majority voting.

Scoring axes

The overall score is a weighted blend of three axes. Each axis is scored deterministically from its criteria’ PASS / FAIL counts (INCONCLUSIVE excluded), then combined — normalized over the axes a task was actually graded on.

  • Functional correctness (40%): does the published extension behave correctly at runtime? Verified via the BC web client and OData queries.
  • Code quality (30%): does the implementation use the right standard BC codeunits and AL idioms rather than stubs, fabricated posting, or reinvented built-ins?
  • Spec understanding (30%): does the submission reflect the requirements as specified — the right entities, fields, workflows and edge cases?
